- source_description: Owning Palette: Reference Data Processing VIs Requires: Sound and Vibration Toolkit Compensates an even-angle signal. You can use the compensated even-angle signal output to display unfiltered orbit and timebase plots with the runout pattern removed. Wire data to the even-angle signal input to deter

### OAT Compensate Even Angle Signal VI

**Owning Palette:**
 Reference Data Processing VIs

**Requires:**
 Sound and Vibration Toolkit

Compensates an even-angle signal. You can use the 
 **compensated even-angle signal**
 output to display unfiltered orbit and timebase plots with the runout pattern removed. Wire data to the 
 **even-angle signal**
 input to determine the polymorphic instance to use or manually select the instance.

| OAT Compensate Even Angle Signal (Even Angle Ref) (1 Ch)OAT Compensate Even Angle Signal (Even Angle Ref) (N Ch)OAT Compensate Even Angle Signal (Vector Ref) (1 Ch)OAT Compensate Even Angle Signal (Vector Ref) (N Ch) |
| --- |

OAT Compensate Even Angle Signal (Even Angle Ref) (1 Ch)

[IMAGE alt='image' src='images/OAT_Compensate_Even_Angle_Signal_Even_1_Ch.gif']

|  | reset? specifies the initialization of the internal states. The first time this VI runs, or when reset? is TRUE, the internal states initialize to zero. If reset? is FALSE, this VI sets the internal states to the final states from the previous call to the VI. The default is FALSE. |
| --- | --- |
|  | even-angle signal specifies the even-angle-resampled data. even-angled signal is synchronous to the rotational speed with a constant number of samples per revolution. r0 specifies the starting index value for counting revolutions. dr specifies the revolution interval between two sequential samples. Y specifies the value of the even-angle-spaced sample in a scaled engineering unit. |
|  | r0 specifies the starting index value for counting revolutions. |
|  | dr specifies the revolution interval between two sequential samples. |
|  | Y specifies the value of the even-angle-spaced sample in a scaled engineering unit. |
|  | even-angle reference specifies the even-angle reference the VI subtracts from even-angle signal . When even-angle reference is empty, this VI automatically chooses the first revolution samples in even-angle signal as references. r0 specifies the start of the revolution index. dr specifies the revolution interval between two sequential samples. Y specifies the values of even-angle spaced samples in a scaled engineering unit. |
|  | r0 specifies the start of the revolution index. |
|  | dr specifies the revolution interval between two sequential samples. |
|  | Y specifies the values of even-angle spaced samples in a scaled engineering unit. |
|  | even-angle position info specifies the position information of even-angle signal . positions [s] specifies the locations of the samples, in seconds. samples/rev specifies the number of samples per revolution. |
|  | positions [s] specifies the locations of the samples, in seconds. |
|  | samples/rev specifies the number of samples per revolution. |
|  | speed profile specifies the tachometer-pulse arrival time and the rotational speed at each pulse. time [s] specifies the time instant of pulses in seconds. speed [RPM] specifies the rotational speed at each pulse time instant in revolutions per minute. |
|  | time [s] specifies the time instant of pulses in seconds. |
|  | speed [RPM] specifies the rotational speed at each pulse time instant in revolutions per minute. |
|  | error in describes error conditions that occur before this node runs. This input provides standard error in functionality. |
|  | compensated even-angle signal returns the compensated even-angle signal. r0 returns the start of the revolution index. dr returns the revolution interval between two sequential samples. Y returns the values of even-angle spaced samples in a scaled engineering unit. |
|  | r0 returns the start of the revolution index. |
|  | dr returns the revolution interval between two sequential samples. |
|  | Y returns the values of even-angle spaced samples in a scaled engineering unit. |
|  | actual even-angle reference returns the even-angle reference the VI actually uses. r0 returns the start of the revolution index. dr returns the revolution interval between two sequential samples. Y returns the values of even-angle spaced samples in a scaled engineering unit. |
|  | r0 returns the start of the revolution index. |
|  | dr returns the revolution interval between two sequential samples. |
|  | Y returns the values of even-angle spaced samples in a scaled engineering unit. |
|  | even-angle position info out returns the position information of compensated even-angle signal . positions [s] returns the locations of the samples in seconds. samples/rev returns the number of samples per revolution. |

## TOPIC 00016: OAT Compensate Vector Data VI

- bundle_id: `labview-sound-and-vibration-toolkit-api-ref`
- source_path: `oat-compensate-vector-data-vi.html`
- source_url: https://docs-be.ni.com/bundle/labview-sound-and-vibration-toolkit-api-ref/raw/resource/enus/oat-compensate-vector-data-vi.html
- document_id: `labview-sound-and-vibration-toolkit-api-ref`
- page_type: `leaf`
- content_type: `reference`
- source_description: Owning Palette: Reference Data Processing VIs Requires: Sound and Vibration Toolkit Compensates for the shaft bow and electrical variations in displacement transducer signals. This VI performs slow-roll compensation by subtracting vector references from the corresponding magnitudes [RMS] and phases

### OAT Compensate Vector Data VI

**Owning Palette:**
 Reference Data Processing VIs

**Requires:**
 Sound and Vibration Toolkit

Compensates for the shaft bow and electrical variations in displacement transducer signals. This VI performs slow-roll compensation by subtracting 
 **vector references**
 from the corresponding 
 **magnitudes [RMS]**
 and 
 **phases [phase lag]**
 . Wire data to the 
 **magnitudes [RMS]**
 and 
 **phases [phase lag]**
 inputs to determine the polymorphic instance to use or manually select the instance.

| OAT Compensate Vector Data (1 Ch)OAT Compensate Vector Data (N Ch) |
| --- |

OAT Compensate Vector Data (1 Ch)

[IMAGE alt='image' src='images/OAT_Compensate_Vector_Data_1_Ch.gif']

|  | reset? specifies the initialization of the internal states. The first time this VI runs, or when reset? is TRUE, the internal states initialize to zero. If reset? is FALSE, this VI sets the internal states to the final states from the previous call to the VI. The default is FALSE. |
| --- | --- |
|  | magnitudes [RMS] specifies the magnitude plot in RMS value. Set magnitudes [RMS] and vector references to the same orders. time/rev/RPM specifies the x-axis value of the plot. magnitude specifies the y-axis value of the plot. |
|  | time/rev/RPM specifies the x-axis value of the plot. |
|  | magnitude specifies the y-axis value of the plot. |
|  | phases [phase lag] specifies the phase plot in phase lag value. Set phases [phase lag] and vector references to the same orders. time/rev/RPM specifies the x-axis value of the plot. phase specifies the y-axis value of the plot, in phase lag value. |
|  | time/rev/RPM specifies the x-axis value of the plot. |
|  | phase specifies the y-axis value of the plot, in phase lag value. |
|  | vector references specifies slow roll vector references the VI subtracts from the vector signals. The default value is an empty array, which causes the VI to use the first data as vector references. The magnitudes and phases parameters specify the vector data. |
|  | error in describes error conditions that occur before this node runs. This input provides standard error in functionality. |
|  | view info specifies view information about the input magnitudes [RMS] and phases [phase lag] . view info contains the same information as the view input in the OAT Order Magnitude and Phase VI. |
|  | compensated magnitudes [RMS] returns the compensated magnitude plots in RMS value. time/rev/RPM returns the x-axis value of the plot. The unit you specified in x-axis selection determines the unit of time/rev/RPM . magnitude returns the y-axis, in RMS values, of the magnitude plot. |
|  | time/rev/RPM returns the x-axis value of the plot. The unit you specified in x-axis selection determines the unit of time/rev/RPM . |
|  | magnitude returns the y-axis, in RMS values, of the magnitude plot. |
|  | compensated phases [phase lag] returns the compensated phase plot in phase lag value. time/rev/RPM returns the x-axis value of the plot. The unit you specified in x-axis selection determines the unit of time/rev/RPM . phase returns the y-axis, in phase lag value, of the phase plot. |
|  | time/rev/RPM returns the x-axis value of the plot. The unit you specified in x-axis selection determines the unit of time/rev/RPM . |
|  | phase returns the y-axis, in phase lag value, of the phase plot. |
|  | actual vector references returns the slow roll vector references this VI actually uses. |
|  | error out contains error information. This output provides standard error out functionality. |

OAT Compensate Vector Data (N Ch)

[IMAGE alt='image' src='images/OAT_Compensate_Vector_Data_N_Ch.gif']

|  | reset? specifies the initialization of the internal states. The first time this VI runs, or when reset? is TRUE, the internal states initialize to zero. If reset? is FALSE, this VI sets the internal states to the final states from the previous call to the VI. The default is FALSE. |
| --- | --- |
|  | magnitudes [RMS] specifies the magnitude plots of multiple channels in RMS value. Set magnitudes [RMS] and vector references to the same orders. time/rev/RPM specifies the x-axis value of the plot. magnitude specifies the y-axis value of the plot. |
|  | time/rev/RPM specifies the x-axis value of the plot. |
|  | magnitude specifies the y-axis value of the plot. |
|  | phases [phase lag] specifies the phase plot of multiple channels in phase lag value. Set phases [phase lag] and vector references to the same orders. time/rev/RPM specifies the x-axis value of the plot. phase specifies the y-axis value of the plot, in phase lag value. |
|  | time/rev/RPM specifies the x-axis value of the plot. |
|  | phase specifies the y-axis value of the plot, in phase lag value. |
|  | vector references specifies slow roll vector references the VI subtracts from the vector signals. The default value is an empty array, which causes the VI to use the first data as vector references. The magnitudes and phases parameters specify the vector data. |
|  | error in describes error conditions that occur before this node runs. This input provides standard error in functionality. |
|  | view info specifies view information about the input magnitudes [RMS] and phases [phase lag] . view info contains the same information as the view input in the OAT Order Magnitude and Phase VI. |
|  | compensated magnitudes [RMS] returns the compensated magnitude plots for each channel in RMS value. time/rev/RPM returns the x-axis value of the plot. The unit you specified in x-axis selection determines the unit of time/rev/RPM . magnitude returns the y-axis, in RMS values, of the magnitude plot. |
|  | time/rev/RPM returns the x-axis value of the plot. The unit you specified in x-axis selection determines the unit of time/rev/RPM . |
|  | magnitude returns the y-axis, in RMS values, of the magnitude plot. |
|  | compensated phases [phase lag] returns the compensated phase plot for each channel in phase lag value. time/rev/RPM returns the x-axis value of the plot. The unit you specified in x-axis selection determines the unit of time/rev/RPM . phase returns the y-axis, in phase lag value, of the phase plot. |
|  | time/rev/RPM returns the x-axis value of the plot. The unit you specified in x-axis selection determines the unit of time/rev/RPM . |
|  | phase returns the y-axis, in phase lag value, of the phase plot. |
|  | actual vector references returns the slow roll vector references this VI actually uses for each channel. |
|  | error out contains error information. This output provides standard error out functionality. |

<!--NI_TOPIC bundle=labview-sound-and-vibration-toolkit-api-ref path=oat-convert-speed-waveform-to-speed-profile-v.html language=enus -->
## TOPIC 00017: OAT Convert Speed Waveform to Speed Profile VI

- bundle_id: `labview-sound-and-vibration-toolkit-api-ref`
- source_path: `oat-convert-speed-waveform-to-speed-profile-v.html`
- source_url: https://docs-be.ni.com/bundle/labview-sound-and-vibration-toolkit-api-ref/raw/resource/enus/oat-convert-speed-waveform-to-speed-profile-v.html
- document_id: `labview-sound-and-vibration-toolkit-api-ref`
- page_type: `leaf`
- content_type: `reference`
- source_description: Owning Palette: Tachometer Processing VIs Requires: Sound and Vibration Toolkit Use this VI to convert a saved history of speed data sampled at regular time intervals to a speed profile suitable for order analysis with the Sound and Vibration Measurement Suite VIs. Minimum Data Requirements This fun

### OAT Convert Speed Waveform to Speed Profile VI

**Owning Palette:**
 Tachometer Processing VIs

**Requires:**
 Sound and Vibration Toolkit

Use this VI to convert a saved history of speed data sampled at regular time intervals to a speed profile suitable for order analysis with the Sound and Vibration Measurement Suite VIs.

**Minimum Data Requirements**
 This function requires a minimum amount of tachometer speed data before an output will be returned. 
 **pulse/revolution**
 and 
 **reset?**
 inputs will affect the amount of data required before an output is returned.

[IMAGE alt='image' src='images/OAT_Convert_Speed_Waveform_to_Speed_Profile.gif']

|  | reset? specifies the initialization of the internal states. The first time this VI runs, or when reset? is TRUE, the internal states initialize to zero. If reset? is FALSE, this VI sets the internal states to the final states from the previous call to the VI. The default is FALSE. |
| --- | --- |
|  | speed waveform is the time history of speeds such as that recorded from a SCADA recording system. Units are in RPM versus time. dt is the interval between samples and t0 is the timestamp of the first sample. |
|  | pulse/revolution specifies the number of pulses the tachometer generates during each revolution. The default is 1. |
|  | error in describes error conditions that occur before this node runs. This input provides standard error in functionality. |
|  | speed profile returns information about the tachometer pulse arrival time and the rotational speed at each pulse. time [s] returns the time instant of pulses in seconds. speed [RPM] returns the rotational speed at each pulse time instant in revolutions per minute. |
|  | time [s] returns the time instant of pulses in seconds. |
|  | speed [RPM] returns the rotational speed at each pulse time instant in revolutions per minute. |
|  | error out contains error information. This output provides standard error out functionality. |

Parent topic:

Tachometer Processing VIs

## TOPIC 00018: OAT Convert Speed Waveform to XY Graph VI

- bundle_id: `labview-sound-and-vibration-toolkit-api-ref`
- source_path: `oat-convert-speed-waveform-to-xy-graph-vi.html`
- source_url: https://docs-be.ni.com/bundle/labview-sound-and-vibration-toolkit-api-ref/raw/resource/enus/oat-convert-speed-waveform-to-xy-graph-vi.html
- document_id: `labview-sound-and-vibration-toolkit-api-ref`
- page_type: `leaf`
- content_type: `reference`
- source_description: Owning Palette: Tachometer Processing VIs Requires: Sound and Vibration Toolkit Converts a waveform speed profile to an XY graph by extracting the information attached to the waveform speed profile. waveform speed profile specifies information about how speed changes versus time. waveform speed prof

### OAT Convert Speed Waveform to XY Graph VI

**Owning Palette:**
 Tachometer Processing VIs

**Requires:**
 Sound and Vibration Toolkit

Converts a waveform speed profile to an XY graph by extracting the information attached to the waveform speed profile.

[IMAGE alt='image' src='images/OAT_Convert_Speed_Waveform_to_XY_Graph.gif']

|  | waveform speed profile specifies information about how speed changes versus time. waveform speed profile uses the output of the OAT Convert Speed XY Graph to Waveform VI. Otherwise, the output speed profile XY graph is empty. |
| --- | --- |
|  | error in describes error conditions that occur before this node runs. This input provides standard error in functionality. |
|  | dup waveform speed profile returns waveform speed profile unchanged. |
|  | speed profile returns information about the tachometer pulse arrival time and the rotational speed at each pulse. time [s] returns the time instant of pulses in seconds. speed [RPM] returns the rotational speed at each pulse time instant in revolutions per minute. |
|  | time [s] returns the time instant of pulses in seconds. |
|  | speed [RPM] returns the rotational speed at each pulse time instant in revolutions per minute. |
|  | pulse/revolution returns the number of pulses the tachometer generates during each revolution. |
|  | rotation direction returns the direction in which the shaft rotates. |
|  | error out contains error information. This output provides standard error out functionality. |

Tachometer Processing VIs

<!--NI_TOPIC bundle=labview-sound-and-vibration-toolkit-api-ref path=oat-convert-speed-xy-graph-to-waveform-vi.html language=enus -->
## TOPIC 00019: OAT Convert Speed XY Graph to Waveform VI

- bundle_id: `labview-sound-and-vibration-toolkit-api-ref`
- source_path: `oat-convert-speed-xy-graph-to-waveform-vi.html`
- source_url: https://docs-be.ni.com/bundle/labview-sound-and-vibration-toolkit-api-ref/raw/resource/enus/oat-convert-speed-xy-graph-to-waveform-vi.html
- document_id: `labview-sound-and-vibration-toolkit-api-ref`
- page_type: `leaf`
- content_type: `reference`
- source_description: Owning Palette: Tachometer Processing VIs Requires: Sound and Vibration Toolkit Converts a speed profile XY graph to a waveform. This VI also attaches the information of the input speed profile XY graph and pulse-per-revolution to the output waveform. reset? specifies the initialization of the inter

### OAT Convert Speed XY Graph to Waveform VI

**Owning Palette:**
 Tachometer Processing VIs

**Requires:**
 Sound and Vibration Toolkit

Converts a speed profile XY graph to a waveform. This VI also attaches the information of the input speed profile XY graph and pulse-per-revolution to the output waveform.

[IMAGE alt='image' src='images/OAT_Convert_Speed_XY_Graph_to_Waveform.gif']

|  | reset? specifies the initialization of the internal states. The first time this VI runs, or when reset? is TRUE, the internal states initialize to zero. If reset? is FALSE, this VI sets the internal states to the final states from the previous call to the VI. The default is FALSE. |
| --- | --- |
|  | speed profile specifies the tachometer-pulse arrival time and the rotational speed at each pulse. time [s] specifies the time instant of pulses in seconds. speed [RPM] specifies the rotational speed at each pulse time instant in revolutions per minute. |
|  | time [s] specifies the time instant of pulses in seconds. |
|  | speed [RPM] specifies the rotational speed at each pulse time instant in revolutions per minute. |
|  | analog tacho signal specifies the input analog tachometer signal. |
|  | pulse/revolution specifies the number of pulses the tachometer generates during each revolution. The default is 1. |
|  | update rate (s) specifies the time interval of the output waveform speed profile. update rate (s) must be greater than 0. When update rate (s) is equal to or less than 0, this VI sets update rate (s) to 0.01. The default is 0.01. |
|  | error in describes error conditions that occur before this node runs. This input provides standard error in functionality. |
|  | rotation direction specifies the direction in which the shaft rotates. 0 CCW (default)—Counterclockwise 1 CW —Clockwise |
| 0 | CCW (default)—Counterclockwise |
| 1 | CW —Clockwise |
|  | waveform speed profile returns the waveform representation of the speed profile . |
|  | error out contains error information. This output provides standard error out functionality. |

Tachometer Processing VIs

## TOPIC 00020: OAT Convert to Even Angle Signal VI

- bundle_id: `labview-sound-and-vibration-toolkit-api-ref`
- source_path: `oat-convert-to-even-angle-signal-vi.html`
- source_url: https://docs-be.ni.com/bundle/labview-sound-and-vibration-toolkit-api-ref/raw/resource/enus/oat-convert-to-even-angle-signal-vi.html
- document_id: `labview-sound-and-vibration-toolkit-api-ref`
- page_type: `leaf`
- content_type: `reference`
- source_description: Owning Palette: Order Analysis VIs Requires: Sound and Vibration Toolkit Converts an even-time-spaced scaled signal to an even-angle-spaced signal by resampling the acquired signal. Wire data to the scaled signal [EU] input to determine the polymorphic instance to use or manually select the instance

### OAT Convert to Even Angle Signal VI

**Owning Palette:**
 Order Analysis VIs

**Requires:**
 Sound and Vibration Toolkit

Converts an even-time-spaced scaled signal to an even-angle-spaced signal by resampling the acquired signal. Wire data to the 
 **scaled signal [EU]**
 input to determine the polymorphic instance to use or manually select the instance.

Minimum Data Requirements

reset?

analysis method

pulse/revolution

max order

zero delay?

Note

Details

| OAT Convert to Even Angle Signal (1 Ch)OAT Convert to Even Angle Signal (N Ch) |
| --- |

OAT Convert to Even Angle Signal (1 Ch)

[IMAGE alt='image' src='images/OAT_Convert_to_Even_Angle_Signal_1_Ch.gif']

|  | reset? specifies the initialization of the internal states. The first time this VI runs, or when reset? is TRUE, the internal states initialize to zero. If reset? is FALSE, this VI sets the internal states to the final states from the previous call to the VI. The default is FALSE. |
| --- | --- |
|  | analysis method specifies the analysis method this VI uses to convert the scaled signal [EU] to even-angle signal . 0 fast (default)The fast method can generate accurate results suitable for most applications and works well with both analog and digital tachometer signals. 1 accurate The accurate method can generate more accurate results than the fast method, especially if you are performing run-up or run-down tests with high speed transition rates. However, the accurate method requires an accurate tachometer pulse measurement to generate accurate results. Typically, using a counter to acquire digital tachometer signals yields an accurate tachometer pulse position. This method also is approximately 30% slower than the fast method. |
| 0 | fast (default)The fast method can generate accurate results suitable for most applications and works well with both analog and digital tachometer signals. |
| 1 | accurate The accurate method can generate more accurate results than the fast method, especially if you are performing run-up or run-down tests with high speed transition rates. However, the accurate method requires an accurate tachometer pulse measurement to generate accurate results. Typically, using a counter to acquire digital tachometer signals yields an accurate tachometer pulse position. This method also is approximately 30% slower than the fast method. |
|  | scaled signal [EU] specifies the scaled signal expressed in the selected engineering units. To obtain a properly scaled signal, you can use the SVL Scale Voltage to EU VI or define a virtual channel in National Instruments Measurement & Automation Explorer (MAX). t0 specifies the start time of the waveform. dt specifies the time interval in seconds between data points in the waveform. Y specifies the data values of the waveform. |
|  | t0 specifies the start time of the waveform. |
|  | dt specifies the time interval in seconds between data points in the waveform. |
|  | Y specifies the data values of the waveform. |
|  | speed profile specifies the tachometer-pulse arrival time and the rotational speed at each pulse. time [s] specifies the time instant of pulses in seconds. speed [RPM] specifies the rotational speed at each pulse time instant in revolutions per minute. |
|  | time [s] specifies the time instant of pulses in seconds. |
|  | speed [RPM] specifies the rotational speed at each pulse time instant in revolutions per minute. |
|  | pulse/revolution specifies the number of pulses the tachometer generates during each revolution. The default is 1. |
|  | error in describes error conditions that occur before this node runs. This input provides standard error in functionality. |
|  | max order specifies the maximum order to analyze. If the value of this parameter is less than or equal to zero, this VI automatically sets the maximum order to analyze. The maximum order used might be larger than the one you specify. Review actual max order entry for calculation. The default is 0 (auto). |
|  | zero delay? specifies whether a filter delay exists between the input signal and the output signal. The default is FALSE. |
|  | even-angle signal returns data resampled at even-angle intervals. This parameter is synchronous to the rotational speed with constant number of samples per revolution. r0 returns the starting index value for counting revolutions. dr returns the revolution interval between two sequential samples. Y returns the values of even-angle spaced samples in a scaled engineering unit. |
|  | r0 returns the starting index value for counting revolutions. |
|  | dr returns the revolution interval between two sequential samples. |
|  | Y returns the values of even-angle spaced samples in a scaled engineering unit. |
|  | even-angle channel info returns information that you can pass to another VI about the measurement configuration for even-angle signal . Note You must wire even-angle channel info when performing extended measurements. Do not modify the even-angle channel info values. If you want to view the information, you can create a custom probe. |
|  | even-angle position info returns the position information of even-angle signals . positions [s] returns the locations of the samples in seconds. samples/rev returns the number of samples per revolution. |
|  | positions [s] returns the locations of the samples in seconds. |
|  | samples/rev returns the number of samples per revolution. |
|  | error out contains error information. This output provides standard error out functionality. |
|  | actual max order is the actual measured maximum order this VI uses. actual max order is calculated using max order and pulse/revolution : |

OAT Convert to Even Angle Signal (N Ch)

[IMAGE alt='image' src='images/OAT_Convert_to_Even_Angle_Signal_N_Ch.gif']

|  | reset? specifies the initialization of the internal states. The first time this VI runs, or when reset? is TRUE, the internal states initialize to zero. If reset? is FALSE, this VI sets the internal states to the final states from the previous call to the VI. The default is FALSE. |
| --- | --- |
|  | analysis method specifies the analysis method this VI uses to convert the scaled signal [EU] to even-angle signal . 0 fast (default)The fast method can generate accurate results suitable for most applications and works well with both analog and digital tachometer signals. 1 accurate The accurate method can generate more accurate results than the fast method, especially if you are performing run-up or run-down tests with high speed transition rates. However, the accurate method requires an accurate tachometer pulse measurement to generate accurate results. Typically, using a counter to acquire digital tachometer signals yields an accurate tachometer pulse position. This method also is approximately 30% slower than the fast method. |
| 0 | fast (default)The fast method can generate accurate results suitable for most applications and works well with both analog and digital tachometer signals. |
| 1 | accurate The accurate method can generate more accurate results than the fast method, especially if you are performing run-up or run-down tests with high speed transition rates. However, the accurate method requires an accurate tachometer pulse measurement to generate accurate results. Typically, using a counter to acquire digital tachometer signals yields an accurate tachometer pulse position. This method also is approximately 30% slower than the fast method. |
|  | scaled signals specifies the scaled signals expressed in the selected engineering units. To obtain a properly scaled signal, you can use the SVL Scale Voltage to EU VI or define a virtual channel in National Instruments Measurement & Automation Explorer (MAX). t0 specifies the start time of the waveform. dt specifies the time interval in seconds between data points in the waveform. Y specifies the data values of the waveform. |
|  | t0 specifies the start time of the waveform. |
|  | dt specifies the time interval in seconds between data points in the waveform. |
|  | Y specifies the data values of the waveform. |
|  | speed profile specifies the tachometer-pulse arrival time and the rotational speed at each pulse. time [s] specifies the time instant of pulses in seconds. speed [RPM] specifies the rotational speed at each pulse time instant in revolutions per minute. |
|  | time [s] specifies the time instant of pulses in seconds. |
|  | speed [RPM] specifies the rotational speed at each pulse time instant in revolutions per minute. |
|  | pulse/revolution specifies the number of pulses the tachometer generates during each revolution. The default is 1. |
|  | error in describes error conditions that occur before this node runs. This input provides standard error in functionality. |
|  | max order specifies the maximum order to analyze. If the value of this parameter is less than or equal to zero, this VI automatically sets the maximum order to analyze. The maximum order used might be larger than the one you specify. Review actual max order entry for calculation. The default is 0 (auto). |
|  | zero delay? specifies whether a filter delay exists between the input signal and the output signal. The default is FALSE. |
|  | even-angle signals returns the data resampled at even-angle intervals for each channel. even-angle signals are synchronous to the rotational speed with constant number of samples per revolution. r0 returns the starting index value for counting revolutions. dr returns the revolution interval between two sequential samples. Y returns the values of even-angle spaced samples in a scaled engineering unit. |
|  | r0 returns the starting index value for counting revolutions. |
|  | dr returns the revolution interval between two sequential samples. |
|  | Y returns the values of even-angle spaced samples in a scaled engineering unit. |
|  | even-angle channel info returns information that you can pass to another VI about the measurement configuration for even-angle signal . Note You must wire even-angle channel info when performing extended measurements. Do not modify the even-angle channel info values. If you want to view the information, you can create a custom probe. |
|  | even-angle position info returns the position information of even-angle signals . positions [s] returns the locations of the samples in seconds. samples/rev returns the number of samples per revolution. |
|  | positions [s] returns the locations of the samples in seconds. |
|  | samples/rev returns the number of samples per revolution. |
|  | error out contains error information. This output provides standard error out functionality. |
|  | actual max order is the actual measured maximum order this VI uses. actual max order is calculated using max order and pulse/revolution : |

OAT Convert to Even Angle Signal Details
 The output signal is synchronous to the rotational speed with a constant number of samples per revolution.

Use the SVPO Convert to Even Angle Signal VI to convert an even-time-spaced scaled signal to an even-angle-spaced signal on embedded targets that have limited computational resources.

Parent topic:

Order Analysis VIs

<!--NI_TOPIC bundle=labview-sound-and-vibration-toolkit-api-ref path=oat-extract-order-waveforms-vi.html language=enus -->
## TOPIC 00021: OAT Extract Order Waveforms VI

- bundle_id: `labview-sound-and-vibration-toolkit-api-ref`
- source_path: `oat-extract-order-waveforms-vi.html`
- source_url: https://docs-be.ni.com/bundle/labview-sound-and-vibration-toolkit-api-ref/raw/resource/enus/oat-extract-order-waveforms-vi.html
- document_id: `labview-sound-and-vibration-toolkit-api-ref`
- page_type: `leaf`
- content_type: `reference`
- source_description: Owning Palette: Order Analysis VIs Requires: Sound and Vibration Toolkit Extracts order waveforms from specified orders in the scaled signal [EU] . This VI can return order waveforms of a single order or combined orders. Use orders to extract to specify the desired orders. This VI does not maintain

### OAT Extract Order Waveforms VI

**Owning Palette:**
 Order Analysis VIs

**Requires:**
 Sound and Vibration Toolkit

Extracts order waveforms from specified orders in the 
 **scaled signal [EU]**
 . This VI can return order waveforms of a single order or combined orders. Use 
 **orders to extract**
 to specify the desired orders. This VI does not maintain state internally; you can use this VI in a loop to process multiple blocks. Wire data to the 
 **scaled signal [EU]**
 input to determine the polymorphic instance to use or manually select the instance.

**Minimum Data Requirements**
 This function requires a minimum amount of signal and speed profile data before an output will be returned. 
 **orders to extract**
 , 
 **bandwidth**
 , and 
 **window info**
 will affect the amount of data required before an output is returned.

| OAT Extract Order Waveforms (Single Order)OAT Extract Order Waveforms (Combined Orders) |
| --- |

OAT Extract Order Waveforms (Single Order)

[IMAGE alt='image' src='images/OAT_Extract_Order_Waveforms_Single.gif']

|  | scaled signal [EU] specifies the scaled signal expressed in the selected engineering units. To obtain a properly scaled signal, you can use the SVL Scale Voltage to EU VI or define a virtual channel in National Instruments Measurement & Automation Explorer (MAX). t0 specifies the start time of the waveform. dt specifies the time interval in seconds between data points in the waveform. Y specifies the data values of the waveform. |
| --- | --- |
|  | t0 specifies the start time of the waveform. |
|  | dt specifies the time interval in seconds between data points in the waveform. |
|  | Y specifies the data values of the waveform. |
|  | speed profile specifies the tachometer-pulse arrival time and the rotational speed at each pulse. time [s] specifies the time instant of pulses in seconds. speed [RPM] specifies the rotational speed at each pulse time instant in revolutions per minute. |
|  | time [s] specifies the time instant of pulses in seconds. |
|  | speed [RPM] specifies the rotational speed at each pulse time instant in revolutions per minute. |
|  | orders to extract specifies the orders from which you want to extract the waveforms. single order specifies the current single order from which this VI extracts the waveform. |
|  | single order specifies the current single order from which this VI extracts the waveform. |
|  | error in describes error conditions that occur before this node runs. This input provides standard error in functionality. |
|  | bandwidth specifies the bandwidth settings that the Gabor filter uses. bandwidth value specifies the value of the bandwidth. The default is 0.5. bandwidth unit specifies the unit of bandwidth. 0 Order (default) 1 Hz |
|  | bandwidth value specifies the value of the bandwidth. The default is 0.5. |
|  | bandwidth unit specifies the unit of bandwidth. 0 Order (default) 1 Hz |
| 0 | Order (default) |
| 1 | Hz |
|  | window info specifies the window information for the Gabor transform and expansion. window type specifies the type of window to apply. 0 None 1 Hanning 2 Hamming 3 Blackman-Harris 4 Exact Blackman 5 Blackman 6 Flat Top 7 4 Term B-Harris 8 7 Term B-Harris 9 Low Sidelobe 10 Gaussian (default) window length specifies the length of the window to apply to the Gabor transform and expansion. window length must be a power of two and greater than or equal to 128. This VI rounds window length to the nearest valid value when window length is less than 128 or not a power of two. Increasing window length can improve the frequency resolution but decreases the time resolution. |
|  | window type specifies the type of window to apply. 0 None 1 Hanning 2 Hamming 3 Blackman-Harris 4 Exact Blackman 5 Blackman 6 Flat Top 7 4 Term B-Harris 8 7 Term B-Harris 9 Low Sidelobe 10 Gaussian (default) |
| 0 | None |
| 1 | Hanning |
| 2 | Hamming |
| 3 | Blackman-Harris |
| 4 | Exact Blackman |
| 5 | Blackman |
| 6 | Flat Top |
| 7 | 4 Term B-Harris |
| 8 | 7 Term B-Harris |
| 9 | Low Sidelobe |
| 10 | Gaussian (default) |
|  | window length specifies the length of the window to apply to the Gabor transform and expansion. window length must be a power of two and greater than or equal to 128. This VI rounds window length to the nearest valid value when window length is less than 128 or not a power of two. Increasing window length can improve the frequency resolution but decreases the time resolution. |
|  | order waveforms returns the order waveforms with the highest power. |
|  | unit label returns the selected engineering units. |
|  | error out contains error information. This output provides standard error out functionality. |
|  | actual window info returns the actual window settings for the Gabor transformation and expansion. window type returns the type of window you apply to the Gabor transform and expansion. 0 None 1 Hanning 2 Hamming 3 Blackman-Harris 4 Exact Blackman 5 Blackman 6 Flat Top 7 4 Term B-Harris 8 7 Term B-Harris 9 Low Sidelobe 10 Gaussian (default) window length specifies the length of the window to apply to the Gabor transform and expansion. window length must be a power of two and greater than or equal to 128. This VI rounds window length to the nearest valid value when window length is less than 128 or not a power of two. Increasing window length can improve the frequency resolution but decreases the time resolution. |
|  | window type returns the type of window you apply to the Gabor transform and expansion. 0 None 1 Hanning 2 Hamming 3 Blackman-Harris 4 Exact Blackman 5 Blackman 6 Flat Top 7 4 Term B-Harris 8 7 Term B-Harris 9 Low Sidelobe 10 Gaussian (default) |

Increasing window length can improve the frequency resolution but decreases the time resolution. |

OAT Extract Order Waveforms (Combined Orders)

[IMAGE alt='image' src='images/OAT_Extract_Order_Waveforms_Combined.gif']

|  | scaled signal [EU] specifies the scaled signal expressed in the selected engineering units. To obtain a properly scaled signal, you can use the SVL Scale Voltage to EU VI or define a virtual channel in National Instruments Measurement & Automation Explorer (MAX). t0 specifies the start time of the waveform. dt specifies the time interval in seconds between data points in the waveform. Y specifies the data values of the waveform. |
| --- | --- |
|  | t0 specifies the start time of the waveform. |
|  | dt specifies the time interval in seconds between data points in the waveform. |
|  | Y specifies the data values of the waveform. |
|  | speed profile specifies the tachometer-pulse arrival time and the rotational speed at each pulse. time [s] specifies the time instant of pulses in seconds. speed [RPM] specifies the rotational speed at each pulse time instant in revolutions per minute. |
|  | time [s] specifies the time instant of pulses in seconds. |
|  | speed [RPM] specifies the rotational speed at each pulse time instant in revolutions per minute. |
|  | orders to extract specifies different groups of orders from which you want to extract the order waveforms. combined orders specifies multiple orders that this VI combines to return an order waveform. |
|  | combined orders specifies multiple orders that this VI combines to return an order waveform. |
|  | error in describes error conditions that occur before this node runs. This input provides standard error in functionality. |
|  | bandwidth specifies the bandwidth settings that the Gabor filter uses. bandwidth value specifies the value of the bandwidth. The default is 0.5. bandwidth unit specifies the unit of bandwidth. 0 Order (default) 1 Hz |
|  | bandwidth value specifies the value of the bandwidth. The default is 0.5. |
|  | bandwidth unit specifies the unit of bandwidth. 0 Order (default) 1 Hz |
| 0 | Order (default) |
| 1 | Hz |

Increasing window length can improve the frequency resolution but decreases the time resolution. |

## TOPIC 00022: OAT IIR Preprocess Filter VI

- bundle_id: `labview-sound-and-vibration-toolkit-api-ref`
- source_path: `oat-iir-preprocess-filter-vi.html`
- source_url: https://docs-be.ni.com/bundle/labview-sound-and-vibration-toolkit-api-ref/raw/resource/enus/oat-iir-preprocess-filter-vi.html
- document_id: `labview-sound-and-vibration-toolkit-api-ref`
- page_type: `leaf`
- content_type: `reference`
- source_description: Owning Palette: Weighting and Filtering VIs Requires: Sound and Vibration Toolkit Designs an IIR filter and filters the scaled signal [EU] . Each instance of this VI maintains its own state; use the N Waveforms instance of this VI to process an array of channels. Wire data to the scaled signal [EU]

### OAT IIR Preprocess Filter VI

**Owning Palette:**
 Weighting and Filtering VIs

**Requires:**
 Sound and Vibration Toolkit

Designs an IIR filter and filters the 
 **scaled signal [EU]**
 . Each instance of this VI maintains its own state; use the N Waveforms instance of this VI to process an array of channels. Wire data to the 
 **scaled signal [EU]**
 input to determine the polymorphic instance to use or manually select the instance.

| OAT IIR Preprocess Filter (1 Ch)OAT IIR Preprocess Filter (N Ch) |
| --- |

OAT IIR Preprocess Filter (1 Ch)

[IMAGE alt='image' src='images/OAT_IIR_Preprocess_Filter_1_Ch.gif']

|  | design method specifies the design method. The default is Butterworth. 0 Butterworth (default) 1 Chebyshev 2 Inverse Chebyshev 3 Elliptic |
| --- | --- |
| 0 | Butterworth (default) |
| 1 | Chebyshev |
| 2 | Inverse Chebyshev |
| 3 | Elliptic |
|  | reset? specifies the initialization of the internal states. The first time this VI runs, or when reset? is TRUE, the internal states initialize to zero. If reset? is FALSE, this VI sets the internal states to the final states from the previous call to the VI. The default is FALSE. |
|  | filter type specifies the type of filter. 0 Lowpass (default) 1 Highpass 2 Bandpass 3 Bandstop |
| 0 | Lowpass (default) |
| 1 | Highpass |
| 2 | Bandpass |
| 3 | Bandstop |
|  | scaled signal [EU] specifies the scaled signals expressed in the selected engineering units. To obtain a properly scaled signal, you can use the SVL Scale Voltage to EU VI or define a virtual channel in National Instruments Measurement & Automation Explorer (MAX). t0 specifies the start time of the waveform. dt specifies the time interval in seconds between data points in the waveform. Y specifies the data values of the waveform. |
|  | t0 specifies the start time of the waveform. |
|  | dt specifies the time interval in seconds between data points in the waveform. |
|  | Y specifies the data values of the waveform. |
|  | order specifies the filter order. The filter order must be greater than zero. When order increases, the transition band becomes narrower, and the time to get a stable output increases. The default is 2. |
|  | freq specs specifies the cut-off frequency in hertz. low cutoff freq specifies the low cutoff frequency. The default is 100 Hz. high cutoff freq specifies the high cutoff frequency. Use high cutoff frequency for bandpass or bandstop filter design. high cutoff freq must be greater than low cutoff freq . The default is 200 Hz. |
|  | low cutoff freq specifies the low cutoff frequency. The default is 100 Hz. |
|  | high cutoff freq specifies the high cutoff frequency. Use high cutoff frequency for bandpass or bandstop filter design. high cutoff freq must be greater than low cutoff freq . The default is 200 Hz. |
|  | ripple specs specifies the ripple level, in decibels, in both the passband and the stopband. passband ripple specifies the ripple level in the passband in decibels. The default is 0.1 dB. stopband attenuation specifies the ripple level in the stopband in decibels. The default is 60 dB. |
|  | passband ripple specifies the ripple level in the passband in decibels. The default is 0.1 dB. |
|  | stopband attenuation specifies the ripple level in the stopband in decibels. The default is 60 dB. |
|  | error in describes error conditions that occur before this node runs. This input provides standard error in functionality. |
|  | filtered scaled signal returns the filtered waveform expressed in the selected engineering units. t0 returns the start time of the waveform. dt returns the time interval, in seconds, between data points in the waveform. Y returns the data values of the waveform. |
|  | t0 returns the start time of the waveform. |
|  | dt returns the time interval, in seconds, between data points in the waveform. |
|  | Y returns the data values of the waveform. |
|  | error out contains error information. This output provides standard error out functionality. |

OAT IIR Preprocess Filter (N Ch)

[IMAGE alt='image' src='images/OAT_IIR_Preprocess_Filter_N_Ch.gif']

|  | design method specifies the design method. The default is Butterworth. 0 Butterworth (default) 1 Chebyshev 2 Inverse Chebyshev 3 Elliptic |
| --- | --- |
| 0 | Butterworth (default) |
| 1 | Chebyshev |
| 2 | Inverse Chebyshev |
| 3 | Elliptic |
|  | reset? specifies the initialization of the internal states. The first time this VI runs, or when reset? is TRUE, the internal states initialize to zero. If reset? is FALSE, this VI sets the internal states to the final states from the previous call to the VI. The default is FALSE. |
|  | filter type specifies the type of filter. 0 Lowpass (default) 1 Highpass 2 Bandpass 3 Bandstop |
| 0 | Lowpass (default) |
| 1 | Highpass |
| 2 | Bandpass |
| 3 | Bandstop |
|  | scaled signals [EU] specifies the scaled signals expressed in the selected engineering units. To obtain a properly scaled signal, you can use the SVL Scale Voltage to EU VI or define a virtual channel in National Instruments Measurement & Automation Explorer (MAX). t0 specifies the start time of the waveform. dt specifies the time interval in seconds between data points in the waveform. Y specifies the data values of the waveform. |
|  | t0 specifies the start time of the waveform. |
|  | dt specifies the time interval in seconds between data points in the waveform. |
|  | Y specifies the data values of the waveform. |
|  | order specifies the filter order. The filter order must be greater than zero. When order increases, the transition band becomes narrower, and the time to get a stable output increases. The default is 2. |
|  | freq specs specifies the cut-off frequency in hertz. low cutoff freq specifies the low cutoff frequency. The default is 100 Hz. high cutoff freq specifies the high cutoff frequency. Use high cutoff frequency for bandpass or bandstop filter design. high cutoff freq must be greater than low cutoff freq . The default is 200 Hz. |
|  | low cutoff freq specifies the low cutoff frequency. The default is 100 Hz. |
|  | high cutoff freq specifies the high cutoff frequency. Use high cutoff frequency for bandpass or bandstop filter design. high cutoff freq must be greater than low cutoff freq . The default is 200 Hz. |
|  | ripple specs specifies the ripple level, in decibels, in both the passband and the stopband. passband ripple specifies the ripple level in the passband in decibels. The default is 0.1 dB. stopband attenuation specifies the ripple level in the stopband in decibels. The default is 60 dB. |
|  | passband ripple specifies the ripple level in the passband in decibels. The default is 0.1 dB. |
|  | stopband attenuation specifies the ripple level in the stopband in decibels. The default is 60 dB. |
|  | error in describes error conditions that occur before this node runs. This input provides standard error in functionality. |
|  | filtered scaled signals returns the filtered waveforms for each channel expressed in the selected engineering units. t0 returns the start time of the waveform. dt returns the time interval, in seconds, between data points in the waveform. Y returns the data values of the waveform. |
|  | t0 returns the start time of the waveform. |
|  | dt returns the time interval, in seconds, between data points in the waveform. |
|  | Y returns the data values of the waveform. |
|  | error out contains error information. This output provides standard error out functionality. |

<!--NI_TOPIC bundle=labview-sound-and-vibration-toolkit-api-ref path=oat-index-2d-xy-signal-vi.html language=enus -->
## TOPIC 00023: OAT Index 2D XY Signal VI

- bundle_id: `labview-sound-and-vibration-toolkit-api-ref`
- source_path: `oat-index-2d-xy-signal-vi.html`
- source_url: https://docs-be.ni.com/bundle/labview-sound-and-vibration-toolkit-api-ref/raw/resource/enus/oat-index-2d-xy-signal-vi.html
- document_id: `labview-sound-and-vibration-toolkit-api-ref`
- page_type: `leaf`
- content_type: `reference`
- source_description: Owning Palette: Utility VIs Requires: Sound and Vibration Toolkit Returns a 1D XY signal array of a 2D XY signal array at a specific channel or order. This VI uses the row or column index value to determine which signal array to return. index selection specifies whether to index the 2D XY signal arr

### OAT Index 2D XY Signal VI

**Owning Palette:**
 Utility VIs

**Requires:**
 Sound and Vibration Toolkit

Returns a 1D XY signal array of a 2D XY signal array at a specific channel or order. This VI uses the row or column index value to determine which signal array to return.

[IMAGE alt='image' src='images/OAT_Index_2D_XY_Signal.gif']

|  | index selection specifies whether to index the 2D XY signal array with channels indexed by row and orders indexed by column. 0 Index Channel (default) 1 Index Order |
| --- | --- |
| 0 | Index Channel (default) |
| 1 | Index Order |
|  | 2D XY signal specifies a 2D XY signal array with channels indexed by row and orders indexed by column. X specifies the x-axis of the 2D XY signal . Y specifies the y-axis of the 2D XY signal . |
|  | X specifies the x-axis of the 2D XY signal . |
|  | Y specifies the y-axis of the 2D XY signal . |
|  | channel or order index specifies the channel or order at which you want to index the 2D XY signal. The default is 0. When channel or order index is less than zero or greater than N, where N is the number of channels or orders in the 2D XY signal , this VI returns an empty array. |
|  | error in describes error conditions that occur before this node runs. This input provides standard error in functionality. |
|  | XY signal returns the 1D XY signal array. X returns the x-axis of the XY signal out . Y specifies the y-axis of XY signal out . |
|  | X returns the x-axis of the XY signal out . |
|  | Y specifies the y-axis of XY signal out . |
|  | error out contains error information. This output provides standard error out functionality. |

Parent topic:

Utility VIs

<!--NI_TOPIC bundle=labview-sound-and-vibration-toolkit-api-ref path=oat-order-magnitude-and-phase-vi.html language=enus -->
## TOPIC 00024: OAT Order Magnitude and Phase VI

- bundle_id: `labview-sound-and-vibration-toolkit-api-ref`
- source_path: `oat-order-magnitude-and-phase-vi.html`
- source_url: https://docs-be.ni.com/bundle/labview-sound-and-vibration-toolkit-api-ref/raw/resource/enus/oat-order-magnitude-and-phase-vi.html
- document_id: `labview-sound-and-vibration-toolkit-api-ref`
- page_type: `leaf`
- content_type: `reference`
- source_description: Owning Palette: Order Analysis VIs Requires: Sound and Vibration Toolkit Computes the magnitudes and phases of designated orders in an even-angle signal. Use this VI to track particular orders specified by orders to compute . Wire data to the even-angle signal input to determine the polymorphic inst

### OAT Order Magnitude and Phase VI

**Owning Palette:**
 Order Analysis VIs

**Requires:**
 Sound and Vibration Toolkit

Computes the magnitudes and phases of designated orders in an even-angle signal. Use this VI to track particular orders specified by 
 **orders to compute**
 . Wire data to the 
 **even-angle signal**
 input to determine the polymorphic instance to use or manually select the instance.

**Minimum Data Requirements**
 This function requires a minimum amount of even angle data before an output will be returned. 
 **reset?**
 , 
 **orders to compute**
 , 
 **bandwidth**
 , 
 **view**
 , and 
 **zero delay?**
 will affect the amount of data required before an output is returned.

| OAT Order Magnitude and Phase (Even Angle Signal) (1 Ch)OAT Order Magnitude and Phase (Even Angle Signal) (N Ch)OAT Order Magnitude and Phase (Order Waveforms) |
| --- |

OAT Order Magnitude and Phase (Even Angle Signal) (1 Ch)

[IMAGE alt='image' src='images/OAT_Order_Magnitude_and_Phase_Even_1_Ch.gif']

|  | reset? specifies the initialization of the internal states. The first time this VI runs, or when reset? is TRUE, the internal states initialize to zero. If reset? is FALSE, this VI sets the internal states to the final states from the previous call to the VI. The default is FALSE. |
| --- | --- |
|  | x-axis selection specifies the scale type to use for the x-axis of the output XY plot. 0 Time 1 Revolution 2 RPM (default) |
| 0 | Time |
| 1 | Revolution |
| 2 | RPM (default) |
|  | even-angle signal specifies the even-angle-resampled data. even-angled signal is synchronous to the rotational speed with a constant number of samples per revolution. r0 specifies the starting index value for counting revolutions. dr specifies the revolution interval between two sequential samples. Y specifies the value of the even-angle-spaced sample in a scaled engineering unit. |
|  | r0 specifies the starting index value for counting revolutions. |
|  | dr specifies the revolution interval between two sequential samples. |
|  | Y specifies the value of the even-angle-spaced sample in a scaled engineering unit. |
|  | even-angle channel info specifies the information about the measurement configuration that this VI uses to acquire the resampled data. Note You must wire even-angle channel info when performing extended measurements. Do not modify the even-angle channel info values. If you want to view the information, you can create a custom probe. |
|  | even-angle position info specifies the position information of even-angle signal . positions [s] specifies the locations of the samples, in seconds. samples/rev specifies the number of samples per revolution. |
|  | positions [s] specifies the locations of the samples, in seconds. |
|  | samples/rev specifies the number of samples per revolution. |
|  | orders to compute specifies the orders the VI uses to compute magnitude and phase. The default is 1.0. |
|  | error in describes error conditions that occur before this node runs. This input provides standard error in functionality. |
|  | bandwidth specifies an order span centered at the order that you specify in orders to compute . Specify a bandwidth [order] value between 0 and 1 . If bandwidth [order] is less than 0 or greater than 1 , this VI sets bandwidth [order] to 1 . The default is 1. |
|  | view specifies the view settings for the magnitudes and phases . dB on? specifies whether to return the magnitude in decibels. If dB on? is FALSE, this VI returns the magnitude linearly. The default is FALSE. unwrap phase? (T) specifies whether to unwrap the phase. If unwrap phase? (T) is FALSE, this VI wraps the phase between 0 and 360 degrees, or between 0 and 2pi. If unwrap phase? (T) is TRUE, this VI removes discontinuities less than 0 degrees or greater than 360 degrees from the phase and does not restrict the phase to values between 0 and 360 degrees. The default is TRUE. unit in degree? specifies whether to return the phases in degrees. If unit in degree? is TRUE, this VI returns the phase in degrees. If unit in degree? is FALSE, this VI returns the phase in radians. The default is TRUE. |
|  | dB on? specifies whether to return the magnitude in decibels. If dB on? is FALSE, this VI returns the magnitude linearly. The default is FALSE. |
|  | unwrap phase? (T) specifies whether to unwrap the phase. If unwrap phase? (T) is FALSE, this VI wraps the phase between 0 and 360 degrees, or between 0 and 2pi. If unwrap phase? (T) is TRUE, this VI removes discontinuities less than 0 degrees or greater than 360 degrees from the phase and does not restrict the phase to values between 0 and 360 degrees. The default is TRUE. |
|  | unit in degree? specifies whether to return the phases in degrees. If unit in degree? is TRUE, this VI returns the phase in degrees. If unit in degree? is FALSE, this VI returns the phase in radians. The default is TRUE. |
|  | zero delay? specifies whether a filter delay exists between the input signal and the output signal. The default is FALSE. |
|  | unit labels returns the unit labels for the resulting magnitude and phase. x unit returns the engineering units for the x-axis. magnitude unit returns the engineering unit for the y-axis of the magnitude plot. phase unit returns the engineering unit for the y-axis of the phase plot. |
|  | x unit returns the engineering units for the x-axis. |
|  | magnitude unit returns the engineering unit for the y-axis of the magnitude plot. |
|  | phase unit returns the engineering unit for the y-axis of the phase plot. |
|  | magnitudes [RMS] returns the magnitude plot, in RMS values, of the specified orders. time/rev/RPM returns the x-axis value of the plot. The unit you specified in x-axis selection determines the unit of time/rev/RPM . magnitude returns the y-axis, in RMS values, of the magnitude plot. |
|  | time/rev/RPM returns the x-axis value of the plot. The unit you specified in x-axis selection determines the unit of time/rev/RPM . |
|  | magnitude returns the y-axis, in RMS values, of the magnitude plot. |
|  | phases [phase lag] returns the phase plot, in phase lag value, of the specified orders. time/rev/RPM returns the x-axis value of the plot. The unit you specified in x-axis selection determines the unit of time/rev/RPM . phase returns the y-axis, in phase lag value, of the phase plot. |
|  | time/rev/RPM returns the x-axis value of the plot. The unit you specified in x-axis selection determines the unit of time/rev/RPM . |
|  | phase returns the y-axis, in phase lag value, of the phase plot. |
|  | magnitude/phase position info returns the position information for magnitudes and phases . positions [s] returns the locations of the samples in seconds. samples/rev returns the number of samples per revolution. |
|  | positions [s] returns the locations of the samples in seconds. |
|  | samples/rev returns the number of samples per revolution. |
|  | magnitude info returns properties of the computed magnitudes. Note You must wire magnitude info when performing extended measurements. Do not modify the magnitude info values. If you want to view the information, you can create a custom probe. |
|  | error out contains error information. This output provides standard error out functionality. |

OAT Order Magnitude and Phase (Even Angle Signal) (N Ch)

[IMAGE alt='image' src='images/OAT_Order_Magnitude_and_Phase_Even_N_Ch.gif']

|  | reset? specifies the initialization of the internal states. The first time this VI runs, or when reset? is TRUE, the internal states initialize to zero. If reset? is FALSE, this VI sets the internal states to the final states from the previous call to the VI. The default is FALSE. |
| --- | --- |
|  | x-axis selection specifies the scale type to use for the x-axis of the output XY plot. 0 Time 1 Revolution 2 RPM (default) |
| 0 | Time |
| 1 | Revolution |
| 2 | RPM (default) |
|  | even-angle signals specifies the even-angle resampled data. even-angle signals are synchronous to the rotational speed with a constant number of samples per revolution. r0 specifies the starting index value for counting revolutions. dr specifies the revolution interval between two sequential samples. Y specifies the value of the even-angle-spaced sample in a scaled engineering unit. |
|  | r0 specifies the starting index value for counting revolutions. |
|  | dr specifies the revolution interval between two sequential samples. |
|  | Y specifies the value of the even-angle-spaced sample in a scaled engineering unit. |
|  | even-angle channel info specifies information that you can pass to another VI about the measurement configuration for even-angle signal . Note You must wire even-angle channel info when performing extended measurements. Do not modify the even-angle channel info values. If you want to view the information, you can create a custom probe. |
|  | even-angle position info specifies the position information of even-angle signals . positions [s] specifies the locations of the samples, in seconds. samples/rev specifies the number of samples per revolution. |
|  | positions [s] specifies the locations of the samples, in seconds. |
|  | samples/rev specifies the number of samples per revolution. |
|  | orders to compute specifies the orders the VI uses to compute magnitude and phase. The default is 1.0. |
|  | error in describes error conditions that occur before this node runs. This input provides standard error in functionality. |
|  | bandwidth specifies an order span centered at the order that you specify in orders to compute . Specify a bandwidth [order] value between 0 and 1 . If bandwidth [order] is less than 0 or greater than 1 , this VI sets bandwidth [order] to 1 . The default is 1. |
|  | view specifies the view settings for the magnitudes and phases . dB on? specifies whether to return the magnitude in decibels. If dB on? is FALSE, this VI returns the magnitude linearly. The default is FALSE. unwrap phase? (T) specifies whether to unwrap the phase. If unwrap phase? (T) is FALSE, this VI wraps the phase between 0 and 360 degrees, or between 0 and 2pi. If unwrap phase? (T) is TRUE, this VI removes discontinuities less than 0 degrees or greater than 360 degrees from the phase and does not restrict the phase to values between 0 and 360 degrees. The default is TRUE. unit in degree? specifies whether to return the phases in degrees. If unit in degree? is TRUE, this VI returns the phase in degrees. If unit in degree? is FALSE, this VI returns the phase in radians. The default is TRUE. |
|  | dB on? specifies whether to return the magnitude in decibels. If dB on? is FALSE, this VI returns the magnitude linearly. The default is FALSE. |
|  | unwrap phase? (T) specifies whether to unwrap the phase. If unwrap phase? (T) is FALSE, this VI wraps the phase between 0 and 360 degrees, or between 0 and 2pi. If unwrap phase? (T) is TRUE, this VI removes discontinuities less than 0 degrees or greater than 360 degrees from the phase and does not restrict the phase to values between 0 and 360 degrees. The default is TRUE. |
|  | unit in degree? specifies whether to return the phases in degrees. If unit in degree? is TRUE, this VI returns the phase in degrees. If unit in degree? is FALSE, this VI returns the phase in radians. The default is TRUE. |
|  | zero delay? specifies whether a filter delay exists between the input signal and the output signal. The default is FALSE. |
|  | unit labels returns the unit labels for the resulting magnitude and phase plot for each channel. x unit returns the engineering units for the x-axis. magnitude unit returns the engineering unit for the y-axis of the magnitude plot. phase unit returns the engineering unit for the y-axis of the phase plot. |
|  | x unit returns the engineering units for the x-axis. |
|  | magnitude unit returns the engineering unit for the y-axis of the magnitude plot. |
|  | phase unit returns the engineering unit for the y-axis of the phase plot. |
|  | magnitudes [RMS] returns the magnitude plot, in RMS values, of the specified orders. time/rev/RPM returns the x-axis value of the plot. The unit you specified in x-axis selection determines the unit of time/rev/RPM . magnitude returns the y-axis, in RMS values, of the magnitude plot. |
|  | time/rev/RPM returns the x-axis value of the plot. The unit you specified in x-axis selection determines the unit of time/rev/RPM . |
|  | magnitude returns the y-axis, in RMS values, of the magnitude plot. |
|  | phases [phase lag] returns the phase plot, in phase lag value, of the specified orders. time/rev/RPM returns the x-axis value of the plot. The unit you specified in x-axis selection determines the unit of time/rev/RPM . phase returns the y-axis, in phase lag value, of the phase plot. |
|  | time/rev/RPM returns the x-axis value of the plot. The unit you specified in x-axis selection determines the unit of time/rev/RPM . |
|  | phase returns the y-axis, in phase lag value, of the phase plot. |
|  | magnitude/phase position info returns the position information for magnitudes and phases . positions [s] returns the locations of the samples in seconds. samples/rev returns the number of samples per revolution. |
|  | positions [s] returns the locations of the samples in seconds. |
|  | samples/rev returns the number of samples per revolution. |
|  | magnitudes info returns properties of the computed magnitudes. Note You must wire magnitudes info when performing extended measurements. Do not modify the magnitudes info values. If you want to view the information, you can create a custom probe. |
|  | error out contains error information. This output provides standard error out functionality. |

OAT Order Magnitude and Phase (Order Waveforms)

[IMAGE alt='image' src='images/OAT_Order_Magnitude_and_Phase_Order_Waveforms.gif']

|  | x-axis selection specifies the scale type to use for the x-axis of the output XY plot. 0 Time 1 Revolution 2 RPM (default) |
| --- | --- |
| 0 | Time |
| 1 | Revolution |
| 2 | RPM (default) |
|  | order waveforms [EU] specifies the time waveforms of orders. |
|  | speed profile specifies the arrival time and the rotational speed of each tachometer pulse. time [s] specifies the arrival time, in seconds, of each tachometer pulse. speed [RPM] specifies the rotational speed, in revolutions per minute, of each tachometer pulse. |
|  | time [s] specifies the arrival time, in seconds, of each tachometer pulse. |
|  | speed [RPM] specifies the rotational speed, in revolutions per minute, of each tachometer pulse. |
|  | pulse/revolution specifies the number of pulses the tachometer generates during each revolution. The default is 1. |
|  | orders to compute specifies the orders the VI uses to compute magnitude and phase. The default is 1.0. |
|  | error in describes error conditions that occur before this node runs. This input provides standard error in functionality. |
|  | window info specifies the window information for the Gabor transform and expansion. window type specifies the type of window to apply. 0 None 1 Hanning 2 Hamming 3 Blackman-Harris 4 Exact Blackman 5 Blackman 6 Flat Top 7 4 Term B-Harris 8 7 Term B-Harris 9 Low Sidelobe 10 Gaussian (default) window length specifies the length of the window to apply to the Gabor transform and expansion. window length must be a power of two and greater than or equal to 128. This VI rounds window length to the nearest valid value when window length is less than 128 or not a power of two. Increasing window length can improve the frequency resolution but decreases the time resolution. |
|  | window type specifies the type of window to apply. 0 None 1 Hanning 2 Hamming 3 Blackman-Harris 4 Exact Blackman 5 Blackman 6 Flat Top 7 4 Term B-Harris 8 7 Term B-Harris 9 Low Sidelobe 10 Gaussian (default) |
| 0 | None |
| 1 | Hanning |
| 2 | Hamming |
| 3 | Blackman-Harris |
| 4 | Exact Blackman |
| 5 | Blackman |
| 6 | Flat Top |
| 7 | 4 Term B-Harris |
| 8 | 7 Term B-Harris |
| 9 | Low Sidelobe |
| 10 | Gaussian (default) |
|  | window length specifies the length of the window to apply to the Gabor transform and expansion. window length must be a power of two and greater than or equal to 128. This VI rounds window length to the nearest valid value when window length is less than 128 or not a power of two. Increasing window length can improve the frequency resolution but decreases the time resolution. |
|  | view specifies the view settings for the magnitudes and phases . dB on? specifies whether to return the magnitude in decibels. If dB on? is FALSE, this VI returns the magnitude linearly. The default is FALSE. unwrap phase? (T) specifies whether to unwrap the phase. If unwrap phase? (T) is FALSE, this VI wraps the phase between 0 and 360 degrees, or between 0 and 2pi. If unwrap phase? (T) is TRUE, this VI removes discontinuities less than 0 degrees or greater than 360 degrees from the phase and does not restrict the phase to values between 0 and 360 degrees. The default is TRUE. unit in degree? specifies whether to return the phases in degrees. If unit in degree? is TRUE, this VI returns the phase in degrees. If unit in degree? is FALSE, this VI returns the phase in radians. The default is TRUE. |
|  | dB on? specifies whether to return the magnitude in decibels. If dB on? is FALSE, this VI returns the magnitude linearly. The default is FALSE. |
|  | unwrap phase? (T) specifies whether to unwrap the phase. If unwrap phase? (T) is FALSE, this VI wraps the phase between 0 and 360 degrees, or between 0 and 2pi. If unwrap phase? (T) is TRUE, this VI removes discontinuities less than 0 degrees or greater than 360 degrees from the phase and does not restrict the phase to values between 0 and 360 degrees. The default is TRUE. |
|  | unit in degree? specifies whether to return the phases in degrees. If unit in degree? is TRUE, this VI returns the phase in degrees. If unit in degree? is FALSE, this VI returns the phase in radians. The default is TRUE. |
|  | unit labels returns the unit labels for the resulting magnitude and phase. x unit returns the engineering units for the x-axis. magnitude unit returns the engineering unit for the y-axis of the magnitude plot. phase unit returns the engineering unit for the y-axis of the phase plot. |

|  | magnitude unit returns the engineering unit for the y-axis of the magnitude plot. |

|  | time/rev/RPM returns the x-axis value of the plot. The unit you specified in x-axis selection determines the unit of time/rev/RPM . |
|  | magnitude returns the y-axis, in RMS values, of the magnitude plot. |
|  | phases [phase lag] returns the phase plot, in phase lag value, of the specified orders. time/rev/RPM returns the x-axis value of the plot. The unit you specified in x-axis selection determines the unit of time/rev/RPM . phase returns the y-axis, in phase lag value, of the phase plot. |
