The dB reference is used for all downstream conversions to dB. Wire data to the scaled signal [EU] input to determine the polymorphic instance to use or manually select the instance. |
| SVL Scale Voltage to EU | Scales signal [V] from volts to the engineering units (EU) you select. Wire data to the signal [V] input to determine the polymorphic instance to use or manually select the instance. |
| SVL Set dB Reference | Sets the specified dB reference [EU] as an attribute in the scaled signal out [EU] waveform. dB reference [EU] is used for all downstream conversions to dB. Wire data to the scaled signal in [EU] input to determine the polymorphic instance to use or manually select the instance. |

| Subpalette | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Calibration (DAQmx) VIs | Use the Calibration (DAQmx) VIs to calibrate microphones, accelerometers, or other sensors and to measure propagation delay with DAQmx devices. |

Parent topic:

Sound and Vibration VIs

<!--NI_TOPIC bundle=labview-sound-and-vibration-toolkit-api-ref path=shaft-centerline-plot.html language=enus -->
## TOPIC 00037: Shaft Centerline Plot

- bundle_id: `labview-sound-and-vibration-toolkit-api-ref`
- source_path: `shaft-centerline-plot.html`
- source_url: https://docs-be.ni.com/bundle/labview-sound-and-vibration-toolkit-api-ref/raw/resource/enus/shaft-centerline-plot.html
- document_id: `labview-sound-and-vibration-toolkit-api-ref`
- page_type: `leaf`
- content_type: `reference`
- source_description: Owning Palette: Machine Vibration Controls Requires: Sound and Vibration Measurement Suite Displays the averaged positions of a shaft center on a customized picture control. You can use the following VIs to prepare and display a shaft centerline plot: SVX Buffer Shaft Centerline Plot VI SVX Shaft Ce

### Shaft Centerline Plot

**Owning Palette:**
 Machine Vibration Controls

 **Requires:**
 Sound and Vibration Measurement Suite

Displays the averaged positions of a shaft center on a customized picture control.

You can use the following VIs to prepare and display a shaft centerline plot:

- SVX Buffer Shaft Centerline Plot VI
- SVX Shaft Centerline Plot VI

[IMAGE alt='image' src='images/OAT_Sht_CtLn.gif']
Properties

You can set Shaft Centerline Plot properties in the 
 [Graph Properties: Shaft Centerline](/csh?context=https://www.ni.com/docs/en-us/bundle/labview-sound-and-vibration-toolkit/page/sndvibtk/shaft_centerline_db.html)
 dialog box. You also can use the following Shaft Centerline Plot graph properties with the Property Node.

| Property | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Clearance Boundary:Color | Color of clearance boundary line. |
| Clearance Boundary:Settings | Clearance boundary settings of all the N-pair input signals. The settings include start point and boundary size. |
| Cursor:Color | The color of the cursor, including its point, arrow, and name. |
| Cursor:Cursor Info:Lock Position | Specifies whether the cursor information is movable with the cursor or fixed at the bottom-left corner. |
| Cursor:Cursor Info:Visible | Shows the cursor information. |
| Cursor:Index | Array index of point to which the cursor is snapped. This property can have a value between 0 and the number of data points minus 1. |
| Cursor:Line Style | Specifies the line style of the active cursor. Valid values are 0–4, as shown on the Cursors page of the Properties dialog box ordered from top to bottom. |
| Cursor:Line Width | Specifies the width of the line used to display the active cursor. Valid values are 0-4, as shown on the Cursors page of the Properties dialog box ordered from top to bottom. |
| Cursor:Point Style | The symbol that, in combination with Style, is drawn at the focal point of the cursor. Valid values are 0-16. |
| Cursor:Position | Position of the cursor in terms of X-Y coordinates. |
| Cursor:Reference | The time/speed/revolution reference value of the cursor. |
| Cursor:Style | Specifies the symbol that, in combination with Point Style, is drawn at the focal point of the cursor. Valid values are 0-8, from top-left to bottom-right, as shown on the Cursors page of the Properties dialog box. |
| Cursor:Visible | Shows the cursor. |
| Event:Cursor Release | Generates event data when you release the cursor after dragging the cursor. This event is useful if you want to register when you finish interacting with a graph cursor. The event data is a cluster of four elements. The first and second elements of the cluster are the cursor position in terms of X-Y coordinates. The third element of the cluster is the index of the cursor. The last element of the cluster is the time reference. |
| Event:Scale Range Change | Generates when you change the minimum or maximum value of the scale of the Shaft Centerline Plot. The minimum or maximum value changes when the user enters a new value in a scale marker. This event is useful if you want to register user changes and then adjust the functionality of the graph. The event data is a cluster of three elements. The first and second elements of the cluster are the minimum and maximum of the scale. The last element of the cluster shows which scale range has been changed. |
| Legend:BG Color | Specifies the background color of the plot legend. |
| Legend:Font Color | Specifies the font color of the plot legend. |
| Legend:Index | Returns an array of cluster. The first element of the cluster is the index value of parents' items. The second element of the cluster is the index value of children items. |
| Legend:Strings[] | Returns a cluster with three elements. The first BOOLEAN one is to specify whether the Shaft Centerline XControl updates the Strings[] of tree legend from helper VI's input or from users customized strings; the second and third one, Row Strings[] and Column Strings[], are the array of the items from which you can select in the tree legend. |
| Legend:Visible | Shows the plot legend. |
| Marker:Color | Color of the marker, including its point, arrow, and name. |
| Marker:Point Style | The symbol that, is drawn at the focal point of the marker. Valid values are 0–16, as shown on the Plot page of the Properties dialog box ordered from top-left to bottom-right. |
| Marker:Visible | Indicates if the marker is visible. |
| Plot Area:Colors:BG Color | Background color of the plotting area. |
| Plot Area:Colors:Direction | Color of the direction. |
| Plot:Color | Color of the plot. |
| Plot:Fill/Point Color | Color of the point and fills. |
| Plot:Line Style | Line style (0–4) as shown on the Plot page of the Properties dialog box ordered from top-left to bottom-right. |
| Plot:Line Width | Line width of the plot (0-5). |
| Plot:Point Style | Point Style (0–16) as shown on the Plot page of the Properties dialog box ordered from top-left to bottom-right. |
| Scale Fit | Scales the X axis and Y axis to fit the data. You can select one of the following options: 0 – Do not Autoscale, 1 – Autoscale once, 2 – Autoscale. |
| X Scale:Format String | Format string of the x-axis of the scale. |
| X Scale:Grid Colors:Major Color | Color of the major grid on the x-axis of the scale. |
| X Scale:Grid Colors:Minor Color | Color of the minor grid on the x-axis of the scale. |
| X Scale:Grid Style | Style of the grid on the scale. You can select one of the following options: 0 – both the major grid color and minor grid color are set to transparent, 1 – the minor grid color is set to transparent and only the major grid color can be set, 2 – both the major grid color control and minor grid color control can be set. |
| X Scale:Name Label:Text | Text of the x-axis scale label. |
| X Scale:Name Label:Visible | Displays the x-axis scale label. |
| X Scale:Range:Maximum | Maximum value of scale. |
| X Scale:Visible | Displays the x-axis scale. |
| Y Scale:Format String | Format string of the y-axis of the scale. |
| Y Scale:Grid Colors:Major Color | Color of the major grid on the y-axis of the scale. |
| Y Scale:Grid Colors:Minor Color | Color of the minor grid on the y-axis of the scale. |
| Y Scale:Grid Style | The style of the grid on the scale. You can select one of the following options: 0 – both the major grid color and minor grid color are set to transparent, 1 – the minor grid color is set to transparent and only the major grid color can be set, 2 – both the major grid color control and minor grid color control can be set. |
| Y Scale:Name Label:Text | Text of the y-axis scale label. |
| Y Scale:Name Label:Visible | Displays the y-axis scale label. |
| Y Scale:Range | Range of y-scale. |
| Y Scale:Visible | Displays the y-axis scale. |

Parent topic:

Machine Vibration Controls

<!--NI_TOPIC bundle=labview-sound-and-vibration-toolkit-api-ref path=sound-and-vibration-vis.html language=enus -->
## TOPIC 00038: Sound and Vibration VIs

- bundle_id: `labview-sound-and-vibration-toolkit-api-ref`
- source_path: `sound-and-vibration-vis.html`
- source_url: https://docs-be.ni.com/bundle/labview-sound-and-vibration-toolkit-api-ref/raw/resource/enus/sound-and-vibration-vis.html
- document_id: `labview-sound-and-vibration-toolkit-api-ref`
- page_type: `leaf`
- content_type: `reference`
- source_description: March 2019, 370006V-01 Use the Sound and Vibration VIs to perform sound and vibration measurements. The Sound and Vibration VIs perform the selected analysis and allow you to view the results with the appropriate engineering units in standard displays such as magnitude/phase, real/imaginary part, an

### Sound and Vibration VIs

March 2019, 370006V-01

Use the Sound and Vibration VIs to perform sound and vibration measurements. The Sound and Vibration VIs perform the selected analysis and allow you to view the results with the appropriate engineering units in standard displays such as magnitude/phase, real/imaginary part, and decibels on/off.

The VIs on this palette can return general LabVIEW error codes or specific sound and vibration error codes.

To navigate this help file, use the 
 **Contents**
 , 
 **Index**
 , and 
 **Search**
 tabs to the left of this window.

| Subpalette | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Advanced VIs | Use the Advanced VIs to perform advanced sound and vibration analysis, such as measurement analysis and condition monitoring on embedded targets that have limited computational resources. |
| Data Evaluation VIs | Use the Data Evaluation VIs to perform limit testing on waveforms, spectra, XY data, identified peaks, or scalar measurements. |
| Display VIs | Use the Display VIs to display signals on several types of graphs and plots. |
| Fault Detection VIs | Use the Fault Detection VIs to perform fault detection on rotating machinery. |
| File I/O VIs | Use the File I/O VIs to save and load dynamic signals and measurement results. Use these VIs to read data from and write data to .tdms files and UFF58 files. Use these VIs with the standard File I/O VIs and functions to add custom properties, customize a file format, and write VIs to convert between file formats. |
| Frequency Analysis VIs | Use the Frequency Analysis VIs to perform averaged and non-averaged, or single block, frequency analysis. The analysis is based on the FFT. |
| Generation VIs | Use the Generation VIs to generate different types of single and multitone signals, function generator signals, noise signals, and chirp signals. |
| Multitone VIs | Use the Multitone VIs to perform multitone distortion analysis. |
| Octave Analysis VIs | Use the Octave Analysis VIs to perform octave analysis, third-octave analysis, and fractional-octave analysis, including 1/1, 1/3, 1/6, 1/12, and 1/24 octave. |
| Order Analysis VIs | Use the Order Analysis VIs to calculate and analyze order-related results such as spectral maps, order spectra, order magnitudes and phases, and order waveforms. |
| Scaling & Calibration VIs | Use the Scaling & Calibration VIs to scale an input signal measured in volts to the appropriate engineering units. You also use these VIs to calibrate microphones, accelerometers, or other sensors and to measure propagation delay with DAQmx devices. |
| Sound Level VIs | Use the Sound Level VIs to perform sound-level measurements, including linear, exponential, and peak hold averaging. Linear averaging is also known as equivalent continuous sound level, or Leq. The Sound Level VIs do not inherently perform any bandlimiting of the signal. Before the sound level is computed, the sound pressure data can be band limited through the use of analog or digital filters. All level results are returned in dB, and are RMS unless otherwise noted. |
| Sound Quality VIs | Use the Sound Quality VIs to perform psychoacoustic measurements associated with the human perception of sound. Use these VIs to perform sound quality measurements, including stationary loudness, time-varying loudness, sharpness, roughness, fluctuation strength, and tonality. The Sound Quality VIs comply with applicable standards for use with product evaluation and as part of a comprehensive sound quality program. |
| Stimulus-Response Test VIs | Use the Stimulus-Response Test VIs to generate reference signals and perform stimulus-response testing. |
| Tachometer Processing VIs | Use the Tachometer Processing VIs to process analog and digital tachometer signals. |
| Tone and Distortion VIs | Use the Tone and Distortion VIs to perform single-tone analysis and distortion measurements. |
| Torsional Vibration Measurement VIs | Use the Torsional Vibration Measurement VIs to calculate torsional vibration signals from analog and digital tachometer signals. |
| Transient Analysis VIs | Use the Transient Analysis VIs to perform analysis of nonstationary signals. |
| Vibration Level VIs | Use the Vibration Level VIs to perform vibration-level measurements on time-domain and angular-domain signals. |
| Weighting and Filtering VIs | Use the Weighting and Filtering VIs to apply a weighting filter on time-domain signals sampled at specific rates. You can choose from acoustic, telecommunication, radiocommunication, and human vibration filters. |

© 1999–2019 National Instruments. All rights reserved.

<!--NI_TOPIC bundle=labview-sound-and-vibration-toolkit-api-ref path=sound-level-vis.html language=enus -->
## TOPIC 00039: Sound Level VIs

- bundle_id: `labview-sound-and-vibration-toolkit-api-ref`
- source_path: `sound-level-vis.html`
- source_url: https://docs-be.ni.com/bundle/labview-sound-and-vibration-toolkit-api-ref/raw/resource/enus/sound-level-vis.html
- document_id: `labview-sound-and-vibration-toolkit-api-ref`
- page_type: `leaf`
- content_type: `reference`
- source_description: Owning Palette: Sound and Vibration VIs Use the Sound Level VIs to perform sound-level measurements, including linear, exponential, and peak hold averaging. Linear averaging is also known as equivalent continuous sound level, or Leq. The Sound Level VIs do not inherently perform any bandlimiting of

### Sound Level VIs

**Owning Palette:**
 Sound and Vibration VIs

Use the Sound Level VIs to perform sound-level measurements, including linear, exponential, and peak hold averaging. Linear averaging is also known as equivalent continuous sound level, or Leq. The Sound Level VIs do not inherently perform any bandlimiting of the signal. Before the sound level is computed, the sound pressure data can be band limited through the use of analog or digital filters. All level results are returned in dB, and are RMS unless otherwise noted.

The VIs on this palette can return general LabVIEW error codes or specific sound and vibration error codes.

Refer to the 
 labview\examples\Sound and Vibration
 directory for an example of using the Sound Level VIs.

| Palette Object | Description |
| --- | --- |
| SVL Decimated Exp Avg Sound Level | Computes the exponential averaged sound level of scaled signal [EU] of exponential mode and decimates this sound level by decimation factor . scaled signal [EU] should have a zero DC component. You can attenuate the DC component with hardware AC-coupling filters and with software high-pass filters. Wire data to the scaled signal [EU] input to determine the polymorphic instance to use or manually select the instance. |
| SVL Exp Avg Sound Level | Computes the exponential averaged sound level of scaled signal [EU] . scaled signal [EU] should have a zero DC component. Wire data to the scaled signal [EU] input to determine the polymorphic instance to use or manually select the instance. |
| SVL Leq Sound Level | Computes the linear averaged, or the equivalent continuous sound level (Leq), of scaled signal [EU] . scaled signal [EU] should have a zero DC component. You can attenuate the DC component with hardware AC-coupling filters and with software high-pass filters. Wire data to the scaled signal [EU] input to determine the polymorphic instance to use or manually select the instance. |
| SVL Peak Sound Level | Computes the peak sound level of scaled signal [EU] . scaled signal [EU] should have a zero DC component. You can attenuate the DC component with hardware AC-coupling filters and with software high-pass filters. The peak sound level is the maximum of the absolute values of all measured values. Wire data to the scaled signal [EU] input to determine the polymorphic instance to use or manually select the instance. |
| SVL Running Leq Sound Level | Computes the running equivalent continuous sound level (Leq) of scaled signal [EU] . scaled signal [EU] should have a zero DC component. Wire data to the scaled signal [EU] input to determine the polymorphic instance to use or manually select the instance. |
| SVT Sound Level | Computes several sound level measurements including equivalent continuous level (Leq), running Leq sound levels, exponential averaged sound level, and the peak sound level. Wire data to the scaled signal [EU] input to determine the polymorphic instance to use or manually select the instance. |

| Subpalette | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Weighting VIs | Use the Weighting VIs to apply a weighting filter on time-domain signals sampled at specific rates. You can choose from acoustic, telecommunication, and radiocommunication filters. |

Parent topic:

Sound and Vibration VIs

<!--NI_TOPIC bundle=labview-sound-and-vibration-toolkit-api-ref path=sound-quality-vis.html language=enus -->
## TOPIC 00040: Sound Quality VIs

- bundle_id: `labview-sound-and-vibration-toolkit-api-ref`
- source_path: `sound-quality-vis.html`
- source_url: https://docs-be.ni.com/bundle/labview-sound-and-vibration-toolkit-api-ref/raw/resource/enus/sound-quality-vis.html
- document_id: `labview-sound-and-vibration-toolkit-api-ref`
- page_type: `leaf`
- content_type: `reference`
- source_description: Owning Palette: Sound and Vibration VIs Use the Sound Quality VIs to perform psychoacoustic measurements associated with the human perception of sound. Use these VIs to perform sound quality measurements, including stationary loudness, time-varying loudness, sharpness, roughness, fluctuation strengt

### Sound Quality VIs

**Owning Palette:**
 Sound and Vibration VIs

Use the Sound Quality VIs to perform psychoacoustic measurements associated with the human perception of sound. Use these VIs to perform sound quality measurements, including stationary loudness, time-varying loudness, sharpness, roughness, fluctuation strength, and tonality. The Sound Quality VIs comply with applicable standards for use with product evaluation and as part of a comprehensive sound quality program.

The VIs on this palette can return general LabVIEW error codes or specific sound and vibration error codes.

Refer to the 
 labview\examples\Sound and Vibration
 directory for an example of using the Sound Quality VIs.

| Palette Object | Description |
| --- | --- |
| SV Aures Roughness | Computes roughness of the sound pressure signal according to Aures' method. |
| SV Aures Sharpness | Computes sharpness from the sound pressure signal waveform, the 1/3-octave band spectrum calculated over the frequency range 25 Hz to 12.5 kHz, or the specific loudness that the SV Stationary Loudness VI returns. Wire data to the sound pressure signal [Pa] input to determine the polymorphic instance to use or manually select the instance. |
| SV Aures Tonality | Computes tonality of the sound pressure signal according to Aures' method on successive 160 ms blocks. |
| SV Fluctuation Strength | Computes fluctuation strength of the sound pressure signal from a pressure-time history. |
| SV Stationary Loudness | Computes stationary loudness from the sound pressure signal waveform or the 1/3-octave band spectrum calculated over the frequency range 25 Hz to 12.5 kHz. Wire data to the sound pressure signal [Pa] input to determine the polymorphic instance to use or manually select the instance. |
| SV Time-Varying Loudness | Computes time-varying loudness of the sound pressure signal from a time-varying 1/3 octave band spectrum. |

Parent topic:

Sound and Vibration VIs

<!--NI_TOPIC bundle=labview-sound-and-vibration-toolkit-api-ref path=sound-vibration-controls.html language=enus -->
## TOPIC 00041: Sound & Vibration Controls

- bundle_id: `labview-sound-and-vibration-toolkit-api-ref`
- source_path: `sound-vibration-controls.html`
- source_url: https://docs-be.ni.com/bundle/labview-sound-and-vibration-toolkit-api-ref/raw/resource/enus/sound-vibration-controls.html
- document_id: `labview-sound-and-vibration-toolkit-api-ref`
- page_type: `leaf`
- content_type: `reference`
- source_description: Requires: Sound and Vibration Measurement Suite Use the Sound & Vibration controls palette to locate controls to use with the Sound and Vibration VIs. Subpalette Description Configuration Controls Use the Configuration indicators for easy front panel configuration of typical sound and vibration appl

### Sound & Vibration Controls

**Requires:**
 Sound and Vibration Measurement Suite

Use the 
 **Sound & Vibration**
 controls palette to locate controls to use with the Sound and Vibration VIs.

| Subpalette | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Configuration Controls | Use the Configuration indicators for easy front panel configuration of typical sound and vibration applications using NI-DAQmx analog input hardware. |
| Graphs with Cursors | Use the Graphs with Cursors indicators for advanced and interactive display of waveform and spectral data. |
| Machine Vibration Controls | Use the Machine Vibration indicators with the Machine Vibration VIs to display dynamic and averaged motion of machine components and to display machine vibration signals on a polar plot, shaft centerline plot, orbit plot, or timebase plot. |
| Octave Spectra Controls | Use the Octave Spectra indicators with the Octave Analysis VIs to display octave spectra with standard formatting on a logarithmic frequency axis. |
| Transient Spectra Controls | Use the Transient Spectra indicators with the Transient Analysis VIs to display multiple spectra on the same graph. Waterfall graphs, colormaps, and intensity graphs are useful to illustrate the frequency content of time-varying signals. These indicators require constant bandwidth, or linear frequency spacing, spectra. |

Parent topic:

Sound and Vibration VIs

<!--NI_TOPIC bundle=labview-sound-and-vibration-toolkit-api-ref path=spectral-data-xcontrol.html language=enus -->
## TOPIC 00042: Spectral Data XControl

- bundle_id: `labview-sound-and-vibration-toolkit-api-ref`
- source_path: `spectral-data-xcontrol.html`
- source_url: https://docs-be.ni.com/bundle/labview-sound-and-vibration-toolkit-api-ref/raw/resource/enus/spectral-data-xcontrol.html
- document_id: `labview-sound-and-vibration-toolkit-api-ref`
- page_type: `leaf`
- content_type: `reference`
- source_description: Owning Palette: Graphs with Cursors Requires: Sound and Vibration Toolkit Use this XControl to display magnitudes of one or multiple channels of spectral data. This control supports interactive data cursors on a waveform graph. This XControl can be combined with other XControls to develop sophistica

### Spectral Data XControl

**Owning Palette:**
 Graphs with Cursors


 **Requires:**
 Sound and Vibration Toolkit

Use this XControl to display magnitudes of one or multiple channels of spectral data. This control supports interactive data cursors on a waveform graph. This XControl can be combined with other XControls to develop sophisticated user interfaces with minimal programming.

[IMAGE alt='image' src='images/spectrum_preview.gif']

Note

Configure Cursors

1. To enable cursors, right-click the data plot and select 
 Configure Cursors 
 .
2. The 
 Configure Measurement Cursors 
 dialog appears.
3. Configure cursors, then click 
 OK 
 .

Move cursors with the Cursor Movement Tool, or by using the X column of the cursor table.
Spectral Data XControl Graph

[IMAGE alt='image' src='images/spectrumxcontrol_cursors.gif']
 
 Graph with all properties visible, including cursors. The 
 **cursor table**
 is shown on the right. Use the moveable splitter to adjust the width of the graph relative to the table.
The Cursor Table

By default, the cursor table contains meta information available in the spectrum attributes, such as the units, FFT window, and FFT resolution. When configured, cursor values and measurements appear here. Show or hide the cursor menu using the button beside the moveable splitter.
 Spectral View Properties

The Spectral Data XControl contains additional graph properties for viewing spectral data. These properties are available by right-clicking the data plot in Run Mode.

| Property | Options |
| --- | --- |
| X Scale Maximum | Traditional, Extended, Nyquist |
| X Scale Mapping | Linear, Log |
| Y Scale Mapping | Linear, Log, dB |
| Spectrum Scaling | Magnitude or Power, RMS, peak, peak-to-peak, spectral density |
| Configure Cursors | XY, Harmonic, Sideband, Band |
| Lock 1X to Peak | Snaps 1X to nearest peak and continuously performs peak search |
| Snap 1X to Peak | Snaps 1X to nearest peak on the current data only |
| Snap C to Peak | Snaps C to nearest peak on the current data only |
| Lock Band Cursor Span | Locks the X-spacing between b1 and b2 |

Programmatic Properties

Access these properties from the property node for the XControl.

**Cursor Positions**
 (Read Only)

- cursor names 
 is an array of strings of the enabled cursors.
- channel names 
 is an array of strings containing each channel name.
- channel units 
 is an array of strings containing the scaled spectral units for each channel.
- Positions.X 
 is a 1D array of double precision values containing the x positions for each cursor.
- Positions.Y 
 is a 2D array of double precision values containing the y positions for each cursor for each channel.

**User Events**
 (Read Only)

- Display State Change 
 fires when properties of the XControl are set by interactive or programmatic configuration
- Data Change 
 fires after most Display State Change events and when new data is sent to the XControl by wiring data to the terminal, the Value property, or the Value (Signaling) property.

**Band Cursors**

- Start 
 writes or reads the x value of the band start cursor (b1).
- Stop 
 writes or reads the x value of the band stop cursor (b2).
- Span 
 writes or reads the x spacing of the band start and band stop cursors. A positive value locks the band spacing.

**Harmonic Cursors**

- X Reference 
 writes or reads the x value of the harmonic 1X reference cursor (1X).
- 1X Locked to Peak 
 writes or reads whether peak search is run on each new data set to automatically detect and track the fundamental frequency.

**Sideband Cursors**

- X Center 
 writes or reads the x value for the center sideband cursor (C).
- X Spacing 
 writes or reads the x spacing between the center (C) and each sideband cursor (.., s2', s1', C, s1, s2, ..).

Additional Information

Customizing Graphs and Charts

Configuring Controls and Indicators

Parent topic:

Graphs with Cursor Controls

<!--NI_TOPIC bundle=labview-sound-and-vibration-toolkit-api-ref path=spectrum-extended-measurements-vis.html language=enus -->
## TOPIC 00043: Spectrum Extended Measurements VIs

- bundle_id: `labview-sound-and-vibration-toolkit-api-ref`
- source_path: `spectrum-extended-measurements-vis.html`
- source_url: https://docs-be.ni.com/bundle/labview-sound-and-vibration-toolkit-api-ref/raw/resource/enus/spectrum-extended-measurements-vis.html
- document_id: `labview-sound-and-vibration-toolkit-api-ref`
- page_type: `leaf`
- content_type: `reference`
- source_description: Owning Palette: Embedded Monitoring VIs, Frequency Analysis VIs, Order Analysis VIs Use the Spectrum Extended Measurements VIs to perform advanced frequency analysis measurements. The VIs on this palette can return general LabVIEW error codes or specific sound and vibration error codes. Palette Obje

### Spectrum Extended Measurements VIs

**Owning Palette:**
 Embedded Monitoring VIs, Frequency Analysis VIs, Order Analysis VIs

Use the Spectrum Extended Measurements VIs to perform advanced frequency analysis measurements.

The VIs on this palette can return general LabVIEW error codes or specific sound and vibration error codes.

| Palette Object | Description |
| --- | --- |
| SV Convert to Data Object | Use this polymorphic VI to convert spectra to a class data type that you can view on the Spectrum with Cursors XControl. You must manually select the polymorphic instance to use. |
| SVC Sort Results | Sorts an array of peaks according to the sort by and sort order parameters. Use this VI to sort peaks that the SVFA Spectrum Peak Search, SVT Spurious Free Dynamic Range, and SVT THD and Harmonic Components VIs return. Wire data to the peaks in input to determine the polymorphic instance to use or manually select the instance. |
| SVFA Compensate Phase for Time Delay | Compensates FFT and frequency response phase spectra for time delay. You can introduce time delays with the DUT, data acquisition hardware, and measurement analysis. Compensation in this VI is restricted to linear phase resulting from a pure time delay. Wire data to the phase spectrum input to determine the polymorphic instance to use or manually select the instance. |
| SVFA Convert Frequency (CPM to CPS) | Converts the unit of a rotational speed from revolutions per minute to revolutions per second, or hertz. |
| SVFA Convert Frequency (CPS to CPM) | Converts the unit of a rotational speed from revolutions per second, or hertz, to revolutions per minute. |
| SVFA Convert Spectrum X Units | Converts a frequency power spectrum to an order power spectrum. Use this VI to convert power spectrum results from constant-speed applications. |
| SVFA Get Spectrum Subset | Extracts the subset of the spectrum specified by range . x0 is updated to reflect the new start frequency, dx is the same as in the input spectrum, and the spectrum array is shortened so the last element corresponds with the stop value specified in the range . Use this VI for operations such as limiting subsequent spectrum operations to the region of interest or removing the part of the spectrum outside the alias-free bandwidth, as well as others. Wire data to the spectrum input to determine the polymorphic instance to use or manually select the instance. |
| SVFA Get Spectrum Value | Extracts the value of the spectrum at the specified frequency. Use the frequency coercion control to specify how the frequency should be coerced if it does not exactly coincide with a frequency line. This VI operates on any real-valued spectrum. Wire data to the spectrum input to determine the polymorphic instance to use or manually select the instance. |
| SVFA Magnitude and Phase to Real and Imaginary | Converts spectra from polar components to rectangular components. This conversion can reduce the computational power necessary to view and process spectra. Wire data to the magnitude spectrum and phase spectrum inputs to determine the polymorphic instance to use or manually select the instance. |
| SVFA Map Frequency to Order | Use this VI to convert a frequency power spectrum to an order power spectrum. Use this VI to convert power spectrum results from constant-speed applications. |
| SVFA Map Order to Frequency | Use this VI to convert an order power spectrum to a frequency power spectrum. Use this VI to convert power spectrum results from constant-speed applications. |
| SVFA Phase Unit Conversion | Converts phase spectra between degrees, radians, and cycles. Wire data to the phase spectrum input to determine the polymorphic instance to use or manually select the instance. |
| SVFA Power in Band | Computes the aggregate power in the range . The SVFA Power in Band VI operates on Power, PSD, COP, and FFT [Mag] spectra. Wire data to the spectrum input to determine the polymorphic instance to use or manually select the instance. |
| SVFA Real and Imaginary to Magnitude and Phase | Converts spectra from rectangular components to polar components. You can display magnitude and phase information on graphs and send this information to other Spectrum Extended Measurements VIs. Wire data to the real spectrum and imaginary spectrum inputs to determine the polymorphic instance to use or manually select the instance. |
| SVFA Spectrum Peak Search | Performs a peak search on a spectrum or spectra. The spectrum or spectra can be in either the frequency domain or the order domain. Detects single or multiple peaks in Power, PSD, and COP spectra. Wire data to the spectrum input to determine the polymorphic instance to use or manually select the instance. |
| SVFA Unit Conversion | Converts between dB/linear, magnitude/magnitude^2, rms/peak/peak-peak, spectrum/spectral density, and dB ref1/dB ref2. Wire data to the spectrum input to determine the polymorphic instance to use or manually select the instance. |
| SVFA Wrap or Unwrap Phase | Wraps the phase spectra to the specified range, so that all phase values are coerced to the specified band. You also can use this VI to unwrap phase spectra to eliminate discontinuities with absolute values exceeding 180 degrees = pi radians = 0.5 cycles. Wire data to the phase spectrum in input to determine the polymorphic instance to use or manually select the instance. |
| SVL Integration (frequency) | Converts acceleration to velocity or displacement using a frequency spectrum as the input. Wire data to the spectrum input to determine the polymorphic instance to use or manually select the instance. |
| SVL Limit Testing | Performs limit testing on waveforms, spectra, XY data, identified peaks, or scalar measurements. You can specify an upper and lower boundary of the envelope to define a pass range. This VI uses the defined pass range to test the input signal. VI outputs apply only to the current input signal. You can wire output values to a graph to view the limits, signal, and failures. Wire data to the signal in input to determine the polymorphic instance to use or manually select the instance. |
| SVPO Average FFT Spectrum (Complex) | Converts a frequency power spectrum to an order power spectrum. Use this VI to convert power spectrum results from constant-speed applications. |
| SVT Human Vibration Weighting Filter (Frequency) | Applies the selected human vibration weighting to FFT-based spectra. This VI weights each spectral bin according to the attenuation of the specified filter at the bin center frequency. This VI does not maintain state internally; you can use this VI in a loop to process multiple blocks. Wire data to the spectrum input to determine the polymorphic instance to use or manually select the instance. |
| SVT Radiocommunications Weighting Filter (Frequency) | Applies the selected radiocommunications weighting to a previously computed frequency spectrum or spectra. This VI weights each spectral bin according to the attenuation of the specified filter at the bin center frequency. You can use this VI in a loop to process multiple blocks. Wire data to the spectrum input to determine the polymorphic instance to use or manually select the instance. |
| SVT Telecommunications Weighting Filter (Frequency) | Applies the selected telecommunications weighting to a previously computed frequency spectrum or spectra. This VI weights each spectral bin according to the attenuation of the specified filter at the bin center frequency. You can use this VI in a loop to process multiple blocks. Wire data to the spectrum input to determine the polymorphic instance to use or manually select the instance. |
| SVT Weighting Filter (frequency) | Applies the selected weighting to the spectrum or spectra in the frequency domain. This VI operates on Power, PSD, COP, FFT [Mag], FFT [Re], and FFT [Imag] spectra. Wire data to the spectrum input to determine the polymorphic instance to use or manually select the instance. |

| Subpalette | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Machine Characteristics VIs | Use the Machine Characteristic VIs to calculate characteristic frequencies or orders of rotating machinery. You then use characteristic frequencies with the Frequency Analysis VIs to perform constant-speed fault analysis. Use characteristic orders with the Order Analysis VIs to perform variable-speed fault analysis. |

Parent topic:

Frequency Analysis VIs

<!--NI_TOPIC bundle=labview-sound-and-vibration-toolkit-api-ref path=stepped-swept-sine-vis.html language=enus -->
## TOPIC 00044: Stepped Swept Sine VIs

- bundle_id: `labview-sound-and-vibration-toolkit-api-ref`
- source_path: `stepped-swept-sine-vis.html`
- source_url: https://docs-be.ni.com/bundle/labview-sound-and-vibration-toolkit-api-ref/raw/resource/enus/stepped-swept-sine-vis.html
- document_id: `labview-sound-and-vibration-toolkit-api-ref`
- page_type: `leaf`
- content_type: `reference`
- source_description: Use the Stepped Swept Sine VIs to perform stepped swept-sine measurements. The VIs on this palette can return general LabVIEW error codes or specific sound and vibration error codes . Palette Object Description SVT Close Swept Sine (DAQmx) Clears analog input and output tasks. SVT Initialize Swept S

### Stepped Swept Sine VIs

Use the Stepped Swept Sine VIs to perform stepped swept-sine measurements.

The VIs on this palette can return general LabVIEW error codes or 
 [specific sound and vibration error codes](/csh?context=https://www.ni.com/docs/en-us/bundle/labview-sound-and-vibration-toolkit/page/sndvibtk/sv_error_codes.html)
 .

| Palette Object | Description |
| --- | --- |
| SVT Close Swept Sine (DAQmx) | Clears analog input and output tasks. |
| SVT Initialize Swept Sine Task (DAQmx) | Initializes swept-sine-analysis parameters on analog input and output devices. |
| SVT Start Swept Sine (DAQmx) | Starts the swept-sine measurement. |
| SVT Swept Sine VI Tree (DAQmx) | Shows all the user-level VIs on the Swept Sine VIs palette in a functional format. Performing swept-sine measurements requires all the VIs in the first column of this palette. Other subpalettes offer additional functionality. Swept-sine measurements developed with the NI Sound and Vibration Toolkit or the Sound and Vibration Measurement Suite follow the data-flow format depicted on the block diagram of this tree VI. |

| Subpalette | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Configure Swept Sine VIs | Use the Configure Swept Sine VIs to create configuration settings for swept-sine measurements. |
| Get Swept Sine Configuration VIs | Use the Get Swept Sine Configuration VIs to retrieve information about swept-sine configuration settings. |
| Read Swept Sine Measurements VIs | Use the Read Swept Sine Measurements VIs to read the results of swept-sine measurements. |

Parent topic:

Stimulus-Response Test VIs

<!--NI_TOPIC bundle=labview-sound-and-vibration-toolkit-api-ref path=stimulus-response-test-vis.html language=enus -->
## TOPIC 00045: Stimulus-Response Test VIs

- bundle_id: `labview-sound-and-vibration-toolkit-api-ref`
- source_path: `stimulus-response-test-vis.html`
- source_url: https://docs-be.ni.com/bundle/labview-sound-and-vibration-toolkit-api-ref/raw/resource/enus/stimulus-response-test-vis.html
- document_id: `labview-sound-and-vibration-toolkit-api-ref`
- page_type: `leaf`
- content_type: `reference`
- source_description: Owning Palette: Sound and Vibration VIs Use the Stimulus-Response Test VIs to generate reference signals and perform stimulus-response testing. The VIs on this palette can return general LabVIEW error codes or specific sound and vibration error codes . Refer to the labview\examples\Sound and Vibrati

### Stimulus-Response Test VIs

**Owning Palette:**
 Sound and Vibration VIs

Use the Stimulus-Response Test VIs to generate reference signals and perform stimulus-response testing.

The VIs on this palette can return general LabVIEW error codes or 
 [specific sound and vibration error codes](/csh?context=https://www.ni.com/docs/en-us/bundle/labview-sound-and-vibration-toolkit/page/sndvibtk/sv_error_codes.html)
 .

Refer to the 
 labview\examples\Sound and Vibration
 directory for an example of using the Stimulus-Response VIs.

| Palette Object | Description |
| --- | --- |
| SV Obtain Buffer | Creates a reference to a buffer of continuous waveforms. Use this VI to create a buffer explicitly. Use the buffer reference to set custom properties of the buffer and to share a reference to the same data buffer between parallel code. Use the Buffer Node to set properties for the waveforms in the buffer. |
| SV Read from Buffer | Adds buffering to stimulus-response applications. You must manually select the polymorphic instance to use. |
| SV Release Buffer | Releases a reference to a buffer of continuous waveforms. Use this VI to clear all data from the buffer and destroy the buffer reference. |
| SV Update Signal | Dynamically updates a signal that is being generated with the Signal Generation API. Wire the current signal configuration to current configuration and the new signal configuration to new configuration . Pass the updated configuration output to SV Signal (waveform) VI to initiate the update. Use this VI when you want to transition from one configuration to another without having to stop the generation. |
| SV Write to Buffer | Appends waveforms to existing data in a buffer of continuous waveforms. Use this VI to include additional waveforms in the referenced buffer. waveforms must have identical dt and length, and all waveforms must have the same timestamp. If input waveforms are not continuous with waveforms already in the buffer, the buffer will be cleared and replaced with the waveforms . |

| Subpalette | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Baseband FFT VIs | Use the Baseband FFT VIs to perform baseband FFT measurements. |
| Continuous Swept Sine VIs | Use the Continuous Swept Sine VIs to perform continuous swept-sine measurements. |
| Multitone VIs | Use the Multitone VIs to perform multitone distortion analysis. |
| Tone and Distortion VIs | Use the Tone and Distortion VIs to perform single-tone analysis and distortion measurements. |

Parent topic:

Sound and Vibration VIs

## TOPIC 00046: SV Append Data VI

- bundle_id: `labview-sound-and-vibration-toolkit-api-ref`
- source_path: `sv-append-data-vi.html`
- source_url: https://docs-be.ni.com/bundle/labview-sound-and-vibration-toolkit-api-ref/raw/resource/enus/sv-append-data-vi.html

- page_type: `leaf`
- content_type: `reference`
- source_description: Owning Palette: Data Evaluation VIs Requires: Sound and Vibration Toolkit Use this VI to buffer data by appending input data to previously buffered data. Set the maximum length to set the buffered array size. The VI functions like a FIFO (First In, First Out) buffer, meaning that the oldest data is

### SV Append Data VI

**Owning Palette:**
 Data Evaluation VIs

**Requires:**
 Sound and Vibration Toolkit

Use this VI to buffer data by appending input data to previously buffered data. Set the 
 **maximum length**
 to set the buffered array size. The VI functions like a FIFO (First In, First Out) buffer, meaning that the oldest data is overwritten as new elements are added to the buffer. Wire data to the 
 **data in**
 input to determine the polymorphic instance to use or manually select the instance.

This VI maintains state. You can buffer data in time to evaluate trends and perform time-bounded analyses. You can concatenate data from multiple channels to perform spatial averaging. You can use data from multiple measurement-analysis VIs for weighted averaging or other custom operations.

| SV Append Data (1 Ch) (DBL)SV Append Data (1 Ch) (SGL)SV Append Data (1 Ch) (I32)SV Append Data (1 Ch) (Variant)SV Append Data ([DBL])SV Append Waveform (1 Ch) (DBL)SV Append Waveform (N Ch) (DBL) |
| --- |

SV Append Data (1 Ch) (DBL)

[IMAGE alt='image' src='images/SV_Append_Data_1_Ch_DBL.gif']

|  | reset specifies whether the history, or internal state, of the VI will be reset. The default is FALSE; data in is appended to previously buffered data. When reset is TRUE, data in replaces all previously buffered data. |
| --- | --- |
|  | data in specifies the acquired sample or measurement-analysis result to append to the buffer. |
|  | maximum length (-1 = unlimited) sets the size of the buffer. –1 Grow Unbounded 0 Keep Current Size >0 Grow to Specified Length |
| –1 | Grow Unbounded |
| 0 | Keep Current Size |
| >0 | Grow to Specified Length |
|  | appended data returns the buffered data samples. |

SV Append Data (1 Ch) (SGL)

[IMAGE alt='image' src='images/SV_Append_Data_1_Ch_SGL.gif']

| --- | --- |
|  | data in specifies the acquired sample or measurement-analysis result to append to the buffer. |
|  | maximum length (-1 = unlimited) sets the size of the buffer. –1 Grow Unbounded 0 Keep Current Size >0 Grow to Specified Length |
| –1 | Grow Unbounded |
| 0 | Keep Current Size |
| >0 | Grow to Specified Length |
|  | appended data returns the buffered data samples. |

SV Append Data (1 Ch) (I32)

[IMAGE alt='image' src='images/SV_Append_Data_1_Ch_I32.gif']

|  | reset specifies whether the history, or internal state, of the VI will be reset. The default is FALSE; data in is appended to previously buffered data. When reset is TRUE, data in replaces all previously buffered data. |
| --- | --- |
|  | data in specifies the acquired sample or measurement-analysis result to append to the buffer. |
|  | maximum length (-1 = unlimited) sets the size of the buffer. –1 Grow Unbounded 0 Keep Current Size >0 Grow to Specified Length |
| –1 | Grow Unbounded |
| 0 | Keep Current Size |
| >0 | Grow to Specified Length |
|  | appended data returns the buffered data samples. |

SV Append Data (1 Ch) (Variant)

[IMAGE alt='image' src='images/SV_Append_Data_1_Ch_Variant.gif']

|  | reset specifies whether the history, or internal state, of the VI will be reset. The default is FALSE; data in is appended to previously buffered data. When reset is TRUE, data in replaces all previously buffered data. |
| --- | --- |
|  | data in specifies the acquired sample or measurement-analysis result to append to the buffer. |
|  | maximum length (-1 = unlimited) sets the size of the buffer. –1 Grow Unbounded 0 Keep Current Size >0 Grow to Specified Length |
| –1 | Grow Unbounded |
| 0 | Keep Current Size |
| >0 | Grow to Specified Length |
|  | appended data returns the buffered data samples. |

SV Append Data ([DBL])

[IMAGE alt='image' src='images/SV_Append_Data_1_Ch_DBL.gif']

|  | reset specifies whether the history, or internal state, of the VI will be reset. The default is FALSE; data in is appended to previously buffered data. When reset is TRUE, data in replaces all previously buffered data. |
| --- | --- |
|  | data in specifies the acquired sample or measurement-analysis result to append to the buffer. |

|  | appended data returns the buffered data samples. |

SV Append Waveform (1 Ch) (DBL)

| --- | --- |
|  | waveform data in specifies the waveform data to buffer. |
|  | maximum length [s] (-1 = unlimited) sets the size of the buffer. –1 Grow Unbounded 0 Keep Current Size >0 Grow to Specified Duration |

|  | error in describes error conditions that occur before this node runs. This input provides standard error in functionality. |
|  | appended waveform data returns the buffered waveform data samples. |

<!--NI_TOPIC bundle=labview-sound-and-vibration-toolkit-api-ref path=sv-aures-roughness-vi.html language=enus -->
## TOPIC 00047: SV Aures Roughness VI

- bundle_id: `labview-sound-and-vibration-toolkit-api-ref`
- source_path: `sv-aures-roughness-vi.html`
- source_url: https://docs-be.ni.com/bundle/labview-sound-and-vibration-toolkit-api-ref/raw/resource/enus/sv-aures-roughness-vi.html
- document_id: `labview-sound-and-vibration-toolkit-api-ref`
- page_type: `leaf`
- content_type: `reference`
- source_description: Owning Palette: Sound Quality VIs Requires: Sound and Vibration Toolkit Computes roughness of the sound pressure signal according to Aures' method. Roughness is a hearing sensation related to loudness modulations at frequencies too high to separately discern, such as modulation frequencies greater t

### SV Aures Roughness VI

**Owning Palette:**
 Sound Quality VIs

**Requires:**
 Sound and Vibration Toolkit

Computes roughness of the sound pressure signal according to Aures' method.

 
 
Roughness is a hearing sensation related to loudness modulations at frequencies too high to separately discern, such as modulation frequencies greater than 30 Hz.

Details Example

[IMAGE alt='image' src='images/SV_Aures_Roughness.gif']

|  | diffuse field specifies if the sound field is diffuse (TRUE) or a free field (FALSE). The default is free field (FALSE). |
| --- | --- |
|  | sound pressure signal [Pa] specifies the sound pressure signal in Pascals. This signal keeps track of the timing of the waveform and the values the waveform contains. t0 specifies the start time of the waveform. dt specifies the time interval in seconds between data points in the waveform. Y specifies the data values of the waveform. |
|  | t0 specifies the start time of the waveform. |
|  | dt specifies the time interval in seconds between data points in the waveform. |
|  | Y specifies the data values of the waveform. |
|  | error in describes error conditions that occur before this node runs. This input provides standard error in functionality. |
|  | roughness spectrum returns the specific roughness at the critical band rate. The critical band rate is in units of bark. A bark is the unit of measurement for critical band rate. A bark, a pure tone represented as a masking curve, produces a plot consistent with frequency and more accurately than linear or logarithmic plots. The bark scale ranges from 1 to 24, resulting in 24 barks, or cumulative critical bands, from 0 to 16 kHz. |
|  | roughness [asper] returns the roughness measurement in units of asper. An asper is the unit of measurement for roughness. One asper has a sine tone of 1 kHz, a sound level of 60 dB, a modulation depth of 1, and is amplitude-modulated at a frequency of 70 Hz. |
|  | roughness info returns information about the roughness measurement. x-axis units returns the units of the x-axis. 0 Frequency The number of occurrences of a repeating event during a period. 1 Critical band The smallest band of frequencies around a given frequency. 2 Critical band rate The frequency scale used in describing characteristics of the human hearing system. x-axis unit label returns the label of the x-axis. y-axis units returns the units of the y-axis. 0 Band power 1 Level 2 Loudness 3 Sharpness 4 Roughness 5 Fluctuation strength 6 Tonality y-axis unit label returns the label of the y-axis. data attributes returns information about the sound quality measurements this VI analyzes. |
|  | x-axis units returns the units of the x-axis. 0 Frequency The number of occurrences of a repeating event during a period. 1 Critical band The smallest band of frequencies around a given frequency. 2 Critical band rate The frequency scale used in describing characteristics of the human hearing system. |
| 0 | Frequency The number of occurrences of a repeating event during a period. |
| 1 | Critical band The smallest band of frequencies around a given frequency. |
| 2 | Critical band rate The frequency scale used in describing characteristics of the human hearing system. |
|  | x-axis unit label returns the label of the x-axis. |
|  | y-axis units returns the units of the y-axis. 0 Band power 1 Level 2 Loudness 3 Sharpness 4 Roughness 5 Fluctuation strength 6 Tonality |
| 0 | Band power |
| 1 | Level |
| 2 | Loudness |
| 3 | Sharpness |
| 4 | Roughness |
| 5 | Fluctuation strength |
| 6 | Tonality |
|  | y-axis unit label returns the label of the y-axis. |
|  | data attributes returns information about the sound quality measurements this VI analyzes. |
|  | error out contains error information. This output provides standard error out functionality. |

SV Aures Roughness Details

The SV Aures Roughness VI measures the energy in 24 barks, computes and filters the envelope of the signal in each band, measures the amplitude modulation of each envelope, and then weights the level in each band by the modulation index and a frequency-dependent weighting function. The VI returns the result as the roughness spectrum versus critical band rate, and then integrates the roughness spectrum to measure the roughness.

You can use this VI to analyze short or long signals. However, National Instruments recommends including several cycles of the desired modulation in the analysis. Therefore, a sound pressure signal length of approximately 100 ms is a practical minimum, while 200 to 250 ms is more common.

 
 

 
 
Example

Refer to the Roughness and Fluctuation Strength (Simulated) VI in the 
 labview\examples\Sound and Vibration\Sound Quality
 directory for an example of using the SV Aures Roughness VI.

[IMAGE alt='image' src='images/open.gif']
 Open example 
 
 [IMAGE alt='image' src='images/find.gif']
 Find related examples

Parent topic:

Sound Quality VIs

<!--NI_TOPIC bundle=labview-sound-and-vibration-toolkit-api-ref path=sv-aures-sharpness-vi.html language=enus -->
## TOPIC 00048: SV Aures Sharpness VI

- bundle_id: `labview-sound-and-vibration-toolkit-api-ref`
- source_path: `sv-aures-sharpness-vi.html`
- source_url: https://docs-be.ni.com/bundle/labview-sound-and-vibration-toolkit-api-ref/raw/resource/enus/sv-aures-sharpness-vi.html
- document_id: `labview-sound-and-vibration-toolkit-api-ref`
- page_type: `leaf`
- content_type: `reference`
- source_description: Owning Palette: Sound Quality VIs Requires: Sound and Vibration Toolkit Computes sharpness from the sound pressure signal waveform, the 1/3-octave band spectrum calculated over the frequency range 25 Hz to 12.5 kHz, or the specific loudness that the SV Stationary Loudness VI returns. Wire data to th

### SV Aures Sharpness VI

**Owning Palette:**
 Sound Quality VIs

**Requires:**
 Sound and Vibration Toolkit

Computes sharpness from the sound pressure signal waveform, the 1/3-octave band spectrum calculated over the frequency range 25 Hz to 12.5 kHz, or the specific loudness that the SV Stationary Loudness VI returns. Wire data to the 
 **sound pressure signal [Pa]**
 input to determine the polymorphic instance to use or manually select the instance.

Sharpness is a hearing sensation related to frequency and independent of loudness.

Details Example

| SV Aures Sharpness (Time) (1 Ch)SV Aures Sharpness (Octave) (1 Ch)SV Aures Sharpness (Loudness) (1 Ch) |
| --- |

SV Aures Sharpness (Time) (1 Ch)

[IMAGE alt='image' src='images/SV_Aures_Sharpness_Time_1_Ch.gif']

|  | diffuse field specifies if the sound field is diffuse (TRUE) or a free field (FALSE). The default is free field (FALSE). |
| --- | --- |
|  | sound pressure signal [Pa] specifies the sound pressure signal in Pascals. This signal keeps track of the timing of the waveform and the values the waveform contains. t0 specifies the start time of the waveform. dt specifies the time interval in seconds between data points in the waveform. Y specifies the data values of the waveform. |
|  | t0 specifies the start time of the waveform. |
|  | dt specifies the time interval in seconds between data points in the waveform. |
|  | Y specifies the data values of the waveform. |
|  | error in describes error conditions that occur before this node runs. This input provides standard error in functionality. |
|  | specific sharpness returns the specific sharpness at the critical band rate. critical band rate returns the critical band rate in units of bark. A bark is the unit of measurement for critical band rate. A bark, a pure tone represented as a masking curve, produces a plot consistent with frequency and more accurately than linear or logarithmic plots. The bark scale ranges from 1 to 24, resulting in 24 barks, or cumulative critical bands, from 0 to 16 kHz. specific sharpness returns the sharpness at each element of the critical band rate . |
|  | critical band rate returns the critical band rate in units of bark. A bark is the unit of measurement for critical band rate. A bark, a pure tone represented as a masking curve, produces a plot consistent with frequency and more accurately than linear or logarithmic plots. The bark scale ranges from 1 to 24, resulting in 24 barks, or cumulative critical bands, from 0 to 16 kHz. |
|  | specific sharpness returns the sharpness at each element of the critical band rate . |
|  | sharpness [acum] returns the sharpness measurement in units of acum. An acum is the unit of measurement for sharpness. One acum equals a narrow-band noise at a frequency of 1 kHz with a bandwidth less than 150 Hz and a sound level of 60 dB. |
|  | sharpness info returns information about the sharpness measurement. x-axis units returns the units of the x-axis. 0 Frequency The number of occurrences of a repeating event during a period. 1 Critical band The smallest band of frequencies around a given frequency. 2 Critical band rate The frequency scale used in describing characteristics of the human hearing system. x-axis unit label returns the label of the x-axis. y-axis units returns the units of the y-axis. 0 Band power 1 Level 2 Loudness 3 Sharpness 4 Roughness 5 Fluctuation strength 6 Tonality y-axis unit label returns the label of the y-axis. data attributes returns information about the sound quality measurements this VI analyzes. |
|  | x-axis units returns the units of the x-axis. 0 Frequency The number of occurrences of a repeating event during a period. 1 Critical band The smallest band of frequencies around a given frequency. 2 Critical band rate The frequency scale used in describing characteristics of the human hearing system. |
| 0 | Frequency The number of occurrences of a repeating event during a period. |
| 1 | Critical band The smallest band of frequencies around a given frequency. |
| 2 | Critical band rate The frequency scale used in describing characteristics of the human hearing system. |
|  | x-axis unit label returns the label of the x-axis. |
|  | y-axis units returns the units of the y-axis. 0 Band power 1 Level 2 Loudness 3 Sharpness 4 Roughness 5 Fluctuation strength 6 Tonality |
| 0 | Band power |
| 1 | Level |
| 2 | Loudness |
| 3 | Sharpness |
| 4 | Roughness |
| 5 | Fluctuation strength |
| 6 | Tonality |
|  | y-axis unit label returns the label of the y-axis. |
|  | data attributes returns information about the sound quality measurements this VI analyzes. |
|  | error out contains error information. This output provides standard error out functionality. |

SV Aures Sharpness (Octave) (1 Ch)

[IMAGE alt='image' src='images/SV_Aures_Sharpness_Octave_1_Ch.gif']

|  | diffuse field specifies if the sound field is diffuse (TRUE) or a free field (FALSE). The default is free field (FALSE). |
| --- | --- |
|  | octave spectrum specifies the octave spectrum to perform the operation on, in band power versus nominal frequencies. nominal frequencies [Hz] specifies the nominal, or preferred, frequencies as opposed to the exact frequencies associated with each 1/ n octave band. band power specifies the band power in decibels associated with each 1/ n octave band. |
|  | nominal frequencies [Hz] specifies the nominal, or preferred, frequencies as opposed to the exact frequencies associated with each 1/ n octave band. |
|  | band power specifies the band power in decibels associated with each 1/ n octave band. |
|  | octave info specifies information about the octave spectrum that you can pass from another VI. Note You must wire octave info when performing extended measurements. Do not modify the octave info values. If you want to view the information, you can right-click the octave info wire and select Custom Probe»Octave Info from the shortcut menu. |
|  | error in describes error conditions that occur before this node runs. This input provides standard error in functionality. |
|  | specific sharpness returns the specific sharpness at the critical band rate. critical band rate returns the critical band rate in units of bark. A bark is the unit of measurement for critical band rate. A bark, a pure tone represented as a masking curve, produces a plot consistent with frequency and more accurately than linear or logarithmic plots. The bark scale ranges from 1 to 24, resulting in 24 barks, or cumulative critical bands, from 0 to 16 kHz. specific sharpness returns the sharpness at each element of the critical band rate . |
|  | critical band rate returns the critical band rate in units of bark. A bark is the unit of measurement for critical band rate. A bark, a pure tone represented as a masking curve, produces a plot consistent with frequency and more accurately than linear or logarithmic plots. The bark scale ranges from 1 to 24, resulting in 24 barks, or cumulative critical bands, from 0 to 16 kHz. |
|  | specific sharpness returns the sharpness at each element of the critical band rate . |
|  | sharpness [acum] returns the sharpness measurement in units of acum. An acum is the unit of measurement for sharpness. One acum equals a narrow-band noise at a frequency of 1 kHz with a bandwidth less than 150 Hz and a sound level of 60 dB. |
|  | sharpness info returns information about the sharpness measurement. x-axis units returns the units of the x-axis. 0 Frequency The number of occurrences of a repeating event during a period. 1 Critical band The smallest band of frequencies around a given frequency. 2 Critical band rate The frequency scale used in describing characteristics of the human hearing system. x-axis unit label returns the label of the x-axis. y-axis units returns the units of the y-axis. 0 Band power 1 Level 2 Loudness 3 Sharpness 4 Roughness 5 Fluctuation strength 6 Tonality y-axis unit label returns the label of the y-axis. data attributes returns information about the sound quality measurements this VI analyzes. |
|  | x-axis units returns the units of the x-axis. 0 Frequency The number of occurrences of a repeating event during a period. 1 Critical band The smallest band of frequencies around a given frequency. 2 Critical band rate The frequency scale used in describing characteristics of the human hearing system. |
| 0 | Frequency The number of occurrences of a repeating event during a period. |
| 1 | Critical band The smallest band of frequencies around a given frequency. |
| 2 | Critical band rate The frequency scale used in describing characteristics of the human hearing system. |
|  | x-axis unit label returns the label of the x-axis. |
|  | y-axis units returns the units of the y-axis. 0 Band power 1 Level 2 Loudness 3 Sharpness 4 Roughness 5 Fluctuation strength 6 Tonality |
| 0 | Band power |
| 1 | Level |
| 2 | Loudness |
| 3 | Sharpness |
| 4 | Roughness |
| 5 | Fluctuation strength |
| 6 | Tonality |
|  | y-axis unit label returns the label of the y-axis. |
|  | data attributes returns information about the sound quality measurements this VI analyzes. |
|  | error out contains error information. This output provides standard error out functionality. |

SV Aures Sharpness (Loudness) (1 Ch)

[IMAGE alt='image' src='images/SV_Aures_Sharpness_Loudness_1_Ch.gif']

|  | specific loudness contains the specific loudness that the SV Stationary Loudness VI computes. Wire the specific loudness output of the SV Stationary Loudness VI to this input. critical band rate specifies the critical band rate in units of bark. specific loudness specifies the loudness at each element of the critical band rate . |
| --- | --- |
|  | critical band rate specifies the critical band rate in units of bark. |
|  | specific loudness specifies the loudness at each element of the critical band rate . |
|  | loudness info specifies the loudness info that the SV Stationary Loudness VI computes. Wire the loudness info output of the SV Stationary Loudness VI to this input. x-axis units specifies the units of the x-axis. The valid values are Frequency , Critical Band , and Critical Band Rate . x-axis unit label specifies the label of the x-axis. y-axis units specifies the units of the y-axis. The valid values are Band Power , Level , Loudness , Sharpness , Roughness , Fluctuation Strength , and Tonality . y-axis unit label specifies the label of the y-axis. data attributes specifies information about the sound quality measurements this VI analyzes. |
|  | x-axis units specifies the units of the x-axis. The valid values are Frequency , Critical Band , and Critical Band Rate . |
|  | x-axis unit label specifies the label of the x-axis. |
|  | y-axis units specifies the units of the y-axis. The valid values are Band Power , Level , Loudness , Sharpness , Roughness , Fluctuation Strength , and Tonality . |
|  | y-axis unit label specifies the label of the y-axis. |
|  | data attributes specifies information about the sound quality measurements this VI analyzes. |
|  | error in describes error conditions that occur before this node runs. This input provides standard error in functionality. |
|  | specific sharpness returns the specific sharpness at the critical band rate. critical band rate returns the critical band rate in units of bark. A bark is the unit of measurement for critical band rate. A bark, a pure tone represented as a masking curve, produces a plot consistent with frequency and more accurately than linear or logarithmic plots. The bark scale ranges from 1 to 24, resulting in 24 barks, or cumulative critical bands, from 0 to 16 kHz. specific sharpness returns the sharpness at each element of the critical band rate . |
|  | critical band rate returns the critical band rate in units of bark. A bark is the unit of measurement for critical band rate. A bark, a pure tone represented as a masking curve, produces a plot consistent with frequency and more accurately than linear or logarithmic plots. The bark scale ranges from 1 to 24, resulting in 24 barks, or cumulative critical bands, from 0 to 16 kHz. |
|  | specific sharpness returns the sharpness at each element of the critical band rate . |
|  | sharpness [acum] returns the sharpness measurement in units of acum. An acum is the unit of measurement for sharpness. One acum equals a narrow-band noise at a frequency of 1 kHz with a bandwidth less than 150 Hz and a sound level of 60 dB. |
|  | sharpness info returns information about the sharpness measurement. x-axis units returns the units of the x-axis. 0 Frequency The number of occurrences of a repeating event during a period. 1 Critical band The smallest band of frequencies around a given frequency. 2 Critical band rate The frequency scale used in describing characteristics of the human hearing system. x-axis unit label returns the label of the x-axis. y-axis units returns the units of the y-axis. 0 Band power 1 Level 2 Loudness 3 Sharpness 4 Roughness 5 Fluctuation strength 6 Tonality y-axis unit label returns the label of the y-axis. data attributes returns information about the sound quality measurements this VI analyzes. |
|  | x-axis units returns the units of the x-axis. 0 Frequency The number of occurrences of a repeating event during a period. 1 Critical band The smallest band of frequencies around a given frequency. 2 Critical band rate The frequency scale used in describing characteristics of the human hearing system. |
| 0 | Frequency The number of occurrences of a repeating event during a period. |
| 1 | Critical band The smallest band of frequencies around a given frequency. |
| 2 | Critical band rate The frequency scale used in describing characteristics of the human hearing system. |
|  | x-axis unit label returns the label of the x-axis. |
|  | y-axis units returns the units of the y-axis. 0 Band power 1 Level 2 Loudness 3 Sharpness 4 Roughness 5 Fluctuation strength 6 Tonality |
| 0 | Band power |
| 1 | Level |
| 2 | Loudness |
| 3 | Sharpness |
| 4 | Roughness |
| 5 | Fluctuation strength |
| 6 | Tonality |
|  | y-axis unit label returns the label of the y-axis. |
|  | data attributes returns information about the sound quality measurements this VI analyzes. |
|  | error out contains error information. This output provides standard error out functionality. |

SV Aures Sharpness Details

The SV Aures Sharpness VI normalizes the specific loudness spectrum by the total loudness and weights the spectrum according to frequency. The VI returns the frequency-weighted result as the specific sharpness versus critical band rate, and then integrates the specific sharpness to measure the sharpness. Higher frequency components in the signal generally result in higher sharpness measurements.

You can use this VI to analyze sharpness measurement of the specified sound pressure signal. This VI measures the excessive high-frequency content in the signal. The higher the frequency, the more unpleasant the signal. This allows for the identification of sound preferences.

 
 

 
 
Example

Refer to the Stationary Loudness and Sharpness (Simulated) VI in the 
 labview\examples\Sound and Vibration\Sound Quality
 directory for an example of using the SV Aures Sharpness VI.

[IMAGE alt='image' src='images/open.gif']
 Open example 
 
 [IMAGE alt='image' src='images/find.gif']
 Find related examples

Sound Quality VIs

## TOPIC 00049: SV Aures Tonality VI

- bundle_id: `labview-sound-and-vibration-toolkit-api-ref`
- source_path: `sv-aures-tonality-vi.html`
- source_url: https://docs-be.ni.com/bundle/labview-sound-and-vibration-toolkit-api-ref/raw/resource/enus/sv-aures-tonality-vi.html
- document_id: `labview-sound-and-vibration-toolkit-api-ref`
- page_type: `leaf`
- content_type: `reference`
- source_description: Owning Palette: Sound Quality VIs Requires: Sound and Vibration Toolkit Computes tonality of the sound pressure signal according to Aures' method on successive 160 ms blocks. Tonality measures the relative strength of the tones in a signal compared to the overall signal. For each time block, this VI

### SV Aures Tonality VI

**Owning Palette:**
 Sound Quality VIs

**Requires:**
 Sound and Vibration Toolkit

Computes tonality of the sound pressure signal according to Aures' method on successive 160 ms blocks.

Tonality measures the relative strength of the tones in a signal compared to the overall signal.

For each time block, this VI first varies the frequency resolution according to the frequency selectivity of human hearing, then searches the spectrum for likely tones, and then compares the loudness of the tones to the loudness of the sound.

[IMAGE alt='image' src='images/SV_Aures_Tonality.gif']

|  | sound pressure signal [Pa] specifies the sound pressure signal in Pascals. This signal keeps track of the timing of the waveform and the values the waveform contains. t0 specifies the start time of the waveform. dt specifies the time interval in seconds between data points in the waveform. Y specifies the data values of the waveform. |
| --- | --- |
|  | t0 specifies the start time of the waveform. |
|  | dt specifies the time interval in seconds between data points in the waveform. |
|  | Y specifies the data values of the waveform. |
|  | error in describes error conditions that occur before this node runs. This input provides standard error in functionality. |
|  | 1/3 octave spectrum returns the 1/3 octave spectrum in terms of XY data, where X is the array of nominal center frequencies in Hz and Y is the array of band powers. You can display the results in an Octave Graph or a Multiplot Octave Graph indicator. |
|  | tonality [tu] returns the tonality measurement in tu. A tu is the unit of measurement for tonality. One tu equals a 1 kHz sine tone with a sound level of 60 dB. The smallest unit, the tu describes the relationship between tonal segments and phonemic segments. |
|  | tonality info returns information about the tonality measurement. x-axis units returns the units of the x-axis. 0Frequency —The number of occurrences of a repeating event during a period. 1Critical band —The smallest band of frequencies around a given frequency. 2Critical band rate —The frequency scale used in describing characteristics of the human hearing system. x-axis unit label returns the label of the x-axis. y-axis units returns the units of the y-axis. 0Band power1Level2Loudness3Sharpness4Roughness5Fluctuation strength6Tonality y-axis unit label returns the label of the y-axis. data attributes returns information about the sound quality measurements this VI analyzes. |
|  | x-axis units returns the units of the x-axis. 0Frequency —The number of occurrences of a repeating event during a period. 1Critical band —The smallest band of frequencies around a given frequency. 2Critical band rate —The frequency scale used in describing characteristics of the human hearing system. |
| 0 | Frequency —The number of occurrences of a repeating event during a period. |
| 1 | Critical band —The smallest band of frequencies around a given frequency. |
| 2 | Critical band rate —The frequency scale used in describing characteristics of the human hearing system. |
|  | x-axis unit label returns the label of the x-axis. |
|  | y-axis units returns the units of the y-axis. 0Band power1Level2Loudness3Sharpness4Roughness5Fluctuation strength6Tonality |
| 0 | Band power |
| 1 | Level |
| 2 | Loudness |
| 3 | Sharpness |
| 4 | Roughness |
| 5 | Fluctuation strength |
| 6 | Tonality |
|  | y-axis unit label returns the label of the y-axis. |
|  | data attributes returns information about the sound quality measurements this VI analyzes. |
|  | error out contains error information. This output provides standard error out functionality. |

## TOPIC 00050: SV Average Spectra VI

- bundle_id: `labview-sound-and-vibration-toolkit-api-ref`
- source_path: `sv-average-spectra-vi.html`
- source_url: https://docs-be.ni.com/bundle/labview-sound-and-vibration-toolkit-api-ref/raw/resource/enus/sv-average-spectra-vi.html
- document_id: `labview-sound-and-vibration-toolkit-api-ref`
- page_type: `leaf`
- content_type: `reference`
- source_description: Owning Palette: Embedded Monitoring VIs Requires: Sound and Vibration Toolkit Computes the linear RMS-averaged power spectrum of real spectra . Use rms averaging to reduce variance in noise component levels in the average spectrum in order to quantify stable signal component levels as a function of

### SV Average Spectra VI

**Owning Palette:**
 Embedded Monitoring VIs

**Requires:**
 Sound and Vibration Toolkit

Computes the linear RMS-averaged power spectrum of 
 **real spectra**
 . Use rms averaging to reduce variance in noise component levels in the average spectrum in order to quantify stable signal component levels as a function of frequency. 
This VI can be used to implement time (block) averaging, spatial (channel) averaging, and custom averaging to combine data from a set of calculated spectra. This VI does not maintain state internally; you can use this VI in a loop to process multiple blocks. Wire data to the 
 **spectra**
 input and manually select the averaging in the polymorphic selector to determine the polymorphic instance to use.

| SV RMS Average Spectra (Real)SV Peak Hold Spectra (Real)SV Vector Average Spectra (Complex)SV RMS Average Spectra (Complex)SV Peak Hold Spectra (Complex) |
| --- |

SV RMS Average Spectra (Real)

[IMAGE alt='image' src='images/SV_RMS_Average_Spectra_(Real).gif']

|  | real spectra specifies the real parts of the rectangular components. f0 specifies the start frequency, in hertz, of the spectrum. df specifies the frequency resolution, in hertz, of the spectrum. magnitude specifies the magnitude of the spectrum. |
| --- | --- |
|  | f0 specifies the start frequency, in hertz, of the spectrum. |
|  | df specifies the frequency resolution, in hertz, of the spectrum. |
|  | magnitude specifies the magnitude of the spectrum. |
|  | spectrum info specifies properties of the computed spectrum. Note You must wire spectrum info when performing extended measurements. Do not modify the spectrum info values. If you want to view the information, you can create a custom probe. |
|  | error in describes error conditions that occur before this node runs. This input provides standard error in functionality. |
|  | rms average spectrum returns the rms average spectrum . |
|  | unit label returns the selected engineering units. |
|  | spectrum info returns information about the spectrum needed by the Spectrum Extended Measurements VIs, the Display VIs, and the Tone and Distortion VIs. Note You must wire spectrum info when performing extended measurements. Do not modify the spectrum info values. If you want to view the information, you can right-click the spectrum info wire and select Custom Probe»Spectrum Info from the shortcut menu. |
|  | error out contains error information. This output provides standard error out functionality. |
|  | averages completed returns the number of averages completed by the VI at that time. |

Note

Note You must wire spectrum info when performing extended measurements. Do not modify the spectrum info values. If you want to view the information, you can right-click the spectrum info wire and select Custom Probe»Spectrum Info from the shortcut menu. |
