<!--NI_TOPIC bundle=labview-sound-and-vibration-toolkit-api-ref path=svfa-get-spectrum-subset-vi.html language=enus -->
## TOPIC 00074: SVFA Get Spectrum Subset VI

- bundle_id: `labview-sound-and-vibration-toolkit-api-ref`
- source_path: `svfa-get-spectrum-subset-vi.html`
- source_url: https://docs-be.ni.com/bundle/labview-sound-and-vibration-toolkit-api-ref/raw/resource/enus/svfa-get-spectrum-subset-vi.html
- document_id: `labview-sound-and-vibration-toolkit-api-ref`
- page_type: `leaf`
- content_type: `reference`
- source_description: Owning Palette: Spectrum Extended Measurements VIs Requires: Sound and Vibration Toolkit Extracts the subset of the spectrum specified by range . x0 is updated to reflect the new start frequency, dx is the same as in the input spectrum, and the spectrum array is shortened so the last element corresp

### SVFA Get Spectrum Subset VI

**Owning Palette:**
 Spectrum Extended Measurements VIs

**Requires:**
 Sound and Vibration Toolkit

Extracts the subset of the spectrum specified by 
 **range**
 . 
 **x0**
 is updated to reflect the new start frequency, 
 **dx**
 is the same as in the input spectrum, and the spectrum array is shortened so the last element corresponds with the stop value specified in the 
 **range**
 . Use this VI for operations such as limiting subsequent spectrum operations to the region of interest or removing the part of the spectrum outside the alias-free bandwidth, as well as others. Wire data to the 
 **spectrum**
 input to determine the polymorphic instance to use or manually select the instance.

Example

| SVFA Get Spectrum Subset (Real Spectrum) (1 Ch)SVFA Get Spectrum Subset (Real Spectrum) (N Ch)SVFA Get Spectrum Subset (Real Spectrum) (NxM)SVFA Get Spectrum Subset (Complex Spectrum) (1 Ch)SVFA Get Spectrum Subset (Complex Spectrum) (N Ch) |
| --- |

SVFA Get Spectrum Subset (Real Spectrum) (1 Ch)

[IMAGE alt='image' src='images/SVFA_Get_Spectrum_Subset_1_Ch.gif']

|  | spectrum specifies the abscissa scaling and the magnitude of the spectrum. x0 specifies the start frequency or order of the spectrum. The default is 0.00. dx specifies the frequency or order resolution of the spectrum. The default is 0.00. magnitude specifies the magnitude of the spectrum. |
| --- | --- |
|  | x0 specifies the start frequency or order of the spectrum. The default is 0.00. |
|  | dx specifies the frequency or order resolution of the spectrum. The default is 0.00. |
|  | magnitude specifies the magnitude of the spectrum. |
|  | range specifies the band to extract from the lowest frequency to the highest frequency. start specifies the lowest frequency or order of interest for the analysis band. The valid range is between 0 and the Nyquist frequency or order. Specify a value of –1 to select the lowest frequency or order as the minimum frequency or order of interest. The default is –1.00. stop specifies the highest frequency or order of interest for the analysis band. The valid range is between 0 and the Nyquist frequency or order. Specify a value of –1 to select the highest frequency or order as the maximum frequency or order of interest. The default is –1.00. |
|  | start specifies the lowest frequency or order of interest for the analysis band. The valid range is between 0 and the Nyquist frequency or order. Specify a value of –1 to select the lowest frequency or order as the minimum frequency or order of interest. The default is –1.00. |
|  | stop specifies the highest frequency or order of interest for the analysis band. The valid range is between 0 and the Nyquist frequency or order. Specify a value of –1 to select the highest frequency or order as the maximum frequency or order of interest. The default is –1.00. |
|  | spectrum info specifies properties of the computed spectrum. Note You must wire spectrum info when performing extended measurements. Do not modify the spectrum info values. If you want to view the information, you can right-click the spectrum info wire and select Custom Probe»Spectrum Info from the shortcut menu. |
|  | error in describes error conditions that occur before this node runs. This input provides standard error in functionality. |
|  | spectrum subset returns the portion of the spectrum within the returned actual range . x0 returns the start frequency or order of the spectrum. dx returns the frequency or order resolution of the spectrum. magnitude returns the magnitude of the spectrum. |
|  | x0 returns the start frequency or order of the spectrum. |
|  | dx returns the frequency or order resolution of the spectrum. |
|  | magnitude returns the magnitude of the spectrum. |
|  | unit label returns the engineering units. |
|  | actual range returns the coerced start and stop frequencies or orders. start returns the lowest frequency or order of the spectrum subset. stop returns the highest frequency or order of the spectrum subset. |
|  | start returns the lowest frequency or order of the spectrum subset. |
|  | stop returns the highest frequency or order of the spectrum subset. |
|  | range label returns a string containing the coerced frequency or order range. |
|  | dup spectrum info returns spectrum info unchanged. Note You must wire dup spectrum info when performing extended measurements. Do not modify the dup spectrum info values. If you want to view the information, you can right-click the dup spectrum info wire and select Custom Probe»Spectrum Info from the shortcut menu. |
|  | error out contains error information. This output provides standard error out functionality. |

SVFA Get Spectrum Subset (Real Spectrum) (N Ch)

[IMAGE alt='image' src='images/SVFA_Get_Spectrum_Subset_N_Ch.gif']

|  | spectra specifies the abscissa scaling and the magnitude for each spectrum. x0 specifies the start frequency or order of the spectrum. The default is 0.00. dx specifies the frequency or order resolution of the spectrum. The default is 0.00. magnitude specifies the magnitude of the spectrum. |
| --- | --- |
|  | x0 specifies the start frequency or order of the spectrum. The default is 0.00. |
|  | dx specifies the frequency or order resolution of the spectrum. The default is 0.00. |
|  | magnitude specifies the magnitude of the spectrum. |
|  | range specifies the band to extract from the lowest frequency to the highest frequency. start specifies the lowest frequency or order of interest for the analysis band. The valid range is between 0 and the Nyquist frequency or order. Specify a value of –1 to select the lowest frequency or order as the minimum frequency or order of interest. The default is –1.00. stop specifies the highest frequency or order of interest for the analysis band. The valid range is between 0 and the Nyquist frequency or order. Specify a value of –1 to select the highest frequency or order as the maximum frequency or order of interest. The default is –1.00. |
|  | start specifies the lowest frequency or order of interest for the analysis band. The valid range is between 0 and the Nyquist frequency or order. Specify a value of –1 to select the lowest frequency or order as the minimum frequency or order of interest. The default is –1.00. |
|  | stop specifies the highest frequency or order of interest for the analysis band. The valid range is between 0 and the Nyquist frequency or order. Specify a value of –1 to select the highest frequency or order as the maximum frequency or order of interest. The default is –1.00. |
|  | spectrum info specifies properties of the computed spectrum. Note You must wire spectrum info when performing extended measurements. Do not modify the spectrum info values. If you want to view the information, you can create a custom probe. |
|  | error in describes error conditions that occur before this node runs. This input provides standard error in functionality. |
|  | spectra subsets returns the portions of the spectra within the returned actual ranges . x0 returns the start frequency or order of the spectrum. dx returns the frequency or order resolution of the spectrum. magnitude returns the magnitude of the spectrum. |
|  | x0 returns the start frequency or order of the spectrum. |
|  | dx returns the frequency or order resolution of the spectrum. |
|  | magnitude returns the magnitude of the spectrum. |
|  | unit labels returns the selected engineering units for each channel. |
|  | actual ranges returns the coerced start and stop frequencies or orders for the returned spectra subsets . start returns the lowest frequency or order of the spectrum subset. stop returns the highest frequency or order of the spectrum subset. |
|  | start returns the lowest frequency or order of the spectrum subset. |
|  | stop returns the highest frequency or order of the spectrum subset. |
|  | range labels returns an array of strings containing the coerced frequency or order ranges. |
|  | dup spectrum info returns spectrum info unchanged. Note You must wire dup spectrum info when performing extended measurements. Do not modify the dup spectrum info values. If you want to view the information, you can create a custom probe. |
|  | error out contains error information. This output provides standard error out functionality. |

|  | stop returns the highest frequency or order of the spectrum subset. |
|  | range labels returns an array of strings containing the coerced frequency or order ranges. |
|  | dup spectrum info returns spectrum info unchanged. Note You must wire dup spectrum info when performing extended measurements. Do not modify the dup spectrum info values. If you want to view the information, you can create a custom probe. |
|  | error out contains error information. This output provides standard error out functionality. |

SVFA Get Spectrum Subset (Complex Spectrum) (1 Ch)

[IMAGE alt='image' src='images/SVFA_Get_Spectrum_Subset_Complex_1_Ch.gif']

|  | complex spectrum specifies the complex form of the spectrum. f0 specifies the start frequency, in hertz, of the spectrum. df specifies the frequency resolution, in hertz, of the spectrum. complex array specifies the complex form of the spectrum. spectrum info specifies properties of the computed spectrum. Note You must wire spectrum info when performing extended measurements. Do not modify the spectrum info values. If you want to view the information, you can unbundle and right-click the spectrum info wire and select Custom Probe»Spectrum Info from the shortcut menu. |
| --- | --- |
|  | f0 specifies the start frequency, in hertz, of the spectrum. |
|  | df specifies the frequency resolution, in hertz, of the spectrum. |
|  | complex array specifies the complex form of the spectrum. |
|  | spectrum info specifies properties of the computed spectrum. Note You must wire spectrum info when performing extended measurements. Do not modify the spectrum info values. If you want to view the information, you can unbundle and right-click the spectrum info wire and select Custom Probe»Spectrum Info from the shortcut menu. |
|  | range specifies the band to extract from the lowest frequency to the highest frequency. start specifies the lowest frequency or order of interest for the analysis band. The valid range is between 0 and the Nyquist frequency or order. Specify a value of –1 to select the lowest frequency or order as the minimum frequency or order of interest. The default is –1.00. stop specifies the highest frequency or order of interest for the analysis band. The valid range is between 0 and the Nyquist frequency or order. Specify a value of –1 to select the highest frequency or order as the maximum frequency or order of interest. The default is –1.00. |
|  | start specifies the lowest frequency or order of interest for the analysis band. The valid range is between 0 and the Nyquist frequency or order. Specify a value of –1 to select the lowest frequency or order as the minimum frequency or order of interest. The default is –1.00. |
|  | stop specifies the highest frequency or order of interest for the analysis band. The valid range is between 0 and the Nyquist frequency or order. Specify a value of –1 to select the highest frequency or order as the maximum frequency or order of interest. The default is –1.00. |
|  | error in describes error conditions that occur before this node runs. This input provides standard error in functionality. |
|  | complex spectrum subset returns the portion of the complex spectrum within the returned actual range . f0 returns the start frequency, in hertz, of the spectrum. df returns the frequency resolution, in hertz, of the spectrum. complex array returns the complex spectrum subset. |
|  | f0 returns the start frequency, in hertz, of the spectrum. |
|  | df returns the frequency resolution, in hertz, of the spectrum. |
|  | complex array returns the complex spectrum subset. |
|  | actual range returns the coerced start and stop frequencies or orders. start returns the lowest frequency or order of the spectrum subset. stop returns the highest frequency or order of the spectrum subset. |
|  | start returns the lowest frequency or order of the spectrum subset. |
|  | stop returns the highest frequency or order of the spectrum subset. |
|  | range label returns a string containing the coerced frequency or order range. |
|  | error out contains error information. This output provides standard error out functionality. |

SVFA Get Spectrum Subset (Complex Spectrum) (N Ch)

[IMAGE alt='image' src='images/SVFA_Get_Spectrum_Subset_Complex_N_Ch.gif']

|  | complex spectra specifies the complex forms of the computed spectra. f0 specifies the start frequency, in hertz, of the spectrum. df specifies the frequency resolution, in hertz, of the spectrum. complex array specifies the complex form of the spectrum. spectrum info specifies properties of the computed spectrum. Note You must wire spectrum info when performing extended measurements. Do not modify the spectrum info values. If you want to view the information, you can unbundle and right-click the spectrum info wire and select Custom Probe»Spectrum Info from the shortcut menu. |
| --- | --- |
|  | f0 specifies the start frequency, in hertz, of the spectrum. |
|  | df specifies the frequency resolution, in hertz, of the spectrum. |
|  | complex array specifies the complex form of the spectrum. |
|  | spectrum info specifies properties of the computed spectrum. Note You must wire spectrum info when performing extended measurements. Do not modify the spectrum info values. If you want to view the information, you can unbundle and right-click the spectrum info wire and select Custom Probe»Spectrum Info from the shortcut menu. |
|  | range specifies the band to extract from the lowest frequency to the highest frequency. start specifies the lowest frequency or order of interest for the analysis band. The valid range is between 0 and the Nyquist frequency or order. Specify a value of –1 to select the lowest frequency or order as the minimum frequency or order of interest. The default is –1.00. stop specifies the highest frequency or order of interest for the analysis band. The valid range is between 0 and the Nyquist frequency or order. Specify a value of –1 to select the highest frequency or order as the maximum frequency or order of interest. The default is –1.00. |
|  | start specifies the lowest frequency or order of interest for the analysis band. The valid range is between 0 and the Nyquist frequency or order. Specify a value of –1 to select the lowest frequency or order as the minimum frequency or order of interest. The default is –1.00. |
|  | stop specifies the highest frequency or order of interest for the analysis band. The valid range is between 0 and the Nyquist frequency or order. Specify a value of –1 to select the highest frequency or order as the maximum frequency or order of interest. The default is –1.00. |
|  | error in describes error conditions that occur before this node runs. This input provides standard error in functionality. |
|  | complex spectra subsets returns the portions of the complex spectra within the returned actual range . f0 returns the start frequency, in hertz, of the spectrum. df returns the frequency resolution, in hertz, of the spectrum. complex array returns the complex spectrum subsets. |
|  | f0 returns the start frequency, in hertz, of the spectrum. |
|  | df returns the frequency resolution, in hertz, of the spectrum. |
|  | complex array returns the complex spectrum subsets. |
|  | actual ranges returns the coerced start and stop frequencies or orders for the returned spectra subsets . start returns the lowest frequency or order of the spectrum subset. stop returns the highest frequency or order of the spectrum subset. |
|  | start returns the lowest frequency or order of the spectrum subset. |
|  | stop returns the highest frequency or order of the spectrum subset. |
|  | range labels returns an array of strings containing the coerced frequency or order ranges. |
|  | error out contains error information. This output provides standard error out functionality. |

Example

Refer to the Impact Test (DAQmx) VI in the 
 labview\examples\Sound and Vibration\Impact Test
 directory for an example of using the SVFA Get Spectrum Subset VI.

<!--NI_TOPIC bundle=labview-sound-and-vibration-toolkit-api-ref path=svfa-get-spectrum-value-vi.html language=enus -->
## TOPIC 00075: SVFA Get Spectrum Value VI

- bundle_id: `labview-sound-and-vibration-toolkit-api-ref`
- source_path: `svfa-get-spectrum-value-vi.html`
- source_url: https://docs-be.ni.com/bundle/labview-sound-and-vibration-toolkit-api-ref/raw/resource/enus/svfa-get-spectrum-value-vi.html
- document_id: `labview-sound-and-vibration-toolkit-api-ref`
- page_type: `leaf`
- content_type: `reference`
- source_description: Owning Palette: Spectrum Extended Measurements VIs Requires: Sound and Vibration Toolkit Extracts the value of the spectrum at the specified frequency. Use the frequency coercion control to specify how the frequency should be coerced if it does not exactly coincide with a frequency line. This VI ope

### SVFA Get Spectrum Value VI

**Owning Palette:**
 Spectrum Extended Measurements VIs

**Requires:**
 Sound and Vibration Toolkit

Extracts the value of the spectrum at the specified frequency. Use the frequency coercion control to specify how the frequency should be coerced if it does not exactly coincide with a frequency line. This VI operates on any real-valued spectrum. Wire data to the 
 **spectrum**
 input to determine the polymorphic instance to use or manually select the instance.

| SVFA Get Spectrum Value (1 Ch)SVFA Get Spectrum Value (N Ch)SVFA Get Spectrum Value (NxM)SVFA Get Waterfall Value (Scalar X)SVFA Get Waterfall Values (Array X) |
| --- |

SVFA Get Spectrum Value (1 Ch)

[IMAGE alt='image' src='images/SVFA_Get_Spectrum_Value_1_Ch.gif']

|  | spectrum specifies the abscissa scaling and the magnitude of the spectrum. x0 specifies the start frequency or order of the spectrum. The default is 0.00. dx specifies the frequency or order resolution of the spectrum. The default is 0.00. magnitude specifies the magnitude of the spectrum. |
| --- | --- |
|  | x0 specifies the start frequency or order of the spectrum. The default is 0.00. |
|  | dx specifies the frequency or order resolution of the spectrum. The default is 0.00. |
|  | magnitude specifies the magnitude of the spectrum. |
|  | x value (1000) specifies the frequency of interest in hertz. The default is 1,000.00. |
|  | coercion (nearest) specifies whether the specified cursor position will be coerced to the closest, next higher, or next lower spectrum value; or whether the spectrum will be interpolated to satisfy the specified cursor position. 0 coerce to nearest 1 coerce to next highest 2 coerce to next lowest 3 interpolate |
| 0 | coerce to nearest |
| 1 | coerce to next highest |
| 2 | coerce to next lowest |
| 3 | interpolate |
|  | spectrum info specifies properties of the computed spectrum. Note You must wire spectrum info when performing extended measurements. Do not modify the spectrum info values. If you want to view the information, you can right-click the spectrum info wire and select Custom Probe»Spectrum Info from the shortcut menu. |
|  | error in describes error conditions that occur before this node runs. This input provides standard error in functionality. |
|  | spectrum value returns the value of the spectrum at the returned actual frequency . |
|  | unit label returns the selected engineering units. |
|  | actual x value returns the coerced frequency corresponding to the returned spectrum value . |
|  | x label returns a string containing the coerced frequency. |
|  | error out contains error information. This output provides standard error out functionality. |

SVFA Get Spectrum Value (N Ch)

[IMAGE alt='image' src='images/SVFA_Get_Spectrum_Value_N_Ch.gif']

|  | spectra specifies the abscissa scaling and the magnitude for each spectrum. x0 specifies the start frequency or order of the spectrum. The default is 0.00. dx specifies the frequency or order resolution of the spectrum. The default is 0.00. magnitude specifies the magnitude of the spectrum. |
| --- | --- |
|  | x0 specifies the start frequency or order of the spectrum. The default is 0.00. |
|  | dx specifies the frequency or order resolution of the spectrum. The default is 0.00. |
|  | magnitude specifies the magnitude of the spectrum. |
|  | x value (1000) specifies the frequency of interest in hertz. The default is 1,000.00. |
|  | coercion (nearest) specifies whether the specified cursor position will be coerced to the closest, next higher, or next lower spectrum value; or whether the spectrum will be interpolated to satisfy the specified cursor position. 0 coerce to nearest 1 coerce to next highest 2 coerce to next lowest 3 interpolate |
| 0 | coerce to nearest |
| 1 | coerce to next highest |
| 2 | coerce to next lowest |
| 3 | interpolate |
|  | spectrum info specifies properties of the computed spectrum. Note You must wire spectrum info when performing extended measurements. Do not modify the spectrum info values. If you want to view the information, you can create a custom probe. |
|  | error in describes error conditions that occur before this node runs. This input provides standard error in functionality. |
|  | spectra values returns the values of the spectra at the returned actual frequencies . |
|  | unit labels returns the selected engineering units for each channel. |
|  | actual x values returns the coerced frequencies corresponding to the returned spectra values . |
|  | x labels returns an array of strings containing the coerced frequencies. |
|  | error out contains error information. This output provides standard error out functionality. |

SVFA Get Spectrum Value (NxM)

[IMAGE alt='image' src='images/SVFA_Get_Spectrum_Value_NxM.gif']

|  | spectra specifies the abscissa scaling and the magnitude for each spectrum. x0 specifies the start frequency or order of the spectrum. The default is 0.00. dx specifies the frequency or order resolution of the spectrum. The default is 0.00. magnitude specifies the magnitude of the spectrum. |
| --- | --- |
|  | x0 specifies the start frequency or order of the spectrum. The default is 0.00. |
|  | dx specifies the frequency or order resolution of the spectrum. The default is 0.00. |
|  | magnitude specifies the magnitude of the spectrum. |
|  | x value (1000) specifies the frequency of interest in hertz. The default is 1,000.00. |
|  | coercion (nearest) specifies whether the specified cursor position will be coerced to the closest, next higher, or next lower spectrum value; or whether the spectrum will be interpolated to satisfy the specified cursor position. 0 coerce to nearest 1 coerce to next highest 2 coerce to next lowest 3 interpolate |
| 0 | coerce to nearest |
| 1 | coerce to next highest |
| 2 | coerce to next lowest |
| 3 | interpolate |
|  | spectrum info specifies the properties of the computed spectrum. Note You must wire spectrum info when performing extended measurements. Do not modify the spectrum info values. If you want to view the information, you can right-click the spectrum info wire and select Custom Probe»Spectrum Info from the shortcut menu. |
|  | error in describes error conditions that occur before this node runs. This input provides standard error in functionality. |
|  | spectra values returns the values of the spectra at the returned actual frequencies . |
|  | unit labels returns the selected engineering units for each channel. |
|  | actual x values returns the coerced frequencies corresponding to the returned spectra values . |
|  | x labels returns an array of strings containing the coerced frequencies. |
|  | error out contains error information. This output provides standard error out functionality. |

SVFA Get Waterfall Value (Scalar X)

[IMAGE alt='image' src='images/SVFA_Get_Waterfall_Value_Scalar_X.gif']

|  | waterfall data specifies the waterfall data from which to derive waterfall values at x value . z0 specifies the initial value of the first plot on the z-axis. dz specifies the interval between plots on the z-axis. plots specifies the data on the waterfall graph. x0 specifies the initial value on the x-axis. dx specifies the interval between points on the waterfall graph. Y specifies the values of the points on the waterfall graph. |
| --- | --- |
|  | z0 specifies the initial value of the first plot on the z-axis. |
|  | dz specifies the interval between plots on the z-axis. |
|  | plots specifies the data on the waterfall graph. x0 specifies the initial value on the x-axis. dx specifies the interval between points on the waterfall graph. Y specifies the values of the points on the waterfall graph. |
|  | x0 specifies the initial value on the x-axis. |
|  | dx specifies the interval between points on the waterfall graph. |
|  | Y specifies the values of the points on the waterfall graph. |
|  | x value (1000) specifies the frequency of interest in hertz. The default is 1,000.00. |
|  | coercion (nearest) specifies whether the specified cursor position will be coerced to the closest, next higher, or next lower spectrum value; or whether the spectrum will be interpolated to satisfy the specified cursor position. 0 coerce to nearest 1 coerce to next highest 2 coerce to next lowest 3 interpolate |
| 0 | coerce to nearest |
| 1 | coerce to next highest |
| 2 | coerce to next lowest |
| 3 | interpolate |
|  | spectrum info specifies properties of the computed spectrum. Note You must wire spectrum info when performing extended measurements. Do not modify the spectrum info values. If you want to view the information, you can right-click the spectrum info wire and select Custom Probe»Spectrum Info from the shortcut menu. |
|  | error in describes error conditions that occur before this node runs. This input provides standard error in functionality. |
|  | waterfall values returns the values of the waterfall data at the x value . |
|  | error out contains error information. This output provides standard error out functionality. |

SVFA Get Waterfall Values (Array X)

[IMAGE alt='image' src='images/SVFA_Get_Waterfall_Value_Array_X.gif']

|  | waterfall data specifies the waterfall data from which to derive waterfall values at x values . z0 specifies the initial value of the first plot on the z-axis. dz specifies the interval between plots on the z-axis. plots specifies the data on the waterfall graph. x0 specifies the initial value on the x-axis. dx specifies the interval between points on the waterfall graph. Y specifies the values of the points on the waterfall graph. |
| --- | --- |
|  | z0 specifies the initial value of the first plot on the z-axis. |
|  | dz specifies the interval between plots on the z-axis. |
|  | plots specifies the data on the waterfall graph. x0 specifies the initial value on the x-axis. dx specifies the interval between points on the waterfall graph. Y specifies the values of the points on the waterfall graph. |
|  | x0 specifies the initial value on the x-axis. |
|  | dx specifies the interval between points on the waterfall graph. |
|  | Y specifies the values of the points on the waterfall graph. |
|  | x values specifies the values of interest. Each index in the array corresponds to one waterfall plot. Specify duplicate x values to return the waterfall value at x for each plot. |
|  | coercion (nearest) specifies whether the specified cursor position will be coerced to the closest, next higher, or next lower spectrum value; or whether the spectrum will be interpolated to satisfy the specified cursor position. 0 coerce to nearest 1 coerce to next highest 2 coerce to next lowest 3 interpolate |
| 0 | coerce to nearest |
| 1 | coerce to next highest |
| 2 | coerce to next lowest |
| 3 | interpolate |
|  | spectrum info specifies properties of the computed spectrum. Note You must wire spectrum info when performing extended measurements. Do not modify the spectrum info values. If you want to view the information, you can right-click the spectrum info wire and select Custom Probe»Spectrum Info from the shortcut menu. |
|  | error in describes error conditions that occur before this node runs. This input provides standard error in functionality. |
|  | waterfall values returns the value of the waterfall data at the x values . |
|  | error out contains error information. This output provides standard error out functionality. |

<!--NI_TOPIC bundle=labview-sound-and-vibration-toolkit-api-ref path=svfa-magnitude-and-phase-to-real-and-imaginar.html language=enus -->
## TOPIC 00076: SVFA Magnitude and Phase to Real and Imaginary VI

- bundle_id: `labview-sound-and-vibration-toolkit-api-ref`
- source_path: `svfa-magnitude-and-phase-to-real-and-imaginar.html`
- source_url: https://docs-be.ni.com/bundle/labview-sound-and-vibration-toolkit-api-ref/raw/resource/enus/svfa-magnitude-and-phase-to-real-and-imaginar.html
- document_id: `labview-sound-and-vibration-toolkit-api-ref`
- page_type: `leaf`
- content_type: `reference`
- source_description: Owning Palette: Spectrum Extended Measurements VIs Requires: Sound and Vibration Toolkit Converts spectra from polar components to rectangular components. This conversion can reduce the computational power necessary to view and process spectra. Wire data to the magnitude spectrum and phase spectrum

### SVFA Magnitude and Phase to Real and Imaginary VI

**Owning Palette:**
 Spectrum Extended Measurements VIs

**Requires:**
 Sound and Vibration Toolkit

Converts spectra from polar components to rectangular components. This conversion can reduce the computational power necessary to view and process spectra. Wire data to the 
 **magnitude spectrum**
 and 
 **phase spectrum**
 inputs to determine the polymorphic instance to use or manually select the instance.

The SVFA Magnitude and Phase to Real and Imaginary VI also returns the Nyquist plot of the rectangular components. You can use this plot to display these components on an XY graph.

You can use this VI to convert one spectrum or N spectra.

Use the SVFA Real and Imaginary to Magnitude and Phase VI to convert rectangular components to polar components.

Example

| SVFA Magnitude and Phase to Real and Imaginary (1 Ch)SVFA Magnitude and Phase to Real and Imaginary (N Ch)SVFA Magnitude and Phase to Real and Imaginary (NxM) |
| --- |

SVFA Magnitude and Phase to Real and Imaginary (1 Ch)

[IMAGE alt='image' src='images/SVFA_Magnitude_and_Phase_to_Real_and_Imaginary_1_Ch.gif']

|  | magnitude spectrum specifies the frequency spacing and the magnitude of the spectrum in units of either amplitude or power. The spectrum info in parameter specifies the type of units. You can use the SVFA Unit Conversion VI to change the type of units. x0 specifies the start frequency or order of the spectrum. The default is 0.00. dx specifies the frequency or order resolution of the spectrum. The default is 0.00. magnitude specifies the magnitude of the spectrum. |
| --- | --- |
|  | x0 specifies the start frequency or order of the spectrum. The default is 0.00. |
|  | dx specifies the frequency or order resolution of the spectrum. The default is 0.00. |
|  | magnitude specifies the magnitude of the spectrum. |
|  | phase spectrum specifies the frequency spacing and the phase of the spectrum in units of degree or radian. The spectrum info in parameter specifies the type of units. You can use the SVFA Phase Unit Conversion VI to change the type of units. x0 specifies the start frequency or order of the spectrum. The default is 0.00. dx specifies the frequency or order resolution of the spectrum. The default is 0.00. phase specifies the phase of the spectrum. |
|  | x0 specifies the start frequency or order of the spectrum. The default is 0.00. |
|  | dx specifies the frequency or order resolution of the spectrum. The default is 0.00. |
|  | phase specifies the phase of the spectrum. |
|  | spectrum info in specifies the properties of the previously computed spectrum. Note You must wire spectrum info in when performing extended measurements. Do not modify the spectrum info in values. If you want to view the information, you can right-click the spectrum info in wire and select Custom Probe»Spectrum Info from the shortcut menu. |
|  | error in describes error conditions that occur before this node runs. This input provides standard error in functionality. |
|  | Nyquist plot returns the Nyquist plot of the real and imaginary spectrum components. You can display this plot on an XY graph. real part returns the real part of the spectrum. imaginary part returns the imaginary part of the spectrum. |
|  | real part returns the real part of the spectrum. |
|  | imaginary part returns the imaginary part of the spectrum. |
|  | real spectrum returns the real part of the rectangular components. x0 returns the start frequency or order of the spectrum. dx returns the frequency or order resolution of the spectrum. real part returns the real part of the spectrum. |
|  | x0 returns the start frequency or order of the spectrum. |
|  | dx returns the frequency or order resolution of the spectrum. |
|  | real part returns the real part of the spectrum. |
|  | imaginary spectrum returns the imaginary part of the rectangular components. x0 returns the start frequency or order of the spectrum. dx returns the frequency or order resolution of the spectrum. imaginary part returns the imaginary part of the spectrum. |
|  | x0 returns the start frequency or order of the spectrum. |
|  | dx returns the frequency or order resolution of the spectrum. |
|  | imaginary part returns the imaginary part of the spectrum. |
|  | unit label returns the selected engineering units. |
|  | spectrum info out returns the properties of the computed spectrum. Note You must wire spectrum info out when performing extended measurements. Do not modify the spectrum info out values. If you want to view the information, you can right-click the spectrum info out wire and select Custom Probe»Spectrum Info from the shortcut menu. |
|  | error out contains error information. This output provides standard error out functionality. |

SVFA Magnitude and Phase to Real and Imaginary (N Ch)

[IMAGE alt='image' src='images/SVFA_Magnitude_and_Phase_to_Real_and_Imaginary_N_Ch.gif']

|  | magnitude spectra specifies the frequency spacings and the magnitudes of the spectra in units of either amplitude or power. The spectrum info in parameter specifies the type of units. You can use the SVFA Unit Conversion VI to change the type of units. x0 specifies the start frequencies or orders of the spectra. dx specifies the frequencies or order resolutions of the spectra. magnitude specifies the magnitudes of the spectra. |
| --- | --- |
|  | x0 specifies the start frequencies or orders of the spectra. |
|  | dx specifies the frequencies or order resolutions of the spectra. |
|  | magnitude specifies the magnitudes of the spectra. |
|  | phase spectra specifies the frequency spacings and the phases of the spectra in units of degree or radian. The spectrum info in parameter specifies the type of units. You can use the SVFA Phase Unit Conversion VI to change the type of units. x0 specifies the start frequencies or orders of the spectra. dx specifies the frequencies or order resolutions of the spectra. phase specifies the phases of the spectra. |
|  | x0 specifies the start frequencies or orders of the spectra. |
|  | dx specifies the frequencies or order resolutions of the spectra. |
|  | phase specifies the phases of the spectra. |
|  | spectrum info in specifies the properties of the previously computed spectrum. Note You must wire spectrum info in when performing extended measurements. Do not modify the spectrum info in values. If you want to view the information, you can create a custom probe. |
|  | error in describes error conditions that occur before this node runs. This input provides standard error in functionality. |
|  | Nyquist plots returns the Nyquist plots of the real and imaginary spectra components. You can display these plots on one or more XY graphs. real part returns the real parts of the spectra. imaginary part returns the imaginary parts of the spectra. |
|  | real part returns the real parts of the spectra. |
|  | imaginary part returns the imaginary parts of the spectra. |
|  | real spectra returns the real parts of the rectangular components. x0 returns the start frequencies or orders of the spectra. dx returns the frequencies or order resolutions of the spectra. real part returns the real parts of the spectra. |
|  | x0 returns the start frequencies or orders of the spectra. |
|  | dx returns the frequencies or order resolutions of the spectra. |
|  | real part returns the real parts of the spectra. |
|  | imaginary spectra returns the imaginary parts of the rectangular components. x0 returns the start frequencies or orders of the spectra. dx returns the frequencies or order resolutions of the spectra. imaginary part returns the imaginary parts of the spectra. |
|  | x0 returns the start frequencies or orders of the spectra. |
|  | dx returns the frequencies or order resolutions of the spectra. |
|  | imaginary part returns the imaginary parts of the spectra. |
|  | unit labels returns the selected engineering units for each channel. |
|  | spectrum info out returns the properties of the computed spectrum. Note You must wire spectrum info out when performing extended measurements. Do not modify the spectrum info out values. If you want to view the information, you can create a custom probe. |
|  | error out contains error information. This output provides standard error out functionality. |

SVFA Magnitude and Phase to Real and Imaginary (NxM)

[IMAGE alt='image' src='images/SVFA_Magnitude_and_Phase_to_Real_and_Imaginary_NxM.gif']

|  | magnitude spectra specifies the frequency spacings and the magnitudes of the spectra in units of either amplitude or power. The spectrum info in parameter specifies the type of units. You can use the SVFA Unit Conversion VI to change the type of units. x0 specifies the start frequencies or orders of the spectra. dx specifies the frequencies or order resolutions of the spectra. magnitude specifies the magnitudes of the spectra. |
| --- | --- |
|  | x0 specifies the start frequencies or orders of the spectra. |
|  | dx specifies the frequencies or order resolutions of the spectra. |
|  | magnitude specifies the magnitudes of the spectra. |
|  | phase spectra specifies the frequency spacings and the phases of the spectra in units of degree or radian. The spectrum info in parameter specifies the type of units. You can use the SVFA Phase Unit Conversion VI to change the type of units. x0 specifies the start frequencies or orders of the spectra. dx specifies the frequencies or order resolutions of the spectra. phase specifies the phases of the spectra. |
|  | x0 specifies the start frequencies or orders of the spectra. |
|  | dx specifies the frequencies or order resolutions of the spectra. |
|  | phase specifies the phases of the spectra. |
|  | spectrum info in specifies the properties of the previously computed spectrum. Note You must wire spectrum info in when performing extended measurements. Do not modify the spectrum info in values. If you want to view the information, you can create a custom probe. |
|  | error in describes error conditions that occur before this node runs. This input provides standard error in functionality. |
|  | Nyquist plots returns the Nyquist plots of the real and imaginary spectra components. You can display these plots on one or more XY graphs. real part returns the real parts of the spectra. imaginary part returns the imaginary parts of the spectra. |
|  | real part returns the real parts of the spectra. |
|  | imaginary part returns the imaginary parts of the spectra. |
|  | real spectra returns the real parts of the rectangular components. x0 returns the start frequencies or orders of the spectra. dx returns the frequencies or order resolutions of the spectra. real part returns the real parts of the spectra. |
|  | x0 returns the start frequencies or orders of the spectra. |
|  | dx returns the frequencies or order resolutions of the spectra. |
|  | real part returns the real parts of the spectra. |
|  | imaginary spectra returns the imaginary parts of the rectangular components. x0 returns the start frequencies or orders of the spectra. dx returns the frequencies or order resolutions of the spectra. imaginary part returns the imaginary parts of the spectra. |
|  | x0 returns the start frequencies or orders of the spectra. |
|  | dx returns the frequencies or order resolutions of the spectra. |
|  | imaginary part returns the imaginary parts of the spectra. |
|  | unit labels returns the selected engineering units for each channel. |

## TOPIC 00077: SVFA Map Frequency to Order VI

- bundle_id: `labview-sound-and-vibration-toolkit-api-ref`
- source_path: `svfa-map-frequency-to-order-vi.html`
- source_url: https://docs-be.ni.com/bundle/labview-sound-and-vibration-toolkit-api-ref/raw/resource/enus/svfa-map-frequency-to-order-vi.html
- document_id: `labview-sound-and-vibration-toolkit-api-ref`
- page_type: `leaf`
- content_type: `reference`
- source_description: Owning Palette: Common Utilities VIs Requires: Sound and Vibration Toolkit Use this VI to convert a frequency power spectrum to an order power spectrum. Use this VI to convert power spectrum results from constant-speed applications. frequency [Hz] specifies the input frequency power spectrum. speed

### SVFA Map Frequency to Order VI

**Owning Palette:**
 Common Utilities VIs

**Requires:**
 Sound and Vibration Toolkit

Use this VI to convert a frequency power spectrum to an order power spectrum. Use this VI to convert power spectrum results from constant-speed applications.

[IMAGE alt='image' src='images/SVFA_Map_Frequency_to_Order.gif']

|  | frequency [Hz] specifies the input frequency power spectrum. |
| --- | --- |
|  | speed [RPM] specifies the rotational speed, in revolutions per minute, for converting the input frequency power spectrum. |
|  | order returns the converted order power spectrum. |

Parent topic:

Common Utilities VIs

Use this VI to convert power spectrum results from constant-speed applications. order specifies the input order power spectrum. speed [RPM] specifi

### SVFA Map Order to Frequency VI

**Owning Palette:**
 Common Utilities VIs

**Requires:**
 Sound and Vibration Toolkit

Use this VI to convert an order power spectrum to a frequency power spectrum. Use this VI to convert power spectrum results from constant-speed applications.

[IMAGE alt='image' src='images/SVFA_Map_Order_to_Frequency.gif']

|  | order specifies the input order power spectrum. |
| --- | --- |
|  | speed [RPM] specifies the rotational speed, in revolutions per minute, for converting the input frequency power spectrum. |
|  | frequency [Hz] returns the converted frequency power spectrum. |

<!--NI_TOPIC bundle=labview-sound-and-vibration-toolkit-api-ref path=svfa-peak-amplitude-and-relative-phase-vi.html language=enus -->
## TOPIC 00079: SVFA Peak Amplitude and Relative Phase VI

- bundle_id: `labview-sound-and-vibration-toolkit-api-ref`
- source_path: `svfa-peak-amplitude-and-relative-phase-vi.html`
- source_url: https://docs-be.ni.com/bundle/labview-sound-and-vibration-toolkit-api-ref/raw/resource/enus/svfa-peak-amplitude-and-relative-phase-vi.html
- document_id: `labview-sound-and-vibration-toolkit-api-ref`
- page_type: `leaf`
- content_type: `reference`
- source_description: Owning Palette: Baseband FFT VIs Requires: Sound and Vibration Toolkit Use this VI to compute FFT spectra with magnitude equal to response magnitude and phase equal to response relative to reference. Use the N responses, 1 stimulus instance to compute phase-assigned auto spectra for multiple respons

### SVFA Peak Amplitude and Relative Phase VI

**Owning Palette:**
 Baseband FFT VIs

**Requires:**
 Sound and Vibration Toolkit

Use this VI to compute FFT spectra with magnitude equal to response magnitude and phase equal to response relative to reference. Use the N responses, 1 stimulus instance to compute phase-assigned auto spectra for multiple response channels.

**reference signal X**
 and 
 **response signal Y**
 should have the same timestamp (t0), sample interval (dt), and length.

| SVFA Peak Amplitude and Relative Phase (1x1)SVFA Peak Amplitude and Relative Phase (Nx1) |
| --- |

SVFA Peak Amplitude and Relative Phase (1x1)

[IMAGE alt='image' src='images/SVFA_Peak_Amplitude_and_Relative_Phase_1x1.gif']

|  | force and exponential window settings specifies the parameters for the force and exponential windows. These windows are specified in percentages and are used to analyze transients. Note These settings are only used when window is set to Force-Exponential force [%] specifies the length of the force window that is used if window is set to Force-Exponential. The default is 50.00 %. force [%] specifies the duration of the force window as a percentage of the total duration of the signal. Setting force window % at 100 has the effect of not applying any window on the stimulus signal. exponential [%] specifies the decay rate of the exponential window that is used if window is set to Force-Exponential. The default is 10.00 %. exponential [%] specifies the remaining level of the applied exponential window at the end of the signal as a percentage. |
| --- | --- |
|  | force [%] specifies the length of the force window that is used if window is set to Force-Exponential. The default is 50.00 %. force [%] specifies the duration of the force window as a percentage of the total duration of the signal. Setting force window % at 100 has the effect of not applying any window on the stimulus signal. |
|  | exponential [%] specifies the decay rate of the exponential window that is used if window is set to Force-Exponential. The default is 10.00 %. exponential [%] specifies the remaining level of the applied exponential window at the end of the signal as a percentage. |
|  | reference signal X specifies the voltage or scaled signal acquired from the reference channel. This reference is not used to scale the magnitude result; it is used only as a zero-phase reference for phase . t0 specifies the start time of the waveform. dt specifies the time interval in seconds between data points in the waveform. Y specifies the data values of the waveform. |
|  | t0 specifies the start time of the waveform. |
|  | dt specifies the time interval in seconds between data points in the waveform. |
|  | Y specifies the data values of the waveform. |
|  | response signal Y [EUy] specifies the scaled response signal expressed in the selected engineering units. t0 specifies the start time of the waveform. dt specifies the time interval in seconds between data points in the waveform. Y specifies the data values of the waveform. |
|  | t0 specifies the start time of the waveform. |
|  | dt specifies the time interval in seconds between data points in the waveform. |
|  | Y specifies the data values of the waveform. |
|  | window specifies the time-domain window to use. The default is Hanning. Note When you use a Force-Exponential window, wire force/exp settings . 0 None 1 Hanning (default) 2 Hamming 3 Blackman-Harris 4 Exact Blackman 5 Blackman 6 Flat Top 7 4 Term B-Harris 8 7 Term B-Harris 9 Low Sidelobe 10 Force-Exponential 11 Blackman Nuttall 30 Triangle 31 Bartlett-Hanning 32 Bohman 33 Parzen 34 Welch 60 Kaiser 61 Dolph-Chebyshev 62 Gaussian |
| 0 | None |
| 1 | Hanning (default) |
| 2 | Hamming |
| 3 | Blackman-Harris |
| 4 | Exact Blackman |
| 5 | Blackman |
| 6 | Flat Top |
| 7 | 4 Term B-Harris |
| 8 | 7 Term B-Harris |
| 9 | Low Sidelobe |
| 10 | Force-Exponential |
| 11 | Blackman Nuttall |
| 30 | Triangle |
| 31 | Bartlett-Hanning |
| 32 | Bohman |
| 33 | Parzen |
| 34 | Welch |
| 60 | Kaiser |
| 61 | Dolph-Chebyshev |
| 62 | Gaussian |
|  | view specifies how the different results from this VI are returned. view allows you to choose whether magnitude results are displayed in dB, phase results are unwrapped, and phase results are converted from radians to degrees. dB On specifies if results are expressed in decibels. The default is FALSE. unwrap phase specifies whether to unwrap the phase. Unwrapping eliminates discontinuities that have an absolute value greater than pi. The default is FALSE, meaning the phase is not unwrapped. When unwrap phase is TRUE, the phase is unwrapped. convert to degree specifies whether the phase results are converted from radians to degrees. The default is TRUE, which means that results are expressed in degrees. |
|  | dB On specifies if results are expressed in decibels. The default is FALSE. |
|  | unwrap phase specifies whether to unwrap the phase. Unwrapping eliminates discontinuities that have an absolute value greater than pi. The default is FALSE, meaning the phase is not unwrapped. When unwrap phase is TRUE, the phase is unwrapped. |
|  | convert to degree specifies whether the phase results are converted from radians to degrees. The default is TRUE, which means that results are expressed in degrees. |
|  | error in describes error conditions that occur before this node runs. This input provides standard error in functionality. |
|  | averaging parameters specifies how the averaging is computed. The specifications of the parameters include the type of averaging, the type of weighting, and the number of averages. Note When averaging mode is set to No averaging, the VI ignores the weighting mode , number of averages , and linear mode settings. averaging mode specifies the averaging mode. 0 No averaging (default) 1 Vector averaging 2 RMS averaging weighting mode specifies the weighting mode. The default is exponential. Note RMS and vector averaging support linear and exponential weighting. 0 Linear 1 Exponential (default) number of averages specifies the number of averages used by the selected weighting mode . The default is 10. If weighting mode is set to Linear , the measurement stops or automatically restarts after the value of number of averages has been reached. Whether the measurement stops or automatically restarts depends on the linear mode setting. linear mode specifies the behavior of the averaging if you set the value of the weighting mode parameter to Linear . If you set weighting mode to Exponential , linear mode is ignored. 0 One shot (default)Specifies that the averaging process stops once the value of number of averages is reached. 1 Auto restart Specifies that the averaging process automatically restarts after the value of number of averages is reached. |
|  | averaging mode specifies the averaging mode. 0 No averaging (default) 1 Vector averaging 2 RMS averaging |
| 0 | No averaging (default) |
| 1 | Vector averaging |
| 2 | RMS averaging |
|  | weighting mode specifies the weighting mode. The default is exponential. Note RMS and vector averaging support linear and exponential weighting. 0 Linear 1 Exponential (default) |
| 0 | Linear |
| 1 | Exponential (default) |
|  | number of averages specifies the number of averages used by the selected weighting mode . The default is 10. If weighting mode is set to Linear , the measurement stops or automatically restarts after the value of number of averages has been reached. Whether the measurement stops or automatically restarts depends on the linear mode setting. |
|  | linear mode specifies the behavior of the averaging if you set the value of the weighting mode parameter to Linear . If you set weighting mode to Exponential , linear mode is ignored. 0 One shot (default)Specifies that the averaging process stops once the value of number of averages is reached. 1 Auto restart Specifies that the averaging process automatically restarts after the value of number of averages is reached. |
| 0 | One shot (default)Specifies that the averaging process stops once the value of number of averages is reached. |
| 1 | Auto restart Specifies that the averaging process automatically restarts after the value of number of averages is reached. |
|  | restart averaging specifies if the selected averaging process has to be restarted. The default is FALSE. When you call this VI for the first time, the averaging process restarts automatically. |
|  | coherence returns information about the coherence. f0 returns the start frequency of the coherence expressed in hertz. df returns the frequency resolution of the coherence expressed in hertz. coherence returns the coherence. |
|  | f0 returns the start frequency of the coherence expressed in hertz. |
|  | df returns the frequency resolution of the coherence expressed in hertz. |
|  | coherence returns the coherence. |
|  | magnitude [EUy pk] returns the magnitude of the response spectrum. f0 returns the start frequency, in hertz, of the spectrum. df returns the frequency resolution, in hertz, of the spectrum. magnitude is the magnitude of the response spectrum. |
|  | f0 returns the start frequency, in hertz, of the spectrum. |
|  | df returns the frequency resolution, in hertz, of the spectrum. |
|  | magnitude is the magnitude of the response spectrum. |
|  | magnitude unit label returns the unit label for the selected magnitude units. |
|  | phase returns the phase of the response channel relative to the reference channel . This phase is equal to the phase of the frequency response of Y/X and the cross spectrum X*Y. f0 returns the start frequency, in hertz, of the spectrum. df returns the frequency resolution, in hertz, of the spectrum. phase returns the phase of the FFT spectrum. |
|  | f0 returns the start frequency, in hertz, of the spectrum. |
|  | df returns the frequency resolution, in hertz, of the spectrum. |
|  | phase returns the phase of the FFT spectrum. |
|  | phase unit label returns the unit label for the selected phase units. |
|  | spectrum info returns information about the spectrum needed by the Spectrum Extended Measurements VIs, the Display VIs, and the Tone and Distortion VIs. Note You must wire spectrum info when performing extended measurements. Do not modify the spectrum info values. If you want to view the information, you can right-click the spectrum info wire and select Custom Probe»Spectrum Info from the shortcut menu. |
|  | error out contains error information. This output provides standard error out functionality. |
|  | averages completed returns the number of averages completed by the VI at that time. |
|  | averaging done returns TRUE when averages completed is greater than or equal to the number of averages specified in averaging parameters . Otherwise, averaging done returns FALSE. averaging done is always TRUE if the selected averaging mode is No averaging . |

SVFA Peak Amplitude and Relative Phase (Nx1)

[IMAGE alt='image' src='images/SVFA_Peak_Amplitude_and_Relative_Phase_Nx1.gif']

The default is 50.00 %. force [%] specifies the duration of the force window as a percentage of the total duration of the signal. The default is 10.00 %. exponential [%] specifies the remaining level of the applied exponential window at the end of the signal as a percentage. |

<!--NI_TOPIC bundle=labview-sound-and-vibration-toolkit-api-ref path=svfa-phase-unit-conversion-vi.html language=enus -->
## TOPIC 00080: SVFA Phase Unit Conversion VI

- bundle_id: `labview-sound-and-vibration-toolkit-api-ref`
- source_path: `svfa-phase-unit-conversion-vi.html`
- source_url: https://docs-be.ni.com/bundle/labview-sound-and-vibration-toolkit-api-ref/raw/resource/enus/svfa-phase-unit-conversion-vi.html
- document_id: `labview-sound-and-vibration-toolkit-api-ref`
- page_type: `leaf`
- content_type: `reference`
- source_description: Owning Palette: Spectrum Extended Measurements VIs Requires: Sound and Vibration Toolkit Converts phase spectra between degrees, radians, and cycles. Wire data to the phase spectrum input to determine the polymorphic instance to use or manually select the instance. Details SVFA Phase Unit Conversion

### SVFA Phase Unit Conversion VI

**Owning Palette:**
 Spectrum Extended Measurements VIs

**Requires:**
 Sound and Vibration Toolkit

Converts phase spectra between degrees, radians, and cycles. Wire data to the 
 **phase spectrum**
 input to determine the polymorphic instance to use or manually select the instance.

Details

| SVFA Phase Unit Conversion (Spectrum) (1 Ch)SVFA Phase Unit Conversion (Spectrum) (N Ch)SVFA Phase Unit Conversion (Spectrum) (NxM)SVFA Phase Unit Conversion (XY Spectrum) (1 Ch)SVFA Phase Unit Conversion (XY Spectrum) (N Ch)SVFA Phase Unit Conversion (XY Spectrum) (1 Ch, M Orders)SVFA Phase Unit Conversion (XY Spectrum) (N Ch, M Orders) |
| --- |

SVFA Phase Unit Conversion (Spectrum) (1 Ch)

[IMAGE alt='image' src='images/SVFA_Phase_Unit_Conversion_1_Ch.gif']

|  | phase spectrum specifies the frequency spacing and the phase of the spectrum. The units for phase spectrum are specified in spectrum info in . f0 specifies the start frequency, in hertz, of the spectrum. df specifies the frequency resolution, in hertz, of the spectrum. phase specifies the phase of the spectrum. |
| --- | --- |
|  | f0 specifies the start frequency, in hertz, of the spectrum. |
|  | df specifies the frequency resolution, in hertz, of the spectrum. |
|  | phase specifies the phase of the spectrum. |
|  | desired units specifies whether the converted phase is returned in units of radians, degrees, or cycles. 0 No change (default) 1 Radians 2 Degrees 3 Cycles |
| 0 | No change (default) |
| 1 | Radians |
| 2 | Degrees |
| 3 | Cycles |
|  | spectrum info in specifies the properties of the previously computed spectrum. Note You must wire spectrum info in when performing extended measurements. Do not modify the spectrum info in values. If you want to view the information, you can right-click the spectrum info in wire and select Custom Probe»Spectrum Info from the shortcut menu. |
|  | error in describes error conditions that occur before this node runs. This input provides standard error in functionality. |
|  | converted phase spectrum returns the converted phase spectrum scaled according to the desired units . f0 returns the start frequency, in hertz, of the spectrum. df returns the frequency resolution, in hertz, of the spectrum. phase returns the phase of the spectrum. |
|  | f0 returns the start frequency, in hertz, of the spectrum. |
|  | df returns the frequency resolution, in hertz, of the spectrum. |
|  | phase returns the phase of the spectrum. |
|  | unit label returns the engineering units. |
|  | spectrum info out returns the properties of the computed spectrum. Note You must wire spectrum info out when performing extended measurements. Do not modify the spectrum info out values. If you want to view the information, you can right-click the spectrum info out wire and select Custom Probe»Spectrum Info from the shortcut menu. |
|  | error out contains error information. This output provides standard error out functionality. |

SVFA Phase Unit Conversion (Spectrum) (N Ch)

[IMAGE alt='image' src='images/SVFA_Phase_Unit_Conversion_N_Ch.gif']

|  | phase spectra specifies the abscissa scaling and the phase for each spectrum. The units for phase spectra are specified in spectrum info in . x0 specifies the start frequency or order of the spectrum. The default is 0.00. dx specifies the frequency or order resolution of the spectrum. The default is 0.00. phase specifies the phase of the spectrum. |
| --- | --- |
|  | x0 specifies the start frequency or order of the spectrum. The default is 0.00. |
|  | dx specifies the frequency or order resolution of the spectrum. The default is 0.00. |
|  | phase specifies the phase of the spectrum. |
|  | desired units specifies whether the converted phase is returned in units of radians, degrees, or cycles. 0 No change (default) 1 Radians 2 Degrees 3 Cycles |
| 0 | No change (default) |
| 1 | Radians |
| 2 | Degrees |
| 3 | Cycles |
|  | spectrum info in specifies the properties of the previously computed spectrum. Note You must wire spectrum info in when performing extended measurements. Do not modify the spectrum info in values. If you want to view the information, you can create a custom probe. |
|  | error in describes error conditions that occur before this node runs. This input provides standard error in functionality. |
|  | converted phase spectra returns the converted phase spectra scaled according to the desired units . x0 returns the start frequency or order of the spectrum. dx returns the frequency or order resolution of the spectrum. phase returns the phase of the spectrum. |
|  | x0 returns the start frequency or order of the spectrum. |
|  | dx returns the frequency or order resolution of the spectrum. |
|  | phase returns the phase of the spectrum. |
|  | unit labels returns the selected engineering units for each channel. |
|  | spectrum info out returns the properties of the computed spectrum. Note You must wire spectrum info out when performing extended measurements. Do not modify the spectrum info out values. If you want to view the information, you can create a custom probe. |
|  | error out contains error information. This output provides standard error out functionality. |

SVFA Phase Unit Conversion (Spectrum) (NxM)

[IMAGE alt='image' src='images/SVFA_Phase_Unit_Conversion_NxM.gif']

|  | phase spectra specifies the abscissa scaling and the phase for each spectrum. The units for phase spectra are specified in spectrum info in . x0 specifies the start frequency or order of the spectrum. The default is 0.00. dx specifies the frequency or order resolution of the spectrum. The default is 0.00. phase specifies the phase of the spectrum. |
| --- | --- |
|  | x0 specifies the start frequency or order of the spectrum. The default is 0.00. |
|  | dx specifies the frequency or order resolution of the spectrum. The default is 0.00. |
|  | phase specifies the phase of the spectrum. |
|  | desired units specifies whether the converted phase is returned in units of radians, degrees, or cycles. 0 No change (default) 1 Radians 2 Degrees 3 Cycles |
| 0 | No change (default) |
| 1 | Radians |
| 2 | Degrees |
| 3 | Cycles |
|  | spectrum info in specifies the properties of the previously computed spectrum. Note You must wire spectrum info in when performing extended measurements. Do not modify the spectrum info in values. If you want to view the information, you can create a custom probe. |
|  | error in describes error conditions that occur before this node runs. This input provides standard error in functionality. |
|  | converted phase spectra returns the converted phase spectra scaled according to the desired units . x0 returns the start frequency or order of the spectrum. dx returns the frequency or order resolution of the spectrum. phase returns the phase of the spectrum. |
|  | x0 returns the start frequency or order of the spectrum. |
|  | dx returns the frequency or order resolution of the spectrum. |
|  | phase returns the phase of the spectrum. |
|  | unit labels returns the selected engineering units for each channel. |
|  | spectrum info out returns the properties of the computed spectrum. Note You must wire spectrum info out when performing extended measurements. Do not modify the spectrum info out values. If you want to view the information, you can create a custom probe. |
|  | error out contains error information. This output provides standard error out functionality. |

SVFA Phase Unit Conversion Details

The following ratios are used to perform phase unit conversions:

180 deg = pi rad = 0.5 cycle
