Spectrum Extended Measurements VIs

<!--NI_TOPIC bundle=labview-sound-and-vibration-toolkit-api-ref path=svfa-power-in-band-vi.html language=enus -->
## TOPIC 00081: SVFA Power in Band VI

- bundle_id: `labview-sound-and-vibration-toolkit-api-ref`
- source_path: `svfa-power-in-band-vi.html`
- source_url: https://docs-be.ni.com/bundle/labview-sound-and-vibration-toolkit-api-ref/raw/resource/enus/svfa-power-in-band-vi.html
- document_id: `labview-sound-and-vibration-toolkit-api-ref`
- page_type: `leaf`
- content_type: `reference`
- source_description: Owning Palette: Spectrum Extended Measurements VIs Requires: Sound and Vibration Toolkit Computes the aggregate power in the range . The SVFA Power in Band VI operates on Power, PSD, COP, and FFT [Mag] spectra. Wire data to the spectrum input to determine the polymorphic instance to use or manually

### SVFA Power in Band VI

**Owning Palette:**
 Spectrum Extended Measurements VIs

**Requires:**
 Sound and Vibration Toolkit

Computes the aggregate power in the 
 **range**
 . The SVFA Power in Band VI operates on Power, PSD, COP, and FFT [Mag] spectra. Wire data to the 
 **spectrum**
 input to determine the polymorphic instance to use or manually select the instance.

Use the SVPO Power in Band VI to compute the aggregate power on embedded targets that have limited computational resources.

Details Example

| SVFA Power in Band (1 Spectrum)SVFA Power in Band (N Spectra) |
| --- |

SVFA Power in Band (1 Spectrum)

[IMAGE alt='image' src='images/SVFA_Power_in_Band_1_Ch.gif']

|  | spectrum specifies the abscissa scaling and the magnitude of the spectrum. x0 specifies the start frequency or order of the spectrum. The default is 0.00. dx specifies the frequency or order resolution of the spectrum. The default is 0.00. magnitude specifies the magnitude of the spectrum. |
| --- | --- |
|  | x0 specifies the start frequency or order of the spectrum. The default is 0.00. |
|  | dx specifies the frequency or order resolution of the spectrum. The default is 0.00. |
|  | magnitude specifies the magnitude of the spectrum. |
|  | range specifies the band of analysis from the lowest frequency to the highest frequency. start specifies the lowest frequency or order of interest for the analysis band. The valid range is between 0 and the Nyquist frequency or order. Specify a value of –1 to select the lowest frequency or order as the minimum frequency or order of interest. The default is –1.00. stop specifies the highest frequency or order of interest for the analysis band. The valid range is between 0 and the Nyquist frequency or order. Specify a value of –1 to select the highest frequency or order as the maximum frequency or order of interest. The default is –1.00. |
|  | start specifies the lowest frequency or order of interest for the analysis band. The valid range is between 0 and the Nyquist frequency or order. Specify a value of –1 to select the lowest frequency or order as the minimum frequency or order of interest. The default is –1.00. |
|  | stop specifies the highest frequency or order of interest for the analysis band. The valid range is between 0 and the Nyquist frequency or order. Specify a value of –1 to select the highest frequency or order as the maximum frequency or order of interest. The default is –1.00. |
|  | spectrum info specifies properties of the computed spectrum. Note You must wire spectrum info when performing extended measurements. Do not modify the spectrum info values. If you want to view the information, you can right-click the spectrum info wire and select Custom Probe»Spectrum Info from the shortcut menu. |
|  | error in describes error conditions that occur before this node runs. This input provides standard error in functionality. |
|  | power in band returns the aggregate power in the specified range. |
|  | unit label returns the selected engineering units. |
|  | error out contains error information. This output provides standard error out functionality. |

SVFA Power in Band (N Spectra)

The SVFA Power in Band (N Spectra) VI only accepts unscaled 
 **spectra**
 .

[IMAGE alt='image' src='images/SVFA_Power_in_Band_N_Ch.gif']

|  | spectra specifies the abscissa scaling and the magnitude for each spectrum. x0 specifies the start frequency or order of the spectrum. The default is 0.00. dx specifies the frequency or order resolution of the spectrum. The default is 0.00. magnitude specifies the magnitude of the spectrum. |
| --- | --- |
|  | x0 specifies the start frequency or order of the spectrum. The default is 0.00. |
|  | dx specifies the frequency or order resolution of the spectrum. The default is 0.00. |
|  | magnitude specifies the magnitude of the spectrum. |
|  | range specifies the band of analysis from the lowest frequency to the highest frequency. start specifies the lowest frequency or order of interest for the analysis band. The valid range is between 0 and the Nyquist frequency or order. Specify a value of –1 to select the lowest frequency or order as the minimum frequency or order of interest. The default is –1.00. stop specifies the highest frequency or order of interest for the analysis band. The valid range is between 0 and the Nyquist frequency or order. Specify a value of –1 to select the highest frequency or order as the maximum frequency or order of interest. The default is –1.00. |
|  | start specifies the lowest frequency or order of interest for the analysis band. The valid range is between 0 and the Nyquist frequency or order. Specify a value of –1 to select the lowest frequency or order as the minimum frequency or order of interest. The default is –1.00. |
|  | stop specifies the highest frequency or order of interest for the analysis band. The valid range is between 0 and the Nyquist frequency or order. Specify a value of –1 to select the highest frequency or order as the maximum frequency or order of interest. The default is –1.00. |
|  | spectrum info specifies properties of the computed spectrum. Note You must wire spectrum info when performing extended measurements. Do not modify the spectrum info values. If you want to view the information, you can create a custom probe. |
|  | error in describes error conditions that occur before this node runs. This input provides standard error in functionality. |
|  | powers in band returns the aggregate powers in the specified ranges. |
|  | unit labels returns the selected engineering units for each channel. |
|  | error out contains error information. This output provides standard error out functionality. |

SVFA Power in Band Details

**range**
 specifies the band for which the power is computed. 
 **start**
 and 
 **stop**
 are coerced to the nearest spectrum bins.

You also can input spectra expressed in dB to the SVFA Power in Band VI. The spectra are converted to linearly-scaled spectra for the computation, and the results are converted back to logarithmically-scaled values for reporting.

This VI always returns 
 **power in band**
 in EU
 ²
 <sup>rms</sup>
 or dB
 <sup>rms</sup>
 .

 
 

 
 
Example

Refer to the Vibration Analysis (DAQmx) VI in the 
 labview\examples\Sound and Vibration\Vibration Level Measurements
 directory for an example of using the SVFA Power in Band VI.

[IMAGE alt='image' src='images/open.gif']
 Open example 
 
 [IMAGE alt='image' src='images/find.gif']
 Find related examples

Parent topic:

Spectrum Extended Measurements VIs

<!--NI_TOPIC bundle=labview-sound-and-vibration-toolkit-api-ref path=svfa-power-spectral-density-subset-vi.html language=enus -->
## TOPIC 00082: SVFA Power Spectral Density Subset VI

- bundle_id: `labview-sound-and-vibration-toolkit-api-ref`
- source_path: `svfa-power-spectral-density-subset-vi.html`
- source_url: https://docs-be.ni.com/bundle/labview-sound-and-vibration-toolkit-api-ref/raw/resource/enus/svfa-power-spectral-density-subset-vi.html
- document_id: `labview-sound-and-vibration-toolkit-api-ref`
- page_type: `leaf`
- content_type: `reference`
- source_description: Owning Palette: Baseband Subset VIs Requires: Sound and Vibration Toolkit Computes a subset of the power spectral density of scaled signal [EU] . The subset is defined by frequency range . Each block of data corresponds to a single FFT block and has to be passed individually to this VI. Wire data to

### SVFA Power Spectral Density Subset VI

**Owning Palette:**
 Baseband Subset VIs

**Requires:**
 Sound and Vibration Toolkit

Computes a subset of the power spectral density of 
 **scaled signal [EU]**
 . The subset is defined by 
 **frequency range**
 . Each block of data corresponds to a single FFT block and has to be passed individually to this VI. Wire data to the 
 **scaled signal [EU]**
 input to determine the polymorphic instance to use or manually select the instance.

| SVFA Power Spectral Density Subset (1 Ch)SVFA Power Spectral Density Subset (N Ch) |
| --- |

SVFA Power Spectral Density Subset (1 Ch)

[IMAGE alt='image' src='images/SVFA_Power_Spectral_Density_Subset_1_Ch.gif']

|  | desired units specifies if the results are expressed in EU^2/Hz or EU/sqrt(Hz). 0 EU^2 / Hz (default) 1 EU / sqrt(Hz) |
| --- | --- |
| 0 | EU^2 / Hz (default) |
| 1 | EU / sqrt(Hz) |
|  | scaled signal [EU] specifies the scaled signal expressed in the selected engineering units. To obtain a properly scaled signal, you can use the SVL Scale Voltage to EU VI or define a virtual channel in National Instruments Measurement & Automation Explorer (MAX). t0 specifies the start time of the waveform. dt specifies the time interval in seconds between data points in the waveform. Y specifies the data values of the waveform. |
|  | t0 specifies the start time of the waveform. |
|  | dt specifies the time interval in seconds between data points in the waveform. |
|  | Y specifies the data values of the waveform. |
|  | window specifies the time-domain window used. The default is Hanning. 0 None 1 Hanning (default) 2 Hamming 3 Blackman-Harris 4 Exact Blackman 5 Blackman 6 Flat Top 7 4 Term B-Harris 8 7 Term B-Harris 9 Low Sidelobe 11 Blackman Nuttall 30 Triangle 31 Bartlett-Hanning 32 Bohman 33 Parzen 34 Welch 60 Kaiser 61 Dolph-Chebyshev 62 Gaussian |
| 0 | None |
| 1 | Hanning (default) |
| 2 | Hamming |
| 3 | Blackman-Harris |
| 4 | Exact Blackman |
| 5 | Blackman |
| 6 | Flat Top |
| 7 | 4 Term B-Harris |
| 8 | 7 Term B-Harris |
| 9 | Low Sidelobe |
| 11 | Blackman Nuttall |
| 30 | Triangle |
| 31 | Bartlett-Hanning |
| 32 | Bohman |
| 33 | Parzen |
| 34 | Welch |
| 60 | Kaiser |
| 61 | Dolph-Chebyshev |
| 62 | Gaussian |
|  | frequency range specifies the frequency band of analysis. The valid range for the start frequency and for the stop frequency is between 0 and the Nyquist frequency. start frequency specifies the lowest frequency of interest for the analysis band in hertz. The valid range is between 0 and the Nyquist frequency. The default is 1,000.00 Hz. stop frequency specifies the highest frequency of interest for the analysis band in hertz. The valid range is between 0 and the Nyquist frequency. Specify a value of –1 to select the highest frequency as the maximum frequency of interest. The default is 5,000.00 Hz. |
|  | start frequency specifies the lowest frequency of interest for the analysis band in hertz. The valid range is between 0 and the Nyquist frequency. The default is 1,000.00 Hz. |
|  | stop frequency specifies the highest frequency of interest for the analysis band in hertz. The valid range is between 0 and the Nyquist frequency. Specify a value of –1 to select the highest frequency as the maximum frequency of interest. The default is 5,000.00 Hz. |
|  | dB On specifies whether the results are expressed in decibels. The default is FALSE. |
|  | error in describes error conditions that occur before this node runs. This input provides standard error in functionality. |
|  | averaging parameters specifies how the VI computes the averaging. The specifications of the parameters include the type of averaging, the type of weighting, and the number of averages. Note When you set averaging mode to No averaging , the VI ignores the weighting mode , number of averages , and linear mode settings. averaging mode specifies the averaging mode. 0 No averaging (default) 1 Vector averaging 2 RMS averaging 3 Peak hold weighting mode specifies the weighting mode. The default is exponential. Note RMS and vector averaging support linear and exponential weighting. 0 Linear 1 Exponential (default) number of averages specifies the number of averages used by the selected weighting mode . The default is 10. If weighting mode is set to Linear , the measurement stops or automatically restarts after the value of number of averages has been reached. Whether the measurement stops or automatically restarts depends on the linear mode setting. linear mode specifies the behavior of the averaging if you set the value of the weighting mode parameter to Linear . If you set weighting mode to Exponential , linear mode is ignored. 0 One shot (default)Specifies that the averaging process stops once the value of number of averages is reached. 1 Auto restart Specifies that the averaging process automatically restarts after the value of number of averages is reached. |
|  | averaging mode specifies the averaging mode. 0 No averaging (default) 1 Vector averaging 2 RMS averaging 3 Peak hold |
| 0 | No averaging (default) |
| 1 | Vector averaging |
| 2 | RMS averaging |
| 3 | Peak hold |
|  | weighting mode specifies the weighting mode. The default is exponential. Note RMS and vector averaging support linear and exponential weighting. 0 Linear 1 Exponential (default) |
| 0 | Linear |
| 1 | Exponential (default) |
|  | number of averages specifies the number of averages used by the selected weighting mode . The default is 10. If weighting mode is set to Linear , the measurement stops or automatically restarts after the value of number of averages has been reached. Whether the measurement stops or automatically restarts depends on the linear mode setting. |
|  | linear mode specifies the behavior of the averaging if you set the value of the weighting mode parameter to Linear . If you set weighting mode to Exponential , linear mode is ignored. 0 One shot (default)Specifies that the averaging process stops once the value of number of averages is reached. 1 Auto restart Specifies that the averaging process automatically restarts after the value of number of averages is reached. |
| 0 | One shot (default)Specifies that the averaging process stops once the value of number of averages is reached. |
| 1 | Auto restart Specifies that the averaging process automatically restarts after the value of number of averages is reached. |
|  | restart averaging specifies if the selected averaging process has to be restarted. The default is FALSE. When you call this VI for the first time, the averaging process restarts automatically. |
|  | power spectral density [EU^2 rms / Hz] returns the power spectral density. f0 returns the start frequency, in hertz, of the spectrum. df returns the frequency resolution, in hertz, of the spectrum. magnitude returns the magnitude of the power spectral density. |
|  | f0 returns the start frequency, in hertz, of the spectrum. |
|  | df returns the frequency resolution, in hertz, of the spectrum. |
|  | magnitude returns the magnitude of the power spectral density. |
|  | unit label returns the selected engineering units. |
|  | spectrum info returns information about the spectrum needed by the Spectrum Extended Measurements VIs, the Display VIs, and the Tone and Distortion VIs. Note You must wire spectrum info when performing extended measurements. Do not modify the spectrum info values. If you want to view the information, you can right-click the spectrum info wire and select Custom Probe»Spectrum Info from the shortcut menu. |
|  | error out contains error information. This output provides standard error out functionality. |
|  | averages completed returns the number of averages completed by the VI at that time. |
|  | averaging done returns TRUE when averages completed is greater than or equal to the number of averages specified in averaging parameters . Otherwise, averaging done returns FALSE. averaging done is always TRUE if the selected averaging mode is No averaging . |

SVFA Power Spectral Density Subset (N Ch)

[IMAGE alt='image' src='images/SVFA_Power_Spectral_Density_Subset_N_Ch.gif']

|  | desired units specifies if the results are expressed in EU^2/Hz or EU/sqrt(Hz). 0 EU^2 / Hz (default) 1 EU / sqrt(Hz) |
| --- | --- |
| 0 | EU^2 / Hz (default) |
| 1 | EU / sqrt(Hz) |
|  | scaled signals [EU] specifies the scaled signals expressed in the selected engineering units. To obtain a properly scaled signal, you can use the SVL Scale Voltage to EU VI or define a virtual channel in National Instruments Measurement & Automation Explorer (MAX). t0 specifies the start time of the waveform. dt specifies the time interval in seconds between data points in the waveform. Y specifies the data values of the waveform. |

|  | Y specifies the data values of the waveform. |
|  | window specifies the time-domain window used. The default is Hanning. 0 None 1 Hanning (default) 2 Hamming 3 Blackman-Harris 4 Exact Blackman 5 Blackman 6 Flat Top 7 4 Term B-Harris 8 7 Term B-Harris 9 Low Sidelobe 11 Blackman Nuttall 30 Triangle 31 Bartlett-Hanning 32 Bohman 33 Parzen 34 Welch 60 Kaiser 61 Dolph-Chebyshev 62 Gaussian |

| 60 | Kaiser |
| 61 | Dolph-Chebyshev |
| 62 | Gaussian |
|  | frequency range specifies the frequency band of analysis. The valid range for the start frequency and for the stop frequency is between 0 and the Nyquist frequency. start frequency specifies the lowest frequency of interest for the analysis band in hertz. The valid range is between 0 and the Nyquist frequency. The default is 1,000.00 Hz. stop frequency specifies the highest frequency of interest for the analysis band in hertz. The valid range is between 0 and the Nyquist frequency. Specify a value of –1 to select the highest frequency as the maximum frequency of interest. The default is 5,000.00 Hz. |
|  | start frequency specifies the lowest frequency of interest for the analysis band in hertz. The valid range is between 0 and the Nyquist frequency. The default is 1,000.00 Hz. |
|  | stop frequency specifies the highest frequency of interest for the analysis band in hertz. The valid range is between 0 and the Nyquist frequency. Specify a value of –1 to select the highest frequency as the maximum frequency of interest. The default is 5,000.00 Hz. |
|  | dB On specifies whether the results are expressed in decibels. |
|  | error in describes error conditions that occur before this node runs. This input provides standard error in functionality. |
|  | averaging parameters specifies how the VI computes the averaging. The specifications of the parameters include the type of averaging, the type of weighting, and the number of averages. Note When you set averaging mode to No averaging , the VI ignores the weighting mode , number of averages , and linear mode settings. averaging mode specifies the averaging mode. 0 No averaging (default) 1 Vector averaging 2 RMS averaging 3 Peak hold weighting mode specifies the weighting mode. The default is exponential. Note RMS and vector averaging support linear and exponential weighting. 0 Linear 1 Exponential (default) number of averages specifies the number of averages used by the selected weighting mode . The default is 10. If weighting mode is set to Linear , the measurement stops or automatically restarts after the value of number of averages has been reached. Whether the measurement stops or automatically restarts depends on the linear mode setting. linear mode specifies the behavior of the averaging if you set the value of the weighting mode parameter to Linear . If you set weighting mode to Exponential , linear mode is ignored. 0 One shot (default)Specifies that the averaging process stops once the value of number of averages is reached. 1 Auto restart Specifies that the averaging process automatically restarts after the value of number of averages is reached. |
|  | averaging mode specifies the averaging mode. 0 No averaging (default) 1 Vector averaging 2 RMS averaging 3 Peak hold |
| 0 | No averaging (default) |
| 1 | Vector averaging |
| 2 | RMS averaging |
| 3 | Peak hold |
|  | weighting mode specifies the weighting mode. The default is exponential. Note RMS and vector averaging support linear and exponential weighting. 0 Linear 1 Exponential (default) |
| 0 | Linear |
| 1 | Exponential (default) |
|  | number of averages specifies the number of averages used by the selected weighting mode . The default is 10. If weighting mode is set to Linear , the measurement stops or automatically restarts after the value of number of averages has been reached. Whether the measurement stops or automatically restarts depends on the linear mode setting. |
|  | linear mode specifies the behavior of the averaging if you set the value of the weighting mode parameter to Linear . If you set weighting mode to Exponential , linear mode is ignored. 0 One shot (default)Specifies that the averaging process stops once the value of number of averages is reached. 1 Auto restart Specifies that the averaging process automatically restarts after the value of number of averages is reached. |
| 0 | One shot (default)Specifies that the averaging process stops once the value of number of averages is reached. |
| 1 | Auto restart Specifies that the averaging process automatically restarts after the value of number of averages is reached. |

|  | error out contains error information. This output provides standard error out functionality. |
|  | averages completed returns the number of averages completed by the VI at that time. |
|  | averaging done returns TRUE when averages completed is greater than or equal to the number of averages specified in averaging parameters . Otherwise, averaging done returns FALSE. averaging done is always TRUE if the selected averaging mode is No averaging . |

Parent topic:

Baseband Subset VIs

<!--NI_TOPIC bundle=labview-sound-and-vibration-toolkit-api-ref path=svfa-power-spectral-density-vi.html language=enus -->
## TOPIC 00083: SVFA Power Spectral Density VI

- bundle_id: `labview-sound-and-vibration-toolkit-api-ref`
- source_path: `svfa-power-spectral-density-vi.html`
- source_url: https://docs-be.ni.com/bundle/labview-sound-and-vibration-toolkit-api-ref/raw/resource/enus/svfa-power-spectral-density-vi.html
- document_id: `labview-sound-and-vibration-toolkit-api-ref`
- page_type: `leaf`
- content_type: `reference`
- source_description: Owning Palette: Baseband FFT VIs Requires: Sound and Vibration Toolkit Computes the power spectral density of scaled signal [EU] and returns the result in the selected units. Use the power spectral density to make repeatable measurements of the noise floor that are independent of the block size. Wir

### SVFA Power Spectral Density VI

**Owning Palette:**
 Baseband FFT VIs

**Requires:**
 Sound and Vibration Toolkit

Computes the power spectral density of 
 **scaled signal [EU]**
 and returns the result in the selected units. Use the power spectral density to make repeatable measurements of the noise floor that are independent of the block size. Wire data to the 
 **scaled signal [EU]**
 input to determine the polymorphic instance to use or manually select the instance.

Details

| SVFA Power Spectral Density (1 Ch)SVFA Power Spectral Density (N Ch) |
| --- |

SVFA Power Spectral Density (1 Ch)

[IMAGE alt='image' src='images/SVFA_Power_Spectral_Density_1_Ch.gif']

|  | desired units specifies if the results are expressed in EU^2/Hz or EU/sqrt(Hz). 0 EU^2 / Hz (default) 1 EU / sqrt(Hz) |
| --- | --- |
| 0 | EU^2 / Hz (default) |
| 1 | EU / sqrt(Hz) |
|  | scaled signal [EU] specifies the scaled signal expressed in the selected engineering units. To obtain a properly scaled signal, you can use the SVL Scale Voltage to EU VI or define a virtual channel in National Instruments Measurement & Automation Explorer (MAX). t0 specifies the start time of the waveform. dt specifies the time interval in seconds between data points in the waveform. Y specifies the data values of the waveform. |
|  | t0 specifies the start time of the waveform. |
|  | dt specifies the time interval in seconds between data points in the waveform. |
|  | Y specifies the data values of the waveform. |
|  | window specifies the time-domain window used. The default is Hanning. 0 None 1 Hanning (default) 2 Hamming 3 Blackman-Harris 4 Exact Blackman 5 Blackman 6 Flat Top 7 4 Term B-Harris 8 7 Term B-Harris 9 Low Sidelobe 11 Blackman Nuttall 30 Triangle 31 Bartlett-Hanning 32 Bohman 33 Parzen 34 Welch 60 Kaiser 61 Dolph-Chebyshev 62 Gaussian |
| 0 | None |
| 1 | Hanning (default) |
| 2 | Hamming |
| 3 | Blackman-Harris |
| 4 | Exact Blackman |
| 5 | Blackman |
| 6 | Flat Top |
| 7 | 4 Term B-Harris |
| 8 | 7 Term B-Harris |
| 9 | Low Sidelobe |
| 11 | Blackman Nuttall |
| 30 | Triangle |
| 31 | Bartlett-Hanning |
| 32 | Bohman |
| 33 | Parzen |
| 34 | Welch |
| 60 | Kaiser |
| 61 | Dolph-Chebyshev |
| 62 | Gaussian |
|  | dB On specifies whether the results are expressed in decibels. The default is FALSE. |
|  | error in describes error conditions that occur before this node runs. This input provides standard error in functionality. |
|  | averaging parameters specifies how the VI computes the averaging. The specifications of the parameters include the type of averaging, the type of weighting, and the number of averages. Note When you set averaging mode to No averaging , the VI ignores the weighting mode , number of averages , and linear mode settings. averaging mode specifies the averaging mode. 0 No averaging (default) 1 Vector averaging 2 RMS averaging 3 Peak hold weighting mode specifies the weighting mode. The default is exponential. Note RMS and vector averaging support linear and exponential weighting. 0 Linear 1 Exponential (default) number of averages specifies the number of averages used by the selected weighting mode . The default is 10. If weighting mode is set to Linear , the measurement stops or automatically restarts after the value of number of averages has been reached. Whether the measurement stops or automatically restarts depends on the linear mode setting. linear mode specifies the behavior of the averaging if you set the value of the weighting mode parameter to Linear . If you set weighting mode to Exponential , linear mode is ignored. 0 One shot (default)Specifies that the averaging process stops once the value of number of averages is reached. 1 Auto restart Specifies that the averaging process automatically restarts after the value of number of averages is reached. |
|  | averaging mode specifies the averaging mode. 0 No averaging (default) 1 Vector averaging 2 RMS averaging 3 Peak hold |
| 0 | No averaging (default) |
| 1 | Vector averaging |
| 2 | RMS averaging |
| 3 | Peak hold |
|  | weighting mode specifies the weighting mode. The default is exponential. Note RMS and vector averaging support linear and exponential weighting. 0 Linear 1 Exponential (default) |
| 0 | Linear |
| 1 | Exponential (default) |
|  | number of averages specifies the number of averages used by the selected weighting mode . The default is 10. If weighting mode is set to Linear , the measurement stops or automatically restarts after the value of number of averages has been reached. Whether the measurement stops or automatically restarts depends on the linear mode setting. |
|  | linear mode specifies the behavior of the averaging if you set the value of the weighting mode parameter to Linear . If you set weighting mode to Exponential , linear mode is ignored. 0 One shot (default)Specifies that the averaging process stops once the value of number of averages is reached. 1 Auto restart Specifies that the averaging process automatically restarts after the value of number of averages is reached. |
| 0 | One shot (default)Specifies that the averaging process stops once the value of number of averages is reached. |
| 1 | Auto restart Specifies that the averaging process automatically restarts after the value of number of averages is reached. |
|  | restart averaging specifies if the selected averaging process has to be restarted. The default is FALSE. When you call this VI for the first time, the averaging process restarts automatically. |
|  | power spectral density [EU^2 rms / Hz] returns the power spectral density. f0 returns the start frequency, in hertz, of the spectrum. df returns the frequency resolution, in hertz, of the spectrum. magnitude returns the magnitude of the power spectral density. |
|  | f0 returns the start frequency, in hertz, of the spectrum. |
|  | df returns the frequency resolution, in hertz, of the spectrum. |
|  | magnitude returns the magnitude of the power spectral density. |
|  | unit label returns the selected engineering units. |
|  | spectrum info returns information about the spectrum needed by the Spectrum Extended Measurements VIs, the Display VIs, and the Tone and Distortion VIs. Note You must wire spectrum info when performing extended measurements. Do not modify the spectrum info values. If you want to view the information, you can right-click the spectrum info wire and select Custom Probe»Spectrum Info from the shortcut menu. |
|  | error out contains error information. This output provides standard error out functionality. |
|  | averages completed returns the number of averages completed by the VI at that time. |
|  | averaging done returns TRUE when averages completed is greater than or equal to the number of averages specified in averaging parameters . Otherwise, averaging done returns FALSE. averaging done is always TRUE if the selected averaging mode is No averaging . |

SVFA Power Spectral Density (N Ch)

[IMAGE alt='image' src='images/SVFA_Power_Spectral_Density_N_Ch.gif']

|  | desired units specifies if the results are expressed in EU^2/Hz or EU/sqrt(Hz). 0 EU^2 / Hz (default) 1 EU / sqrt(Hz) |
| --- | --- |
| 0 | EU^2 / Hz (default) |
| 1 | EU / sqrt(Hz) |
|  | scaled signals [EU] specifies the scaled signals expressed in the selected engineering units. To obtain a properly scaled signal, you can use the SVL Scale Voltage to EU VI or define virtual channels in National Instruments Measurement & Automation Explorer (MAX). t0 specifies the start time of the waveform. dt specifies the time interval in seconds between data points in the waveform. Y specifies the data values of the waveform. |

|  | dB On specifies whether the results are expressed in decibels. The default is FALSE. |
|  | error in describes error conditions that occur before this node runs. This input provides standard error in functionality. |
|  | averaging parameters specifies how the VI computes the averaging. The specifications of the parameters include the type of averaging, the type of weighting, and the number of averages. Note When you set averaging mode to No averaging , the VI ignores the weighting mode , number of averages , and linear mode settings. averaging mode specifies the averaging mode. 0 No averaging (default) 1 Vector averaging 2 RMS averaging 3 Peak hold weighting mode specifies the weighting mode. The default is exponential. Note RMS and vector averaging support linear and exponential weighting. 0 Linear 1 Exponential (default) number of averages specifies the number of averages used by the selected weighting mode . The default is 10. If weighting mode is set to Linear , the measurement stops or automatically restarts after the value of number of averages has been reached. Whether the measurement stops or automatically restarts depends on the linear mode setting. linear mode specifies the behavior of the averaging if you set the value of the weighting mode parameter to Linear . If you set weighting mode to Exponential , linear mode is ignored. 0 One shot (default)Specifies that the averaging process stops once the value of number of averages is reached. 1 Auto restart Specifies that the averaging process automatically restarts after the value of number of averages is reached. |
|  | averaging mode specifies the averaging mode. 0 No averaging (default) 1 Vector averaging 2 RMS averaging 3 Peak hold |
| 0 | No averaging (default) |
| 1 | Vector averaging |
| 2 | RMS averaging |
| 3 | Peak hold |

| 1 | Exponential (default) |
|  | number of averages specifies the number of averages used by the selected weighting mode . The default is 10. If weighting mode is set to Linear , the measurement stops or automatically restarts after the value of number of averages has been reached. Whether the measurement stops or automatically restarts depends on the linear mode setting. |
|  | linear mode specifies the behavior of the averaging if you set the value of the weighting mode parameter to Linear . If you set weighting mode to Exponential , linear mode is ignored. 0 One shot (default)Specifies that the averaging process stops once the value of number of averages is reached. 1 Auto restart Specifies that the averaging process automatically restarts after the value of number of averages is reached. |
| 0 | One shot (default)Specifies that the averaging process stops once the value of number of averages is reached. |
| 1 | Auto restart Specifies that the averaging process automatically restarts after the value of number of averages is reached. |
|  | restart averaging specifies if the selected averaging process has to be restarted. The default is FALSE. When you call this VI for the first time, the averaging process restarts automatically. |
|  | power spectral densities [EU^2 rms / Hz] returns the power spectral density for each channel. f0 returns the start frequency, in hertz, of the spectrum. df returns the frequency resolution, in hertz, of the spectrum. magnitude returns the magnitude of the power spectral density. |
|  | f0 returns the start frequency, in hertz, of the spectrum. |
|  | df returns the frequency resolution, in hertz, of the spectrum. |
|  | magnitude returns the magnitude of the power spectral density. |
|  | unit labels returns the selected engineering units for each channel. |
|  | spectrum info is an array of clusters that contain information about the spectrum needed by the Spectrum Extended Measurements VIs and the Display VIs. Note You must wire spectrum info when performing extended measurements. Do not modify the spectrum info values. If you want to view the information, you can create a custom probe. |
|  | error out contains error information. This output provides standard error out functionality. |
|  | averages completed returns the number of averages completed by the VI at that time. |
|  | averaging done returns TRUE when averages completed is greater than or equal to the number of averages specified in averaging parameters . Otherwise, averaging done returns FALSE. averaging done is always TRUE if the selected averaging mode is No averaging . |

SVFA Power Spectral Density Details

Each block of data corresponds to a single FFT block and has to be passed individually to the SVFA Power Spectral Density VI.

Parent topic:

Baseband FFT VIs

## TOPIC 00084: SVFA Power Spectrum Subset VI

- bundle_id: `labview-sound-and-vibration-toolkit-api-ref`
- source_path: `svfa-power-spectrum-subset-vi.html`
- source_url: https://docs-be.ni.com/bundle/labview-sound-and-vibration-toolkit-api-ref/raw/resource/enus/svfa-power-spectrum-subset-vi.html
- document_id: `labview-sound-and-vibration-toolkit-api-ref`
- page_type: `leaf`
- content_type: `reference`
- source_description: Owning Palette: Baseband Subset VIs Requires: Sound and Vibration Toolkit Computes a subset of the power spectrum of scaled signal [EU] . The subset is defined by frequency range . Each block of data corresponds to a single FFT block and has to be passed individually to this VI. Wire data to the sca

### SVFA Power Spectrum Subset VI

**Owning Palette:**
 Baseband Subset VIs

**Requires:**
 Sound and Vibration Toolkit

Computes a subset of the power spectrum of 
 **scaled signal [EU]**
 . The subset is defined by 
 **frequency range**
 . Each block of data corresponds to a single FFT block and has to be passed individually to this VI. Wire data to the 
 **scaled signal [EU]**
 input to determine the polymorphic instance to use or manually select the instance.

Example

| SVFA Power Spectrum Subset (1 Ch)SVFA Power Spectrum Subset (N Ch) |
| --- |

SVFA Power Spectrum Subset (1 Ch)

[IMAGE alt='image' src='images/SVFA_Power_Spectrum_Subset_1_Ch.gif']

|  | scaled signal [EU] specifies the scaled signal expressed in the selected engineering units. To obtain a properly scaled signal, you can use the SVL Scale Voltage to EU VI or define a virtual channel in National Instruments Measurement & Automation Explorer (MAX). t0 specifies the start time of the waveform. dt specifies the time interval in seconds between data points in the waveform. Y specifies the data values of the waveform. |
| --- | --- |
|  | t0 specifies the start time of the waveform. |
|  | dt specifies the time interval in seconds between data points in the waveform. |
|  | Y specifies the data values of the waveform. |
|  | window specifies the time-domain window used. The default is Hanning. 0 None 1 Hanning (default) 2 Hamming 3 Blackman-Harris 4 Exact Blackman 5 Blackman 6 Flat Top 7 4 Term B-Harris 8 7 Term B-Harris 9 Low Sidelobe 11 Blackman Nuttall 30 Triangle 31 Bartlett-Hanning 32 Bohman 33 Parzen 34 Welch 60 Kaiser 61 Dolph-Chebyshev 62 Gaussian |
| 0 | None |
| 1 | Hanning (default) |
| 2 | Hamming |
| 3 | Blackman-Harris |
| 4 | Exact Blackman |
| 5 | Blackman |
| 6 | Flat Top |
| 7 | 4 Term B-Harris |
| 8 | 7 Term B-Harris |
| 9 | Low Sidelobe |
| 11 | Blackman Nuttall |
| 30 | Triangle |
| 31 | Bartlett-Hanning |
| 32 | Bohman |
| 33 | Parzen |
| 34 | Welch |
| 60 | Kaiser |
| 61 | Dolph-Chebyshev |
| 62 | Gaussian |
|  | frequency range specifies the frequency band of analysis. The valid range for the start frequency and for the stop frequency is between 0 and the Nyquist frequency. start frequency specifies the lowest frequency of interest for the analysis band in hertz. The valid range is between 0 and the Nyquist frequency. The default is 1,000.00 Hz. stop frequency specifies the highest frequency of interest for the analysis band in hertz. The valid range is between 0 and the Nyquist frequency. Specify a value of –1 to select the highest frequency as the maximum frequency of interest. The default is 5,000.00 Hz. |
|  | start frequency specifies the lowest frequency of interest for the analysis band in hertz. The valid range is between 0 and the Nyquist frequency. The default is 1,000.00 Hz. |
|  | stop frequency specifies the highest frequency of interest for the analysis band in hertz. The valid range is between 0 and the Nyquist frequency. Specify a value of –1 to select the highest frequency as the maximum frequency of interest. The default is 5,000.00 Hz. |
|  | dB On specifies if results are expressed in decibels. The default is TRUE. The dB reference is specified when the scaling from voltage to engineering units is applied. You can use the SVFA Unit Conversion VI to change the dB reference for spectra. |
|  | error in describes error conditions that occur before this node runs. This input provides standard error in functionality. |
|  | averaging parameters specifies how the VI computes the averaging. The specifications of the parameters include the type of averaging, the type of weighting, and the number of averages. Note When you set averaging mode to No averaging , the VI ignores the weighting mode , number of averages , and linear mode settings. averaging mode specifies the averaging mode. 0 No averaging (default) 1 Vector averaging 2 RMS averaging 3 Peak hold weighting mode specifies the weighting mode. The default is exponential. Note RMS and vector averaging support linear and exponential weighting. 0 Linear 1 Exponential (default) number of averages specifies the number of averages used by the selected weighting mode . The default is 10. If weighting mode is set to Linear , the measurement stops or automatically restarts after the value of number of averages has been reached. Whether the measurement stops or automatically restarts depends on the linear mode setting. linear mode specifies the behavior of the averaging if you set the value of the weighting mode parameter to Linear . If you set weighting mode to Exponential , linear mode is ignored. 0 One shot (default)Specifies that the averaging process stops once the value of number of averages is reached. 1 Auto restart Specifies that the averaging process automatically restarts after the value of number of averages is reached. |
|  | averaging mode specifies the averaging mode. 0 No averaging (default) 1 Vector averaging 2 RMS averaging 3 Peak hold |
| 0 | No averaging (default) |
| 1 | Vector averaging |
| 2 | RMS averaging |
| 3 | Peak hold |
|  | weighting mode specifies the weighting mode. The default is exponential. Note RMS and vector averaging support linear and exponential weighting. 0 Linear 1 Exponential (default) |
| 0 | Linear |
| 1 | Exponential (default) |
|  | number of averages specifies the number of averages used by the selected weighting mode . The default is 10. If weighting mode is set to Linear , the measurement stops or automatically restarts after the value of number of averages has been reached. Whether the measurement stops or automatically restarts depends on the linear mode setting. |
|  | linear mode specifies the behavior of the averaging if you set the value of the weighting mode parameter to Linear . If you set weighting mode to Exponential , linear mode is ignored. 0 One shot (default)Specifies that the averaging process stops once the value of number of averages is reached. 1 Auto restart Specifies that the averaging process automatically restarts after the value of number of averages is reached. |
| 0 | One shot (default)Specifies that the averaging process stops once the value of number of averages is reached. |
| 1 | Auto restart Specifies that the averaging process automatically restarts after the value of number of averages is reached. |
|  | restart averaging specifies if the selected averaging process has to be restarted. The default is FALSE. When you call this VI for the first time, the averaging process restarts automatically. |
|  | power spectrum [dB EU] returns the power spectrum. f0 returns the start frequency, in hertz, of the spectrum. df returns the frequency resolution, in hertz, of the spectrum. magnitude returns the magnitude of the power spectrum. |
|  | f0 returns the start frequency, in hertz, of the spectrum. |
|  | df returns the frequency resolution, in hertz, of the spectrum. |
|  | magnitude returns the magnitude of the power spectrum. |
|  | unit label returns the selected engineering units. |
|  | spectrum info returns information about the spectrum needed by the Spectrum Extended Measurements VIs, the Display VIs, and the Tone and Distortion VIs. Note You must wire spectrum info when performing extended measurements. Do not modify the spectrum info values. If you want to view the information, you can right-click the spectrum info wire and select Custom Probe»Spectrum Info from the shortcut menu. |
|  | error out contains error information. This output provides standard error out functionality. |
|  | averages completed returns the number of averages completed by the VI at that time. |
|  | averaging done returns TRUE when averages completed is greater than or equal to the number of averages specified in averaging parameters . Otherwise, averaging done returns FALSE. averaging done is always TRUE if the selected averaging mode is No averaging . |

[IMAGE alt='image' src='images/SVFA_Power_Spectrum_Subset_N_Ch.gif']

 labview\examples\Sound and Vibration\Frequency Analysis
 directory for an example of using the SVFA Power Spectrum Subset VI.

<!--NI_TOPIC bundle=labview-sound-and-vibration-toolkit-api-ref path=svfa-power-spectrum-vi.html language=enus -->
## TOPIC 00085: SVFA Power Spectrum VI

- bundle_id: `labview-sound-and-vibration-toolkit-api-ref`
- source_path: `svfa-power-spectrum-vi.html`
- source_url: https://docs-be.ni.com/bundle/labview-sound-and-vibration-toolkit-api-ref/raw/resource/enus/svfa-power-spectrum-vi.html
- document_id: `labview-sound-and-vibration-toolkit-api-ref`
- page_type: `leaf`
- content_type: `reference`
- source_description: Owning Palette: Baseband FFT VIs Requires: Sound and Vibration Toolkit Computes the power spectrum of scaled signal [EU] . Use the power spectrum to quantify signal components as a function of frequency. Wire data to the scaled signal [EU] input to determine the polymorphic instance to use or manual

### SVFA Power Spectrum VI

**Owning Palette:**
 Baseband FFT VIs

**Requires:**
 Sound and Vibration Toolkit

Computes the power spectrum of 
 **scaled signal [EU]**
 . Use the power spectrum to quantify signal components as a function of frequency. Wire data to the 
 **scaled signal [EU]**
 input to determine the polymorphic instance to use or manually select the instance.

Use the SVPO Power Spectrum VI to compute the power spectrum of scaled signals on embedded targets that have limited computational resources.

Details Example

| SVFA Power Spectrum (1 Ch)SVFA Power Spectrum (N Ch) |
| --- |

SVFA Power Spectrum (1 Ch)

[IMAGE alt='image' src='images/SVFA_Power_Spectrum_1_Ch.gif']

|  | scaled signal [EU] specifies the scaled signal expressed in the selected engineering units. To obtain a properly scaled signal, you can use the SVL Scale Voltage to EU VI or define a virtual channel in National Instruments Measurement & Automation Explorer (MAX). t0 specifies the start time of the waveform. dt specifies the time interval in seconds between data points in the waveform. Y specifies the data values of the waveform. |
| --- | --- |
|  | t0 specifies the start time of the waveform. |
|  | dt specifies the time interval in seconds between data points in the waveform. |
|  | Y specifies the data values of the waveform. |
|  | window specifies the time-domain window used. The default is Hanning. 0 None 1 Hanning (default) 2 Hamming 3 Blackman-Harris 4 Exact Blackman 5 Blackman 6 Flat Top 7 4 Term B-Harris 8 7 Term B-Harris 9 Low Sidelobe 11 Blackman Nuttall 30 Triangle 31 Bartlett-Hanning 32 Bohman 33 Parzen 34 Welch 60 Kaiser 61 Dolph-Chebyshev 62 Gaussian |
| 0 | None |
| 1 | Hanning (default) |
| 2 | Hamming |
| 3 | Blackman-Harris |
| 4 | Exact Blackman |
| 5 | Blackman |
| 6 | Flat Top |
| 7 | 4 Term B-Harris |
| 8 | 7 Term B-Harris |
| 9 | Low Sidelobe |
| 11 | Blackman Nuttall |
| 30 | Triangle |
| 31 | Bartlett-Hanning |
| 32 | Bohman |
| 33 | Parzen |
| 34 | Welch |
| 60 | Kaiser |
| 61 | Dolph-Chebyshev |
| 62 | Gaussian |
|  | dB On specifies if results are expressed in decibels. The default is TRUE. The dB reference is specified when the scaling from voltage to engineering units is applied. You can use the SVFA Unit Conversion VI to change the dB reference for spectra. |
|  | error in describes error conditions that occur before this node runs. This input provides standard error in functionality. |
|  | averaging parameters specifies how the VI computes the averaging. The specifications of the parameters include the type of averaging, the type of weighting, and the number of averages. Note When you set averaging mode to No averaging , the VI ignores the weighting mode , number of averages , and linear mode settings. averaging mode specifies the averaging mode. 0 No averaging (default) 1 Vector averaging 2 RMS averaging 3 Peak hold weighting mode specifies the weighting mode. The default is exponential. Note RMS and vector averaging support linear and exponential weighting. 0 Linear 1 Exponential (default) number of averages specifies the number of averages used by the selected weighting mode . The default is 10. If weighting mode is set to Linear , the measurement stops or automatically restarts after the value of number of averages has been reached. Whether the measurement stops or automatically restarts depends on the linear mode setting. linear mode specifies the behavior of the averaging if you set the value of the weighting mode parameter to Linear . If you set weighting mode to Exponential , linear mode is ignored. 0 One shot (default)Specifies that the averaging process stops once the value of number of averages is reached. 1 Auto restart Specifies that the averaging process automatically restarts after the value of number of averages is reached. |
|  | averaging mode specifies the averaging mode. 0 No averaging (default) 1 Vector averaging 2 RMS averaging 3 Peak hold |
| 0 | No averaging (default) |
| 1 | Vector averaging |
| 2 | RMS averaging |
| 3 | Peak hold |
|  | weighting mode specifies the weighting mode. The default is exponential. Note RMS and vector averaging support linear and exponential weighting. 0 Linear 1 Exponential (default) |
| 0 | Linear |
| 1 | Exponential (default) |
|  | number of averages specifies the number of averages used by the selected weighting mode . The default is 10. If weighting mode is set to Linear , the measurement stops or automatically restarts after the value of number of averages has been reached. Whether the measurement stops or automatically restarts depends on the linear mode setting. |
|  | linear mode specifies the behavior of the averaging if you set the value of the weighting mode parameter to Linear . If you set weighting mode to Exponential , linear mode is ignored. 0 One shot (default)Specifies that the averaging process stops once the value of number of averages is reached. 1 Auto restart Specifies that the averaging process automatically restarts after the value of number of averages is reached. |
| 0 | One shot (default)Specifies that the averaging process stops once the value of number of averages is reached. |
| 1 | Auto restart Specifies that the averaging process automatically restarts after the value of number of averages is reached. |
|  | restart averaging specifies if the selected averaging process has to be restarted. The default is FALSE. When you call this VI for the first time, the averaging process restarts automatically. |
|  | power spectrum [dB EU] returns the power spectrum. f0 returns the start frequency, in hertz, of the spectrum. df returns the frequency resolution, in hertz, of the spectrum. magnitude returns the magnitude of the power spectrum. |
|  | f0 returns the start frequency, in hertz, of the spectrum. |
|  | df returns the frequency resolution, in hertz, of the spectrum. |
|  | magnitude returns the magnitude of the power spectrum. |
|  | unit label returns the selected engineering units. |
|  | spectrum info returns information about the spectrum needed by the Spectrum Extended Measurements VIs, the Display VIs, and the Tone and Distortion VIs. Note You must wire spectrum info when performing extended measurements. Do not modify the spectrum info values. If you want to view the information, you can right-click the spectrum info wire and select Custom Probe»Spectrum Info from the shortcut menu. |
|  | error out contains error information. This output provides standard error out functionality. |
|  | averages completed returns the number of averages completed by the VI at that time. |
|  | averaging done returns TRUE when averages completed is greater than or equal to the number of averages specified in averaging parameters . Otherwise, averaging done returns FALSE. averaging done is always TRUE if the selected averaging mode is No averaging . |

SVFA Power Spectrum (N Ch)

[IMAGE alt='image' src='images/SVFA_Power_Spectrum_N_Ch.gif']

|  | scaled signals [EU] specifies the scaled signals expressed in the selected engineering units. To obtain a properly scaled signal, you can use the SVL Scale Voltage to EU VI or define virtual channels in National Instruments Measurement & Automation Explorer (MAX). t0 specifies the start time of the waveform. dt specifies the time interval in seconds between data points in the waveform. Y specifies the data values of the waveform. |
| --- | --- |
|  | t0 specifies the start time of the waveform. |
|  | dt specifies the time interval in seconds between data points in the waveform. |
|  | Y specifies the data values of the waveform. |
|  | window specifies the time-domain window used. The default is Hanning. 0 None 1 Hanning (default) 2 Hamming 3 Blackman-Harris 4 Exact Blackman 5 Blackman 6 Flat Top 7 4 Term B-Harris 8 7 Term B-Harris 9 Low Sidelobe 11 Blackman Nuttall 30 Triangle 31 Bartlett-Hanning 32 Bohman 33 Parzen 34 Welch 60 Kaiser 61 Dolph-Chebyshev 62 Gaussian |

|  | dB On specifies if results are expressed in decibels. The default is TRUE. The dB reference is specified when the scaling from voltage to engineering units is applied. You can use the SVFA Unit Conversion VI to change the dB reference for spectra. |
|  | error in describes error conditions that occur before this node runs. This input provides standard error in functionality. |
|  | averaging parameters specifies how the VI computes the averaging. The specifications of the parameters include the type of averaging, the type of weighting, and the number of averages. Note When you set averaging mode to No averaging , the VI ignores the weighting mode , number of averages , and linear mode settings. averaging mode specifies the averaging mode. 0 No averaging (default) 1 Vector averaging 2 RMS averaging 3 Peak hold weighting mode specifies the weighting mode. The default is exponential. Note RMS and vector averaging support linear and exponential weighting. 0 Linear 1 Exponential (default) number of averages specifies the number of averages used by the selected weighting mode . The default is 10. If weighting mode is set to Linear , the measurement stops or automatically restarts after the value of number of averages has been reached. Whether the measurement stops or automatically restarts depends on the linear mode setting. linear mode specifies the behavior of the averaging if you set the value of the weighting mode parameter to Linear . If you set weighting mode to Exponential , linear mode is ignored. 0 One shot (default)Specifies that the averaging process stops once the value of number of averages is reached. 1 Auto restart Specifies that the averaging process automatically restarts after the value of number of averages is reached. |
|  | averaging mode specifies the averaging mode. 0 No averaging (default) 1 Vector averaging 2 RMS averaging 3 Peak hold |
| 0 | No averaging (default) |
| 1 | Vector averaging |
| 2 | RMS averaging |
| 3 | Peak hold |
|  | weighting mode specifies the weighting mode. The default is exponential. Note RMS and vector averaging support linear and exponential weighting. 0 Linear 1 Exponential (default) |
| 0 | Linear |
| 1 | Exponential (default) |
|  | number of averages specifies the number of averages used by the selected weighting mode . The default is 10. If weighting mode is set to Linear , the measurement stops or automatically restarts after the value of number of averages has been reached. Whether the measurement stops or automatically restarts depends on the linear mode setting. |
|  | linear mode specifies the behavior of the averaging if you set the value of the weighting mode parameter to Linear . If you set weighting mode to Exponential , linear mode is ignored. 0 One shot (default)Specifies that the averaging process stops once the value of number of averages is reached. 1 Auto restart Specifies that the averaging process automatically restarts after the value of number of averages is reached. |
| 0 | One shot (default)Specifies that the averaging process stops once the value of number of averages is reached. |
| 1 | Auto restart Specifies that the averaging process automatically restarts after the value of number of averages is reached. |
|  | restart averaging specifies if the selected averaging process has to be restarted. The default is FALSE. When you call this VI for the first time, the averaging process restarts automatically. |
|  | power spectra [dB EU] returns the power spectrum for each channel. f0 returns the start frequency, in hertz, of the spectrum. df returns the frequency resolution, in hertz, of the spectrum. magnitude returns the magnitude of the power spectrum. |
|  | f0 returns the start frequency, in hertz, of the spectrum. |
|  | df returns the frequency resolution, in hertz, of the spectrum. |
|  | magnitude returns the magnitude of the power spectrum. |
|  | unit labels returns the selected engineering units for each channel. |
|  | spectrum info is an array of clusters that contain information about the spectrum needed by the Spectrum Extended Measurements VIs and the Display VIs. Note You must wire spectrum info when performing extended measurements. Do not modify the spectrum info values. If you want to view the information, you can create a custom probe. |
|  | error out contains error information. This output provides standard error out functionality. |
|  | averages completed returns the number of averages completed by the VI at that time. |
|  | averaging done returns TRUE when averages completed is greater than or equal to the number of averages specified in averaging parameters . Otherwise, averaging done returns FALSE. averaging done is always TRUE if the selected averaging mode is No averaging . |

SVFA Power Spectrum Details

Each block of data corresponds to a single FFT block and has to be passed individually to the SVFA Power Spectrum VI.

 
 

 
 
Example

Refer to the Multifunction FFT (Simulated) VI in the 
 labview\examples\Sound and Vibration\Frequency Analysis
 directory for an example of using the SVFA Power Spectrum VI.

[IMAGE alt='image' src='images/open.gif']
 Open example 
 
 [IMAGE alt='image' src='images/find.gif']
 Find related examples

Parent topic:

Baseband FFT VIs

<!--NI_TOPIC bundle=labview-sound-and-vibration-toolkit-api-ref path=svfa-real-and-imaginary-to-magnitude-and-phas.html language=enus -->
## TOPIC 00086: SVFA Real and Imaginary to Magnitude and Phase VI

- bundle_id: `labview-sound-and-vibration-toolkit-api-ref`
- source_path: `svfa-real-and-imaginary-to-magnitude-and-phas.html`
- source_url: https://docs-be.ni.com/bundle/labview-sound-and-vibration-toolkit-api-ref/raw/resource/enus/svfa-real-and-imaginary-to-magnitude-and-phas.html
- document_id: `labview-sound-and-vibration-toolkit-api-ref`
- page_type: `leaf`
- content_type: `reference`
- source_description: Owning Palette: Spectrum Extended Measurements VIs Requires: Sound and Vibration Toolkit Converts spectra from rectangular components to polar components. You can display magnitude and phase information on graphs and send this information to other Spectrum Extended Measurements VIs. Wire data to the

### SVFA Real and Imaginary to Magnitude and Phase VI

**Owning Palette:**
 Spectrum Extended Measurements VIs

**Requires:**
 Sound and Vibration Toolkit

Converts spectra from rectangular components to polar components. You can display magnitude and phase information on graphs and send this information to other Spectrum Extended Measurements VIs. Wire data to the 
 **real spectrum**
 and 
 **imaginary spectrum**
 inputs to determine the polymorphic instance to use or manually select the instance.

You can use this VI to convert one spectrum or N spectra.

Use the SVFA Magnitude and Phase to Real and Imaginary VI to convert polar components to rectangular components.

| SVFA Real and Imaginary to Magnitude and Phase (1 Ch)SVFA Real and Imaginary to Magnitude and Phase (N Ch)SVFA Real and Imaginary to Magnitude and Phase (NxM) |
| --- |

SVFA Real and Imaginary to Magnitude and Phase (1 Ch)

[IMAGE alt='image' src='images/SVFA_Real_and_Imaginary_to_Magnitude_and_Phase_1_Ch.gif']

|  | view specifies how this VI returns the converted spectrum or spectra. dB On specifies whether the results are expressed in decibels. The default is FALSE. unwrap phase specifies whether this VI unwraps the phase. Phase unwrapping eliminates discontinuities with an absolute value greater than pi. If you set unwrap phase to TRUE, this VI unwraps the phase. The default is FALSE, which means this VI wraps the phase between -pi and pi. convert to degree specifies whether this VI expresses phase results in degrees or radians. If you set convert to degree to FALSE, this VI expresses phase results in radians. The default is TRUE, which means this VI expresses phase results in degrees. |
| --- | --- |
|  | dB On specifies whether the results are expressed in decibels. The default is FALSE. |
|  | unwrap phase specifies whether this VI unwraps the phase. Phase unwrapping eliminates discontinuities with an absolute value greater than pi. If you set unwrap phase to TRUE, this VI unwraps the phase. The default is FALSE, which means this VI wraps the phase between -pi and pi. |
|  | convert to degree specifies whether this VI expresses phase results in degrees or radians. If you set convert to degree to FALSE, this VI expresses phase results in radians. The default is TRUE, which means this VI expresses phase results in degrees. |
|  | real spectrum specifies the real part of the rectangular components. x0 specifies the start frequency or order of the spectrum. The default is 0.00. dx specifies the frequency or order resolution of the spectrum. The default is 0.00. real part specifies the real part of the spectrum. |
|  | x0 specifies the start frequency or order of the spectrum. The default is 0.00. |
|  | dx specifies the frequency or order resolution of the spectrum. The default is 0.00. |
|  | real part specifies the real part of the spectrum. |
|  | imaginary spectrum specifies the imaginary parts of the spectrum, including the spectra's start frequencies and order resolutions. x0 specifies the start frequency or order of the spectrum. The default is 0.00. dx specifies the frequency or order resolution of the spectrum. The default is 0.00. imaginary part specifies the imaginary part of the spectrum. |
|  | x0 specifies the start frequency or order of the spectrum. The default is 0.00. |
|  | dx specifies the frequency or order resolution of the spectrum. The default is 0.00. |
|  | imaginary part specifies the imaginary part of the spectrum. |
|  | spectrum info in specifies the properties of the previously computed spectrum. Note You must wire spectrum info in when performing extended measurements. Do not modify the spectrum info in values. If you want to view the information, you can right-click the spectrum info in wire and select Custom Probe»Spectrum Info from the shortcut menu. |
|  | error in describes error conditions that occur before this node runs. This input provides standard error in functionality. |
|  | magnitude spectrum returns the frequency spacing and the magnitude of the spectrum in units of either amplitude or power. The spectrum info out parameter specifies the type of units. You can use the SVFA Unit Conversion VI to change the type of units. x0 returns the start frequency or order of the spectrum. dx returns the frequency or order resolution of the spectrum. magnitude returns the magnitude of the spectrum. |
|  | x0 returns the start frequency or order of the spectrum. |
|  | dx returns the frequency or order resolution of the spectrum. |
|  | magnitude returns the magnitude of the spectrum. |
|  | magnitude unit label returns the unit label for the magnitude spectrum. |
|  | phase spectrum returns the frequency spacing and the phase of the spectrum in units of degree or radian. The spectrum info out parameter specifies the type of units. You can use the SVFA Phase Unit Conversion VI to change the type of units. x0 returns the start frequency or order of the spectrum. dx returns the frequency or order resolution of the spectrum. phase returns the phase of the spectrum. |
|  | x0 returns the start frequency or order of the spectrum. |
|  | dx returns the frequency or order resolution of the spectrum. |
|  | phase returns the phase of the spectrum. |
|  | phase unit label returns the unit label for the phase spectrum. |
|  | spectrum info out returns the properties of the computed spectrum. Note You must wire spectrum info out when performing extended measurements. Do not modify the spectrum info out values. If you want to view the information, you can right-click the spectrum info out wire and select Custom Probe»Spectrum Info from the shortcut menu. |
|  | error out contains error information. This output provides standard error out functionality. |

SVFA Real and Imaginary to Magnitude and Phase (N Ch)

[IMAGE alt='image' src='images/SVFA_Real_and_Imaginary_to_Magnitude_and_Phase_N_Ch.gif']

|  | view specifies how this VI returns the converted spectrum or spectra. dB On specifies whether the results are expressed in decibels. The default is FALSE. unwrap phase specifies whether this VI unwraps the phase. Phase unwrapping eliminates discontinuities with an absolute value greater than pi. If you set unwrap phase to TRUE, this VI unwraps the phase. The default is FALSE, which means this VI wraps the phase between -pi and pi. convert to degree specifies whether this VI expresses phase results in degrees or radians. If you set convert to degree to FALSE, this VI expresses phase results in radians. The default is TRUE, which means this VI expresses phase results in degrees. |
| --- | --- |

|  | convert to degree specifies whether this VI expresses phase results in degrees or radians. If you set convert to degree to FALSE, this VI expresses phase results in radians. The default is TRUE, which means this VI expresses phase results in degrees. |

|  | phase spectra returns the frequency spacings and the phases of the spectra in units of degree or radian. The spectrum info out parameter specifies the type of units. You can use the SVFA Phase Unit Conversion VI to change the type of units. x0 returns the start frequencies or orders of the spectra. dx returns the frequencies or order resolutions of the spectra. phase returns the phases of the spectra. |

<!--NI_TOPIC bundle=labview-sound-and-vibration-toolkit-api-ref path=svfa-spectrum-peak-search-vi.html language=enus -->
## TOPIC 00087: SVFA Spectrum Peak Search VI

- bundle_id: `labview-sound-and-vibration-toolkit-api-ref`
- source_path: `svfa-spectrum-peak-search-vi.html`
- source_url: https://docs-be.ni.com/bundle/labview-sound-and-vibration-toolkit-api-ref/raw/resource/enus/svfa-spectrum-peak-search-vi.html
- document_id: `labview-sound-and-vibration-toolkit-api-ref`
- page_type: `leaf`
- content_type: `reference`
- source_description: Owning Palette: Spectrum Extended Measurements VIs Requires: Sound and Vibration Toolkit Performs a peak search on a spectrum or spectra. The spectrum or spectra can be in either the frequency domain or the order domain. Detects single or multiple peaks in Power, PSD, and COP spectra. Wire data to t

### SVFA Spectrum Peak Search VI

**Owning Palette:**
 Spectrum Extended Measurements VIs

**Requires:**
 Sound and Vibration Toolkit

Performs a peak search on a spectrum or spectra. The spectrum or spectra can be in either the frequency domain or the order domain. Detects single or multiple peaks in Power, PSD, and COP spectra. Wire data to the 
 **spectrum**
 input to determine the polymorphic instance to use or manually select the instance.

The time-domain window previously applied to the signal is used to accurately estimate the frequency and amplitude of the peak.

Details Example

| SVFA Spectrum Peak Search (1 Spectrum)SVFA Spectrum Peak Search (N Spectra) |
| --- |

SVFA Spectrum Peak Search (1 Spectrum)

[IMAGE alt='image' src='images/SVFA_Spectrum_Peak_Search_1_Ch.gif']

|  | spectrum specifies the abscissa scaling and the magnitude of the spectrum. x0 specifies the start frequency or order of the spectrum. The default is 0.00. dx specifies the frequency or order resolution of the spectrum. The default is 0.00. magnitude specifies the magnitude of the spectrum. |
| --- | --- |
|  | x0 specifies the start frequency or order of the spectrum. The default is 0.00. |
|  | dx specifies the frequency or order resolution of the spectrum. The default is 0.00. |
|  | magnitude specifies the magnitude of the spectrum. |
|  | peak search settings specifies the criteria for the peak search. single/multiple specifies whether the VI returns a single max peak or multiple peaks (default). 0 Multiple Peaks (default) 1 Single Max Peak threshold specifies the level a peak must exceed to be considered a valid peak. The default is Inf. Set threshold to the same units as spectrum . |
|  | single/multiple specifies whether the VI returns a single max peak or multiple peaks (default). 0 Multiple Peaks (default) 1 Single Max Peak |
| 0 | Multiple Peaks (default) |
| 1 | Single Max Peak |
|  | threshold specifies the level a peak must exceed to be considered a valid peak. The default is Inf. Set threshold to the same units as spectrum . |
|  | spectrum info specifies properties of the computed spectrum. Note You must wire spectrum info when performing extended measurements. Do not modify the spectrum info values. If you want to view the information, you can right-click the spectrum info wire and select Custom Probe»Spectrum Info from the shortcut menu. |
|  | error in describes error conditions that occur before this node runs. This input provides standard error in functionality. |
|  | peaks returns the frequency or order and value for each peak that meets the search criteria. freq. or order returns the frequency or order of the peak. value returns the peak value of the peak in the same units as the spectrum input. |
|  | freq. or order returns the frequency or order of the peak. |
|  | value returns the peak value of the peak in the same units as the spectrum input. |
|  | number of peaks indicates the number of peaks that satisfied the search criteria. |
|  | unit label returns the selected engineering units. |
|  | error out contains error information. This output provides standard error out functionality. |

SVFA Spectrum Peak Search (N Spectra)

[IMAGE alt='image' src='images/SVFA_Spectrum_Peak_Search_N_Ch.gif']

|  | spectra specifies the abscissa scaling and the magnitude for each spectrum. x0 specifies the start frequency or order of the spectrum. The default is 0.00. dx specifies the frequency or order resolution of the spectrum. The default is 0.00. magnitude specifies the magnitude of the spectrum. |
| --- | --- |
|  | x0 specifies the start frequency or order of the spectrum. The default is 0.00. |
|  | dx specifies the frequency or order resolution of the spectrum. The default is 0.00. |
|  | magnitude specifies the magnitude of the spectrum. |
|  | peak search settings specifies the criteria for the peak search. single/multiple specifies whether the VI returns a single max peak or multiple peaks (default). 0 Multiple Peaks (default) 1 Single Max Peak threshold specifies the level a peak must exceed to be considered a valid peak. The default is Inf. Set threshold to the same units as spectrum . |
|  | single/multiple specifies whether the VI returns a single max peak or multiple peaks (default). 0 Multiple Peaks (default) 1 Single Max Peak |
| 0 | Multiple Peaks (default) |
| 1 | Single Max Peak |
|  | threshold specifies the level a peak must exceed to be considered a valid peak. The default is Inf. Set threshold to the same units as spectrum . |
|  | spectrum info specifies properties of the computed spectrum. Note You must wire spectrum info when performing extended measurements. Do not modify the spectrum info values. If you want to view the information, you can create a custom probe. |
|  | error in describes error conditions that occur before this node runs. This input provides standard error in functionality. |
|  | peaks returns the frequency or order and value for each peak that meets the search criteria. freq. or order returns the frequency or order of the peak. value returns the peak value of the peak in the same units as the spectrum input. |
|  | freq. or order returns the frequency or order of the peak. |
|  | value returns the peak value of the peak in the same units as the spectrum input. |
|  | number of peaks indicates the number of peaks that satisfied the search criteria for each spectrum. |
|  | unit labels returns the selected engineering units for each channel. |
|  | error out contains error information. This output provides standard error out functionality. |

SVFA Spectrum Peak Search Details

The SVFA Spectrum Peak Search VI finds all the peaks within the spectrum and performs amplitude/frequency or amplitude/order estimation on each individual peak. The VI operates on magnitude and power spectra.

Usually, the spectrum is computed based on a windowed input signal. The closed form of any cosine window, such as Hanning, Hamming, Blackman-Harris, and so on, is known. The presence of three dominant bins indicates a local maximum on the power spectrum. Therefore, when the SVFA Spectrum Peak Search VI locates three dominant bins, a curve fitting algorithm maps the window shape onto the three bins and estimates the true frequency and amplitude of that particular tone. The following illustration diagrams the concept of the algorithm.

[IMAGE alt='image' src='images/algoritm.gif']
 

 
 

 
 
Example

Refer to the Peak Search (WAV File) VI in the 
 labview\examples\Sound and Vibration\Frequency Analysis\Extended Measurements
 directory for an example of using the SVFA Spectrum Peak Search VI.

[IMAGE alt='image' src='images/open.gif']
 Open example 
 
 [IMAGE alt='image' src='images/find.gif']
 Find related examples

Parent topic:

Spectrum Extended Measurements VIs

Wire data to the spectrum input to determine the polymorphic instance to use or manually sel

### SVFA Unit Conversion VI

**Owning Palette:**
 Spectrum Extended Measurements VIs

**Requires:**
 Sound and Vibration Toolkit

Converts between dB/linear, magnitude/magnitude^2, rms/peak/peak-peak, spectrum/spectral density, and dB ref1/dB ref2. Wire data to the 
 **spectrum**
 input to determine the polymorphic instance to use or manually select the instance.

Details Example

| SVFA Unit Conversion (Real Spectrum) (1 Ch)SVFA Unit Conversion (Real Spectrum) (N Ch)SVFA Unit Conversion (Peaks) (1 Ch)SVFA Unit Conversion (Peaks) (N Ch)SVFA Unit Conversion (Complex Spectrum) (1Ch)SVFA Unit Conversion (Complex Spectrum) (N Ch)SVFA Unit Conversion (XY Spectrum) (1 Ch)SVFA Unit Conversion (XY Spectrum) (N Ch)SVFA Unit Conversion (XY Spectrum) (1 Ch, M Orders)SVFA Unit Conversion (XY Spectrum) (N Ch, M Orders)SVFA Unit Conversion (Waterfall)SVFA Unit Conversion (Colormap) |
| --- |

|  | unwrap phase specifies whether this VI unwraps the phase. Phase unwrapping eliminates discontinuities with an absolute value greater than pi. If you set unwrap phase to TRUE, this VI unwraps the phase. The default is FALSE, which means this VI wraps the phase between -pi and pi. |
|  | convert to degree specifies whether this VI expresses phase results in degrees or radians. If you set convert to degree to FALSE, this VI expresses phase results in radians. The default is TRUE, which means this VI expresses phase results in degrees. |

|  | dx returns the frequency or order resolution of the spectrum. |

The default is 0.00, or no change. |

Y specifies the values of the points on the waterfall graph. |

SVFA Unit Conversion (Colormap)

[IMAGE alt='image' src='images/SVFA_Unit_Conversion_Colormap.gif']

|  | colormap specifies the spectral results of a transient analysis on an intensity graph with a rainbow color scale. spectral map specifies the 2D array of magnitude data for the spectra. X scale specifies the scaling information associated with the first index of the 2D spectral map. offset and multiplier specify scaling values such that the final value equals (deltaX)*X + Xo. offset specifies the offset, or initial value, for scaling data using (deltaX)*X + Xo. If you change the offset, the scale no longer uses 0 as the origin of the plot. multiplier specifies the multiplier, or interval, for scaling data using (deltaX)*X + Xo. Y scale specifies the scaling information associated with the first index of the 2D spectral map. offset and multiplier specify scaling values such that the final value equals (deltaY)*Y + Yo. offset specifies the offset, or initial value, for scaling data using (deltaY)*Y + Yo. If you change the offset, the scale no longer uses 0 as the origin of the plot. multiplier specifies the multiplier, or interval, for scaling data using (deltaY)*Y + Yo. attribute specifies additional information about the spectra. LabVIEW uses this information to display the data properly and to perform extended measurements. |
| --- | --- |

If you change the offset, the scale no longer uses 0 as the origin of the plot. multiplier specifies the multiplier, or interval, for scaling data using (deltaX)*X + Xo. |

The default is 0.00, or no change. |

You can display this cluster Colormap Graph to show the transient results on an intensity graph with a rainbow color scale. spectral map returns the 2D array of magnitude data converted according to the scaling parameters. X scale returns the scaling information associated with the first index of the 2D spectral map. offset returns the x value associated with elements (0, n) of spectral map . multiplier returns the x-axis spacing between consecutive elements in a column. Y scale returns the scaling information associated with the second index of the 2D spectral map. offset returns the y value associated with elements (n, 0) of spectral map . multiplier returns the y-axis spacing between consecutive elements in a row. attribute returns additional information about the spectra. LabVIEW uses this information to display the data properly and to perform extended measurements. |
|  | spectral map returns the 2D array of magnitude data converted according to the scaling parameters. |
|  | X scale returns the scaling information associated with the first index of the 2D spectral map. offset returns the x value associated with elements (0, n) of spectral map . multiplier returns the x-axis spacing between consecutive elements in a column. |

|  | multiplier returns the x-axis spacing between consecutive elements in a column. |
|  | Y scale returns the scaling information associated with the second index of the 2D spectral map. offset returns the y value associated with elements (n, 0) of spectral map . multiplier returns the y-axis spacing between consecutive elements in a row. |

|  | multiplier returns the y-axis spacing between consecutive elements in a row. |
