- source_description: Owning Palette: Sound Level VIs Requires: Sound and Vibration Toolkit Computes the running equivalent continuous sound level (Leq) of scaled signal [EU] . scaled signal [EU] should have a zero DC component. Wire data to the scaled signal [EU] input to determine the polymorphic instance to use or man

### SVL Running Leq Sound Level VI

**Owning Palette:**
 Sound Level VIs

**Requires:**
 Sound and Vibration Toolkit

Computes the running equivalent continuous sound level (Leq) of 
 **scaled signal [EU]**
 . 
 **scaled signal [EU]**
 should have a zero DC component. Wire data to the 
 **scaled signal [EU]**
 input to determine the polymorphic instance to use or manually select the instance.

The Sound Level VIs return respective sound pressure levels in dB rms relative to the reference. By default, a sound pressure level of 20 uPa rms is used as the reference for conversion to dB. In contrast, peak and peak-to-peak sound pressure levels are referenced to 20 uPa pk.

Details

| SVL Running Leq Sound Level (1 Ch)SVL Running Leq Sound Level (N Ch) |
| --- |

SVL Running Leq Sound Level (1 Ch)

[IMAGE alt='image' src='images/SVL_Running_Leq_Sound_Level_1_Ch.gif']

|  | scaled signal [EU] specifies the scaled signal expressed in the selected engineering units. To obtain a properly scaled signal, you can use the SVL Scale Voltage to EU VI or define a virtual channel in National Instruments Measurement & Automation Explorer (MAX). t0 specifies the start time of the waveform. dt specifies the time interval in seconds between data points in the waveform. Y specifies the data values of the waveform. |
| --- | --- |
|  | t0 specifies the start time of the waveform. |
|  | dt specifies the time interval in seconds between data points in the waveform. |
|  | Y specifies the data values of the waveform. |
|  | integration time specifies the duration over which the computation occurs in seconds. The default is 1.00. integration time is rounded to a duration corresponding to an integer multiple of the duration of a single block of time-domain data. The actual value used by this VI is returned in actual integration time . |
|  | error in describes error conditions that occur before this node runs. This input provides standard error in functionality. |
|  | restart averaging specifies if the selected averaging process has to be restarted. The default is FALSE. When you call this VI for the first time, the averaging process restarts automatically. |
|  | running Leq sound level [dB EU] returns the running equivalent continuous sound level (Leq) in decibels. |
|  | unit label returns the selected engineering units. |
|  | actual integration time [s] returns the actual duration over which the computation occurred in seconds. |
|  | error out contains error information. This output provides standard error out functionality. |

SVL Running Leq Sound Level (N Ch)

[IMAGE alt='image' src='images/SVL_Running_Leq_Sound_Level_N_Ch.gif']

|  | scaled signals [EU] specifies the scaled signals expressed in the selected engineering units. To obtain a properly scaled signal, you can use the SVL Scale Voltage to EU VI or define virtual channels in National Instruments Measurement & Automation Explorer (MAX). t0 specifies the start time of the waveform. dt specifies the time interval in seconds between data points in the waveform. Y specifies the data values of the waveform. |
| --- | --- |
|  | t0 specifies the start time of the waveform. |
|  | dt specifies the time interval in seconds between data points in the waveform. |
|  | Y specifies the data values of the waveform. |
|  | integration time specifies the duration over which the computation occurs in seconds. The default is 1.00. integration time is rounded to a duration corresponding to an integer multiple of the duration of a single block of time-domain data. The actual value used by this VI is returned in actual integration time . |
|  | error in describes error conditions that occur before this node runs. This input provides standard error in functionality. |
|  | restart averaging specifies if the selected averaging process has to be restarted. The default is FALSE. When you call this VI for the first time, the averaging process restarts automatically. |
|  | running Leq sound levels [dB EU] returns the running equivalent continuous sound levels (Leq) in decibels. |
|  | unit labels returns the selected engineering units for each channel. |
|  | actual integration time [s] returns the actual duration over which the computation occurred in seconds. |
|  | error out contains error information. This output provides standard error out functionality. |

SVL Running Leq Sound Level Details

The SVL Running Leq Sound Level VI requires a continuous input data stream. An internal mechanism automatically restarts the filters if the input data flow is not a continuous flow.

You can attenuate the DC component with hardware AC-coupling filters and with software high-pass filters. A new running Leq value is returned each time you call this VI. A shift register internal to this VI maintains the result of past runs. The new result is an averaged level that includes the latest block of samples and the level history in the specified integration time.

Parent topic:

Sound Level VIs

## TOPIC 00119: SVL Running RMS Level VI

- bundle_id: `labview-sound-and-vibration-toolkit-api-ref`
- source_path: `svl-running-rms-level-vi.html`
- source_url: https://docs-be.ni.com/bundle/labview-sound-and-vibration-toolkit-api-ref/raw/resource/enus/svl-running-rms-level-vi.html
- document_id: `labview-sound-and-vibration-toolkit-api-ref`
- page_type: `leaf`
- content_type: `reference`
- source_description: Owning Palette: Vibration Level VIs Requires: Sound and Vibration Toolkit Computes the running root-mean-square (RMS) level of scaled signal [EU] or even-angle signal [EU] . scaled signal [EU] should have a zero DC component. You can attenuate the DC component with hardware AC-coupling filters and w

### SVL Running RMS Level VI

**Owning Palette:**
 Vibration Level VIs

**Requires:**
 Sound and Vibration Toolkit

Computes the running root-mean-square (RMS) level of 
 **scaled signal [EU]**
 or 
 **even-angle signal [EU]**
 . 
 **scaled signal [EU]**
 should have a zero DC component. You can attenuate the DC component with hardware AC-coupling filters and with software high-pass filters. 
 **integration time [s]**
 and 
 **integration revolutions**
 specify the duration over which the RMS level is computed. A new running RMS value is returned each time you call this VI. A shift register internal to this VI maintains the result of past runs. The new result is an averaged level that includes the latest block of samples and the level history in the specified integration time. Wire data to the 
 **scaled signal [EU]**
 input to determine the polymorphic instance to use or manually select the instance.

Example

| SVL Running RMS Level (Time Waveform) (1 Ch)SVL Running RMS Level (Time Waveform) (N Ch)SVL Running RMS Level (Even Angle) (1 Ch)SVL Running RMS Level (Even Angle) (N Ch) |
| --- |

SVL Running RMS Level (Time Waveform) (1 Ch)

[IMAGE alt='image' src='images/SVL_Running_RMS_Level_1_Ch.gif']

|  | scaled signal [EU] specifies the scaled signal expressed in the selected engineering units. To obtain a properly scaled signal, you can use the SVL Scale Voltage to EU VI or define a virtual channel in National Instruments Measurement & Automation Explorer (MAX). t0 specifies the start time of the waveform. dt specifies the time interval in seconds between data points in the waveform. Y specifies the data values of the waveform. |
| --- | --- |
|  | t0 specifies the start time of the waveform. |
|  | dt specifies the time interval in seconds between data points in the waveform. |
|  | Y specifies the data values of the waveform. |
|  | integration time specifies the duration over which the computation occurs in seconds. The default is 1.00. integration time is rounded to a duration corresponding to an integer multiple of the duration of a single block of time-domain data. The actual value used by this VI is returned in actual integration time . |
|  | error in describes error conditions that occur before this node runs. This input provides standard error in functionality. |
|  | restart averaging specifies if the selected averaging process has to be restarted. The default is FALSE. When you call this VI for the first time, the averaging process restarts automatically. |
|  | running RMS level returns the running RMS level in engineering units (EU rms). |
|  | unit label returns the selected engineering units. |
|  | actual integration time [s] returns the actual duration over which the computation occurred in seconds. |
|  | error out contains error information. This output provides standard error out functionality. |

SVL Running RMS Level (Time Waveform) (N Ch)

[IMAGE alt='image' src='images/SVL_Running_RMS_Level_N_Ch.gif']

|  | scaled signals [EU] specifies the scaled signals expressed in the selected engineering units. To obtain a properly scaled signal, you can use the SVL Scale Voltage to EU VI or define virtual channels in National Instruments Measurement & Automation Explorer (MAX). t0 specifies the start time of the waveform. dt specifies the time interval in seconds between data points in the waveform. Y specifies the data values of the waveform. |
| --- | --- |
|  | t0 specifies the start time of the waveform. |
|  | dt specifies the time interval in seconds between data points in the waveform. |
|  | Y specifies the data values of the waveform. |
|  | integration time specifies the duration over which the computation occurs in seconds. The default is 1.00. integration time is rounded to a duration corresponding to an integer multiple of the duration of a single block of time-domain data. The actual value used by this VI is returned in actual integration time . |
|  | error in describes error conditions that occur before this node runs. This input provides standard error in functionality. |
|  | restart averaging specifies if the selected averaging process has to be restarted. The default is FALSE. When you call this VI for the first time, the averaging process restarts automatically. |
|  | running RMS levels [EU rms] returns the running RMS levels. |
|  | unit labels returns the selected engineering units for each channel. |
|  | actual integration time [s] returns the actual duration over which the computation occurred in seconds. |
|  | error out contains error information. This output provides standard error out functionality. |

SVL Running RMS Level (Even Angle) (1 Ch)

[IMAGE alt='image' src='images/SVL_Running_RMS_Level_Resampled_1_Ch.gif']

|  | even-angle signal [EU] specifies the signal, in engineering units, resampled at an even-angle interval. r0 specifies the start of the revolution index. dr specifies the revolution interval between two sequential samples. even-angle signal specifies data resampled at even-angle intervals. The samples are synchronous to the rotational speed. |
| --- | --- |
|  | r0 specifies the start of the revolution index. |
|  | dr specifies the revolution interval between two sequential samples. |
|  | even-angle signal specifies data resampled at even-angle intervals. The samples are synchronous to the rotational speed. |
|  | even-angle channel info specifies information about the measurement configuration used to acquire the resampled data. Note You must wire even-angle channel info when performing extended measurements. Do not modify the even-angle channel info values. If you want to view the information, you can create a custom probe. |
|  | integration revolutions specifies the duration, in revolutions, over which the RMS computation occurs. The default is 1.00. |
|  | error in describes error conditions that occur before this node runs. This input provides standard error in functionality. |
|  | restart averaging specifies if the selected averaging process has to be restarted. The default is FALSE. When you call this VI for the first time, the averaging process restarts automatically. |
|  | running RMS level returns the running RMS level in engineering units (EU rms). |
|  | unit label returns the selected engineering units. |
|  | actual integration revolutions returns the actual duration, in revolutions, over which the RMS computation occurred. |
|  | error out contains error information. This output provides standard error out functionality. |

SVL Running RMS Level (Even Angle) (N Ch)

[IMAGE alt='image' src='images/SVL_Running_RMS_Level_Resampled_N_Ch.gif']

|  | even-angle signals [EU] specifies the signals, in engineering units, resampled at even-angle intervals. r0 specifies the start of the revolution index. dr specifies the revolution interval between two sequential samples. even-angle signal specifies data resampled at even-angle intervals. The samples are synchronous to the rotational speed. |
| --- | --- |
|  | r0 specifies the start of the revolution index. |
|  | dr specifies the revolution interval between two sequential samples. |
|  | even-angle signal specifies data resampled at even-angle intervals. The samples are synchronous to the rotational speed. |
|  | even-angle channel info specifies information about the measurement configuration this VI uses to acquire the resampled data. Note You must wire even-angle channel info when performing extended measurements. Do not modify the even-angle channel info values. If you want to view the information, you can create a custom probe. |
|  | integration revolutions specifies the duration, in revolutions, over which the RMS computation occurs. The default is 1.00. |
|  | error in describes error conditions that occur before this node runs. This input provides standard error in functionality. |
|  | restart averaging specifies if the selected averaging process has to be restarted. The default is FALSE. When you call this VI for the first time, the averaging process restarts automatically. |
|  | running RMS levels [EU rms] returns the running RMS levels. |
|  | unit labels returns the selected engineering units for each channel. |
|  | actual integration revolutions returns the actual duration, in revolutions, over which the RMS computation occurred. |
|  | error out contains error information. This output provides standard error out functionality. |

Example

Refer to the Level with Human Vibration Filter (DAQmx) VI in the 
 labview\examples\Sound and Vibration\Vibration Level Measurements
 directory for an example of using the SVL Running RMS Level VI.

[IMAGE alt='image' src='images/open.gif']
 Open example 
 
 [IMAGE alt='image' src='images/find.gif']
 Find related examples

Parent topic:

Vibration Level VIs

## TOPIC 00120: SVL Scale Voltage to EU VI

- bundle_id: `labview-sound-and-vibration-toolkit-api-ref`
- source_path: `svl-scale-voltage-to-eu-vi.html`
- source_url: https://docs-be.ni.com/bundle/labview-sound-and-vibration-toolkit-api-ref/raw/resource/enus/svl-scale-voltage-to-eu-vi.html
- document_id: `labview-sound-and-vibration-toolkit-api-ref`
- page_type: `leaf`
- content_type: `reference`
- source_description: Owning Palette: Scaling & Calibration VIs Requires: Sound and Vibration Toolkit Scales signal [V] from volts to the engineering units (EU) you select. Wire data to the signal [V] input to determine the polymorphic instance to use or manually select the instance. Use the SVPO Scale Voltage to EU VI t

### SVL Scale Voltage to EU VI

**Owning Palette:**
 Scaling & Calibration VIs

**Requires:**
 Sound and Vibration Toolkit

Scales 
 **signal [V]**
 from volts to the engineering units (EU) you select. Wire data to the 
 **signal [V]**
 input to determine the polymorphic instance to use or manually select the instance.

Use the SVPO Scale Voltage to EU VI to scale a single-precision signal from volts to engineering units (EU) on embedded targets that have limited computational resources.

Details Example

| SVL Scale Voltage to EU (1 Ch)SVL Scale Voltage to EU (N Ch, 1 Info)SVL Scale Voltage to EU (N Ch, N Info) |
| --- |

SVL Scale Voltage to EU (1 Ch)

[IMAGE alt='image' src='images/SVL_Scale_Voltage_to_EU_1_Ch.gif']

|  | write waveform attributes? specifies whether to write channel info into the waveform attributes of scaled signal [EU] . The default is TRUE, which means that the channel info attributes will be written to the scaled signal [EU] output waveform every time this VI is executed. You can set write waveform attributes? to FALSE to save time when scaling continuously and using the VIs in the Embedded Monitoring subpalette. |
| --- | --- |
|  | signal [V] specifies the signal to be scaled expressed in volts. If the signal is scaled to a different engineering unit prior to reaching the VI, the VI does not apply any scaling. t0 specifies the start time of the waveform. dt specifies the time interval in seconds between data points in the waveform. Y specifies the data values of the waveform. |
|  | t0 specifies the start time of the waveform. |
|  | dt specifies the time interval in seconds between data points in the waveform. |
|  | Y specifies the data values of the waveform. |
|  | channel info specifies information about the measurement system that you used before the signal reaches the DAQ device. sensor sensitivity [mV/EU] specifies the sensitivity of the sensor in mV/EU. The default is 1000.00. engineering units specifies the engineering units. The default is V. If you are using a microphone, select Pa. If you are using an accelerometer, select g, m/s^2, or in/s^2. If the units you need are not listed, select custom and enter the unit to use in custom label . 0 custom 1 V (default) 2 Pa 3 g 4 m/s^2 5 m/s 6 m 7 in/s^2 8 in/s 9 in 10 mil/s 11 mil 12 mm/s 13 mm 14 micron/s 15 micron 16 rad/s^2 17 rad/s 18 rad 19 strain 20 N 21 A 22 RPM 23 cycle/s^2 24 Hz 25 cycle 26 deg/s^2 27 deg/s 28 deg 30 C 31 pC 32 s dB reference [EU] specifies the reference value to use when you compute the results in decibels and when you scale a signal to engineering units. dB reference [EU] uses the unit that you specify in engineering units . The default is 1. When engineering units is V, use a dB reference [EU] of 1. When engineering units is m, use a dB reference [EU] of 0.7746. When engineering units is Pa, use a dB reference [EU] of 20E–6. custom label specifies the string used for units when engineering units is set to custom. If engineering units is not set to custom, this parameter is ignored. The default is EU. weighting filter specifies if any weighting filter has been applied to the signal. This control does not apply any weighting to the signal. weighting filter must always return linear for calibration measurements. The default is linear . Use weighting filter only if the acquired signal has been weighted by an external device before it is acquired by a DAQ device. If a weighting filter was applied to the signal before reaching the DAQ device, using weighting filter prevents subsequent VIs from applying any additional weighting to the weighted signal. pregain [dB] specifies the pregain applied by an external amplifier expressed in decibels. A value of 0 dB specifies no pregain. The default is 0.00 dB. Use pregain [dB] only if an external device amplifies the signal before a DAQ device acquires the signal. If you apply any gain with the DAQ device itself, the driver software takes this into account and you do not need to enter the information. |
|  | sensor sensitivity [mV/EU] specifies the sensitivity of the sensor in mV/EU. The default is 1000.00. |
|  | engineering units specifies the engineering units. The default is V. If you are using a microphone, select Pa. If you are using an accelerometer, select g, m/s^2, or in/s^2. If the units you need are not listed, select custom and enter the unit to use in custom label . 0 custom 1 V (default) 2 Pa 3 g 4 m/s^2 5 m/s 6 m 7 in/s^2 8 in/s 9 in 10 mil/s 11 mil 12 mm/s 13 mm 14 micron/s 15 micron 16 rad/s^2 17 rad/s 18 rad 19 strain 20 N 21 A 22 RPM 23 cycle/s^2 24 Hz 25 cycle 26 deg/s^2 27 deg/s 28 deg 30 C 31 pC 32 s |
| 0 | custom |
| 1 | V (default) |
| 2 | Pa |
| 3 | g |
| 4 | m/s^2 |
| 5 | m/s |
| 6 | m |
| 7 | in/s^2 |
| 8 | in/s |
| 9 | in |
| 10 | mil/s |
| 11 | mil |
| 12 | mm/s |
| 13 | mm |
| 14 | micron/s |
| 15 | micron |
| 16 | rad/s^2 |
| 17 | rad/s |
| 18 | rad |
| 19 | strain |
| 20 | N |
| 21 | A |
| 22 | RPM |
| 23 | cycle/s^2 |
| 24 | Hz |
| 25 | cycle |
| 26 | deg/s^2 |
| 27 | deg/s |
| 28 | deg |
| 30 | C |
| 31 | pC |
| 32 | s |
|  | dB reference [EU] specifies the reference value to use when you compute the results in decibels and when you scale a signal to engineering units. dB reference [EU] uses the unit that you specify in engineering units . The default is 1. When engineering units is V, use a dB reference [EU] of 1. When engineering units is m, use a dB reference [EU] of 0.7746. When engineering units is Pa, use a dB reference [EU] of 20E–6. |
|  | custom label specifies the string used for units when engineering units is set to custom. If engineering units is not set to custom, this parameter is ignored. The default is EU. |
|  | weighting filter specifies if any weighting filter has been applied to the signal. This control does not apply any weighting to the signal. weighting filter must always return linear for calibration measurements. The default is linear . Use weighting filter only if the acquired signal has been weighted by an external device before it is acquired by a DAQ device. If a weighting filter was applied to the signal before reaching the DAQ device, using weighting filter prevents subsequent VIs from applying any additional weighting to the weighted signal. |
|  | pregain [dB] specifies the pregain applied by an external amplifier expressed in decibels. A value of 0 dB specifies no pregain. The default is 0.00 dB. Use pregain [dB] only if an external device amplifies the signal before a DAQ device acquires the signal. If you apply any gain with the DAQ device itself, the driver software takes this into account and you do not need to enter the information. |
|  | error in describes error conditions that occur before this node runs. This input provides standard error in functionality. |
|  | scaled signal [EU] returns the scaled signal expressed in the selected engineering units . scaled signal [EU] also contains channel info as an attribute of the waveform. t0 specifies the start time of the waveform. dt specifies the time interval in seconds between data points in the waveform. Y specifies the data values of the waveform. |
|  | t0 specifies the start time of the waveform. |
|  | dt specifies the time interval in seconds between data points in the waveform. |
|  | Y specifies the data values of the waveform. |
|  | unit label returns the selected engineering units. |
|  | error out contains error information. This output provides standard error out functionality. |

SVL Scale Voltage to EU (N Ch, 1 Info)

[IMAGE alt='image' src='images/SVL_Scale_Voltage_to_EU_N_Ch_to_1_Info.gif']

|  | write waveform attributes? specifies whether to write channel info into the waveform attributes of scaled signals [EU] . The default is TRUE, which means that the channel info attributes will be written to the scaled signals [EU] output waveform every time this VI is executed. You can set write waveform attributes? to FALSE to save time when scaling continuously and using the VIs in the Embedded Monitoring subpalette. |
| --- | --- |
|  | signals [V] specifies the signals to be scaled expressed in volts. t0 specifies the start time of the waveform. dt specifies the time interval in seconds between data points in the waveform. Y specifies the data values of the waveform. |
|  | t0 specifies the start time of the waveform. |
|  | dt specifies the time interval in seconds between data points in the waveform. |
|  | Y specifies the data values of the waveform. |
|  | channel info specifies information about the measurement system that you used before the signal reaches the DAQ device. sensor sensitivity [mV/EU] specifies the sensitivity of the sensor in mV/EU. The default is 1. When engineering units is V, use a dB reference [EU] of 1. When engineering units is m, use a dB reference [EU] of 0.7746. A value of 0 dB specifies no pregain. The default is 0.00 dB. Use pregain [dB] only if an external device amplifies the signal before a DAQ device acquires the signal. If you apply any gain with the DAQ device itself, the driver software takes this into account and you do not need to enter the information. |

|  | engineering units specifies the engineering units. The default is V. If you are using a microphone, select Pa. If you are using an accelerometer, select g, m/s^2, or in/s^2. If the units you need are not listed, select custom and enter the unit to use in custom label . 0 custom 1 V (default) 2 Pa 3 g 4 m/s^2 5 m/s 6 m 7 in/s^2 8 in/s 9 in 10 mil/s 11 mil 12 mm/s 13 mm 14 micron/s 15 micron 16 rad/s^2 17 rad/s 18 rad 19 strain 20 N 21 A 22 RPM 23 cycle/s^2 24 Hz 25 cycle 26 deg/s^2 27 deg/s 28 deg 30 C 31 pC 32 s |

To obtain a properly scaled signal, you can use the SVL Scale Voltage to EU VI or define a virtual channel in National Instruments Measurement & Automation Explorer (MAX). t0 returns the start time of the waveform. dt returns the time interval, in seconds, between data points in the waveform. Y returns the data values of the waveform. |

[IMAGE alt='image' src='images/SVL_Scale_Voltage_to_EU_N_Ch_to_N_Info.gif']

|  | write waveform attributes? specifies whether to write channel info into the waveform attributes of scaled signals [EU] . The default is TRUE, which means that the channel info attributes will be written to the scaled signals [EU] output waveform every time this VI is executed. You can set write waveform attributes? to FALSE to save time when scaling continuously and using the VIs in the Embedded Monitoring subpalette. |
| --- | --- |
|  | signals [V] specifies the signals to be scaled expressed in volts. t0 specifies the start time of the waveform. dt specifies the time interval in seconds between data points in the waveform. Y specifies the data values of the waveform. |
|  | t0 specifies the start time of the waveform. |
|  | dt specifies the time interval in seconds between data points in the waveform. |
|  | Y specifies the data values of the waveform. |
|  | channel info specifies information about the measurement system used before the signal reaches the DAQ device. sensor sensitivity [mV/EU] specifies the sensitivity of the sensor in mV/EU. The default is 1000.00. engineering units specifies the engineering units. The value is V. If you are using a microphone, select Pa. If you are using an accelerometer, select g, m/s^2, or in/s^2. If the units you need are not listed, select custom and enter the unit to use in custom label . dB reference [EU] specifies the reference value to use when you compute the results in decibels and when you scale a signal to engineering units. dB reference [EU] uses the unit that you specify in engineering units . The default is 1. When engineering units is V, use a dB reference [EU] of 1. When engineering units is m, use a dB reference [EU] of 0.7746. When engineering units is Pa, use a dB reference [EU] of 20E–6. custom label specifies the string used for units when engineering units is set to custom. If engineering units is not set to custom, this parameter is ignored. The default is EU. weighting filter specifies if any weighting filter has been applied to the signal. This control does not apply any weighting to the signal. weighting filter must always return linear for calibration measurements. The default is linear . Use weighting filter only if the acquired signal has been weighted by an external device before it is acquired by a DAQ device. If a weighting filter was applied to the signal before reaching the DAQ device, using weighting filter prevents subsequent VIs from applying any additional weighting to the weighted signal. pregain [dB] specifies the pregain applied by an external amplifier expressed in decibels. A value of 0 dB specifies no pregain. The default is 0.00 dB. Use pregain [dB] only if an external device amplifies the signal before a DAQ device acquires the signal. If you apply any gain with the DAQ device itself, the driver software takes this into account and you do not need to enter the information. |
|  | sensor sensitivity [mV/EU] specifies the sensitivity of the sensor in mV/EU. The default is 1000.00. |
|  | engineering units specifies the engineering units. The value is V. If you are using a microphone, select Pa. If you are using an accelerometer, select g, m/s^2, or in/s^2. If the units you need are not listed, select custom and enter the unit to use in custom label . |
|  | dB reference [EU] specifies the reference value to use when you compute the results in decibels and when you scale a signal to engineering units. dB reference [EU] uses the unit that you specify in engineering units . The default is 1. When engineering units is V, use a dB reference [EU] of 1. When engineering units is m, use a dB reference [EU] of 0.7746. When engineering units is Pa, use a dB reference [EU] of 20E–6. |
|  | custom label specifies the string used for units when engineering units is set to custom. If engineering units is not set to custom, this parameter is ignored. The default is EU. |
|  | weighting filter specifies if any weighting filter has been applied to the signal. This control does not apply any weighting to the signal. weighting filter must always return linear for calibration measurements. The default is linear . Use weighting filter only if the acquired signal has been weighted by an external device before it is acquired by a DAQ device. If a weighting filter was applied to the signal before reaching the DAQ device, using weighting filter prevents subsequent VIs from applying any additional weighting to the weighted signal. |
|  | pregain [dB] specifies the pregain applied by an external amplifier expressed in decibels. A value of 0 dB specifies no pregain. The default is 0.00 dB. Use pregain [dB] only if an external device amplifies the signal before a DAQ device acquires the signal. If you apply any gain with the DAQ device itself, the driver software takes this into account and you do not need to enter the information. |
|  | error in describes error conditions that occur before this node runs. This input provides standard error in functionality. |
|  | scaled signals [EU] returns the scaled signals expressed in the selected engineering units. To obtain a properly scaled signal, you can use the SVL Scale Voltage to EU VI or define a virtual channel in National Instruments Measurement & Automation Explorer (MAX). t0 returns the start time of the waveform. dt returns the time interval, in seconds, between data points in the waveform. Y returns the data values of the waveform. |
|  | t0 returns the start time of the waveform. |
|  | dt returns the time interval, in seconds, between data points in the waveform. |
|  | Y returns the data values of the waveform. |
|  | unit labels returns the selected engineering units for each channel. |
|  | error out contains error information. This output provides standard error out functionality. |

SVL Scale Voltage to EU Details

Use the SVL Scale Voltage to EU (1 Ch_Info N Ch) instance during the configuration phase of a measurement when all the sensors have the same sensitivity and before each channel has been independently calibrated. After the sensitivity of each channel has been determined, use the SVL Scale Voltage to EU (N Ch_Info N Ch) instance to perform the measurement.

channel info

channel info

scaled signal [EU]

Note

channel info

Note

sensor sensitivity

channel info

sensor sensitivity

calibrated sensor sensitivity

Refer to the Transient Analysis (DAQmx) VI in the 
 labview\examples\Sound and Vibration\Transient Analysis
 directory for an example of using the SVL Scale Voltage to EU VI.

 Open example 
 
 [IMAGE alt='image' src='images/find.gif']
 Find related examples

## TOPIC 00121: SVL Set dB Reference VI

- bundle_id: `labview-sound-and-vibration-toolkit-api-ref`

- document_id: `labview-sound-and-vibration-toolkit-api-ref`
- page_type: `leaf`
- content_type: `reference`
- source_description: Owning Palette: Scaling & Calibration VIs Requires: Sound and Vibration Toolkit Sets the specified dB reference [EU] as an attribute in the scaled signal out [EU] waveform. dB reference [EU] is used for all downstream conversions to dB. Wire data to the scaled signal in [EU] input to determine the p

### SVL Set dB Reference VI

**Owning Palette:**
 Scaling & Calibration VIs

**Requires:**
 Sound and Vibration Toolkit

Sets the specified 
 **dB reference [EU]**
 as an attribute in the 
 **scaled signal out [EU]**
 waveform. 
 **dB reference [EU]**
 is used for all downstream conversions to dB. Wire data to the 
 **scaled signal in [EU]**
 input to determine the polymorphic instance to use or manually select the instance.

Details

| SVL Set dB Reference (1 Ch, 1 reference)SVL Set dB Reference (N Ch, 1 reference)SVL Set dB Reference (N Ch, N references) |
| --- |

SVL Set dB Reference (1 Ch, 1 reference)

[IMAGE alt='image' src='images/SVL_Set_dB_Reference_1_Ch.gif']

|  | scaled signal in [EU] specifies the scaled signal expressed in the selected engineering units. To obtain a properly scaled signal, you can use the SVL Scale Voltage to EU VI or define a virtual channel in National Instruments Measurement & Automation Explorer (MAX). t0 specifies the start time of the waveform. dt specifies the time interval in seconds between data points in the waveform. Y specifies the data values of the waveform. |
| --- | --- |
|  | t0 specifies the start time of the waveform. |
|  | dt specifies the time interval in seconds between data points in the waveform. |
|  | Y specifies the data values of the waveform. |
|  | dB reference [EU] specifies the dB reference to add to the waveform attributes. The specified dB reference overwrites an existing reference. |
|  | error in describes error conditions that occur before this node runs. This input provides standard error in functionality. |
|  | scaled signal out [EU] returns the modified scaled signal which has the specified dB reference embedded in the waveform attributes. t0 returns the start time of the waveform. dt returns the time interval, in seconds, between data points in the waveform. Y returns the data values of the waveform. |
|  | t0 returns the start time of the waveform. |
|  | dt returns the time interval, in seconds, between data points in the waveform. |
|  | Y returns the data values of the waveform. |
|  | error out contains error information. This output provides standard error out functionality. |

Y specifies the data values of the waveform. |

Y returns the data values of the waveform. |

SVL Set dB Reference Details

The dB reference, 
 reference
 , can be used to convert a measurement result, 
 value
 , to dB by the following equation:

dB = 20 * log
 <sub>10</sub>
 (
 value
 / 
 reference
 ) = 10 * log
 <sub>10</sub>
 (
 value
 / 
 reference
 )
 ²

After a spectrum has been computed, you can use the SVFA Unit Conversion VI to change the dB reference and convert between linear and dB scales.

## TOPIC 00122: SVPO Analog Tacho Process VI

- bundle_id: `labview-sound-and-vibration-toolkit-api-ref`
- source_path: `svpo-analog-tacho-process-vi.html`
- source_url: https://docs-be.ni.com/bundle/labview-sound-and-vibration-toolkit-api-ref/raw/resource/enus/svpo-analog-tacho-process-vi.html
- document_id: `labview-sound-and-vibration-toolkit-api-ref`
- page_type: `leaf`
- content_type: `reference`
- source_description: Owning Palette: Embedded Monitoring VIs Requires: Sound and Vibration Toolkit Locates tachometer pulse positions in analog tachometer signals using the number of generated pulses per revolution, the pulse width, and the arrival edge of the tachometer pulse. Use this VI on embedded targets that have

### SVPO Analog Tacho Process VI

**Owning Palette:**
 Embedded Monitoring VIs

**Requires:**
 Sound and Vibration Toolkit

Locates tachometer pulse positions in analog tachometer signals using the number of generated pulses per revolution, the pulse width, and the arrival edge of the tachometer pulse. Use this VI on embedded targets that have limited computational resources. Wire data to the 
 **analog tacho signal**
 input to determine the polymorphic instance to use or manually select the instance.

| SVPO Analog Tacho Process (1 Ch DBL Array)SVPO Analog Tacho Process (1 Ch DBL Array) with ICSVPO Analog Tacho Process (1 Ch DBL WDT)SVPO Analog Tacho Process (1 Ch DBL WDT) with IC |
| --- |

SVPO Analog Tacho Process (1 Ch DBL Array)

[IMAGE alt='image' src='images/SVPO_Analog_Tacho_Process_1_Ch_DBL_Array.gif']

|  | reset? specifies the initialization of the internal states. The first time this VI runs, or when reset? is TRUE, the internal states initialize to zero. If reset? is FALSE, this VI sets the internal states to the final states from the previous call to the VI. The default is FALSE. |
| --- | --- |
|  | analog tacho signal specifies the input analog tachometer signal. |
|  | sampling rate [Hz] specifies the sampling rate of the input analog tachometer signal in hertz. |
|  | threshold specifies the trigger level that a tachometer pulse must cross to be considered valid. The default is 0.00. |
|  | pulse/revolution specifies the number of pulses the tachometer generates during each revolution. The default is 1. |
|  | hysteresis specifies a level above or below the threshold level. Use hysteresis to reduce false triggering that noise in an analog tachometer signal might cause. When the slope is Rising , the analog tachometer signal must fall below threshold minus hysteresis before this VI detects the next pulse. When the slope is Falling , the analog tachometer signal must rise above threshold plus hysteresis before this VI detects the next pulse. The default is 0.00. |
|  | pulse width specifies the minimum number of points a valid tachometer pulse must contain. pulse width must be greater than zero. The default is 2. |
|  | slope specifies the type of edge that indicates the arrival of a tachometer pulse. 0 Rising (default)—Specifies that a rising edge indicates the arrival of a tachometer pulse. 1 Falling —Specifies that a falling edge indicates the arrival of a tachometer pulse. |
| 0 | Rising (default)—Specifies that a rising edge indicates the arrival of a tachometer pulse. |
| 1 | Falling —Specifies that a falling edge indicates the arrival of a tachometer pulse. |
|  | speed position info returns the position information for each tachometer signal. positions [s] returns the locations of the samples in seconds. samples/rev returns the number of samples per revolution. |
|  | positions [s] returns the locations of the samples in seconds. |
|  | samples/rev returns the number of samples per revolution. |
|  | current speed [RPM] returns the current rotational speed, in revolutions per minute. |

SVPO Analog Tacho Process (1 Ch DBL Array) with IC

[IMAGE alt='image' src='images/SVPO_Analog_Tacho_Process_1_Ch_DBL_Array_with_IC.gif']

|  | reset? specifies the initialization of the internal states. The first time this VI runs, or when reset? is TRUE, the internal states initialize to zero. If reset? is FALSE, this VI sets the internal states to the final states from the previous call to the VI. The default is FALSE. |
| --- | --- |
|  | initial conditions specifies the initial internal state of this VI. This input should come from the previous output of final conditions from the last call to this VI in order to operate on samples continuously. |
|  | analog tacho signal specifies the input analog tachometer signal. |
|  | sampling rate [Hz] specifies the sampling rate of the input analog tachometer signal in hertz. |
|  | threshold specifies the trigger level that a tachometer pulse must cross to be considered valid. The default is 0.00. |
|  | pulse/revolution specifies the number of pulses the tachometer generates during each revolution. The default is 1. |
|  | hysteresis specifies a level above or below the threshold level. Use hysteresis to reduce false triggering that noise in an analog tachometer signal might cause. When the slope is Rising , the analog tachometer signal must fall below threshold minus hysteresis before this VI detects the next pulse. When the slope is Falling , the analog tachometer signal must rise above threshold plus hysteresis before this VI detects the next pulse. The default is 0.00. |
|  | pulse width specifies the minimum number of points a valid tachometer pulse must contain. pulse width must be greater than zero. The default is 2. |
|  | slope specifies the type of edge that indicates the arrival of a tachometer pulse. 0 Rising (default)—Specifies that a rising edge indicates the arrival of a tachometer pulse. 1 Falling —Specifies that a falling edge indicates the arrival of a tachometer pulse. |
| 0 | Rising (default)—Specifies that a rising edge indicates the arrival of a tachometer pulse. |
| 1 | Falling —Specifies that a falling edge indicates the arrival of a tachometer pulse. |
| final conditions returns the final internal state of this VI. This output should be passed to the initial conditions input on the next call to this VI in order to operate on samples continuously. |  |
|  | speed position info returns the position information for each tachometer signal. positions [s] returns the locations of the samples in seconds. samples/rev returns the number of samples per revolution. |
|  | positions [s] returns the locations of the samples in seconds. |
|  | samples/rev returns the number of samples per revolution. |
|  | current speed [RPM] returns the current rotational speed, in revolutions per minute. |

SVPO Analog Tacho Process (1 Ch DBL WDT)

[IMAGE alt='image' src='images/SVPO_Analog_Tacho_Process_1_Ch_DBL_WDT.gif']

## TOPIC 00123: SVPO Average Dual-Channel Spectrum Measurements VI

- bundle_id: `labview-sound-and-vibration-toolkit-api-ref`
- source_path: `svpo-average-dual-channel-spectrum-measuremen.html`
- source_url: https://docs-be.ni.com/bundle/labview-sound-and-vibration-toolkit-api-ref/raw/resource/enus/svpo-average-dual-channel-spectrum-measuremen.html
- document_id: `labview-sound-and-vibration-toolkit-api-ref`
- page_type: `leaf`
- content_type: `reference`
- source_description: Owning Palette: Embedded Monitoring VIs Requires: Sound and Vibration Toolkit Measures the frequency response of the input signals and the coherence based on the current and previous input signals. This VI returns results such as Magnitude , Phase , Coherence , Real , and Imaginary . SVPO Average Du

### SVPO Average Dual-Channel Spectrum Measurements VI

**Owning Palette:**
 Embedded Monitoring VIs

**Requires:**
 Sound and Vibration Toolkit

Measures the frequency response of the input signals and the coherence based on the current and previous input signals. This VI returns results such as 
 **Magnitude**
 , 
 **Phase**
 , 
 **Coherence**
 , 
 **Real**
 , and 
 **Imaginary**
 .

| SVPO Average Dual-Channel Spectrum Measurements (Complex)SVPO Average Dual-Channel Spectrum Measurements (Complex) with IC |
| --- |

SVPO Average Dual-Channel Spectrum Measurements (Complex)

[IMAGE alt='image' src='images/SVPO_Average_Dual-Channel_Spectrum_Measurements_(Complex).gif']

|  | measurement (Cross) specifies how to handle the frequency response of multiple inputs. |
| --- | --- |
|  | complex spectrum X specifies the complex form of the computed spectrum for the channel. |
|  | complex spectrum Y specifies the complex form of the computed spectrum for the channel. |
|  | averaging mode (RMS) specifies the averaging mode from the following options: RMS averaging (Default) Vector averaging No averaging |
|  | weighting mode (Linear) specifies either Exponential or Linear weighting. Exponential averaging applies more weight to the most recent data, and linear averaging applies equal weighting to all the data. The default is Linear. Select an averaging mode (RMS) to enable this option. |
|  | restart averaging specifies if the selected averaging process has to be restarted. The default is FALSE. When you call this VI for the first time, the averaging process restarts automatically. |
|  | coherence returns information about the coherence. f0 returns the start frequency of the coherence expressed in hertz. df returns the frequency resolution of the coherence expressed in hertz. coherence returns the coherence. |
|  | f0 returns the start frequency of the coherence expressed in hertz. |
|  | df returns the frequency resolution of the coherence expressed in hertz. |
|  | coherence returns the coherence. |
|  | average measurement spectrum out returns the complex form of the computed spectrum. |
|  | averages completed out returns the number of averages completed by the VI at that time. |

SVPO Average Dual-Channel Spectrum Measurements (Complex) with IC

[IMAGE alt='image' src='images/SVPO_Average_Dual-Channel_Spectrum_Measurements_(Complex)_with_IC.gif']

|  | measurement (Cross) specifies how to handle the frequency response of multiple inputs. |
| --- | --- |
|  | complex spectrum X specifies the complex form of the computed spectrum for the channel. |
|  | complex spectrum Y specifies the complex form of the computed spectrum for the channel. |
|  | averaging mode (RMS) specifies the averaging mode from the following options: RMS averaging (Default) Vector averaging No averaging |
|  | weighting mode (Linear) specifies either Exponential or Linear weighting. Exponential averaging applies more weight to the most recent data, and linear averaging applies equal weighting to all the data. The default is Linear. Select an averaging mode (RMS) to enable this option. |
|  | initial conditions specifies the initial internal state of this VI. This input should come from the previous output of final conditions from the last call to this VI in order to operate on samples continuously. |
|  | restart averaging specifies if the selected averaging process has to be restarted. The default is FALSE. When you call this VI for the first time, the averaging process restarts automatically. |
|  | coherence returns information about the coherence. f0 returns the start frequency of the coherence expressed in hertz. df returns the frequency resolution of the coherence expressed in hertz. coherence returns the coherence. |
|  | f0 returns the start frequency of the coherence expressed in hertz. |
|  | df returns the frequency resolution of the coherence expressed in hertz. |
|  | coherence returns the coherence. |
|  | average measurement spectrum out returns the complex form of the computed spectrum. |
| final conditions returns the final internal state of this VI. This output should be passed to the initial conditions input on the next call to this VI in order to operate on samples continuously. |  |
|  | averages completed out returns the number of averages completed by the VI at that time. |

Parent topic:

Embedded Monitoring VIs

<!--NI_TOPIC bundle=labview-sound-and-vibration-toolkit-api-ref path=svpo-average-fft-spectrum-complex-vi.html language=enus -->
## TOPIC 00124: SVPO Average FFT Spectrum (Complex) VI

- bundle_id: `labview-sound-and-vibration-toolkit-api-ref`
- source_path: `svpo-average-fft-spectrum-complex-vi.html`
- source_url: https://docs-be.ni.com/bundle/labview-sound-and-vibration-toolkit-api-ref/raw/resource/enus/svpo-average-fft-spectrum-complex-vi.html
- document_id: `labview-sound-and-vibration-toolkit-api-ref`
- page_type: `leaf`
- content_type: `reference`
- source_description: Owning Palette: Spectrum Extended Measurements VIs Converts a frequency power spectrum to an order power spectrum. Use this VI to convert power spectrum results from constant-speed applications. SVPO RMS Average Power Spectrum (Complex) (1 Ch).viSVPO RMS Average Power Spectrum (Complex) (N Ch)SVPO R

### SVPO Average FFT Spectrum (Complex) VI

**Owning Palette:**
 Spectrum Extended Measurements VIs

Converts a frequency power spectrum to an order power spectrum. Use this VI to convert power spectrum results from constant-speed applications.

| SVPO RMS Average Power Spectrum (Complex) (1 Ch).viSVPO RMS Average Power Spectrum (Complex) (N Ch)SVPO RMS Average Power Spectrum (Complex) with ICSVPO Vector Average Spectrum (Complex) (1 Ch)SVPO Vector Average Spectrum (Complex) (N Ch)SVPO Vector Average Spectrum (Complex) with IC |
| --- |

SVPO RMS Average Power Spectrum (Complex) (1 Ch).vi

[IMAGE alt='image' src='images/SVPO_RMS_Average_Power_Spectrum_(Complex)_(1_Ch).gif']

|  | complex spectrum [EU rms] specifies the complex form of the spectrum. Complex spectra can be converted to magnitude and phase components. complex spectrum response specifies the complex form of the computed spectrum for the response channel. f0 specifies the start frequency, in hertz, of the spectrum. df specifies the frequency resolution, in hertz, of the spectrum. complex spectrum specifies the complex form of the computed spectrum. spectrum info specifies the properties of the previously computed spectrum. Note You must wire spectrum info when performing extended measurements. Do not modify the spectrum info values. If you want to view the information, you can unbundle and right-click the spectrum info wire and select Custom Probe»Spectrum Info from the shortcut menu. |
| --- | --- |
|  | complex spectrum response specifies the complex form of the computed spectrum for the response channel. f0 specifies the start frequency, in hertz, of the spectrum. df specifies the frequency resolution, in hertz, of the spectrum. complex spectrum specifies the complex form of the computed spectrum. |
|  | f0 specifies the start frequency, in hertz, of the spectrum. |
|  | df specifies the frequency resolution, in hertz, of the spectrum. |
|  | complex spectrum specifies the complex form of the computed spectrum. |
|  | spectrum info specifies the properties of the previously computed spectrum. Note You must wire spectrum info when performing extended measurements. Do not modify the spectrum info values. If you want to view the information, you can unbundle and right-click the spectrum info wire and select Custom Probe»Spectrum Info from the shortcut menu. |
|  | weighting mode specifies the weighting mode for averaging. This option is available only when averaging mode is RMS averaging . 0 Exponential (default)Emphasizes more on new spectral data than on old spectral data by adding more weights to the new spectral data. 1 Linear Combines N spectral records with equal weighting. |
| 0 | Exponential (default)Emphasizes more on new spectral data than on old spectral data by adding more weights to the new spectral data. |
| 1 | Linear Combines N spectral records with equal weighting. |
|  | restart averaging specifies if the selected averaging process has to be restarted. The default is FALSE. When you call this VI for the first time, the averaging process restarts automatically. |
|  | average power spectrum [EU^2 rms] returns the calculated average power spectrum. f0 returns the start frequency, in hertz, of the spectrum. df returns the frequency resolution, in hertz, of the spectrum. magnitude returns the magnitude of the spectrum. |
|  | f0 returns the start frequency, in hertz, of the spectrum. |
|  | df returns the frequency resolution, in hertz, of the spectrum. |
|  | magnitude returns the magnitude of the spectrum. |
|  | spectrum info returns information about the spectrum needed by the Spectrum Extended Measurements VIs, the Display VIs, and the Tone and Distortion VIs. Note You must wire spectrum info when performing extended measurements. Do not modify the spectrum info values. If you want to view the information, you can right-click the spectrum info wire and select Custom Probe»Spectrum Info from the shortcut menu. |
|  | averages completed out returns the number of averages completed by the VI at that time. |

<!--NI_TOPIC bundle=labview-sound-and-vibration-toolkit-api-ref path=svpo-convert-to-even-angle-signal-vi.html language=enus -->
## TOPIC 00125: SVPO Convert to Even Angle Signal VI

- bundle_id: `labview-sound-and-vibration-toolkit-api-ref`
- source_path: `svpo-convert-to-even-angle-signal-vi.html`
- source_url: https://docs-be.ni.com/bundle/labview-sound-and-vibration-toolkit-api-ref/raw/resource/enus/svpo-convert-to-even-angle-signal-vi.html
- document_id: `labview-sound-and-vibration-toolkit-api-ref`
- page_type: `leaf`
- content_type: `reference`
- source_description: Owning Palette: Embedded Monitoring VIs Requires: Sound and Vibration Toolkit Converts an even-time-spaced scaled signal to an even-angle-spaced signal by resampling the acquired signal. The output signal is synchronous to the rotational speed with a constant number of samples per revolution. Use th

### SVPO Convert to Even Angle Signal VI

**Owning Palette:**
 Embedded Monitoring VIs

**Requires:**
 Sound and Vibration Toolkit

Converts an even-time-spaced scaled signal to an even-angle-spaced signal by resampling the acquired signal. The output signal is synchronous to the rotational speed with a constant number of samples per revolution. Use this VI on embedded targets that have limited computational resources. Wire data to the 
 **scaled signal [EU]**
 input to determine the polymorphic instance to use or manually select the instance.

| SVPO Convert to Even Angle Signal (1 Ch DBL Array)SVPO Convert to Even Angle Signal (N Ch DBL Array)SVPO Convert to Even Angle Signal (1 Ch DBL Array) with ICSVPO Convert to Even Angle Signal (1 Ch DBL WDT)SVPO Convert to Even Angle Signal (N Ch DBL WDT)SVPO Convert to Even Angle Signal (1 Ch DBL WDT) with IC |
| --- |

SVPO Convert to Even Angle Signal (1 Ch DBL Array)

[IMAGE alt='image' src='images/SVPO_Convert_to_Even_Angle_Signal_1_Ch_DBL_Array.gif']

|  | reset? specifies the initialization of the internal states. The first time this VI runs, or when reset? is TRUE, the internal states initialize to zero. If reset? is FALSE, this VI sets the internal states to the final states from the previous call to the VI. The default is FALSE. If sampling rate or max order change in a continuous measurement, you must manually reset this VI, the SVPO Analog Tacho Process VI, and VIs that use the output of this VI in the application. You also need to manually set reset? to TRUE if the input values of SVPO Analog Tacho Process VI change in a continuous measurement. |
| --- | --- |
|  | scaled signal [EU] specifies the scaled signal expressed in the selected engineering units. You can use the SVPO Scale Voltage to EU VI to obtain a properly scaled signal. |
|  | sampling rate [Hz] specifies the sampling rate of the input signal in hertz. |
|  | speed position info specifies the position information of the input signal. positions [s] specifies the locations of the samples in seconds. samples/rev specifies the number of samples per revolution. |
|  | positions [s] specifies the locations of the samples in seconds. |
|  | samples/rev specifies the number of samples per revolution. |
|  | max order specifies the maximum order to analyze. The default is 16. |
|  | even-angle signal returns data resampled at even-angle intervals. This parameter is synchronous to the rotational speed with constant number of samples per revolution. r0 returns the starting index value for counting revolutions. dr returns the revolution interval between two sequential samples. Y returns the values of even-angle spaced samples in a scaled engineering unit. |
|  | r0 returns the starting index value for counting revolutions. |
|  | dr returns the revolution interval between two sequential samples. |
|  | Y returns the values of even-angle spaced samples in a scaled engineering unit. |
|  | even-angle position info returns the position information of even-angle signal . positions [s] returns the locations of the samples in seconds. samples/rev returns the number of samples per revolution. |
|  | positions [s] returns the locations of the samples in seconds. |
|  | samples/rev returns the number of samples per revolution. |

|  | samples/rev returns the number of samples per revolution. |
| final conditions returns the final internal state of this VI. This output should be passed to the initial conditions input on the next call to this VI in order to operate on samples continuously. |  |

|  | positions [s] returns the locations of the samples in seconds. |
|  | samples/rev returns the number of samples per revolution. |
| final conditions returns the final internal state of this VI. This output should be passed to the initial conditions input on the next call to this VI in order to operate on samples continuously. |  |

Parent topic:

Embedded Monitoring VIs

<!--NI_TOPIC bundle=labview-sound-and-vibration-toolkit-api-ref path=svpo-envelope-detection-vi.html language=enus -->
## TOPIC 00126: SVPO Envelope Detection VI

- bundle_id: `labview-sound-and-vibration-toolkit-api-ref`
- source_path: `svpo-envelope-detection-vi.html`
- source_url: https://docs-be.ni.com/bundle/labview-sound-and-vibration-toolkit-api-ref/raw/resource/enus/svpo-envelope-detection-vi.html
- document_id: `labview-sound-and-vibration-toolkit-api-ref`
- page_type: `leaf`
- content_type: `reference`
- source_description: Owning Palette: Embedded Monitoring VIs Requires: Sound and Vibration Toolkit Extracts the modulating signal, or envelope signal, from an amplitude-modulated signal. You can use the envelope detection technique to identify the mechanical faults that have amplitude-modulating effect on the vibration

### SVPO Envelope Detection VI

**Owning Palette:**
 Embedded Monitoring VIs

**Requires:**
 Sound and Vibration Toolkit

Extracts the modulating signal, or envelope signal, from an amplitude-modulated signal. You can use the envelope detection technique to identify the mechanical faults that have amplitude-modulating effect on the vibration signal of a machine. Use this VI on embedded targets that have limited computational resources. Wire data to the 
 **scaled signal [EU]**
 input to determine the polymorphic instance to use or manually select the instance.

| SVPO Envelope Detection (1 Ch DBL Array)SVPO Envelope Detection (N Ch DBL Array)SVPO Envelope Detection (1 Ch DBL Array) with ICSVPO Envelope Detection (1 Ch DBL WDT)SVPO Envelope Detection (N Ch DBL WDT)SVPO Envelope Detection (1 Ch DBL WDT) with IC |
| --- |

SVPO Envelope Detection (1 Ch DBL Array)

[IMAGE alt='image' src='images/SVPO_Envelope_Detection_1_Ch_DBL_Array.gif']

|  | reset? specifies the initialization of the internal states. The first time this VI runs, or when reset? is TRUE, the internal states initialize to zero. If reset? is FALSE, this VI sets the internal states to the final states from the previous call to the VI. The default is FALSE. You must manually set reset? to TRUE if sampling rate or band specification changes in a continuous measurement. |
| --- | --- |
|  | scaled signal [EU] specifies the scaled signal expressed in the selected engineering units. You can use the SVPO Scale Voltage to EU VI to obtain a properly scaled signal. |
|  | sampling rate [Hz] specifies the sampling rate of the input signal in hertz. |
|  | band specification specifies the frequency band used to calculate the envelope signal of scaled signal [EU] . center frequency [Hz] specifies the center frequency of the frequency band to use to calculate the envelope signal. The default is 4800. span [Hz] specifies the frequency bandwidth to calculate the envelope signal. The value of span [Hz] defines half of the frequency band, which is from center frequency [Hz] to the frequency band upper limit. This VI removes the frequency components outside the frequency range that span [Hz] defines. span [Hz] also determines the sampling rate of the output envelope signal. The default is 2000. |
|  | center frequency [Hz] specifies the center frequency of the frequency band to use to calculate the envelope signal. The default is 4800. |
|  | span [Hz] specifies the frequency bandwidth to calculate the envelope signal. The value of span [Hz] defines half of the frequency band, which is from center frequency [Hz] to the frequency band upper limit. This VI removes the frequency components outside the frequency range that span [Hz] defines. span [Hz] also determines the sampling rate of the output envelope signal. The default is 2000. |
|  | envelope signal [EU] returns the envelope signal of scaled signal [EU] . |
|  | sampling rate out [Hz] returns the sampling rate for envelope signal [EU] . |

SVPO Envelope Detection (N Ch DBL Array)

[IMAGE alt='image' src='images/SVPO_Envelope_Detection_N_Ch_DBL_Array.gif']

<!--NI_TOPIC bundle=labview-sound-and-vibration-toolkit-api-ref path=svpo-fft-spectrum-complex-vi.html language=enus -->
## TOPIC 00127: SVPO FFT Spectrum (Complex) VI

- bundle_id: `labview-sound-and-vibration-toolkit-api-ref`
- source_path: `svpo-fft-spectrum-complex-vi.html`
- source_url: https://docs-be.ni.com/bundle/labview-sound-and-vibration-toolkit-api-ref/raw/resource/enus/svpo-fft-spectrum-complex-vi.html
- document_id: `labview-sound-and-vibration-toolkit-api-ref`
- page_type: `leaf`
- content_type: `reference`
- source_description: Owning Palette: Embedded Monitoring VIs Requires: Sound and Vibration Toolkit Computes the Fast Fourier Transform (FFT) spectrum of scaled signal [EU] . This VI returns FFT results as the complex form of the FFT spectrum. Wire data to the scaled signal [EU] input to determine the polymorphic instanc

### SVPO FFT Spectrum (Complex) VI

**Owning Palette:**
 Embedded Monitoring VIs

**Requires:**
 Sound and Vibration Toolkit

Computes the Fast Fourier Transform (FFT) spectrum of 
 **scaled signal [EU]**
 . This VI returns FFT results as the complex form of the FFT spectrum. Wire data to the 
 **scaled signal [EU]**
 input to determine the polymorphic instance to use or manually select the instance.

This VI is designed to operate on a single block of data, as opposed to being called repeatedly to operate on a continuous flow of data. Use this VI after performing a finite acquisition, or when you need to analyze a single block of data without previously analyzed blocks affecting the result.

The results of this VI are only dependent on the inputs to the VI, and not on previous calls to this VI. This VI does not store state internally, and can be called from a shared clone re-entrant VI.

| SVPO Single Block FFT Spectrum (Complex) (1 Ch DBL Array)SVPO Single Block FFT Spectrum (Complex) (N Ch DBL Array)SVPO Single Block FFT Spectrum (Complex) (N Ch DBL WDT)SVPO Single Block FFT Spectrum (Complex) (1 Ch DBL WDT) |
| --- |

SVPO Single Block FFT Spectrum (Complex) (1 Ch DBL Array)

[IMAGE alt='image' src='images/SVPO_Single_Block_FFT_Spectrum_(Complex)_(1_Ch_DBL_Array).gif']

|  | force and exponential window settings specifies the parameters for the force and exponential windows. These windows are specified in percentages and are used to analyze transients. force window [%] specifies the length of the force window that is used if window is set to Force-Exponential. The default is 50.00%. force window [%] specifies the duration of the force window as a percentage of the total duration of the signal. Setting force window % at 100 has the effect of not applying any window on the stimulus signal. exp window [%] specifies the decay rate of the exponential window that is used if window is set to Force-Exponential. The default is 10.00%. exp window [%] specifies the remaining level of the applied exponential window at the end of the signal as a percentage. |
| --- | --- |
|  | force window [%] specifies the length of the force window that is used if window is set to Force-Exponential. The default is 50.00%. force window [%] specifies the duration of the force window as a percentage of the total duration of the signal. Setting force window % at 100 has the effect of not applying any window on the stimulus signal. |
|  | exp window [%] specifies the decay rate of the exponential window that is used if window is set to Force-Exponential. The default is 10.00%. exp window [%] specifies the remaining level of the applied exponential window at the end of the signal as a percentage. |
|  | scaled signal [EU] specifies the scaled signal expressed in the selected engineering units. You can use the SVPO Scale Voltage to EU VI to obtain a properly scaled signal. |
|  | sampling rate [Hz] specifies the sampling rate of the input signal in hertz. |
|  | window specifies the time-domain window to use. 0 None 1 Hanning (default) 2 Hamming 3 Blackman-Harris 4 Exact Blackman 5 Blackman 6 Flat Top 7 4 Term B-Harris 8 7 Term B-Harris 9 Low Sidelobe 10 Blackman Nuttall 11 Triangle 12 Bartlett-Hanning 13 Bohman 14 Parzen 15 Welch 16 Kaiser 17 Dolph-Chebyshev 18 Gaussian |
| 0 | None |
| 1 | Hanning (default) |
| 2 | Hamming |
| 3 | Blackman-Harris |
| 4 | Exact Blackman |
| 5 | Blackman |
| 6 | Flat Top |
| 7 | 4 Term B-Harris |
| 8 | 7 Term B-Harris |
| 9 | Low Sidelobe |
| 10 | Blackman Nuttall |
| 11 | Triangle |
| 12 | Bartlett-Hanning |
| 13 | Bohman |
| 14 | Parzen |
| 15 | Welch |
| 16 | Kaiser |
| 17 | Dolph-Chebyshev |
| 18 | Gaussian |
|  | complex spectrum returns the complex form of the spectrum. f0 returns the start frequency, in hertz, of the spectrum. df returns the frequency resolution, in hertz, of the spectrum. complex spectrum returns the complex form of the computed spectrum. |
|  | f0 returns the start frequency, in hertz, of the spectrum. |
|  | df returns the frequency resolution, in hertz, of the spectrum. |
|  | complex spectrum returns the complex form of the computed spectrum. |

SVPO Single Block FFT Spectrum (Complex) (N Ch DBL Array)

[IMAGE alt='image' src='images/SVPO_Single_Block_FFT_Spectrum_(Complex)_(N_Ch_DBL_Array).gif']

|  | window parameters is the beta parameter for a Kaiser window; the ratio, s , of the mainlobe to the sidelobe for a Dolph-Chebyshev window; and the standard deviation for a Gaussian window. If the window type is any other window, this VI ignores this input. |
| --- | --- |
|  | scaled signals [EU] returns the scaled signals expressed in the selected engineering units . scaled signals [EU] also contains channel info as an attribute of the waveform. t0 returns the start time of the waveform. dt returns the time interval in seconds between data points in the waveform. Y returns the data values of the waveform. |
|  | t0 returns the start time of the waveform. |
|  | dt returns the time interval in seconds between data points in the waveform. |
|  | Y returns the data values of the waveform. |
|  | sampling rate [Hz] specifies the sampling rate of the input signal in hertz. |
|  | window specifies the time-domain window to use. 0 None 1 Hanning (default) 2 Hamming 3 Blackman-Harris 4 Exact Blackman 5 Blackman 6 Flat Top 7 4 Term B-Harris 8 7 Term B-Harris 9 Low Sidelobe 10 Blackman Nuttall 11 Triangle 12 Bartlett-Hanning 13 Bohman 14 Parzen 15 Welch 16 Kaiser 17 Dolph-Chebyshev 18 Gaussian |
| 0 | None |
| 1 | Hanning (default) |
| 2 | Hamming |
| 3 | Blackman-Harris |
| 4 | Exact Blackman |
| 5 | Blackman |
| 6 | Flat Top |
| 7 | 4 Term B-Harris |
| 8 | 7 Term B-Harris |
| 9 | Low Sidelobe |
| 10 | Blackman Nuttall |
| 11 | Triangle |
| 12 | Bartlett-Hanning |
| 13 | Bohman |
| 14 | Parzen |
| 15 | Welch |
| 16 | Kaiser |
| 17 | Dolph-Chebyshev |
| 18 | Gaussian |
|  | complex spectra specifies the complex forms of the computed spectra. f0 specifies the start frequency, in hertz, of the spectrum. df specifies the frequency resolution, in hertz, of the spectrum. complex array specifies the complex form of the spectrum. spectrum info specifies properties of the computed spectrum. Note You must wire spectrum info when performing extended measurements. Do not modify the spectrum info values. If you want to view the information, you can unbundle and right-click the spectrum info wire and select Custom Probe»Spectrum Info from the shortcut menu. |
|  | f0 specifies the start frequency, in hertz, of the spectrum. |
|  | df specifies the frequency resolution, in hertz, of the spectrum. |
|  | complex array specifies the complex form of the spectrum. |
|  | spectrum info specifies properties of the computed spectrum. Note You must wire spectrum info when performing extended measurements. Do not modify the spectrum info values. If you want to view the information, you can unbundle and right-click the spectrum info wire and select Custom Probe»Spectrum Info from the shortcut menu. |

<!--NI_TOPIC bundle=labview-sound-and-vibration-toolkit-api-ref path=svpo-integration-vi.html language=enus -->
## TOPIC 00128: SVPO Integration VI

- bundle_id: `labview-sound-and-vibration-toolkit-api-ref`
- source_path: `svpo-integration-vi.html`
- source_url: https://docs-be.ni.com/bundle/labview-sound-and-vibration-toolkit-api-ref/raw/resource/enus/svpo-integration-vi.html
- document_id: `labview-sound-and-vibration-toolkit-api-ref`
- page_type: `leaf`
- content_type: `reference`
- source_description: Owning Palette: Embedded Monitoring VIs Requires: Sound and Vibration Toolkit Converts acceleration to velocity or velocity to displacement using a time-domain waveform as the input. Wire data to the scaled signal [EU] input to determine the polymorphic instance to use or manually select the instanc

### SVPO Integration VI

**Owning Palette:**
 Embedded Monitoring VIs

**Requires:**
 Sound and Vibration Toolkit

Converts acceleration to velocity or velocity to displacement using a time-domain waveform as the input. Wire data to the 
 **scaled signal [EU]**
 input to determine the polymorphic instance to use or manually select the instance. This VI performs Single Integration only. You can pass the integrated signal [EU*s] output to the scaled signal [EU] input of second call to this VI to perform double integration.

Details

| SVPO Integration (1 Ch Dbl Array)SVPO Integration (N Ch DBL Array)SVPO Integration (1 Ch DBL Array) with ICSVPO Single-Block Integration (1 Ch DBL Array)SVPO Integration (1 Ch DBL WDT)SVPO Integration (N Ch DBL WDT)SVPO Integration (1 Ch DBL WDT) with ICSVPO Single-Block Integration (1 Ch DBL WDT) |
| --- |

SVPO Integration (1 Ch Dbl Array)

[IMAGE alt='image' src='images/SVPO_Integration_1_CH_DBL_Array.gif']

|  | scaled signal [EU] specifies the scaled signal expressed in the selected engineering units. To obtain a properly scaled signal, you can use the SVPO Scale Voltage to EU VI or define a virtual channel in National Instruments Measurement & Automation Explorer (MAX). |
| --- | --- |
|  | sampling rate [Hz] specifies the sampling rate of the input signal in hertz. |
|  | highpass cutoff frequency [Hz] specifies the cutoff frequency for the highpass filter. The default is 1.00. Frequency components less than highpass cutoff frequency are attenuated by the highpass filter. Use this parameter if you know the lower limit of the frequency response of your transducer. For example, if you are using an accelerometer that shows poor response below 20 Hz, set highpass cutoff frequency [Hz] to 20 Hz. |
|  | reset filters specifies if the various filters used for the integration have to be reset. The default is FALSE. The filters are automatically reset the first time this VI is called. |
|  | integrated signal [EU*s] returns a single-integrated signal. |

SVPO Integration (N Ch DBL Array)

[IMAGE alt='image' src='images/SVPO_Integration_N_CH_DBL_Array.gif']

|  | scaled signals [EU] specifies the scaled signals expressed in the selected engineering units. You can use the SVPO Scale Voltage to EU VI to obtain a properly scaled signal. |
| --- | --- |
|  | sampling rate [Hz] specifies the sampling rate of the input signal in hertz. |
|  | highpass cutoff frequency [Hz] specifies the cutoff frequency for the highpass filter. The default is 1.00. Frequency components less than highpass cutoff frequency are attenuated by the highpass filter. Use this parameter if you know the lower limit of the frequency response of your transducer. For example, if you are using an accelerometer that shows poor response below 20 Hz, set highpass cutoff frequency [Hz] to 20 Hz. |
|  | reset filters specifies if the various filters used for the integration have to be reset. The default is FALSE. The filters are automatically reset the first time this VI is called. |
|  | integrated signals [EU*s] returns single-integrated signals. |

SVPO Integration (1 Ch DBL Array) with IC

[IMAGE alt='image' src='images/SVPO_Integration_1_CH_DBL_Array_with_IC.gif']

|  | scaled signal [EU] specifies the scaled signal expressed in the selected engineering units. To obtain a properly scaled signal, you can use the SVPO Scale Voltage to EU VI or define a virtual channel in National Instruments Measurement & Automation Explorer (MAX). |
| --- | --- |
|  | sampling rate [Hz] specifies the sampling rate of the input signal in hertz. |
|  | highpass cutoff frequency [Hz] specifies the cutoff frequency for the highpass filter. The default is 1.00. Frequency components less than highpass cutoff frequency are attenuated by the highpass filter. Use this parameter if you know the lower limit of the frequency response of your transducer. For example, if you are using an accelerometer that shows poor response below 20 Hz, set highpass cutoff frequency [Hz] to 20 Hz. |
|  | initial conditions specifies the initial internal state of this VI. This input should come from the previous output of final conditions from the last call to this VI in order to operate on samples continuously. |
|  | reset filters specifies if the various filters used for the integration have to be reset. The default is FALSE. The filters are automatically reset the first time this VI is called. |
|  | integrated signal [EU*s] returns a single-integrated signal. |
| final conditions returns the final internal state of this VI. This output should be passed to the initial conditions input on the next call to this VI in order to operate on samples continuously. |  |

SVPO Single-Block Integration (1 Ch DBL Array)

This VI is designed to operate on a single block of data, as opposed to being called repeatedly to operate on a continuous flow of data. The results of this VI are only dependent on the inputs to the VI, and not on previous calls to this VI. Use this VI after performing a finite acquisition, or when you need to analyze a single block of data without previous blocks affecting the result. This VI does not store state internally, and can be called from a shared clone re-entrant VI.

[IMAGE alt='image' src='images/SVPO_Single_Block_Integration_1_Ch_DBL_Array.gif']

|  | scaled signal [EU] specifies the scaled signal expressed in the selected engineering units. To obtain a properly scaled signal, you can use the SVPO Scale Voltage to EU VI or define a virtual channel in National Instruments Measurement & Automation Explorer (MAX). |
| --- | --- |
|  | sampling rate [Hz] specifies the sampling rate of the input signal in hertz. |
|  | known condition is used to specify how the integrated signal [EU*s] should be adjusted. condition type determines the location in the integrated signal [EU*s] to base the adjustment off of. This can be Initial Value , Mean Value , or Final Value . known value determines what value the integrated signal [EU*s] should be adjusted to. The default is NaN , which will cause no adjustment to be made. |
|  | condition type determines the location in the integrated signal [EU*s] to base the adjustment off of. This can be Initial Value , Mean Value , or Final Value . |
|  | known value determines what value the integrated signal [EU*s] should be adjusted to. The default is NaN , which will cause no adjustment to be made. |
|  | integrated signal [EU*s] returns a single-integrated signal. |

SVPO Integration (1 Ch DBL WDT)

[IMAGE alt='image' src='images/SVPO_Integration_1_CH_DBL_WDT.gif']

|  | desired base units for length specifies the desired units for length in the integrated spectrum. Use this parameter to change the base units for the integrated spectrum. 0 no change 1 m (default) 2 mm 3 microns 4 in 5 mils |
| --- | --- |
| 0 | no change |
| 1 | m (default) |
| 2 | mm |
| 3 | microns |
| 4 | in |
| 5 | mils |
|  | scaled signal [EU] specifies the scaled signal expressed in the selected engineering units. You can use the SVPO Scale Voltage to EU VI to obtain a properly scaled signal. t0 specifies the start time of the waveform. dt specifies the time interval in seconds between data points in the waveform. Y specifies the data values of the waveform. |
|  | t0 specifies the start time of the waveform. |
|  | dt specifies the time interval in seconds between data points in the waveform. |
|  | Y specifies the data values of the waveform. |
|  | highpass cutoff frequency [Hz] specifies the cutoff frequency for the highpass filter. The default is 1.00. Frequency components less than highpass cutoff frequency are attenuated by the highpass filter. Use this parameter if you know the lower limit of the frequency response of your transducer. For example, if you are using an accelerometer that shows poor response below 20 Hz, set highpass cutoff frequency [Hz] to 20 Hz. |
|  | reset filters specifies if the various filters used for the integration have to be reset. The default is FALSE. The filters are automatically reset the first time this VI is called. |
|  | integrated signal [EU*s] returns a single-integrated signal. |

SVPO Integration (N Ch DBL WDT)

[IMAGE alt='image' src='images/SVPO_Integration_N_CH_DBL_WDT.gif']

Y specifies the data values of the waveform. |

|  | dt specifies the time interval in seconds between data points in the waveform. |
|  | Y specifies the data values of the waveform. |
|  | highpass cutoff frequency [Hz] specifies the cutoff frequency for the highpass filter. The default is 1.00. Frequency components less than highpass cutoff frequency are attenuated by the highpass filter. Use this parameter if you know the lower limit of the frequency response of your transducer. For example, if you are using an accelerometer that shows poor response below 20 Hz, set highpass cutoff frequency [Hz] to 20 Hz. |
|  | reset filters specifies if the various filters used for the integration have to be reset. The default is FALSE. The filters are automatically reset the first time this VI is called. |
|  | integrated signals [EU*s] returns an array of single-integrated signals. |

SVPO Integration (1 Ch DBL WDT) with IC

[IMAGE alt='image' src='images/SVPO_Integration_1_CH_DBL_WDT_with_IC.gif']

|  | desired base units for length specifies the desired units for length in the integrated spectrum. Use this parameter to change the base units for the integrated spectrum. 0 no change 1 m (default) 2 mm 3 microns 4 in 5 mils |

Y specifies the data values of the waveform. |
