|  | reset filters specifies if the various filters used for the integration have to be reset. The default is FALSE. The filters are automatically reset the first time this VI is called. |
|  | integrated signal [EU*s] returns a single-integrated signal. |
| final conditions returns the final internal state of this VI. This output should be passed to the initial conditions input on the next call to this VI in order to operate on samples continuously. |  |

SVPO Single-Block Integration (1 Ch DBL WDT)

This VI is designed to operate on a single block of data, as opposed to being called repeatedly to operate on a continuous flow of data. The results of this VI are only dependent on the inputs to the VI, and not on previous calls to this VI. Use this VI after performing a finite acquisition, or when you need to analyze a single block of data without previous blocks affecting the result. This VI does not store state internally, and can be called from a shared clone re-entrant VI.

[IMAGE alt='image' src='images/SVPO_Single_Block_Integration_1_Ch_DBL_WDT.gif']

|  | desired base units for length specifies the desired units for length in the integrated spectrum. Use this parameter to change the base units for the integrated spectrum. 0 no change 1 m (default) 2 mm 3 microns 4 in 5 mils |
| --- | --- |
| 0 | no change |
| 1 | m (default) |
| 2 | mm |
| 3 | microns |
| 4 | in |
| 5 | mils |
|  | scaled signal [EU] specifies the scaled signal expressed in the selected engineering units. You can use the SVPO Scale Voltage to EU VI to obtain a properly scaled signal. t0 specifies the start time of the waveform. dt specifies the time interval in seconds between data points in the waveform. Y specifies the data values of the waveform. |
|  | t0 specifies the start time of the waveform. |
|  | dt specifies the time interval in seconds between data points in the waveform. |
|  | Y specifies the data values of the waveform. |
|  | known condition is used to specify how the integrated signal [EU*s] should be adjusted. condition type determines the location in the integrated signal [EU*s] to base the adjustment off of. This can be Initial Value , Mean Value , or Final Value . known value determines what value the integrated signal [EU*s] should be adjusted to. The default is NaN , which will cause no adjustment to be made. |
|  | condition type determines the location in the integrated signal [EU*s] to base the adjustment off of. This can be Initial Value , Mean Value , or Final Value . |
|  | known value determines what value the integrated signal [EU*s] should be adjusted to. The default is NaN , which will cause no adjustment to be made. |
|  | integrated signal [EU*s] returns a single-integrated signal. |

SVPO Integration Details

This Integration VI uses digital filters to integrate the input signal, a lowpass filter with a known rolloff to do the actual integration and a highpass filter to remove the DC component from the signal. If you know the lower limit of the frequency response of your transducer, use the 
 **highpass cutoff frequency**
 parameter.

Parent topic:

Embedded Monitoring VIs

<!--NI_TOPIC bundle=labview-sound-and-vibration-toolkit-api-ref path=svpo-limit-testing-vi.html language=enus -->
## TOPIC 00129: SVPO Limit Testing VI

- bundle_id: `labview-sound-and-vibration-toolkit-api-ref`
- source_path: `svpo-limit-testing-vi.html`
- source_url: https://docs-be.ni.com/bundle/labview-sound-and-vibration-toolkit-api-ref/raw/resource/enus/svpo-limit-testing-vi.html
- document_id: `labview-sound-and-vibration-toolkit-api-ref`
- page_type: `leaf`
- content_type: `reference`
- source_description: Owning Palette: Embedded Monitoring VIs Requires: Sound and Vibration Toolkit Performs limit testing on single-precision waveforms, single-precision scaled arrays, or double-precision scalar measurements. You can specify an upper and lower boundary of the envelope to define a pass range. This VI use

### SVPO Limit Testing VI

**Owning Palette:**
 Embedded Monitoring VIs

**Requires:**
 Sound and Vibration Toolkit

Performs limit testing on single-precision waveforms, single-precision scaled arrays, or double-precision scalar measurements. You can specify an upper and lower boundary of the envelope to define a pass range. This VI uses the defined pass range to test the input signal. VI outputs apply to all the tests since the last VI reset. Use this VI on embedded targets that have limited computational resources. Wire data to the 
 **value in**
 input to determine the polymorphic instance to use or manually select the instance.

| SVPO Limit Testing (Scalar) (DBL)SVPO Limit Testing (Scalar) (DBL) with ICSVPO Limit Testing (1 Ch) (SGL Array)SVPO Limit Testing (N Ch) (SGL Array)SVPO Limit Testing (1 Ch) (SGL Array) with ICSVPO Limit Testing (1 Ch) (SGL Waveform)SVPO Limit Testing (N Ch) (SGL Waveform)SVPO Limit Testing (1 Ch) (SGL Waveform) with IC |
| --- |

SVPO Limit Testing (Scalar) (DBL)

[IMAGE alt='image' src='images/SVPO_Limit_Testing_DBL.gif']

|  | reset? specifies whether to reset the limit testing. When you call this VI for the first time, the limit testing state and outputs reset automatically. After the first call, you must set reset? to TRUE in order to reset any or all of the outputs of this VI. Set reset? to TRUE to process only the latest block of data. Set reset? to FALSE to perform limit testing over multiple iterations and maintain the minimum clearances over these iterations. The default is FALSE. |
| --- | --- |
|  | value in specifies the double-precision scalar value to test to make sure the value lies in the envelope bounded by upper limit and lower limit . |
|  | upper limit specifies the upper boundary of the envelope. |
|  | lower limit specifies the lower boundary of the envelope. |
|  | test passed? indicates the result of limit mask testing. If test passed? is TRUE, value in is less than or equal to upper limit and greater than or equal to lower limit and the limit testing passed. If test passed? is FALSE, the limit testing did not pass. |
|  | upper limit clearance returns the minimum distance between the input signal and upper limit . If the signal points fail at any point along upper limit , upper limit clearance returns a negative value. upper limit clearance returns Inf if you do not specify upper limit , which means this VI did not test the signal against upper limit or the signal passed upper limit with maximum clearance. |
|  | lower limit clearance returns the minimum distance between the input signal and lower limit . If the signal points fail at any point along lower limit , lower limit clearance returns a negative value. upper limit clearance returns Inf if you do not specify lower limit , which means this VI did not test the signal against lower limit or the signal passed lower limit with maximum clearance. |

SVPO Limit Testing (Scalar) (DBL) with IC

[IMAGE alt='image' src='images/SVPO_Limit_Testing_(Scalar)_(DBL)_with_IC.gif']

|  | reset? (F) specifies whether to reset the limit testing. Set reset? to TRUE to process only the latest block of data. Set reset? to FALSE to perform limit testing over multiple iterations and maintain the minimum clearances over these iterations. The default is FALSE. |
| --- | --- |
|  | value in specifies the double-precision scalar value to test to make sure the value lies in the envelope bounded by upper limit and lower limit . |
|  | upper limit specifies the upper boundary of the envelope. |
|  | lower limit specifies the lower boundary of the envelope. |
|  | initial conditions specifies the initial internal state of this VI. This input should come from the previous output of final conditions from the last call to this VI in order to operate on samples continuously. |
|  | test passed? indicates the result of limit mask testing. If test passed? is TRUE, value in is less than or equal to upper limit and greater than or equal to lower limit and the limit testing passed. If test passed? is FALSE, the limit testing did not pass. |
|  | upper limit clearance returns the minimum distance between the input signal and upper limit . If the signal points fail at any point along upper limit , upper limit clearance returns a negative value. upper limit clearance returns Inf if you do not specify upper limit , which means this VI did not test the signal against upper limit or the signal passed upper limit with maximum clearance. |
|  | lower limit clearance returns the minimum distance between the input signal and lower limit . If the signal points fail at any point along lower limit , lower limit clearance returns a negative value. upper limit clearance returns Inf if you do not specify lower limit , which means this VI did not test the signal against lower limit or the signal passed lower limit with maximum clearance. |
| final conditions returns the final internal state of this VI. This output should be passed to the initial conditions input on the next call to this VI in order to operate on samples continuously. |  |

|  | reset? specifies whether to reset the limit testing. When you call this VI for the first time, the limit testing state and outputs reset automatically. After the first call, you must set reset? to TRUE in order to reset any or all of the outputs of this VI. Set reset? to TRUE to process only the latest block of data. |  | timestamps returns three timestamps of the limit testing result. reset returns the date and time when the limit testing starts or when reset? is set to TRUE. first failure returns the date and time of the first failure. first failure returns an empty timestamp when there's no failure. last failure returns the date and time of the last failure. last failure returns an empty timestamp when there's no failure. |
|  | reset returns the date and time when the limit testing starts or when reset? is set to TRUE. |
|  | first failure returns the date and time of the first failure. first failure returns an empty timestamp when there's no failure. |

|  | test passed? indicates the result of limit mask testing. If test passed? is TRUE, value in is less than or equal to upper limit and greater than or equal to lower limit and the limit testing passed. If test passed? is FALSE, the limit testing did not pass. |
|  | upper limit clearances returns the minimum distance between the input signal and upper limit . If the signal points fail at any point along upper limit , upper limit clearances returns a negative value. upper limit clearances returns Inf if you do not specify upper limit , which means this VI did not test the signal against upper limit or the signal passed upper limit with maximum clearance. |
|  | lower limit clearances returns the minimum distance between the input signal and lower limit . If the signal points fail at any point along lower limit , lower limit clearances returns a negative value. upper limit clearances returns Inf if you do not specify lower limit , which means this VI did not test the signal against lower limit or the signal passed lower limit with maximum clearance. |
| final conditions returns the final internal state of this VI. This output should be passed to the initial conditions input on the next call to this VI in order to operate on samples continuously. |  |

Parent topic:

Embedded Monitoring VIs

<!--NI_TOPIC bundle=labview-sound-and-vibration-toolkit-api-ref path=svpo-order-metrics-for-periodic-inspection-vi.html language=enus -->
## TOPIC 00130: SVPO Order Metrics for Periodic Inspection VI

- bundle_id: `labview-sound-and-vibration-toolkit-api-ref`
- source_path: `svpo-order-metrics-for-periodic-inspection-vi.html`
- source_url: https://docs-be.ni.com/bundle/labview-sound-and-vibration-toolkit-api-ref/raw/resource/enus/svpo-order-metrics-for-periodic-inspection-vi.html
- document_id: `labview-sound-and-vibration-toolkit-api-ref`
- page_type: `leaf`
- content_type: `reference`
- source_description: Owning Palette: Embedded Monitoring VIs Requires: Sound and Vibration Toolkit Computes the metrics of designated orders in an even-angle signal, such as magnitudes, phases, speed, DC gap, RMS level, and max-min level. This VI calculates metrics values from the latest integration cycles of the even-a

### SVPO Order Metrics for Periodic Inspection VI

**Owning Palette:**
 Embedded Monitoring VIs

**Requires:**
 Sound and Vibration Toolkit

Computes the metrics of designated orders in an even-angle signal, such as magnitudes, phases, speed, DC gap, RMS level, and max-min level. This VI calculates metrics values from the latest integration cycles of the even-angle signal. Use this VI on embedded targets that have limited computational resources. Wire data to the 
 **even-angle signal**
 input to determine the polymorphic instance to use or manually select the instance.

| SVPO Order Metrics for Periodic Inspection (1 Ch)SVPO Order Metrics for Periodic Inspection (N Ch)SVPO Order Metrics for Periodic Inspection (1 Ch) with IC |
| --- |

SVPO Order Metrics for Periodic Inspection (1 Ch)

[IMAGE alt='image' src='images/SVPO_Order_Metrics_for_Periodic_Inspection_1_Ch.gif']

|  | reset? specifies the initialization of the internal states. The first time this VI runs, or when reset? is TRUE, the internal states initialize to zero. If reset? is FALSE, this VI sets the internal states to the final states from the previous call to the VI. The default is FALSE. You must manually set reset? to TRUE if integration cycles changes in a continuous measurement. You also need to set reset? to TRUE if the input values of the SVPO Analog Tacho Process VI and SVPO Convert to Even Angle Signal VI change in a continuous measurement. |
| --- | --- |
|  | update? specifies whether this VI computes the order metrics. The default is TRUE. You can set update? programmatically so that this VI returns the metrics computation at a specific time interval. |
|  | even-angle signal specifies the even-angle-resampled data. even-angled signal is synchronous to the rotational speed with a constant number of samples per revolution. r0 specifies the starting index value for counting revolutions. dr specifies the revolution interval between two sequential samples. Y specifies the value of the even-angle-spaced sample in a scaled engineering unit. |
|  | r0 specifies the starting index value for counting revolutions. |
|  | dr specifies the revolution interval between two sequential samples. |
|  | Y specifies the value of the even-angle-spaced sample in a scaled engineering unit. |
|  | even-angle position info returns the position information of even-angle signal . positions [s] returns the locations of the samples in seconds. samples/rev returns the number of samples per revolution. |
|  | positions [s] returns the locations of the samples in seconds. |
|  | samples/rev returns the number of samples per revolution. |
|  | orders specifies the orders this VI uses to compute the metrics. The default is 1.0. |
|  | integration cycles specifies the duration of the measurement in cycles. The default is 8. If the number of cycles in each iteration is smaller than integration cycles , the output metrics values are valid only when the number of passing cycles is greater than integration cycles . If the number of cycles in each iteration is greater than integration cycles , this VI calculates the output metrics values from the latest integration cycles. |
|  | measurement selection specifies the order metrics to calculate. By default, this VI returns all the measurements in measurement selection . You can set some of the measurements that you do not need to FALSE to save computational resources. speed specifies whether to return the rotational speed. The default is TRUE. DC gap specifies whether to return the value of the DC gap. The default is TRUE. RMS level specifies whether to return the value of the RMS level. The default is TRUE. max-min level specifies whether to return the value of the max-min level. The default is TRUE. magnitude specifies whether to return the value of the magnitude. The default is TRUE. phase specifies whether to return the value of the phase. The default is TRUE. |
|  | speed specifies whether to return the rotational speed. The default is TRUE. |
|  | DC gap specifies whether to return the value of the DC gap. The default is TRUE. |
|  | RMS level specifies whether to return the value of the RMS level. The default is TRUE. |
|  | max-min level specifies whether to return the value of the max-min level. The default is TRUE. |
|  | magnitude specifies whether to return the value of the magnitude. The default is TRUE. |
|  | phase specifies whether to return the value of the phase. The default is TRUE. |
|  | speed [RPM] returns the averaged rotational speed in revolutions per minute. |
|  | DC gap returns the value of the DC gap of the even-angle signal. |
|  | RMS level returns the RMS level of the even-angle signal. |
|  | max-min level returns the max-min level of the even-angle signal. |
|  | magnitudes [RMS] returns the magnitudes, in RMS value, of the specified order. |
|  | phase [phase lag] returns the wrapped radian phase, in phase lag value, of the specified order. |

<!--NI_TOPIC bundle=labview-sound-and-vibration-toolkit-api-ref path=svpo-peak-level-vi.html language=enus -->
## TOPIC 00131: SVPO Peak Level VI

- bundle_id: `labview-sound-and-vibration-toolkit-api-ref`
- source_path: `svpo-peak-level-vi.html`
- source_url: https://docs-be.ni.com/bundle/labview-sound-and-vibration-toolkit-api-ref/raw/resource/enus/svpo-peak-level-vi.html
- document_id: `labview-sound-and-vibration-toolkit-api-ref`
- page_type: `leaf`
- content_type: `reference`
- source_description: Owning Palette: Embedded Monitoring VIs Requires: Sound and Vibration Toolkit Uses a peak detector and computes the level of scaled time waveform signals and scaled array signals. scaled signal [EU] should have a zero DC component. You can attenuate the DC component with hardware AC-coupling filters

### SVPO Peak Level VI

**Owning Palette:**
 Embedded Monitoring VIs

**Requires:**
 Sound and Vibration Toolkit

Uses a peak detector and computes the level of scaled time waveform signals and scaled array signals. 
 **scaled signal [EU]**
 should have a zero DC component. You can attenuate the DC component with hardware AC-coupling filters and with software high-pass filters. Use this VI on embedded targets that have limited computational resources. Wire data to the scaled signal [EU] input to determine the polymorphic instance to use or manually select the instance.

| SVPO Peak Level (1 Ch DBL Array)SVPO Peak Level (N Ch DBL Array)SVPO Peak Level (1 Ch DBL Array) with ICSVPO Peak Level (1 Ch DBL WDT)SVPO Peak Level (N Ch DBL WDT)SVPO Peak Level (1 Ch DBL WDT) with IC |
| --- |

SVPO Peak Level (1 Ch DBL Array)

[IMAGE alt='image' src='images/SVPO_Peak_Level_1_Ch_DBL_Array.gif']

|  | scaled signal [EU] specifies the scaled signal expressed in the selected engineering units. You can use the SVPO Scale Voltage to EU VI to obtain a properly scaled signal. |
| --- | --- |
|  | sampling rate [Hz] specifies the sampling rate of the input signal in hertz. |
|  | restart averaging? specifies whether to restart the averaging process. When you call this VI for the first time, the averaging process restarts automatically. The default is FALSE, which means this VI returns levels and duration [s] since the last reset. Leave restart averaging? as FALSE if you want to measure the equivalent continuous level over multiple iterations. Set restart averaging? to TRUE to process the latest block of data continuously. |
|  | peak level returns the peak level in engineering units. |
|  | maximum level [EU] returns the maximum level in engineering units [EU]. |
|  | minimum level [EU] returns the minimum level in engineering units [EU]. |
|  | duration [s] returns the time duration since the level measurement starts or since you restart the averaging. |

SVPO Peak Level (N Ch DBL Array)

[IMAGE alt='image' src='images/SVPO_Peak_Level_N_Ch_DBL_Array.gif']

|  | scaled signals [EU] specifies the scaled signals expressed in the selected engineering units. You can use the SVPO Scale Voltage to EU VI to obtain a properly scaled signal. |
| --- | --- |
|  | sampling rate [Hz] specifies the sampling rate of the input signal in hertz. |
|  | restart averaging? specifies whether to restart the averaging process. When you call this VI for the first time, the averaging process restarts automatically. The default is FALSE, which means this VI returns levels and duration [s] since the last reset. Leave restart averaging? as FALSE if you want to measure the equivalent continuous level over multiple iterations. Set restart averaging? to TRUE to process the latest block of data continuously. |
|  | peak levels returns the peak level for each channel in engineering units. |
|  | maximum levels [EU] returns the maximum levels in engineering units [EU]. |
|  | minimum levels [EU] returns the maximum levels in engineering units [EU]. |
|  | durations [s] returns, for each channel, the time duration since the level measurement starts or since you restart the averaging. |

SVPO Peak Level (1 Ch DBL Array) with IC

This VI does not store state internally. Use this VI when you need to manage the state at the calling VI's level, or when the calling VI is set to shared-clone re-entrant. Because this VI does not store state internally, and is set to shared clone re-entrant, it is safe to call from Vis that are also set to shared clone re-entrant.

[IMAGE alt='image' src='images/SVPO_Peak_Level_(1_Ch_DBL_Array)_with_IC.gif']

|  | scaled signal [EU] specifies the scaled signal expressed in the selected engineering units. You can use the SVPO Scale Voltage to EU VI to obtain a properly scaled signal. |
| --- | --- |
|  | sampling rate [Hz] specifies the sampling rate of the input signal in hertz. |
|  | initial conditions specifies the initial internal state of this VI. This input should come from the previous output of final conditions from the last call to this VI in order to operate on samples continuously. |
|  | restart averaging? specifies whether to restart the RMS averaging process. The default is FALSE, which means this VI returns RMS level and duration [s] since the last reset. Set restart averaging? to FALSE if you want to measure the equivalent continuous level over multiple iterations. Set restart averaging? to TRUE to process the latest block of data continuously. |
|  | peak level returns the peak level in engineering units. |
|  | maximum level [EU] returns the maximum level in engineering units [EU]. |
|  | minimum level [EU] returns the minimum level in engineering units [EU]. |
| final conditions returns the final internal state of this VI. This output should be passed to the initial conditions input on the next call to this VI in order to operate on samples continuously. |  |
|  | duration [s] returns the time duration since the level measurement starts or since you restart the averaging. |

SVPO Peak Level (1 Ch DBL WDT)

[IMAGE alt='image' src='images/SVPO_Peak_Level_1_Ch_DBL_WDT.gif']

|  | scaled signal [EU] specifies the scaled signal expressed in the selected engineering units. To obtain a properly scaled signal, you can use the SVL Scale Voltage to EU VI or define a virtual channel in National Instruments Measurement & Automation Explorer (MAX). t0 specifies the start time of the waveform. dt specifies the time interval in seconds between data points in the waveform. Y specifies the data values of the waveform. |
| --- | --- |
|  | t0 specifies the start time of the waveform. |
|  | dt specifies the time interval in seconds between data points in the waveform. |
|  | Y specifies the data values of the waveform. |
|  | restart averaging? specifies whether to restart the averaging process. When you call this VI for the first time, the averaging process restarts automatically. The default is FALSE, which means this VI returns levels and duration [s] since the last reset. Leave restart averaging? as FALSE if you want to measure the equivalent continuous level over multiple iterations. Set restart averaging? to TRUE to process the latest block of data continuously. |
|  | timestamp returns the start time of the level measurement or the time when you set restart averaging? to TRUE. |
|  | peak level returns the peak level in engineering units. |
|  | maximum level [EU] returns the maximum level in engineering units [EU]. |
|  | minimum level [EU] returns the minimum level in engineering units [EU]. |
|  | duration [s] returns the time duration since the level measurement starts or since you restart the averaging. |

SVPO Peak Level (N Ch DBL WDT)

[IMAGE alt='image' src='images/SVPO_Peak_Level_N_Ch_DBL_Array.gif']

|  | scaled signals [EU] specifies the single-precision scaled signals expressed in the selected engineering units. You can use the SVPO Scale Voltage to EU VI to obtain a properly scaled signal. t0 specifies the start time of the waveform. dt specifies the time interval in seconds between data points in the waveform. Y specifies the data values of the waveform. |
| --- | --- |
|  | t0 specifies the start time of the waveform. |
|  | dt specifies the time interval in seconds between data points in the waveform. |
|  | Y specifies the data values of the waveform. |
|  | restart averaging? specifies whether to restart the averaging process. When you call this VI for the first time, the averaging process restarts automatically. The default is FALSE, which means this VI returns levels and duration [s] since the last reset. Leave restart averaging? as FALSE if you want to measure the equivalent continuous level over multiple iterations. Set restart averaging? to TRUE to process the latest block of data continuously. |
|  | timestamps returns the start time of the level measurement or the time when you set restart averaging? to TRUE. |
|  | peak levels returns the peak level for each channel in engineering units. |
|  | maximum levels [EU] returns the maximum levels in engineering units [EU]. |
|  | minimum levels [EU] returns the maximum levels in engineering units [EU]. |
|  | durations [s] returns, for each channel, the time duration since the level measurement starts or since you restart the averaging. |

SVPO Peak Level (1 Ch DBL WDT) with IC

This VI does not store state internally. Use this VI when you need to manage the state at the calling VI's level, or when the calling VI is set to shared-clone re-entrant. Because this VI does not store state internally, and is set to shared clone re-entrant, it is safe to call from Vis that are also set to shared clone re-entrant.

[IMAGE alt='image' src='images/SVPO_Peak_Level_(1_Ch_DBL_WDT)_with_IC.gif']

|  | scaled signal [EU] specifies the scaled signal expressed in the selected engineering units. To obtain a properly scaled signal, you can use the SVL Scale Voltage to EU VI or define a virtual channel in National Instruments Measurement & Automation Explorer (MAX). t0 specifies the start time of the waveform. dt specifies the time interval in seconds between data points in the waveform. Y specifies the data values of the waveform. |
| --- | --- |
|  | t0 specifies the start time of the waveform. |
|  | dt specifies the time interval in seconds between data points in the waveform. |
|  | Y specifies the data values of the waveform. |
|  | initial conditions specifies the initial internal state of this VI. This input should come from the previous output of final conditions from the last call to this VI in order to operate on samples continuously. |
|  | restart averaging? specifies whether to restart the RMS averaging process. The default is FALSE, which means this VI returns RMS level and duration [s] since the last reset. Set restart averaging? to FALSE if you want to measure the equivalent continuous level over multiple iterations. Set restart averaging? to TRUE to process the latest block of data continuously. |
|  | timestamp returns the start time of the level measurement or the time when you set restart averaging? to TRUE. |
|  | peak level returns the peak level in engineering units. |
|  | maximum level [EU] returns the maximum level in engineering units [EU]. |
|  | minimum level [EU] returns the minimum level in engineering units [EU]. |
| final conditions returns the final internal state of this VI. This output should be passed to the initial conditions input on the next call to this VI in order to operate on samples continuously. |  |
|  | duration [s] returns the time duration since the level measurement starts or since you restart the averaging. |

## TOPIC 00132: SVPO Power in Band VI

- bundle_id: `labview-sound-and-vibration-toolkit-api-ref`
- source_path: `svpo-power-in-band-vi.html`
- source_url: https://docs-be.ni.com/bundle/labview-sound-and-vibration-toolkit-api-ref/raw/resource/enus/svpo-power-in-band-vi.html
- document_id: `labview-sound-and-vibration-toolkit-api-ref`
- page_type: `leaf`
- content_type: `reference`
- source_description: Owning Palette: Embedded Monitoring VIs Requires: Sound and Vibration Toolkit Computes the aggregate power of a linear-scale power spectrum within the specified frequency range of the input spectrum. Use this VI on embedded targets that have limited computational resources. Wire data to the spectrum

### SVPO Power in Band VI

**Owning Palette:**
 Embedded Monitoring VIs

**Requires:**
 Sound and Vibration Toolkit

range

spectrum [EU^2 rms]

Note

dB On

| SVPO Power in Band (1 Ch)SVPO Power in Band (N Ch) |
| --- |

SVPO Power in Band (1 Ch)

[IMAGE alt='image' src='images/SVPO_Power_in_Band_1_Ch.gif']

|  | spectrum [EU^2 rms] specifies the abscissa scaling and the power spectrum in a linear scale. x0 specifies the start frequency or order of the spectrum. The default is 0.00. dx specifies the frequency or order resolution of the spectrum. The default is 1.00. magnitude specifies the power spectrum in a linear scale. The default is 0.00. |
| --- | --- |
|  | x0 specifies the start frequency or order of the spectrum. The default is 0.00. |
|  | dx specifies the frequency or order resolution of the spectrum. The default is 1.00. |
|  | magnitude specifies the power spectrum in a linear scale. The default is 0.00. |
|  | range specifies the band of analysis from the lowest frequency to the highest frequency. start specifies the minimum value for the scale. The default is 0.00 . stop specifies the maximum value for the scale. The default is 1000.00 . |
|  | start specifies the minimum value for the scale. The default is 0.00 . |
|  | stop specifies the maximum value for the scale. The default is 1000.00 . |
|  | spectrum info specifies properties of the computed spectrum. Note You must wire spectrum info when performing extended measurements. Do not modify the spectrum info values. If you want to view the information, you can right-click the spectrum info wire and select Custom Probe»Spectrum Info from the shortcut menu. |
|  | power in band [EU^2 rms] returns the aggregate power in the specified range. |

SVPO Power in Band (N Ch)

[IMAGE alt='image' src='images/SVPO_Power_in_Band_N_Ch.gif']

|  | spectra [EU^2 rms] specifies the abscissa scaling and the power spectra in a linear scale. x0 specifies the start frequency or order of the spectrum. The default is 0.00. dx specifies the frequency or order resolution of the spectrum. The default is 1.00. magnitude specifies the power spectrum in a linear scale. The default is 0.00. |
| --- | --- |
|  | x0 specifies the start frequency or order of the spectrum. The default is 0.00. |
|  | dx specifies the frequency or order resolution of the spectrum. The default is 1.00. |
|  | magnitude specifies the power spectrum in a linear scale. The default is 0.00. |
|  | range specifies the band of analysis from the lowest frequency to the highest frequency. start specifies the minimum value for the scale. The default is 0.00 . stop specifies the maximum value for the scale. The default is 1000.00 . |
|  | start specifies the minimum value for the scale. The default is 0.00 . |
|  | stop specifies the maximum value for the scale. The default is 1000.00 . |
|  | spectrum info specifies properties of the computed spectrum. Note You must wire spectrum info when performing extended measurements. Do not modify the spectrum info values. If you want to view the information, you can right-click the spectrum info wire and select Custom Probe»Spectrum Info from the shortcut menu. |
|  | powers in band [EU^2 rms] returns the aggregate powers in the specified ranges. |

<!--NI_TOPIC bundle=labview-sound-and-vibration-toolkit-api-ref path=svpo-power-spectrum-vi.html language=enus -->
## TOPIC 00133: SVPO Power Spectrum VI

- bundle_id: `labview-sound-and-vibration-toolkit-api-ref`
- source_path: `svpo-power-spectrum-vi.html`
- source_url: https://docs-be.ni.com/bundle/labview-sound-and-vibration-toolkit-api-ref/raw/resource/enus/svpo-power-spectrum-vi.html
- document_id: `labview-sound-and-vibration-toolkit-api-ref`
- page_type: `leaf`
- content_type: `reference`
- source_description: Owning Palette: Embedded Monitoring VIs Requires: Sound and Vibration Toolkit Computes the linear RMS-averaged power spectrum of scaled signal [EU] in a linear scale. Use the power spectrum to quantify signal components as a function of frequency. Use this VI on embedded targets that have limited co

### SVPO Power Spectrum VI

**Owning Palette:**
 Embedded Monitoring VIs

**Requires:**
 Sound and Vibration Toolkit

Computes the linear RMS-averaged power spectrum of 
 **scaled signal [EU]**
 in a linear scale. Use the power spectrum to quantify signal components as a function of frequency. Use this VI on embedded targets that have limited computational resources. Wire data to the 
 **scaled signal [EU]**
 input to determine the polymorphic instance to use or manually select the instance.

| SVPO Power Spectrum (1 Ch DBL Array)SVPO Power Spectrum (N Ch DBL Array)SVPO Single Block Power Spectrum (1 Ch DBL Array)SVPO Single Block Power Spectrum (N Ch DBL Array)SVPO Power Spectrum (1 Ch DBL WDT)SVPO Power Spectrum (N Ch DBL WDT)SVPO Single Block Power Spectrum (1 Ch DBL WDT)SVPO Single Block Power Spectrum (N Ch DBL WDT).vi |
| --- |

SVPO Power Spectrum (1 Ch DBL Array)

[IMAGE alt='image' src='images/SVPO_Power_Spectrum_1_Ch_DBL_Array.gif']

|  | scaled signal [EU] specifies the scaled signal expressed in the selected engineering units. You can use the SVPO Scale Voltage to EU VI to obtain a properly scaled signal. |
| --- | --- |
|  | sampling rate [Hz] specifies the sampling rate of the input signal in hertz. |
|  | window specifies the time-domain window to use. 0 None 1 Hanning (default) 2 Hamming 3 Blackman-Harris 4 Exact Blackman 5 Blackman 6 Flat Top 7 4 Term B-Harris 8 7 Term B-Harris 9 Low Sidelobe 10 Blackman Nuttall 11 Triangle 12 Bartlett-Hanning 13 Bohman 14 Parzen 15 Welch 16 Kaiser 17 Dolph-Chebyshev 18 Gaussian |
| 0 | None |
| 1 | Hanning (default) |
| 2 | Hamming |
| 3 | Blackman-Harris |
| 4 | Exact Blackman |
| 5 | Blackman |
| 6 | Flat Top |
| 7 | 4 Term B-Harris |
| 8 | 7 Term B-Harris |
| 9 | Low Sidelobe |
| 10 | Blackman Nuttall |
| 11 | Triangle |
| 12 | Bartlett-Hanning |
| 13 | Bohman |
| 14 | Parzen |
| 15 | Welch |
| 16 | Kaiser |
| 17 | Dolph-Chebyshev |
| 18 | Gaussian |
|  | restart averaging? specifies if the selected averaging process has to be restarted. The default is FALSE. When you call this VI for the first time, the averaging process restarts automatically. You must manually set restart averaging? to TRUE if the length of scaled signal [EU] , sampling rate , window , or number of channels is changed during a continuous measurement. |
|  | power spectrum [EU^2 rms] returns the power spectrum. f0 returns the start frequency, in hertz, of the spectrum. df returns the frequency resolution, in hertz, of the spectrum. magnitude returns the power spectrum in a linear scale. |
|  | f0 returns the start frequency, in hertz, of the spectrum. |
|  | df returns the frequency resolution, in hertz, of the spectrum. |
|  | magnitude returns the power spectrum in a linear scale. |
|  | spectrum info returns information about the spectrum needed by the Spectrum Extended Measurements VIs, the Display VIs, and the Tone and Distortion VIs. Note You must wire spectrum info when performing extended measurements. Do not modify the spectrum info values. If you want to view the information, you can right-click the spectrum info wire and select Custom Probe»Spectrum Info from the shortcut menu. |
|  | averages completed returns the number of averages completed by the VI at that time. |

SVPO Power Spectrum (N Ch DBL Array)

[IMAGE alt='image' src='images/SVPO_Power_Spectrum_N_Ch_DBL_Array.gif']

|  | scaled signals [EU] specifies the scaled signals expressed in the selected engineering units. You can use the SVPO Scale Voltage to EU VI to obtain a properly scaled signal. |
| --- | --- |
|  | sampling rate [Hz] specifies the sampling rate of the input signal in hertz. |
|  | window specifies the time-domain window to use. 0 None 1 Hanning (default) 2 Hamming 3 Blackman-Harris 4 Exact Blackman 5 Blackman 6 Flat Top 7 4 Term B-Harris 8 7 Term B-Harris 9 Low Sidelobe 10 Blackman Nuttall 11 Triangle 12 Bartlett-Hanning 13 Bohman 14 Parzen 15 Welch 16 Kaiser 17 Dolph-Chebyshev 18 Gaussian |
| 0 | None |
| 1 | Hanning (default) |
| 2 | Hamming |
| 3 | Blackman-Harris |
| 4 | Exact Blackman |
| 5 | Blackman |
| 6 | Flat Top |
| 7 | 4 Term B-Harris |
| 8 | 7 Term B-Harris |
| 9 | Low Sidelobe |
| 10 | Blackman Nuttall |
| 11 | Triangle |
| 12 | Bartlett-Hanning |
| 13 | Bohman |
| 14 | Parzen |
| 15 | Welch |
| 16 | Kaiser |
| 17 | Dolph-Chebyshev |
| 18 | Gaussian |
|  | restart averaging? specifies if the selected averaging process has to be restarted. The default is FALSE. When you call this VI for the first time, the averaging process restarts automatically. You must manually set restart averaging? to TRUE if the length of scaled signal [EU] , sampling rate , window , or number of channels is changed during a continuous measurement. |
|  | power spectra [EU^2 rms] returns the power spectrum for each channel. f0 returns the start frequency, in hertz, of the spectrum. df returns the frequency resolution, in hertz, of the spectrum. magnitude returns the power spectrum in a linear scale. |
|  | f0 returns the start frequency, in hertz, of the spectrum. |
|  | df returns the frequency resolution, in hertz, of the spectrum. |
|  | magnitude returns the power spectrum in a linear scale. |
|  | spectrum info returns information about the spectrum needed by the Spectrum Extended Measurements VIs, the Display VIs, and the Tone and Distortion VIs. Note You must wire spectrum info when performing extended measurements. Do not modify the spectrum info values. If you want to view the information, you can right-click the spectrum info wire and select Custom Probe»Spectrum Info from the shortcut menu. |
|  | averages completed returns the number of averages completed by the VI at that time. |

SVPO Single Block Power Spectrum (1 Ch DBL Array)

This VI is designed to operate on a single block of data, as opposed to being called repeatedly to operate on a continuous flow of data. Use this VI after performing a finite acquisition, or when you need to analyze a single block of data without previously analyzed blocks affecting the result. The results of this VI are only dependent on the inputs to the VI, and not on previous calls to this VI. Use this VI after performing a finite acquisition, or when you need to analyze a single block of data without previous blocks affecting the result. This VI does not store state internally, and can be called from a shared clone re-entrant VI.

[IMAGE alt='image' src='images/SVPO_Single_Block_Power_Spectrum_(1_Ch_DBL_Array).gif']

|  | scaled signal [EU] specifies the scaled signal expressed in the selected engineering units. You can use the SVPO Scale Voltage to EU VI to obtain a properly scaled signal. |
| --- | --- |
|  | sampling rate [Hz] specifies the sampling rate of the input signal in hertz. |
|  | window specifies the time-domain window to use. 0 None 1 Hanning (default) 2 Hamming 3 Blackman-Harris 4 Exact Blackman 5 Blackman 6 Flat Top 7 4 Term B-Harris 8 7 Term B-Harris 9 Low Sidelobe 10 Blackman Nuttall 11 Triangle 12 Bartlett-Hanning 13 Bohman 14 Parzen 15 Welch 16 Kaiser 17 Dolph-Chebyshev 18 Gaussian |
| 0 | None |
| 1 | Hanning (default) |
| 2 | Hamming |
| 3 | Blackman-Harris |
| 4 | Exact Blackman |
| 5 | Blackman |
| 6 | Flat Top |
| 7 | 4 Term B-Harris |
| 8 | 7 Term B-Harris |
| 9 | Low Sidelobe |
| 10 | Blackman Nuttall |
| 11 | Triangle |
| 12 | Bartlett-Hanning |
| 13 | Bohman |
| 14 | Parzen |
| 15 | Welch |
| 16 | Kaiser |
| 17 | Dolph-Chebyshev |
| 18 | Gaussian |
|  | power spectrum [EU^2 rms] returns the power spectrum. f0 returns the start frequency, in hertz, of the spectrum. df returns the frequency resolution, in hertz, of the spectrum. magnitude returns the power spectrum in a linear scale. |
|  | f0 returns the start frequency, in hertz, of the spectrum. |
|  | df returns the frequency resolution, in hertz, of the spectrum. |
|  | magnitude returns the power spectrum in a linear scale. |
|  | spectrum info returns information about the spectrum needed by the Spectrum Extended Measurements VIs, the Display VIs, and the Tone and Distortion VIs. Note You must wire spectrum info when performing extended measurements. Do not modify the spectrum info values. If you want to view the information, you can right-click the spectrum info wire and select Custom Probe»Spectrum Info from the shortcut menu. |

SVPO Single Block Power Spectrum (N Ch DBL Array)

This VI is designed to operate on a single block of data, as opposed to being called repeatedly to operate on a continuous flow of data. Use this VI after performing a finite acquisition, or when you need to analyze a single block of data without previously analyzed blocks affecting the result. The results of this VI are only dependent on the inputs to the VI, and not on previous calls to this VI. Use this VI after performing a finite acquisition, or when you need to analyze a single block of data without previous blocks affecting the result. This VI does not store state internally, and can be called from a shared clone re-entrant VI.

[IMAGE alt='image' src='images/SVPO_Single_Block_Power_Spectrum_(N_Ch_DBL_Array).gif']

| --- | --- |
|  | sampling rate [Hz] specifies the sampling rate of the input signal in hertz. |
|  | window specifies the time-domain window to use. 0 None 1 Hanning (default) 2 Hamming 3 Blackman-Harris 4 Exact Blackman 5 Blackman 6 Flat Top 7 4 Term B-Harris 8 7 Term B-Harris 9 Low Sidelobe 10 Blackman Nuttall 11 Triangle 12 Bartlett-Hanning 13 Bohman 14 Parzen 15 Welch 16 Kaiser 17 Dolph-Chebyshev 18 Gaussian |

|  | spectrum info returns information about the spectrum needed by the Spectrum Extended Measurements VIs, the Display VIs, and the Tone and Distortion VIs. Note You must wire spectrum info when performing extended measurements. Do not modify the spectrum info values. If you want to view the information, you can right-click the spectrum info wire and select Custom Probe»Spectrum Info from the shortcut menu. |

Y specifies the data values of the waveform. |

## TOPIC 00134: SVPO RMS Average Power Spectrum (Real) VI

- bundle_id: `labview-sound-and-vibration-toolkit-api-ref`
- source_path: `svpo-rms-average-power-spectrum-real-vi.html`
- source_url: https://docs-be.ni.com/bundle/labview-sound-and-vibration-toolkit-api-ref/raw/resource/enus/svpo-rms-average-power-spectrum-real-vi.html
- document_id: `labview-sound-and-vibration-toolkit-api-ref`
- page_type: `leaf`
- content_type: `reference`
- source_description: Owning Palette: Embedded Monitoring VIs Requires: Sound and Vibration Toolkit Computes linear RMS-averaged power spectrum of instantaneous input power spectrum. Use this VI when you require more flexibility to compute multiple/different averages while reusing the same implementation of the device un

### SVPO RMS Average Power Spectrum (Real) VI

**Owning Palette:**
 Embedded Monitoring VIs

**Requires:**
 Sound and Vibration Toolkit

Computes linear RMS-averaged power spectrum of instantaneous input power spectrum. Use this VI when you require more flexibility to compute multiple/different averages while reusing the same implementation of the device under test [DUT].

| SVPO RMS Average Power Spectrum (Real) (1 Ch)SVPO RMS Average Power Spectrum (Real) (N Ch)SVPO RMS Average Power Spectrum (Real) with IC |
| --- |

SVPO RMS Average Power Spectrum (Real) (1 Ch)

[IMAGE alt='image' src='images/SVPO_RMS_Average_Power_Spectrum_(Real)_(1_Ch).gif']

|  | exponential weight (10%) specifies the weight, in percent, the single-block power spectrum has in the calculated average when weighting mode is set to Exponential . The range of supported values is from 0 to 100. The default is 10%. |
| --- | --- |
|  | power spectrum [EU^2 rms] specifies the abscissa scaling and offset and the power spectrum in a linear scale [EU^2 rms]. f0 specifies the start frequency, in hertz, of the spectrum. df specifies the frequency resolution, in hertz, of the spectrum. magnitude specifies the magnitude of the spectrum in units of EU^2 rms. |
|  | f0 specifies the start frequency, in hertz, of the spectrum. |
|  | df specifies the frequency resolution, in hertz, of the spectrum. |
|  | magnitude specifies the magnitude of the spectrum in units of EU^2 rms. |
|  | averaging weighting mode specifies the weighting mode. The default is linear. Note RMS and vector averaging support linear and exponential weighting. 0 Linear (Default) 1 Exponential linear mode specifies the behavior of the averaging if you set the value of the weighting mode parameter to Linear . If you set weighting mode to Exponential , linear mode is ignored. 0 One shot (default)Specifies that the averaging process stops once the value of number of averages is reached. 1 Auto restart Specifies that the averaging process automatically restarts after the value of number of averages is reached. |
| 0 | Linear (Default) |
| 1 | Exponential |
|  | linear mode specifies the behavior of the averaging if you set the value of the weighting mode parameter to Linear . If you set weighting mode to Exponential , linear mode is ignored. 0 One shot (default)Specifies that the averaging process stops once the value of number of averages is reached. 1 Auto restart Specifies that the averaging process automatically restarts after the value of number of averages is reached. |
| 0 | One shot (default)Specifies that the averaging process stops once the value of number of averages is reached. |
| 1 | Auto restart Specifies that the averaging process automatically restarts after the value of number of averages is reached. |
|  | spectrum info in specifies properties of the computed spectrum. Note You must wire spectrum info in when performing extended measurements. Do not modify the spectrum info in values. If you want to view the information, you can create a custom probe. |
|  | restart averaging specifies if the selected averaging process has to be restarted. The default is FALSE. When you call this VI for the first time, the averaging process restarts automatically. |
|  | average power spectrum [EU^2 rms] returns the calculated average power spectrum. f0 returns the start frequency, in hertz, of the spectrum. df returns the frequency resolution, in hertz, of the spectrum. magnitude returns the magnitude of the spectrum. |
|  | f0 returns the start frequency, in hertz, of the spectrum. |
|  | df returns the frequency resolution, in hertz, of the spectrum. |
|  | magnitude returns the magnitude of the spectrum. |
|  | spectrum info out returns information about the spectrum needed by the Spectrum Extended Measurements VIs, the Display VIs, and the Tone and Distortion VIs. Note You must wire spectrum info out when performing extended measurements. Do not modify the spectrum info out values. If you want to view the information, you can create a custom probe. |
|  | averages completed out returns the number of averages completed by the VI at that time. |

SVPO RMS Average Power Spectrum (Real) (N Ch)

[IMAGE alt='image' src='images/SVPO_RMS_Average_Power_Spectrum_(Real)_(N_Ch).gif']

|  | exponential weight (10%) specifies the weight, in percent, the single-block power spectrum has in the calculated average when weighting mode is set to Exponential . The range of supported values is from 0 to 100. The default is 10%. |
| --- | --- |
|  | power spectra [EU^2 rms] specifies the power spectra for each channel. The power spectra [EU^2 rms] should be single-block power spectra with no averaging, number of averages, or restart averaging = TRUE. |
|  | averaging weighting mode specifies the weighting mode. The default is linear. Note RMS and vector averaging support linear and exponential weighting. 0 Linear (Default) 1 Exponential linear mode specifies the behavior of the averaging if you set the value of the weighting mode parameter to Linear . If you set weighting mode to Exponential , linear mode is ignored. 0 One shot (default)Specifies that the averaging process stops once the value of number of averages is reached. 1 Auto restart Specifies that the averaging process automatically restarts after the value of number of averages is reached. |
| 0 | Linear (Default) |
| 1 | Exponential |
|  | linear mode specifies the behavior of the averaging if you set the value of the weighting mode parameter to Linear . If you set weighting mode to Exponential , linear mode is ignored. 0 One shot (default)Specifies that the averaging process stops once the value of number of averages is reached. 1 Auto restart Specifies that the averaging process automatically restarts after the value of number of averages is reached. |
| 0 | One shot (default)Specifies that the averaging process stops once the value of number of averages is reached. |
| 1 | Auto restart Specifies that the averaging process automatically restarts after the value of number of averages is reached. |
|  | spectrum info in specifies properties of the computed spectrum. Note You must wire spectrum info in when performing extended measurements. Do not modify the spectrum info in values. If you want to view the information, you can create a custom probe. |
|  | restart averaging specifies if the selected averaging process has to be restarted. The default is FALSE. When you call this VI for the first time, the averaging process restarts automatically. |
|  | average power spectra [EU^2 rms] returns the power spectra average for each channel. |
|  | spectrum info out returns information about the spectrum needed by the Spectrum Extended Measurements VIs, the Display VIs, and the Tone and Distortion VIs. Note You must wire spectrum info out when performing extended measurements. Do not modify the spectrum info out values. If you want to view the information, you can create a custom probe. |
|  | averages completed out returns the number of averages completed by the VI at that time. |

SVPO RMS Average Power Spectrum (Real) with IC

This VI maintains no state information internally. Use this instance for advanced application development when state must be explicitly managed.

[IMAGE alt='image' src='images/SVPO_RMS_Average_Power_Spectrum_(Real)_with_IC.gif']

|  | exponential weight (10%) specifies the weight, in percent, the single-block power spectrum has in the calculated average when weighting mode is set to Exponential . The range of supported values is from 0 to 100. The default is 10%. |
| --- | --- |
|  | power spectrum [EU^2 rms] specifies the abscissa scaling and offset and the power spectrum in a linear scale [EU^2 rms]. f0 specifies the start frequency, in hertz, of the spectrum. df specifies the frequency resolution, in hertz, of the spectrum. magnitude specifies the magnitude of the spectrum in units of EU^2 rms. |
|  | f0 specifies the start frequency, in hertz, of the spectrum. |
|  | df specifies the frequency resolution, in hertz, of the spectrum. |
|  | magnitude specifies the magnitude of the spectrum in units of EU^2 rms. |
|  | averaging weighting mode specifies the weighting mode. The default is linear. Note RMS and vector averaging support linear and exponential weighting. 0 Linear (Default) 1 Exponential linear mode specifies the behavior of the averaging if you set the value of the weighting mode parameter to Linear . If you set weighting mode to Exponential , linear mode is ignored. 0 One shot (default)Specifies that the averaging process stops once the value of number of averages is reached. 1 Auto restart Specifies that the averaging process automatically restarts after the value of number of averages is reached. |
| 0 | Linear (Default) |
| 1 | Exponential |
|  | linear mode specifies the behavior of the averaging if you set the value of the weighting mode parameter to Linear . If you set weighting mode to Exponential , linear mode is ignored. 0 One shot (default)Specifies that the averaging process stops once the value of number of averages is reached. 1 Auto restart Specifies that the averaging process automatically restarts after the value of number of averages is reached. |
| 0 | One shot (default)Specifies that the averaging process stops once the value of number of averages is reached. |
| 1 | Auto restart Specifies that the averaging process automatically restarts after the value of number of averages is reached. |
|  | spectrum info in specifies information needed by the Spectrum Averaging Vis. |
|  | average power spectrum in specifies the previous average spectrum. Do not modify the average spectrum between calls to this VI, as this may produce invalid results. |
|  | averages completed in specifies the number of averages for average spectrum in . |
|  | restart averaging specifies if the selected averaging process has to be restarted. The default is FALSE. When you call this VI for the first time, the averaging process restarts automatically. |
|  | spectrum info out returns the properties of the average power spectrum out. Note You must wire spectrum info out when performing extended measurements. Do not modify the spectrum info out values. If you want to view the information, you can create a custom probe. |
|  | average power spectrum out returns the updated average power spectrum in units of EU^2 rms. |
|  | averages completed out returns the number of averages completed by the VI at that time. |

Parent topic:

Embedded Monitoring VIs

<!--NI_TOPIC bundle=labview-sound-and-vibration-toolkit-api-ref path=svpo-rms-level-vi.html language=enus -->
## TOPIC 00135: SVPO RMS Level VI

- bundle_id: `labview-sound-and-vibration-toolkit-api-ref`
- source_path: `svpo-rms-level-vi.html`
- source_url: https://docs-be.ni.com/bundle/labview-sound-and-vibration-toolkit-api-ref/raw/resource/enus/svpo-rms-level-vi.html
- document_id: `labview-sound-and-vibration-toolkit-api-ref`
- page_type: `leaf`
- content_type: `reference`
- source_description: Owning Palette: Embedded Monitoring VIs Requires: Sound and Vibration Toolkit Computes the root-mean-square (RMS) level of scaled time waveform signals and scaled array signals. scaled signal [EU] should have a zero DC component. You can attenuate the DC component with hardware AC-coupling filters a

### SVPO RMS Level VI

**Owning Palette:**
 Embedded Monitoring VIs

**Requires:**
 Sound and Vibration Toolkit

Computes the root-mean-square (RMS) level of scaled time waveform signals and scaled array signals. 
 **scaled signal [EU]**
 should have a zero DC component. You can attenuate the DC component with hardware AC-coupling filters and with software high-pass filters. Use this VI on embedded targets that have limited computational resources. Wire data to the 
 **scaled signal [EU]**
 input to determine the polymorphic instance to use or manually select the instance.

| SVPO RMS Level (1 Ch DBL Array)SVPO RMS Level (N Ch DBL Array)SVPO RMS Level (1 Ch DBL Array) with ICSVPO RMS Level (1 Ch DBL WDT)SVPO RMS Level (N Ch DBL WDT)SVPO RMS Level (1Ch DBL WDT) with IC |
| --- |

SVPO RMS Level (1 Ch DBL Array)

[IMAGE alt='image' src='images/SVPO_RMS_Levels_1_Ch_DBL_Array.gif']

|  | scaled signal [EU] specifies the signal for which you want to compute the RMS level. |
| --- | --- |
|  | sampling rate [Hz] specifies the sampling rate of the input signal in hertz. |
|  | restart averaging? specifies whether to restart the RMS averaging process. When you call this VI for the first time, the averaging process restarts automatically. The default is FALSE, which means this VI returns RMS level and duration [s] since the last reset. Set restart averaging? to FALSE if you want to measure the equivalent continuous level over multiple iterations. Set restart averaging? to TRUE to process the latest block of data continuously. |
|  | RMS level returns the RMS level of the input signal. |
|  | duration [s] returns the time duration since the RMS level measurement starts or since you restart the averaging. |

SVPO RMS Level (N Ch DBL Array)

[IMAGE alt='image' src='images/SVPO_RMS_Levels_N_Ch_DBL_Array.gif']

|  | scaled signals [EU] specifies the signals for which you want to compute the RMS level. |
| --- | --- |
|  | sampling rate [Hz] specifies the sampling rate of the input signal in hertz. |
|  | restart averaging? specifies whether to restart the RMS averaging process. When you call this VI for the first time, the averaging process restarts automatically. The default is FALSE, which means this VI returns RMS level and duration [s] since the last reset. Set restart averaging? to FALSE if you want to measure the equivalent continuous level over multiple iterations. Set restart averaging? to TRUE to process the latest block of data continuously. |
|  | RMS levels returns the RMS levels of the input signals. |
|  | durations [s] returns, for each channel, the time duration since the RMS level measurement starts or since you restart the averaging. |

SVPO RMS Level (1 Ch DBL Array) with IC

This VI does not store state internally. Instead, it accepts 
 **initial conditions**
 as an input and outputs 
 **final conditions**
 . Operate on samples continuously by ensuring the 
 **final conditions**
 output is wired to the 
 **initial conditions**
 input on the next call to this VI.

Use this VI when you need to manage the state at the calling VI's level, or when the calling VI is set to shared-clone re-entrant. Because this VI does not store state internally, and is set to shared clone re-entrant, it is safe to call from Vis that are also set to shared clone re-entrant.

[IMAGE alt='image' src='images/SVPO_RMS_Level_(1_Ch_DBL_Array)_with_IC.gif']

|  | scaled signal [EU] specifies the signal for which you want to compute the RMS level. |
| --- | --- |
|  | sampling rate [Hz] specifies the sampling rate of the input signal in hertz. |
|  | initial conditions specifies the initial internal state of this VI. This input should come from the previous output of final conditions from the last call to this VI in order to operate on samples continuously. |
|  | restart averaging? specifies whether to restart the RMS averaging process. The default is FALSE, which means this VI returns RMS level and duration [s] since the last reset. Set restart averaging? to FALSE if you want to measure the equivalent continuous level over multiple iterations. Set restart averaging? to TRUE to process the latest block of data continuously. |
|  | RMS level returns the RMS level of the input signal. |
| final conditions returns the final internal state of this VI. This output should be passed to the initial conditions input on the next call to this VI in order to operate on samples continuously. |  |
|  | duration [s] returns the time duration since the RMS level measurement starts or since you restart the averaging. |

SVPO RMS Level (1 Ch DBL WDT)

[IMAGE alt='image' src='images/SVPO_RMS_Levels_1_Ch_DBL_WDT.gif']

|  | scaled signal [EU] specifies the signal for which you want to compute the RMS level. t0 specifies the start time of the waveform. dt specifies the time interval in seconds between data points in the waveform. Y specifies the data values of the waveform. |
| --- | --- |
|  | t0 specifies the start time of the waveform. |
|  | dt specifies the time interval in seconds between data points in the waveform. |
|  | Y specifies the data values of the waveform. |
|  | restart averaging? specifies whether to restart the RMS averaging process. When you call this VI for the first time, the averaging process restarts automatically. The default is FALSE, which means this VI returns RMS level and duration [s] since the last reset. Set restart averaging? to FALSE if you want to measure the equivalent continuous level over multiple iterations. Set restart averaging? to TRUE to process the latest block of data continuously. |
|  | timestamp returns the start time of the RMS level measurement or the time when you set restart averaging? to TRUE. |
|  | RMS level returns the RMS level of the input signal. |
|  | duration [s] returns the time duration since the RMS level measurement starts or since you restart the averaging. |

SVPO RMS Level (N Ch DBL WDT)

[IMAGE alt='image' src='images/SVPO_RMS_Levels_N_Ch_DBL_WDT.gif']

|  | scaled signals [EU] specifies the signals for which you want to compute the RMS level. t0 specifies the start time of the waveform. dt specifies the time interval in seconds between data points in the waveform. Y specifies the data values of the waveform. |
| --- | --- |
|  | t0 specifies the start time of the waveform. |
|  | dt specifies the time interval in seconds between data points in the waveform. |
|  | Y specifies the data values of the waveform. |
|  | restart averaging? specifies whether to restart the RMS averaging process. When you call this VI for the first time, the averaging process restarts automatically. The default is FALSE, which means this VI returns RMS level and duration [s] since the last reset. Set restart averaging? to FALSE if you want to measure the equivalent continuous level over multiple iterations. Set restart averaging? to TRUE to process the latest block of data continuously. |
|  | timestamps returns the start time of the RMS level measurement or the time when you set restart averaging? to TRUE. |
|  | RMS levels returns the RMS levels of the input signals. |
|  | durations [s] returns, for each channel, the time duration since the RMS level measurement starts or since you restart the averaging. |

SVPO RMS Level (1Ch DBL WDT) with IC

This VI does not store state internally. Instead, it accepts 
 **initial conditions**
 as an input and outputs 
 **final conditions**
 . Operate on samples continuously by ensuring the 
 **final conditions**
 output is wired to the 
 **initial conditions**
 input on the next call to this VI.

Use this VI when you need to manage the state at the calling VI's level, or when the calling VI is set to shared-clone re-entrant. Because this VI does not store state internally, and is set to shared clone re-entrant, it is safe to call from Vis that are also set to shared clone re-entrant.

[IMAGE alt='image' src='images/SVPO_RMS_Level_(1_Ch_DBL_WDT)_with_IC.gif']

|  | scaled signal [EU] specifies the signal for which you want to compute the RMS level. t0 specifies the start time of the waveform. dt specifies the time interval in seconds between data points in the waveform. Y specifies the data values of the waveform. |
| --- | --- |
|  | t0 specifies the start time of the waveform. |
|  | dt specifies the time interval in seconds between data points in the waveform. |
|  | Y specifies the data values of the waveform. |
|  | initial conditions specifies the initial internal state of this VI. This input should come from the previous output of final conditions from the last call to this VI in order to operate on samples continuously. |
|  | restart averaging? specifies whether to restart the RMS averaging process. The default is FALSE, which means this VI returns RMS level and duration [s] since the last reset. Set restart averaging? to FALSE if you want to measure the equivalent continuous level over multiple iterations. Set restart averaging? to TRUE to process the latest block of data continuously. |
|  | timestamp returns the start time of the RMS level measurement or the time when you set restart averaging? to TRUE. |
|  | RMS level returns the RMS level of the input signal. |
| final conditions returns the final internal state of this VI. This output should be passed to the initial conditions input on the next call to this VI in order to operate on samples continuously. |  |
|  | duration [s] returns the time duration since the RMS level measurement starts or since you restart the averaging. |

<!--NI_TOPIC bundle=labview-sound-and-vibration-toolkit-api-ref path=svpo-scale-voltage-to-eu-vi.html language=enus -->
## TOPIC 00136: SVPO Scale Voltage to EU VI

- bundle_id: `labview-sound-and-vibration-toolkit-api-ref`
- source_path: `svpo-scale-voltage-to-eu-vi.html`
- source_url: https://docs-be.ni.com/bundle/labview-sound-and-vibration-toolkit-api-ref/raw/resource/enus/svpo-scale-voltage-to-eu-vi.html
- document_id: `labview-sound-and-vibration-toolkit-api-ref`
- page_type: `leaf`
- content_type: `reference`
- source_description: Owning Palette: Embedded Monitoring VIs Requires: Sound and Vibration Toolkit Scales the single-precision signal [V] from volts to the engineering units (EU) you select. Scale signals to appropriate EU before applying limits, saving to disk, and performing measurement analyses. Use this VI on embedd

### SVPO Scale Voltage to EU VI

**Owning Palette:**
 Embedded Monitoring VIs

**Requires:**
 Sound and Vibration Toolkit

Scales the single-precision 
 **signal [V]**
 from volts to the engineering units (EU) you select. Scale signals to appropriate EU before applying limits, saving to disk, and performing measurement analyses. Use this VI on embedded targets that have limited computational resources. Wire data to the 
 **signal [V]**
 input to determine the polymorphic instance to use or manually select the instance.

| SVPO Scale Voltage to EU (1 Ch SGL Array)SVPO Scale Voltage to EU (N Ch - 1 Info SGL Array)SVPO Scale Voltage to EU (N Ch - N Info SGL Array)SVPO Scale Voltage to EU (1 Ch SGL WDT)SVPO Scale Voltage to EU (N Ch - 1 Info SGL WDT)SVPO Scale Voltage to EU (N Ch - N Info SGL WDT) |
| --- |

SVPO Scale Voltage to EU (1 Ch SGL Array)

[IMAGE alt='image' src='images/SVPO_Scale_Voltage_to_EU_1_Ch_SGL_Array.gif']

|  | signal [V] specifies the single-precision signal to scale in volts. |
| --- | --- |
|  | channel info specifies information about the measurement system that you used before the signal reaches the DAQ device. sensor sensitivity [mV/EU] specifies the sensitivity of the sensor in mV/EU. The default is 1000.00. engineering units specifies the engineering units. The default is V. If you are using a microphone, select Pa. If you are using an accelerometer, select g, m/s^2, or in/s^2. If the units you need are not listed, select custom and enter the unit to use in custom label . 0 custom 1 V (default) 2 Pa 3 g 4 m/s^2 5 m/s 6 m 7 in/s^2 8 in/s 9 in 10 mil/s 11 mil 12 mm/s 13 mm 14 micron/s 15 micron 16 rad/s^2 17 rad/s 18 rad 19 strain 20 N 21 A 22 RPM 23 cycle/s^2 24 Hz 25 cycle 26 deg/s^2 27 deg/s 28 deg 30 C 31 pC 32 s dB reference [EU] specifies the reference value to use when you compute the results in decibels and when you scale a signal to engineering units. dB reference [EU] uses the unit that you specify in engineering units . The default is 1. When engineering units is V, use a dB reference [EU] of 1. When engineering units is m, use a dB reference [EU] of 0.7746. When engineering units is Pa, use a dB reference [EU] of 20E–6. custom label specifies the string used for units when engineering units is set to custom. If engineering units is not set to custom, this parameter is ignored. The default is EU. weighting filter specifies if any weighting filter has been applied to the signal. This control does not apply any weighting to the signal. weighting filter must always return linear for calibration measurements. The default is linear . Use weighting filter only if the acquired signal has been weighted by an external device before it is acquired by a DAQ device. If a weighting filter was applied to the signal before reaching the DAQ device, using weighting filter prevents subsequent VIs from applying any additional weighting to the weighted signal. pregain [dB] specifies the pregain applied by an external amplifier expressed in decibels. A value of 0 dB specifies no pregain. The default is 0.00 dB. Use pregain [dB] only if an external device amplifies the signal before a DAQ device acquires the signal. If you apply any gain with the DAQ device itself, the driver software takes this into account and you do not need to enter the information. |
|  | sensor sensitivity [mV/EU] specifies the sensitivity of the sensor in mV/EU. The default is 1000.00. |
|  | engineering units specifies the engineering units. The default is V. If you are using a microphone, select Pa. If you are using an accelerometer, select g, m/s^2, or in/s^2. If the units you need are not listed, select custom and enter the unit to use in custom label . 0 custom 1 V (default) 2 Pa 3 g 4 m/s^2 5 m/s 6 m 7 in/s^2 8 in/s 9 in 10 mil/s 11 mil 12 mm/s 13 mm 14 micron/s 15 micron 16 rad/s^2 17 rad/s 18 rad 19 strain 20 N 21 A 22 RPM 23 cycle/s^2 24 Hz 25 cycle 26 deg/s^2 27 deg/s 28 deg 30 C 31 pC 32 s |
| 0 | custom |
| 1 | V (default) |
| 2 | Pa |
| 3 | g |
| 4 | m/s^2 |
| 5 | m/s |
| 6 | m |
| 7 | in/s^2 |
| 8 | in/s |
| 9 | in |
| 10 | mil/s |
| 11 | mil |
| 12 | mm/s |
| 13 | mm |
| 14 | micron/s |
| 15 | micron |
| 16 | rad/s^2 |
| 17 | rad/s |
| 18 | rad |
| 19 | strain |
| 20 | N |
| 21 | A |
| 22 | RPM |
| 23 | cycle/s^2 |
| 24 | Hz |
| 25 | cycle |
| 26 | deg/s^2 |
| 27 | deg/s |
| 28 | deg |
| 30 | C |
| 31 | pC |
| 32 | s |
|  | dB reference [EU] specifies the reference value to use when you compute the results in decibels and when you scale a signal to engineering units. dB reference [EU] uses the unit that you specify in engineering units . The default is 1. When engineering units is V, use a dB reference [EU] of 1. When engineering units is m, use a dB reference [EU] of 0.7746. When engineering units is Pa, use a dB reference [EU] of 20E–6. |
|  | custom label specifies the string used for units when engineering units is set to custom. If engineering units is not set to custom, this parameter is ignored. The default is EU. |
|  | weighting filter specifies if any weighting filter has been applied to the signal. This control does not apply any weighting to the signal. weighting filter must always return linear for calibration measurements. The default is linear . Use weighting filter only if the acquired signal has been weighted by an external device before it is acquired by a DAQ device. If a weighting filter was applied to the signal before reaching the DAQ device, using weighting filter prevents subsequent VIs from applying any additional weighting to the weighted signal. |
|  | pregain [dB] specifies the pregain applied by an external amplifier expressed in decibels. A value of 0 dB specifies no pregain. The default is 0.00 dB. Use pregain [dB] only if an external device amplifies the signal before a DAQ device acquires the signal. If you apply any gain with the DAQ device itself, the driver software takes this into account and you do not need to enter the information. |
|  | scaled signal [EU] returns the scaled signal expressed in the selected engineering units . scaled signal [EU] also contains channel info as an attribute of the waveform. |
|  | unit label returns the unit label of scaled signal [EU] . |

SVPO Scale Voltage to EU (N Ch - 1 Info SGL Array)

[IMAGE alt='image' src='images/SVPO_Scale_Voltage_to_EU_N_Ch_1_Info_SGL_Array.gif']

| --- | --- |
|  | channel info specifies information about the measurement system that you used before the signal reaches the DAQ device. sensor sensitivity [mV/EU] specifies the sensitivity of the sensor in mV/EU. The default is 1. When engineering units is V, use a dB reference [EU] of 1. When engineering units is m, use a dB reference [EU] of 0.7746. This control does not apply any weighting to the signal. weighting filter must always return linear for calibration measurements. The default is linear . Use weighting filter only if the acquired signal has been weighted by an external device before it is acquired by a DAQ device. If you apply any gain with the DAQ device itself, the driver software takes this into account and you do not need to enter the information. |

|  | dB reference [EU] specifies the reference value to use when you compute the results in decibels and when you scale a signal to engineering units. dB reference [EU] uses the unit that you specify in engineering units . If you apply any gain with the DAQ device itself, the driver software takes this into account and you do not need to enter the information. |
|  | scaled signals [EU] returns the scaled signals expressed in the selected engineering units . scaled signals [EU] also contains channel info as an attribute of the waveform. |
|  | unit labels returns the unit labels of scaled signals [EU] . |

SVPO Scale Voltage to EU (1 Ch SGL WDT)

[IMAGE alt='image' src='images/SVPO_Scale_Voltage_to_EU_1_Ch_SGL_WDT.gif']

|  | write waveform attributes? specifies whether to write channel info into the waveform attributes of scaled signal [EU] . The default is TRUE. You can set write waveform attributes? to TRUE the first time this VI runs, and then set it to FALSE to speed up the application. |
| --- | --- |
|  | signal [V] specifies the single-precision signal to be scaled, in volts. t0 specifies the start time of the waveform. dt specifies the time interval in seconds between data points in the waveform. Y specifies the data values of the waveform. |
|  | t0 specifies the start time of the waveform. |
|  | dt specifies the time interval in seconds between data points in the waveform. |
|  | Y specifies the data values of the waveform. |
|  | channel info specifies information about the measurement system that you used before the signal reaches the DAQ device. sensor sensitivity [mV/EU] specifies the sensitivity of the sensor in mV/EU. The default is 1000.00. engineering units specifies the engineering units. The default is V. If you are using a microphone, select Pa. If you are using an accelerometer, select g, m/s^2, or in/s^2. If the units you need are not listed, select custom and enter the unit to use in custom label . 0 custom 1 V (default) 2 Pa 3 g 4 m/s^2 5 m/s 6 m 7 in/s^2 8 in/s 9 in 10 mil/s 11 mil 12 mm/s 13 mm 14 micron/s 15 micron 16 rad/s^2 17 rad/s 18 rad 19 strain 20 N 21 A 22 RPM 23 cycle/s^2 24 Hz 25 cycle 26 deg/s^2 27 deg/s 28 deg 30 C 31 pC 32 s dB reference [EU] specifies the reference value to use when you compute the results in decibels and when you scale a signal to engineering units. dB reference [EU] uses the unit that you specify in engineering units . The default is 1. When engineering units is V, use a dB reference [EU] of 1. When engineering units is m, use a dB reference [EU] of 0.7746. When engineering units is Pa, use a dB reference [EU] of 20E–6. custom label specifies the string used for units when engineering units is set to custom. If engineering units is not set to custom, this parameter is ignored. The default is EU. weighting filter specifies if any weighting filter has been applied to the signal. This control does not apply any weighting to the signal. weighting filter must always return linear for calibration measurements. The default is linear . Use weighting filter only if the acquired signal has been weighted by an external device before it is acquired by a DAQ device. If a weighting filter was applied to the signal before reaching the DAQ device, using weighting filter prevents subsequent VIs from applying any additional weighting to the weighted signal. pregain [dB] specifies the pregain applied by an external amplifier expressed in decibels. A value of 0 dB specifies no pregain. The default is 0.00 dB. Use pregain [dB] only if an external device amplifies the signal before a DAQ device acquires the signal. If you apply any gain with the DAQ device itself, the driver software takes this into account and you do not need to enter the information. |

|  | engineering units specifies the engineering units. The default is V. If you are using a microphone, select Pa. If you are using an accelerometer, select g, m/s^2, or in/s^2.
