<!--NI_TOPIC bundle=labview-sound-and-vibration-toolkit-api-ref path=svpo-tdms-write-vi.html language=enus -->
## TOPIC 00137: SVPO TDMS Write VI

- bundle_id: `labview-sound-and-vibration-toolkit-api-ref`
- source_path: `svpo-tdms-write-vi.html`
- source_url: https://docs-be.ni.com/bundle/labview-sound-and-vibration-toolkit-api-ref/raw/resource/enus/svpo-tdms-write-vi.html
- document_id: `labview-sound-and-vibration-toolkit-api-ref`
- page_type: `leaf`
- content_type: `reference`
- source_description: Owning Palette: Embedded Monitoring VIs Requires: Sound and Vibration Toolkit Writes single-precision waveform or scaled array data to the specified .tdms file in a format specific to the polymorphic instance. Use this VI on embedded targets that have limited computational resources. Wire data to th

### SVPO TDMS Write VI

**Owning Palette:**
 Embedded Monitoring VIs

**Requires:**
 Sound and Vibration Toolkit

Writes single-precision waveform or scaled array data to the specified .tdms file in a format specific to the polymorphic instance. Use this VI on embedded targets that have limited computational resources. Wire data to the 
 **scaled signal [EU]**
 input to determine the polymorphic instance to use or manually select the instance.

| SVPO TDMS Write SGL Waveform (1 Ch)SVPO TDMS Write SGL Waveform (N Ch)SVPO TDMS Write SGL Array (1 Ch)SVPO TDMS Write SGL Array (N Ch 1 Info)SVPO TDMS Write SGL Array (N Ch N Info) |
| --- |

SVPO TDMS Write SGL Waveform (1 Ch)

[IMAGE alt='image' src='images/SVPO_TDMS_Write_SGL_Waveform_1_Ch.gif']

|  | write channel properties? specifies whether to write the channel properties when the VI runs. You can set this input to TRUE the first time the VI runs, and thereafter set it to FALSE to speed up the VI. The default is TRUE. |
| --- | --- |
|  | tdms file specifies a reference number to the .tdms file on which to perform the operation. Use the TDMS Open function to open the refnum. |
|  | group name in specifies the channel group on which to perform the operation. |
|  | channel name in specifies the channel to perform the operation on. |
|  | scaled signal [EU] specifies the single-precision scaled signal expressed in the selected engineering units. You can use the SVPO Scale Voltage to EU VI to obtain a properly scaled signal. t0 specifies the start time of the waveform. dt specifies the time interval in seconds between data points in the waveform. Y specifies the data values of the waveform. |
|  | t0 specifies the start time of the waveform. |
|  | dt specifies the time interval in seconds between data points in the waveform. |
|  | Y specifies the data values of the waveform. |
|  | tdms file out returns a TDMS file reference to the .tdms file on which you performed the operation. |
|  | group name out returns the name of the channel group on which you performed the operation. |
|  | channel name out returns the name of the channel on which you performed the operation. |

SVPO TDMS Write SGL Waveform (N Ch)

[IMAGE alt='image' src='images/SVPO_TDMS_Write_SGL_Waveform_N_Ch.gif']

|  | write channel properties? specifies whether to write the channel properties when the VI runs. You can set this input to TRUE the first time the VI runs, and thereafter set it to FALSE to speed up the VI. The default is TRUE. |
| --- | --- |
|  | tdms file specifies a reference number to the .tdms file on which to perform the operation. Use the TDMS Open function to open the refnum. |
|  | group name in specifies the channel group on which to perform the operation. |
|  | channel names in specifies the channels to perform the operation on. |
|  | scaled signals [EU] specifies the single-precision scaled signals expressed in the selected engineering units. You can use the SVPO Scale Voltage to EU VI to obtain a properly scaled signal. t0 specifies the start time of the waveform. dt specifies the time interval in seconds between data points in the waveform. Y specifies the data values of the waveform. |
|  | t0 specifies the start time of the waveform. |
|  | dt specifies the time interval in seconds between data points in the waveform. |
|  | Y specifies the data values of the waveform. |
|  | tdms file out returns a TDMS file reference to the .tdms file on which you performed the operation. |
|  | group name out returns the name of the channel group on which you performed the operation. |
|  | channel names out returns the names of the channels you performed the operation on. |

[IMAGE alt='image' src='images/SVPO_TDMS_Write_SGL_Array_1_Ch.gif']

|  | write channel properties? specifies whether to write the channel properties when the VI runs. You can set this input to TRUE the first time the VI runs, and thereafter set it to FALSE to speed up the VI. The default is TRUE. |
| --- | --- |
|  | tdms file specifies a reference number to the .tdms file on which to perform the operation. Use the TDMS Open function to open the refnum. |
|  | group name in specifies the channel group on which to perform the operation. |
|  | channel name in specifies the channel to perform the operation on. |
|  | scaled signal [EU] specifies the single-precision scaled signal expressed in the selected engineering units. You can use the SVPO Scale Voltage to EU VI to obtain a properly scaled signal. |
|  | sampling rate [Hz] specifies the sampling rate of the input signal in hertz. |
|  | unit label specifies the unit label of scaled signal [EU] . |
|  | tdms file out returns a TDMS file reference to the .tdms file on which you performed the operation. |
|  | group name out returns the name of the channel group on which you performed the operation. |

<!--NI_TOPIC bundle=labview-sound-and-vibration-toolkit-api-ref path=svt-a-b-c-weighting-filter-fixed-rates-vi.html language=enus -->
## TOPIC 00138: SVT A, B, C Weighting Filter (Fixed Rates) VI

- bundle_id: `labview-sound-and-vibration-toolkit-api-ref`
- source_path: `svt-a-b-c-weighting-filter-fixed-rates-vi.html`
- source_url: https://docs-be.ni.com/bundle/labview-sound-and-vibration-toolkit-api-ref/raw/resource/enus/svt-a-b-c-weighting-filter-fixed-rates-vi.html
- document_id: `labview-sound-and-vibration-toolkit-api-ref`
- page_type: `leaf`
- content_type: `reference`
- source_description: Owning Palette: Weighting and Filtering VIs Requires: Sound and Vibration Toolkit Applies the selected A-, B-, and C- weighting to scaled signal [EU] . This VI complies with ANSI S1.4-2014 and IEC 61672-1:2013 standards. Each instance of this VI maintains its own state; use the N Waveforms instance

### SVT A, B, C Weighting Filter (Fixed Rates) VI

**Owning Palette:**
 Weighting and Filtering VIs

**Requires:**
 Sound and Vibration Toolkit

Applies the selected A-, B-, and C- weighting to 
 **scaled signal [EU]**
 . This VI complies with ANSI S1.4-2014 and IEC 61672-1:2013 standards. Each instance of this VI maintains its own state; use the N Waveforms instance of this VI to process an array of channels. This VI operates on time-continuous data; it resets on a discontinuity. Wire data to the 
 **scaled signal [EU]**
 input to determine the polymorphic instance to use or manually select the instance.

| SVT A, B, C Weighting Filter (Fixed Rates) (1 Waveform)SVT A, B, C Weighting Filter (Fixed Rates) (N Waveforms) |
| --- |

SVT A, B, C Weighting Filter (Fixed Rates) (1 Waveform)

[IMAGE alt='image' src='images/SVT_ABC_Weighting_Filter_FixedRates_1_Ch.gif']

|  | scaled signal [EU] specifies the scaled signal expressed in the selected engineering units. To obtain a properly scaled signal, you can use the SVL Scale Voltage to EU VI or define a virtual channel in National Instruments Measurement & Automation Explorer (MAX). t0 specifies the start time of the waveform. dt specifies the time interval in seconds between data points in the waveform. Y specifies the data values of the waveform. |
| --- | --- |
|  | t0 specifies the start time of the waveform. |
|  | dt specifies the time interval in seconds between data points in the waveform. |
|  | Y specifies the data values of the waveform. |
|  | weighting specifies the type of weighting to apply to the spectrum. The weighting is applied to the spectrum in the frequency domain. Use Linear if no weighting is needed. 0 Linear 1 A-weighting 2 B-weighting B-weighting 3 C-weighting |
| 0 | Linear |
| 1 | A-weighting |
| 2 | B-weighting B-weighting |
| 3 | C-weighting |
|  | error in describes error conditions that occur before this node runs. This input provides standard error in functionality. |
|  | reset filter specifies whether this VI resets the weighting filter. The default is FALSE. The first time you call this VI, the filter restarts automatically. |
|  | weighted signal [EU] returns the weighted signal based on the signal and weighting input specifications. t0 returns the start time of the waveform. dt returns the time interval, in seconds, between data points in the waveform. Y returns the data values of the waveform. |
|  | t0 returns the start time of the waveform. |
|  | dt returns the time interval, in seconds, between data points in the waveform. |
|  | Y returns the data values of the waveform. |
|  | error out contains error information. This output provides standard error out functionality. |

## TOPIC 00139: SVT Audio Filter VI

- bundle_id: `labview-sound-and-vibration-toolkit-api-ref`
- source_path: `svt-audio-filter-vi.html`
- source_url: https://docs-be.ni.com/bundle/labview-sound-and-vibration-toolkit-api-ref/raw/resource/enus/svt-audio-filter-vi.html
- document_id: `labview-sound-and-vibration-toolkit-api-ref`
- page_type: `leaf`
- content_type: `reference`
- source_description: Owning Palette: Weighting and Filtering VIs Requires: Sound and Vibration Toolkit Applies the selected audio weighting filter to the scaled signal [EU] . Wire data to the scaled signal [EU] input to determine the polymorphic instance to use or manually select the instance. The SVT Audio Filter VI ad

### SVT Audio Filter VI

**Owning Palette:**
 Weighting and Filtering VIs

**Requires:**
 Sound and Vibration Toolkit

Applies the selected audio weighting filter to the 
 **scaled signal [EU]**
 . Wire data to the 
 **scaled signal [EU]**
 input to determine the polymorphic instance to use or manually select the instance.

The SVT Audio Filter VI adheres to the AES17-1998 standards.

| SVT Audio Filter (1 Ch)SVT Audio Filter (N Ch) |
| --- |

SVT Audio Filter (1 Ch)

[IMAGE alt='image' src='images/SVT_Audio_Filter_1_Ch.gif']

|  | scaled signal [EU] specifies the scaled signal expressed in the selected engineering units. To obtain a properly scaled signal, you can use the SVL Scale Voltage to EU VI or define a virtual channel in National Instruments Measurement & Automation Explorer (MAX). t0 specifies the start time of the waveform. dt specifies the time interval in seconds between data points in the waveform. Y specifies the data values of the waveform. |
| --- | --- |
|  | t0 specifies the start time of the waveform. |
|  | dt specifies the time interval in seconds between data points in the waveform. |
|  | Y specifies the data values of the waveform. |
|  | filter type specifies the type of filter to apply to the input time-domain signal. Use Linear if you do not need any weighting. The default is Linear. 0 Linear (default)—Provides no filtering processes for the input signal. 1 AES17 20 kHz Lowpass —Specifies a passband response deviation of no more than ± 0.1 dB for frequencies 10 Hz to 20 kHz and a stopband attenuation of at least 60 dB for frequencies above 24 kHz. 2 AES17 40 kHz Lowpass —Specifies a passband response deviation of no more than ± 0.1 dB for frequencies 10 Hz to 40 kHz and a stopband attenuation of at least 60 dB for frequencies above 48 kHz. 3 AES17 20 kHz Highpass —Specifies a passband response deviation of no more than ± 0.5 dB for frequencies 26 kHz to 200 kHz and a stopband attenuation of at least 40 dB for frequencies from 10 Hz to 20 kHz. 4 AES17 40 kHz Highpass —Specifies a passband response deviation of no more than ± 0.5 dB for frequencies 52 kHz to 200 kHz and a stopband attenuation of at least 40 dB for frequencies from 10 Hz to 40 kHz. |
| 0 | Linear (default)—Provides no filtering processes for the input signal. |
| 1 | AES17 20 kHz Lowpass —Specifies a passband response deviation of no more than ± 0.1 dB for frequencies 10 Hz to 20 kHz and a stopband attenuation of at least 60 dB for frequencies above 24 kHz. |
| 2 | AES17 40 kHz Lowpass —Specifies a passband response deviation of no more than ± 0.1 dB for frequencies 10 Hz to 40 kHz and a stopband attenuation of at least 60 dB for frequencies above 48 kHz. |
| 3 | AES17 20 kHz Highpass —Specifies a passband response deviation of no more than ± 0.5 dB for frequencies 26 kHz to 200 kHz and a stopband attenuation of at least 40 dB for frequencies from 10 Hz to 20 kHz. |
| 4 | AES17 40 kHz Highpass —Specifies a passband response deviation of no more than ± 0.5 dB for frequencies 52 kHz to 200 kHz and a stopband attenuation of at least 40 dB for frequencies from 10 Hz to 40 kHz. |
|  | error in describes error conditions that occur before this node runs. This input provides standard error in functionality. |
|  | reset filter specifies whether this VI resets the weighting filter. The default is FALSE. The first time you call this VI, the filter restarts automatically. |
|  | filtered signal [EU] returns the filtered signal. t0 returns the start time of the waveform. dt returns the time interval, in seconds, between data points in the waveform. Y returns the data values of the waveform. |
|  | t0 returns the start time of the waveform. |
|  | dt returns the time interval, in seconds, between data points in the waveform. |
|  | Y returns the data values of the waveform. |
|  | magnitude response returns the magnitude of the filtered signal. f0 returns the start frequency, in hertz, of the filtered signal. df returns the frequency resolution, in hertz, of the filtered signal. Mag returns the magnitude of the filtered signal. |
|  | f0 returns the start frequency, in hertz, of the filtered signal. |
|  | df returns the frequency resolution, in hertz, of the filtered signal. |
|  | Mag returns the magnitude of the filtered signal. |
|  | phase response returns the phase of the filtered signal. f0 returns the start frequency, in hertz, of the filtered signal. df returns the frequency resolution, in hertz, of the filtered signal. Phase returns the phase of the filtered signal. |
|  | f0 returns the start frequency, in hertz, of the filtered signal. |
|  | df returns the frequency resolution, in hertz, of the filtered signal. |
|  | Phase returns the phase of the filtered signal. |
|  | error out contains error information. This output provides standard error out functionality. |

SVT Audio Filter (N Ch)

[IMAGE alt='image' src='images/SVT_Audio_Filter_N_Ch.gif']

|  | scaled signals [EU] specifies the scaled signals expressed in the selected engineering units. To obtain a properly scaled signal, you can use the SVL Scale Voltage to EU VI or define a virtual channel in National Instruments Measurement & Automation Explorer (MAX). t0 specifies the start time of the waveform. dt specifies the time interval in seconds between data points in the waveform. Y specifies the data values of the waveform. |
| --- | --- |
|  | t0 specifies the start time of the waveform. |
|  | dt specifies the time interval in seconds between data points in the waveform. |
|  | Y specifies the data values of the waveform. |
|  | filter type specifies the type of filter to apply to the input time-domain signal. Use Linear if you do not need any weighting. The default is Linear. 0 Linear (default)—Provides no filtering processes for the input signal. 1 AES17 20 kHz Lowpass —Specifies a passband response deviation of no more than ± 0.1 dB for frequencies 10 Hz to 20 kHz and a stopband attenuation of at least 60 dB for frequencies above 24 kHz. 2 AES17 40 kHz Lowpass —Specifies a passband response deviation of no more than ± 0.1 dB for frequencies 10 Hz to 40 kHz and a stopband attenuation of at least 60 dB for frequencies above 48 kHz. 3 AES17 20 kHz Highpass —Specifies a passband response deviation of no more than ± 0.5 dB for frequencies 26 kHz to 200 kHz and a stopband attenuation of at least 40 dB for frequencies from 10 Hz to 20 kHz. 4 AES17 40 kHz Highpass —Specifies a passband response deviation of no more than ± 0.5 dB for frequencies 52 kHz to 200 kHz and a stopband attenuation of at least 40 dB for frequencies from 10 Hz to 40 kHz. |
| 0 | Linear (default)—Provides no filtering processes for the input signal. |
| 1 | AES17 20 kHz Lowpass —Specifies a passband response deviation of no more than ± 0.1 dB for frequencies 10 Hz to 20 kHz and a stopband attenuation of at least 60 dB for frequencies above 24 kHz. |
| 2 | AES17 40 kHz Lowpass —Specifies a passband response deviation of no more than ± 0.1 dB for frequencies 10 Hz to 40 kHz and a stopband attenuation of at least 60 dB for frequencies above 48 kHz. |
| 3 | AES17 20 kHz Highpass —Specifies a passband response deviation of no more than ± 0.5 dB for frequencies 26 kHz to 200 kHz and a stopband attenuation of at least 40 dB for frequencies from 10 Hz to 20 kHz. |
| 4 | AES17 40 kHz Highpass —Specifies a passband response deviation of no more than ± 0.5 dB for frequencies 52 kHz to 200 kHz and a stopband attenuation of at least 40 dB for frequencies from 10 Hz to 40 kHz. |
|  | error in describes error conditions that occur before this node runs. This input provides standard error in functionality. |
|  | reset filter specifies whether this VI resets the weighting filter. The default is FALSE. The first time you call this VI, the filter restarts automatically. |
|  | filtered signals [EU] returns the filtered signals. t0 returns the start time of the waveform. dt returns the time interval, in seconds, between data points in the waveform. Y returns the data values of the waveform. |
|  | t0 returns the start time of the waveform. |
|  | dt returns the time interval, in seconds, between data points in the waveform. |
|  | Y returns the data values of the waveform. |
|  | magnitude response returns the magnitude of the filtered signal. f0 returns the start frequency, in hertz, of the filtered signal. df returns the frequency resolution, in hertz, of the filtered signal. Mag returns the magnitude of the filtered signal. |
|  | f0 returns the start frequency, in hertz, of the filtered signal. |
|  | df returns the frequency resolution, in hertz, of the filtered signal. |
|  | Mag returns the magnitude of the filtered signal. |
|  | phase response returns the phase of the filtered signal. f0 returns the start frequency, in hertz, of the filtered signal. df returns the frequency resolution, in hertz, of the filtered signal. Phase returns the phase of the filtered signal. |
|  | f0 returns the start frequency, in hertz, of the filtered signal. |
|  | df returns the frequency resolution, in hertz, of the filtered signal. |
|  | Phase returns the phase of the filtered signal. |
|  | error out contains error information. This output provides standard error out functionality. |

<!--NI_TOPIC bundle=labview-sound-and-vibration-toolkit-api-ref path=svt-bearings-vi.html language=enus -->
## TOPIC 00140: SVT Bearings VI

- bundle_id: `labview-sound-and-vibration-toolkit-api-ref`
- source_path: `svt-bearings-vi.html`
- source_url: https://docs-be.ni.com/bundle/labview-sound-and-vibration-toolkit-api-ref/raw/resource/enus/svt-bearings-vi.html
- document_id: `labview-sound-and-vibration-toolkit-api-ref`
- page_type: `leaf`
- content_type: `reference`
- source_description: Owning Palette: Machine Characteristics VIs Requires: Sound and Vibration Toolkit Calculates characteristic frequencies or orders of rolling-element bearings or hydrodynamic journal bearings. You must manually select the polymorphic instance you want to use. Details SVT Rolling Elements BearingSVT H

### SVT Bearings VI

**Owning Palette:**
 Machine Characteristics VIs

**Requires:**
 Sound and Vibration Toolkit

Calculates characteristic frequencies or orders of rolling-element bearings or hydrodynamic journal bearings. You must manually select the polymorphic instance you want to use.

Details

| SVT Rolling Elements BearingSVT Hydrodynamic Journal Bearing |
| --- |

SVT Rolling Elements Bearing

[IMAGE alt='image' src='images/SVT_Rolling-Element_Bearing.gif']

|  | desired units specifies whether this VI returns characteristic frequencies or orders. 0 Hz (default)Specifies to return characteristic frequencies. 1 Order Specifies to return characteristic orders. |
| --- | --- |
| 0 | Hz (default)Specifies to return characteristic frequencies. |
| 1 | Order Specifies to return characteristic orders. |
|  | nominal speed [RPM] specifies the rotational speed, in revolutions per minute, of the bearing. Because the calculation of characteristic orders does not require rotational speed information, this VI ignores nominal speed [RPM] when you set desired units to Order . The default is 1200. |
|  | bearing parameters specifies the bearing parameters that this VI uses to calculate characteristic frequencies or orders. Note This VI does not specify units for pitch diameter and ball/roller diameter . You must use the same unit for pitch diameter and ball/roller diameter . number of rolling elements specifies the number of rolling elements that the bearing contains. The default is 8. pitch diameter specifies the pitch diameter of the bearing. The default is 1. ball/roller diameter specifies the diameter of the balls or rollers. The default is 0.2. contact angle [deg] specifies the contact angle, in degrees, of the bearing. The default is 0. |
|  | number of rolling elements specifies the number of rolling elements that the bearing contains. The default is 8. |
|  | pitch diameter specifies the pitch diameter of the bearing. The default is 1. |
|  | ball/roller diameter specifies the diameter of the balls or rollers. The default is 0.2. |
|  | contact angle [deg] specifies the contact angle, in degrees, of the bearing. The default is 0. |
|  | error in describes error conditions that occur before this node runs. This input provides standard error in functionality. |
|  | outer race frequency returns the characteristic frequency or order of the outer race of the bearing. |
|  | inner race frequency returns the characteristic frequency or order of the inner race of the bearing. |
|  | fundamental train frequency returns the characteristic frequency or order of the fundamental train of the bearing. |
|  | ball spin frequency returns the characteristic frequency or order of the ball spin. |
|  | error out contains error information. This output provides standard error out functionality. |

SVT Hydrodynamic Journal Bearing

[IMAGE alt='image' src='images/SVT_Hydrodynamic_Journal_Bearing.gif']

|  | nominal speed [RPM] specifies the rotational speed, in revolutions per minute, of the bearing. The default is 1200. |
| --- | --- |
|  | oil whirl order specifies the characteristic order of oil whirl, which is typically 0.38 to 0.48. The default is 0.45. |
|  | error in describes error conditions that occur before this node runs. This input provides standard error in functionality. |
|  | oil whirl frequency returns the characteristic frequency of the oil whirl. |
|  | error out contains error information. This output provides standard error out functionality. |

SVT Bearings Details

This VI uses the following equations to calculate characteristic frequencies or orders of a rolling-element bearing.

[IMAGE alt='image' src='images/eq_bearings.gif']

where 
 f
 <sub>s</sub>
 is the rotational frequency of the shaft in revolutions per second, and 
 N
 is the number of rollers or balls.

Parent topic:

Machine Characteristics VIs

<!--NI_TOPIC bundle=labview-sound-and-vibration-toolkit-api-ref path=svt-belts-vi.html language=enus -->
## TOPIC 00141: SVT Belts VI

- bundle_id: `labview-sound-and-vibration-toolkit-api-ref`
- source_path: `svt-belts-vi.html`
- source_url: https://docs-be.ni.com/bundle/labview-sound-and-vibration-toolkit-api-ref/raw/resource/enus/svt-belts-vi.html
- document_id: `labview-sound-and-vibration-toolkit-api-ref`
- page_type: `leaf`
- content_type: `reference`
- source_description: Owning Palette: Machine Characteristics VIs Requires: Sound and Vibration Toolkit Calculates characteristic frequencies or orders of belts. Details desired units specifies whether this VI returns characteristic frequencies or orders. 0 Hz (default)Specifies to return characteristic frequencies. 1

### SVT Belts VI

**Owning Palette:**
 Machine Characteristics VIs

**Requires:**
 Sound and Vibration Toolkit

Calculates characteristic frequencies or orders of belts.

Details

[IMAGE alt='image' src='images/SVT_Belts.gif']

|  | desired units specifies whether this VI returns characteristic frequencies or orders. 0 Hz (default)Specifies to return characteristic frequencies. 1 Order Specifies to return characteristic orders. |
| --- | --- |
| 0 | Hz (default)Specifies to return characteristic frequencies. |
| 1 | Order Specifies to return characteristic orders. |
|  | pulley1 nominal speed [RPM] specifies the rotational speed, in revolutions per minute, of one pulley of the belt. This VI assigns this pulley as pulley1 and the other pulley as pulley2. Because the calculation of characteristic orders does not require rotational speed information, this VI ignores pulley1 nominal speed [RPM] when you set desired units to Order . The default is 1200. |
|  | pulley1 pitch diameter specifies the pitch diameter of pulley1. The default is 1. You must use the same unit for pulley1 pitch diameter , pulley2 pitch diameter , and center distance . |
|  | pulley2 pitch diameter specifies the pitch diameter of pulley2. The default is 2. You must use the same unit for pulley1 pitch diameter , pulley2 pitch diameter , and center distance . |
|  | center distance specifies the center distance between pulley1 and pulley2. center distance must be greater than half the sum of pulley1 pitch diameter and pulley2 pitch diameter . The default is 10. You must use the same unit for pulley1 pitch diameter , pulley2 pitch diameter , and center distance . |
|  | error in describes error conditions that occur before this node runs. This input provides standard error in functionality. |
|  | teeth parameters specifies the teeth and their parameters that this VI uses to calculate characteristic frequencies or orders. type specifies the type of teeth to use. 0 Belt Teeth (default)Specifies to use the teeth of the timing belt. 1 Pulley1 Teeth Specifies to use the teeth of pulley1. 2 Pulley2 Teeth Specifies to use the teeth of pulley2. number of teeth specifies the number of teeth. The default is 37. |
|  | type specifies the type of teeth to use. 0 Belt Teeth (default)Specifies to use the teeth of the timing belt. 1 Pulley1 Teeth Specifies to use the teeth of pulley1. 2 Pulley2 Teeth Specifies to use the teeth of pulley2. |
| 0 | Belt Teeth (default)Specifies to use the teeth of the timing belt. |
| 1 | Pulley1 Teeth Specifies to use the teeth of pulley1. |
| 2 | Pulley2 Teeth Specifies to use the teeth of pulley2. |
|  | number of teeth specifies the number of teeth. The default is 37. |
|  | pulley2 nominal speed [RPM] returns the rotational speed, in revolutions per minute, of pulley2. |
|  | pulley1 misalign/eccen frequency returns the characteristic frequency or order of the misalignment or eccentricity on pulley1. |
|  | pulley2 misalign/eccen frequency returns the characteristic frequency or order of the misalignment or eccentricity on pulley2. |
|  | belt frequency returns the characteristic frequency or order of the belt. |
|  | timing belt frequency returns the characteristic frequency or order of the timing belt. |
|  | error out contains error information. This output provides standard error out functionality. |
|  | belt length returns the length of the belt. This VI returns belt length in the same unit as the input pulley parameters. |

SVT Belts Details

This VI uses the following equations to calculate characteristic frequencies or orders of belts.

[IMAGE alt='image' src='images/eq_beltlength.gif']

[IMAGE alt='image' src='images/eq_rpmpulley2.gif']

[IMAGE alt='image' src='images/eq_beltfreq.gif']

[IMAGE alt='image' src='images/eq_timingbeltfreq.gif']

Parent topic:

Machine Characteristics VIs

<!--NI_TOPIC bundle=labview-sound-and-vibration-toolkit-api-ref path=svt-build-result-table-vi.html language=enus -->
## TOPIC 00142: SVT Build Result Table VI

- bundle_id: `labview-sound-and-vibration-toolkit-api-ref`
- source_path: `svt-build-result-table-vi.html`
- source_url: https://docs-be.ni.com/bundle/labview-sound-and-vibration-toolkit-api-ref/raw/resource/enus/svt-build-result-table-vi.html
- document_id: `labview-sound-and-vibration-toolkit-api-ref`
- page_type: `leaf`
- content_type: `reference`
- source_description: Owning Palette: Octave Extended Measurements VIs Requires: Sound and Vibration Toolkit Generates a table of the results of a fractional-octave analysis. The first column of the table contains the nominal frequencies in Hz. The second and subsequent columns contain the 1/n band power with the appropr

### SVT Build Result Table VI

**Owning Palette:**
 Octave Extended Measurements VIs

**Requires:**
 Sound and Vibration Toolkit

Generates a table of the results of a fractional-octave analysis. The first column of the table contains the nominal frequencies in Hz. The second and subsequent columns contain the 1/n band power with the appropriate unit label. Wire data to the 
 **octave spectrum**
 input to determine the polymorphic instance to use or manually select the instance.

Example

| SVT Build Result Table (1 Octave Spectrum)SVT Build Result Table (N Octave Spectra) |
| --- |

SVT Build Result Table (1 Octave Spectrum)

[IMAGE alt='image' src='images/SVT_Build_Result_Table_1_Ch.gif']

|  | octave spectrum specifies the octave spectrum to perform the operation on, in band power versus nominal frequencies. nominal frequencies [Hz] specifies the nominal, or preferred, frequencies as opposed to the exact frequencies associated with each 1/ n octave band. band power specifies the band power in decibels associated with each 1/ n octave band. |
| --- | --- |
|  | nominal frequencies [Hz] specifies the nominal, or preferred, frequencies as opposed to the exact frequencies associated with each 1/ n octave band. |
|  | band power specifies the band power in decibels associated with each 1/ n octave band. |
|  | precision specifies the number of digits to display after the decimal point. The default is 1 digit. |
|  | octave info specifies octave information generated by the octave spectrum and needed by the Octave Extended Measurements VIs and the Display VIs. Note You must wire octave info when performing extended measurements. Do not modify the octave info values. If you want to view the information, you can right-click the octave info wire and select Custom Probe»Octave Info from the shortcut menu. |
|  | error in describes error conditions that occur before this node runs. This input provides standard error in functionality. |
|  | include units with values (T) includes units with values in the table. |
|  | result table returns a table that contains the nominal frequencies in the first column and the 1/n octave band power in the second column. |
|  | column headers returns the column headers. |
|  | error out contains error information. This output provides standard error out functionality. |

SVT Build Result Table (N Octave Spectra)

[IMAGE alt='image' src='images/SVT_Build_Result_Table_N_Ch.gif']

|  | octave spectra specifies the octave spectra to perform the operation on, in band power versus nominal frequencies. nominal frequencies [Hz] specifies the nominal, or preferred, frequencies as opposed to the exact frequencies associated with each 1/ n octave band. band power specifies the band power in decibels associated with each 1/ n octave band. |
| --- | --- |
|  | nominal frequencies [Hz] specifies the nominal, or preferred, frequencies as opposed to the exact frequencies associated with each 1/ n octave band. |
|  | band power specifies the band power in decibels associated with each 1/ n octave band. |
|  | precision specifies the number of digits to display after the decimal point. The default is 1 digit. |
|  | octave info specifies octave information generated by the octave spectrum and needed by the Octave Extended Measurements VIs and the Display VIs. Note You must wire octave info when performing extended measurements. Do not modify the octave info values. If you want to view the information, you can create a custom probe. |
|  | error in describes error conditions that occur before this node runs. This input provides standard error in functionality. |
|  | include units with values (T) includes units with values in the table. |
|  | result table returns a table that contains the nominal frequencies in the first column and the 1/n octave band power in the second and subsequent columns. |
|  | column headers returns the column headers. |
|  | error out contains error information. This output provides standard error out functionality. |

Example

Refer to the Present Octave Results (DAQmx) VI in the 
 labview\examples\Sound and Vibration\Octave Analysis
 directory for an example of using the SVT Build Result Table VI.

[IMAGE alt='image' src='images/open.gif']
 Open example 
 
 [IMAGE alt='image' src='images/find.gif']
 Find related examples

Parent topic:

Octave Extended Measurements VIs

<!--NI_TOPIC bundle=labview-sound-and-vibration-toolkit-api-ref path=svt-close-swept-sine-daqmx-vi.html language=enus -->
## TOPIC 00143: SVT Close Swept Sine (DAQmx) VI

- bundle_id: `labview-sound-and-vibration-toolkit-api-ref`
- source_path: `svt-close-swept-sine-daqmx-vi.html`
- source_url: https://docs-be.ni.com/bundle/labview-sound-and-vibration-toolkit-api-ref/raw/resource/enus/svt-close-swept-sine-daqmx-vi.html
- document_id: `labview-sound-and-vibration-toolkit-api-ref`
- page_type: `leaf`
- content_type: `reference`
- source_description: Owning Palette: Stepped Swept Sine VIs Requires: Sound and Vibration Toolkit Clears analog input and output tasks. The SVT Close Swept Sine VI will stop the generation and acquisition of data signals before clearing the task completely. Example swept sine task in specifies the swept-sine parameters.

### SVT Close Swept Sine (DAQmx) VI

**Owning Palette:**
 Stepped Swept Sine VIs

**Requires:**
 Sound and Vibration Toolkit

Clears analog input and output tasks.

The SVT Close Swept Sine VI will stop the generation and acquisition of data signals before clearing the task completely.

Example

[IMAGE alt='image' src='images/SVT_Close_Swept_Sine_DAQmx.gif']

|  | swept sine task in specifies the swept-sine parameters. Note Do not modify the swept sine task in values. |
| --- | --- |
|  | error in describes error conditions that occur before this node runs. This input provides standard error in functionality. |
|  | error out contains error information. This output provides standard error out functionality. |

Example

Refer to the Frequency Response with Swept Sine (DAQmx) VI in the 
 labview\examples\Sound and Vibration\Swept Sine
 directory for an example of using the SVT Close Swept Sine (DAQmx) VI.

[IMAGE alt='image' src='images/open.gif']
 Open example 
 
 [IMAGE alt='image' src='images/find.gif']
 Find related examples

Parent topic:

Stepped Swept Sine VIs

<!--NI_TOPIC bundle=labview-sound-and-vibration-toolkit-api-ref path=svt-configure-swept-sine-vi.html language=enus -->
## TOPIC 00144: SVT Configure Swept Sine VI

- bundle_id: `labview-sound-and-vibration-toolkit-api-ref`
- source_path: `svt-configure-swept-sine-vi.html`
- source_url: https://docs-be.ni.com/bundle/labview-sound-and-vibration-toolkit-api-ref/raw/resource/enus/svt-configure-swept-sine-vi.html
- document_id: `labview-sound-and-vibration-toolkit-api-ref`
- page_type: `leaf`
- content_type: `reference`
- source_description: Owning Palette: Configure Swept Sine VIs Requires: Sound and Vibration Toolkit Configures swept-sine parameters. The sine source frequency steps through the number of steps , starting at start frequency and ending at stop frequency . The frequency steps in a linear or logarithmic progression accordi

### SVT Configure Swept Sine VI

**Owning Palette:**
 Configure Swept Sine VIs

**Requires:**
 Sound and Vibration Toolkit

Configures swept-sine parameters.

The sine source frequency steps through the 
 **number of steps**
 , starting at 
 **start frequency**
 and ending at 
 **stop frequency**
 . The frequency steps in a linear or logarithmic progression according to the 
 **frequency spacing**
 setting.

Example

[IMAGE alt='image' src='images/SVT_Configure_Swept_Sine.gif']

|  | DC offset [V] specifies the DC offset, or average amplitude, of the source in volts-peak. The default is 0.00 V. |
| --- | --- |
|  | amplitude [V] specifies the amplitude of the source in volts-peak. The default is 1.00 V. |
|  | swept sine task in specifies the swept-sine parameters. Note Do not modify the swept sine task in values. |
|  | sweep frequencies specifies the frequency parameters of the sine sweep. Changes to this setting during a sweep do not take effect until the start of the next sweep. start [Hz] specifies the starting frequency of the sine sweep in hertz. The default is 100.00 Hz. stop [Hz] specifies the stop frequency of the sine sweep in hertz. The default is 10,000.00 Hz. number of steps specifies the number of steps of the sine sweep. The default is 100 steps. |
|  | start [Hz] specifies the starting frequency of the sine sweep in hertz. The default is 100.00 Hz. |
|  | stop [Hz] specifies the stop frequency of the sine sweep in hertz. The default is 10,000.00 Hz. |
|  | number of steps specifies the number of steps of the sine sweep. The default is 100 steps. |
|  | frequency spacing specifies how the frequency steps occur. Changes to this setting during a sweep do not take effect until the start of the next sweep. 0 Linear (default) 1 Logarithmic |
| 0 | Linear (default) |
| 1 | Logarithmic |
|  | error in describes error conditions that occur before this node runs. This input provides standard error in functionality. |
|  | repeat specifies whether the sweep automatically repeats after reaching the stop frequency. 0 Off (default) 1 On |
| 0 | Off (default) |
| 1 | On |
|  | swept sine task out returns the swept-sine parameters. |
|  | error out contains error information. This output provides standard error out functionality. |

Example

Refer to the Frequency Response with Swept Sine (DAQmx) VI in the 
 labview\examples\Sound and Vibration\Swept Sine
 directory for an example of using the SVT Configure Swept Sine VI.

[IMAGE alt='image' src='images/open.gif']
 Open example 
 
 [IMAGE alt='image' src='images/find.gif']
 Find related examples

Parent topic:

Configure Swept Sine VIs

## TOPIC 00145: SVT Crosstalk VI

- bundle_id: `labview-sound-and-vibration-toolkit-api-ref`
- source_path: `svt-crosstalk-vi.html`
- source_url: https://docs-be.ni.com/bundle/labview-sound-and-vibration-toolkit-api-ref/raw/resource/enus/svt-crosstalk-vi.html
- document_id: `labview-sound-and-vibration-toolkit-api-ref`
- page_type: `leaf`
- content_type: `reference`
- source_description: Owning Palette: Tone and Distortion VIs Requires: Sound and Vibration Toolkit Computes the crosstalk between stimulus signal X and idle-channel response signal Y to a response signal at the fundamental frequency. This VI returns the detected tone stimulus, detected tone idle-channel response, and cr

### SVT Crosstalk VI

**Owning Palette:**
 Tone and Distortion VIs

**Requires:**
 Sound and Vibration Toolkit

 
 
Computes the crosstalk between 
 **stimulus signal X**
 and 
 **idle-channel response signal Y**
 to a response signal at the fundamental frequency. This VI returns the detected tone stimulus, detected tone idle-channel response, and crosstalk. This VI does not maintain state internally; you can use this VI in a loop to process multiple blocks. Wire data to the 
 **stimulus signal X**
 and 
 **idle-channel response signal Y**
 inputs to determine the polymorphic instance to use or manually select the instance.

| SVT Crosstalk (Time)SVT Crosstalk (Spectrum) |
| --- |

SVT Crosstalk (Time)

[IMAGE alt='image' src='images/SVT_Crosstalk_Time.gif']

|  | desired units specifies the engineering units of the returned measurements. crosstalk units specifies the units for crosstalk . 0 dB (default) 1 Linear ratio phase specifies the units for phase measurements. 0 deg (default) 1 rad detected tone stimulus amplitude units specifies the units for the amplitude of the fundamental tone as measured on the stimulus channel. 0 EU rms (default) 1 EU peak 2 EU peak-peak detected tone response amplitude units specifies the units for the amplitude of the fundamental tone as measured on the response channel. 0 EU rms (default) 1 EU peak 2 EU peak-peak |
| --- | --- |
|  | crosstalk units specifies the units for crosstalk . 0 dB (default) 1 Linear ratio |
| 0 | dB (default) |
| 1 | Linear ratio |
|  | phase specifies the units for phase measurements. 0 deg (default) 1 rad |
| 0 | deg (default) |
| 1 | rad |
|  | detected tone stimulus amplitude units specifies the units for the amplitude of the fundamental tone as measured on the stimulus channel. 0 EU rms (default) 1 EU peak 2 EU peak-peak |
| 0 | EU rms (default) |
| 1 | EU peak |
| 2 | EU peak-peak |
|  | detected tone response amplitude units specifies the units for the amplitude of the fundamental tone as measured on the response channel. 0 EU rms (default) 1 EU peak 2 EU peak-peak |
| 0 | EU rms (default) |
| 1 | EU peak |
| 2 | EU peak-peak |
|  | stimulus signal X [EUx] specifies the scaled signal acquired from the stimulus channel expressed in the selected engineering units. t0 specifies the start time of the waveform. dt specifies the time interval in seconds between data points in the waveform. Y specifies the data values of the waveform. |
|  | t0 specifies the start time of the waveform. |
|  | dt specifies the time interval in seconds between data points in the waveform. |
|  | Y specifies the data values of the waveform. |
|  | idle-channel response signal Y [Euy] specifies the scaled signal acquired from the idle channel expressed in the selected engineering units. |
|  | advanced span [Hz] specifies a frequency span centered at the identified fundamental frequency in which noise and spurious components are ignored for the measurement. Specify a value of 0 if the frequency is known and time invariant. Specify a value of –1 to permit the measurement to automatically compute the minimum frequency span suitable for the time-domain window applied to the signal. The default is –0 Hz. |
|  | error in describes error conditions that occur before this node runs. This input provides standard error in functionality. |
|  | expected fundamental frequency specifies the expected frequency of the fundamental tone in hertz. Use expected fundamental frequency when the fundamental tone is not the largest amplitude component of the input signal. The default is –1.00 Hz. |
|  | unit labels returns the unit label for each member of desired units . crosstalk unit label returns a string that corresponds to the selected crosstalk units. phase unit label returns the unit label for the selected phase units. detected tone stimulus amplitude unit label returns a string that corresponds to the selected amplitude units for the amplitude of the fundamental tone as measured on the stimulus channel. detected tone response amplitude unit label returns a string that corresponds to the selected amplitude units for the amplitude of the fundamental tone as measured on the response channel. |
|  | crosstalk unit label returns a string that corresponds to the selected crosstalk units. |
|  | phase unit label returns the unit label for the selected phase units. |
|  | detected tone stimulus amplitude unit label returns a string that corresponds to the selected amplitude units for the amplitude of the fundamental tone as measured on the stimulus channel. |
|  | detected tone response amplitude unit label returns a string that corresponds to the selected amplitude units for the amplitude of the fundamental tone as measured on the response channel. |
|  | complex spectrum stimulus X returns the complex form of the computed spectrum for the stimulus channel. complex spectrum response returns the complex form of the spectrum for the stimulus channel. f0 returns the start frequency, in hertz, of the spectrum. df returns the frequency resolution, in hertz, of the spectrum. complex spectrum returns the complex form of the computed spectrum. spectrum info returns information about the computed spectrum for the stimulus channel. |
|  | complex spectrum response returns the complex form of the spectrum for the stimulus channel. f0 returns the start frequency, in hertz, of the spectrum. df returns the frequency resolution, in hertz, of the spectrum. complex spectrum returns the complex form of the computed spectrum. |
|  | f0 returns the start frequency, in hertz, of the spectrum. |
|  | df returns the frequency resolution, in hertz, of the spectrum. |
|  | complex spectrum returns the complex form of the computed spectrum. |
|  | spectrum info returns information about the computed spectrum for the stimulus channel. |
|  | complex spectrum idle-channel response Y returns the complex form of the computed spectrum for the response channel. complex spectrum returns the complex form of the spectrum for the response channel. f0 returns the start frequency, in hertz, of the spectrum. df returns the frequency resolution, in hertz, of the spectrum. complex spectrum returns the complex form of the computed spectrum. spectrum info returns information about the computed spectrum for the response channel. |
|  | complex spectrum returns the complex form of the spectrum for the response channel. f0 returns the start frequency, in hertz, of the spectrum. df returns the frequency resolution, in hertz, of the spectrum. complex spectrum returns the complex form of the computed spectrum. |
|  | f0 returns the start frequency, in hertz, of the spectrum. |
|  | df returns the frequency resolution, in hertz, of the spectrum. |
|  | complex spectrum returns the complex form of the computed spectrum. |
|  | spectrum info returns information about the computed spectrum for the response channel. |
|  | crosstalk returns the ratio of the response amplitude to the stimulus amplitude at the detected fundamental frequency. Separation measurements are equivalent to crosstalk measurements. |
|  | phase lag returns the phase delay of the tone at the detected fundamental frequency between the stimulus and response channels. |
|  | error out contains error information. This output provides standard error out functionality. |
|  | detected tone stimulus returns frequency and amplitude of the detected stimulus tone. frequency [Hz] returns the frequency of the detected tone in Hertz. amplitude returns the amplitude of the detected tone. |
|  | frequency [Hz] returns the frequency of the detected tone in Hertz. |
|  | amplitude returns the amplitude of the detected tone. |
|  | detected tone idle-channel response returns the frequency and amplitude of the detected response tone. frequency [Hz] returns the frequency of the detected tone in Hertz. amplitude returns the amplitude of the detected tone. |
|  | frequency [Hz] returns the frequency of the detected tone in Hertz. |
|  | amplitude returns the amplitude of the detected tone. |
|  | complex spectrum returns the complex form of the spectrum for the response channel. f0 returns the start frequency, in hertz, of the spectrum. df returns the frequency resolution, in hertz, of the spectrum. complex spectrum returns the complex form of the computed spectrum. |
|  | f0 returns the start frequency, in hertz, of the spectrum. |
|  | df returns the frequency resolution, in hertz, of the spectrum. |
|  | complex spectrum returns the complex form of the computed spectrum. |

SVT Crosstalk (Spectrum)

[IMAGE alt='image' src='images/SVT_Crosstalk_Frequency.gif']

|  | desired units specifies the engineering units of the returned measurements. crosstalk units specifies the units for crosstalk . 0 dB (default) 1 Linear ratio phase specifies the units for phase measurements. 0 deg (default) 1 rad detected tone stimulus amplitude units specifies the units for the amplitude of the fundamental tone as measured on the stimulus channel. 0 EU rms (default) 1 EU peak 2 EU peak-peak detected tone response amplitude units specifies the units for the amplitude of the fundamental tone as measured on the response channel. 0 EU rms (default) 1 EU peak 2 EU peak-peak |
| --- | --- |
|  | crosstalk units specifies the units for crosstalk . 0 dB (default) 1 Linear ratio |
| 0 | dB (default) |
| 1 | Linear ratio |
|  | phase specifies the units for phase measurements. 0 deg (default) 1 rad |
| 0 | deg (default) |
| 1 | rad |

| 0 | EU rms (default) |
| 1 | EU peak |
| 2 | EU peak-peak |
|  | detected tone response amplitude units specifies the units for the amplitude of the fundamental tone as measured on the response channel. 0 EU rms (default) 1 EU peak 2 EU peak-peak |
| 0 | EU rms (default) |
| 1 | EU peak |
| 2 | EU peak-peak |
|  | complex spectrum stimulus X specifies the complex form of the computed spectrum for the stimulus channel. f0 specifies the start frequency, in hertz, of the spectrum. df specifies the frequency resolution, in hertz, of the spectrum. complex spectrum specifies the complex form of the computed spectrum. |

|  | df specifies the frequency resolution, in hertz, of the spectrum. |

|  | f0 specifies the start frequency, in hertz, of the spectrum. |
|  | df specifies the frequency resolution, in hertz, of the spectrum. |
|  | complex spectrum specifies the complex form of the computed spectrum. |
|  | advanced span [Hz] specifies a frequency span centered at the identified fundamental frequency in which noise and spurious components are ignored for the measurement. Specify a value of 0 if the frequency is known and time invariant. Specify a value of –1 to permit the measurement to automatically compute the minimum frequency span suitable for the time-domain window applied to the signal. The default is –0 Hz. |
|  | error in describes error conditions that occur before this node runs. This input provides standard error in functionality. |
|  | expected fundamental frequency specifies the expected frequency of the fundamental tone in hertz. Use expected fundamental frequency when the fundamental tone is not the largest amplitude component of the input signal. The default is –1.00 Hz. |
|  | unit labels returns the unit label for each member of desired units . crosstalk unit label returns a string that corresponds to the selected crosstalk units. phase unit label returns the unit label for the selected phase units. detected tone stimulus amplitude unit label returns a string that corresponds to the selected amplitude units for the amplitude of the fundamental tone as measured on the stimulus channel. detected tone response amplitude unit label returns a string that corresponds to the selected amplitude units for the amplitude of the fundamental tone as measured on the response channel. |
|  | crosstalk unit label returns a string that corresponds to the selected crosstalk units. |
|  | phase unit label returns the unit label for the selected phase units. |
|  | detected tone stimulus amplitude unit label returns a string that corresponds to the selected amplitude units for the amplitude of the fundamental tone as measured on the stimulus channel. |
|  | detected tone response amplitude unit label returns a string that corresponds to the selected amplitude units for the amplitude of the fundamental tone as measured on the response channel. |
|  | dup complex spectrum stimulus X returns complex spectrum stimulus X unchanged. complex spectrum response returns the complex form of the spectrum for the stimulus channel. f0 returns the start frequency, in hertz, of the spectrum. df returns the frequency resolution, in hertz, of the spectrum. complex spectrum returns the complex form of the computed spectrum. spectrum info returns information about the computed spectrum for the stimulus channel. |

<!--NI_TOPIC bundle=labview-sound-and-vibration-toolkit-api-ref path=svt-dynamic-range-vi.html language=enus -->
## TOPIC 00146: SVT Dynamic Range VI

- bundle_id: `labview-sound-and-vibration-toolkit-api-ref`
- source_path: `svt-dynamic-range-vi.html`
- source_url: https://docs-be.ni.com/bundle/labview-sound-and-vibration-toolkit-api-ref/raw/resource/enus/svt-dynamic-range-vi.html
- document_id: `labview-sound-and-vibration-toolkit-api-ref`
- page_type: `leaf`
- content_type: `reference`
- source_description: Owning Palette: Tone and Distortion VIs Requires: Sound and Vibration Toolkit Computes the dynamic range of a channel based on scaled signal and full scale amplitude and returns the detected fundamental frequency, detected fundamental amplitude, and dynamic range. This VI does not maintain state int

### SVT Dynamic Range VI

**Owning Palette:**
 Tone and Distortion VIs

**Requires:**
 Sound and Vibration Toolkit

Computes the dynamic range of a channel based on
 **scaled signal**
 and 
 **full scale amplitude**
 and returns the detected fundamental frequency, detected fundamental amplitude, and dynamic range. This VI does not maintain state internally; you can use this VI in a loop to process multiple blocks. Wire data to the 
 **scaled signal [EU]**
 input to determine the polymorphic instance to use or manually select the instance.

Example

| SVT Dynamic Range (Time) (1 Ch)SVT Dynamic Range (Spectrum) (1 Ch) |
| --- |

SVT Dynamic Range (Time) (1 Ch)

[IMAGE alt='image' src='images/SVT_Dynamic_Range_Time.gif']

|  | desired units specifies the engineering units of the returned measurements. dynamic range units specifies the units for dynamic range . 0 dB (default) 1 ENOB (Equivalent Number of Bits) detected fundamental amplitude units specifies the units for the amplitude of the detected fundamental tone. 0 EU rms 1 EU peak (default) 2 EU peak-peak |
| --- | --- |
|  | dynamic range units specifies the units for dynamic range . 0 dB (default) 1 ENOB (Equivalent Number of Bits) |
| 0 | dB (default) |
| 1 | ENOB (Equivalent Number of Bits) |
|  | detected fundamental amplitude units specifies the units for the amplitude of the detected fundamental tone. 0 EU rms 1 EU peak (default) 2 EU peak-peak |
| 0 | EU rms |
| 1 | EU peak (default) |
| 2 | EU peak-peak |
|  | scaled signal [EU] specifies the scaled signal expressed in the selected engineering units. To obtain a properly scaled signal, you can use the SVL Scale Voltage to EU VI or define a virtual channel in National Instruments Measurement & Automation Explorer (MAX). t0 specifies the start time of the waveform. dt specifies the time interval in seconds between data points in the waveform. Y specifies the data values of the waveform. |
|  | t0 specifies the start time of the waveform. |
|  | dt specifies the time interval in seconds between data points in the waveform. |
|  | Y specifies the data values of the waveform. |
|  | frequency range specifies the frequency band of analysis. start frequency specifies the lowest frequency of interest for the analysis band in hertz. Specify a value of –1 to select f0 as the minimum frequency of interest. The default is –1.00 Hz. stop frequency specifies the highest frequency of interest for the analysis band in hertz. The valid range is between 0 and the Nyquist frequency. Specify a value of –1 to select the highest frequency as the maximum frequency of interest. The default is –1.00 Hz. |
|  | start frequency specifies the lowest frequency of interest for the analysis band in hertz. Specify a value of –1 to select f0 as the minimum frequency of interest. The default is –1.00 Hz. |
|  | stop frequency specifies the highest frequency of interest for the analysis band in hertz. The valid range is between 0 and the Nyquist frequency. Specify a value of –1 to select the highest frequency as the maximum frequency of interest. The default is –1.00 Hz. |
|  | advanced span [Hz] specifies a frequency span centered at the identified fundamental frequency in which noise and spurious components are ignored for the measurement. Specify a value of 0 if the frequency is known and time invariant. Specify a value of –1 to permit the measurement to automatically compute the minimum frequency span suitable for the time-domain window applied to the signal. The default is –1 Hz. |
|  | error in describes error conditions that occur before this node runs. This input provides standard error in functionality. |
|  | full scale amplitude [EU peak] specifies the full-scale amplitude of the measurement, typically determined by the full-scale amplitude of the device under test. The default is 10.00 EU peak. |
|  | expected fundamental frequency specifies the expected frequency of the fundamental tone in hertz. Use expected fundamental frequency when the fundamental tone is not the largest amplitude component of the input signal. The default is –1.00 Hz. |
|  | unit labels returns the unit label for each member of desired units . dynamic range unit label returns a string that corresponds to the selected dynamic range units. detected fundamental amplitude unit label returns the unit label for the amplitude of the detected fundamental tone. |
|  | dynamic range unit label returns a string that corresponds to the selected dynamic range units. |
|  | detected fundamental amplitude unit label returns the unit label for the amplitude of the detected fundamental tone. |
|  | complex spectrum returns the complex form of the computed spectrum. f0 returns the start frequency, in hertz, of the spectrum. df returns the frequency resolution, in hertz, of the spectrum. complex spectrum returns the complex form of the computed spectrum. |
|  | f0 returns the start frequency, in hertz, of the spectrum. |
|  | df returns the frequency resolution, in hertz, of the spectrum. |
|  | complex spectrum returns the complex form of the computed spectrum. |
|  | dynamic range returns the ratio, in decibels, of the highest signal level the device under test can handle to the lowest signal level it can handle. |
|  | error out contains error information. This output provides standard error out functionality. |
|  | detected fundamental amplitude returns the amplitude of the detected fundamental tone. High amplitude means an increase in the rate of energy transport, resulting in a louder sound. |
|  | detected fundamental frequency [Hz] returns the frequency of the detected fundamental tone in hertz. High frequency means a short wavelength, resulting in a higher pitch. |

SVT Dynamic Range (Spectrum) (1 Ch)

[IMAGE alt='image' src='images/SVT_Dynamic_Range_Frequency.gif']

|  | desired units specifies the engineering units of the returned measurements. dynamic range units specifies the units for dynamic range . 0 dB (default) 1 ENOB (Equivalent Number of Bits) detected fundamental amplitude units specifies the units for the amplitude of the detected fundamental tone. 0 EU rms 1 EU peak (default) 2 EU peak-peak |
| --- | --- |
|  | dynamic range units specifies the units for dynamic range . 0 dB (default) 1 ENOB (Equivalent Number of Bits) |
| 0 | dB (default) |
| 1 | ENOB (Equivalent Number of Bits) |
|  | detected fundamental amplitude units specifies the units for the amplitude of the detected fundamental tone. 0 EU rms 1 EU peak (default) 2 EU peak-peak |
| 0 | EU rms |
| 1 | EU peak (default) |
| 2 | EU peak-peak |
|  | complex spectrum specifies the complex form of the computed spectrum. f0 specifies the start frequency, in hertz, of the spectrum. df specifies the frequency resolution, in hertz, of the spectrum. complex spectrum specifies the complex form of the computed spectrum. |
|  | f0 specifies the start frequency, in hertz, of the spectrum. |
|  | df specifies the frequency resolution, in hertz, of the spectrum. |
|  | complex spectrum specifies the complex form of the computed spectrum. |
|  | frequency range specifies the frequency band of analysis. start frequency specifies the lowest frequency of interest for the analysis band in hertz. Specify a value of –1 to select f0 as the minimum frequency of interest. The default is –1.00 Hz. stop frequency specifies the highest frequency of interest for the analysis band in hertz. The valid range is between 0 and the Nyquist frequency. Specify a value of –1 to select the highest frequency as the maximum frequency of interest. The default is –1.00 Hz. |
|  | start frequency specifies the lowest frequency of interest for the analysis band in hertz. Specify a value of –1 to select f0 as the minimum frequency of interest. The default is –1.00 Hz. |
|  | stop frequency specifies the highest frequency of interest for the analysis band in hertz. The valid range is between 0 and the Nyquist frequency. Specify a value of –1 to select the highest frequency as the maximum frequency of interest. The default is –1.00 Hz. |
|  | advanced span [Hz] specifies a frequency span centered at the identified fundamental frequency in which noise and spurious components are ignored for the measurement. Specify a value of 0 if the frequency is known and time invariant. Specify a value of –1 to permit the measurement to automatically compute the minimum frequency span suitable for the time-domain window applied to the signal. The default is –1 Hz. |
|  | error in describes error conditions that occur before this node runs. This input provides standard error in functionality. |
|  | full scale amplitude [EU peak] specifies the full-scale amplitude of the measurement, typically determined by the full-scale amplitude of the device under test. The default is 10.00 EU peak. |
|  | expected fundamental frequency specifies the expected frequency of the fundamental tone in hertz. Use expected fundamental frequency when the fundamental tone is not the largest amplitude component of the input signal. The default is –1.00 Hz. |
|  | unit labels returns the unit label for each member of desired units . dynamic range unit label returns a string that corresponds to the selected dynamic range units. detected fundamental amplitude unit label returns the unit label for the amplitude of the detected fundamental tone. |
|  | dynamic range unit label returns a string that corresponds to the selected dynamic range units. |
|  | detected fundamental amplitude unit label returns the unit label for the amplitude of the detected fundamental tone. |
|  | dup complex spectrum returns the complex form of the computed spectrum. f0 returns the start frequency, in hertz, of the spectrum. df returns the frequency resolution, in hertz, of the spectrum. complex spectrum returns the complex form of the computed spectrum. |
|  | f0 returns the start frequency, in hertz, of the spectrum. |
|  | df returns the frequency resolution, in hertz, of the spectrum. |
|  | complex spectrum returns the complex form of the computed spectrum. |
|  | dynamic range returns the ratio, in decibels, of the highest signal level the device under test can handle to the lowest signal level it can handle. |
|  | error out contains error information. This output provides standard error out functionality. |
|  | detected fundamental amplitude returns the amplitude of the detected fundamental tone. High amplitude means an increase in the rate of energy transport, resulting in a louder sound. |
|  | detected fundamental frequency [Hz] returns the frequency of the detected fundamental tone in hertz. High frequency means a short wavelength, resulting in a higher pitch. |

Example

Refer to the Settled Dynamic Range (DAQmx) VI in the 
 labview\examples\Sound and Vibration\Audio Measurements
 directory for an example of using the SVT Dynamic Range VI.

 [IMAGE alt='image' src='images/find.gif']

## TOPIC 00147: SVT Fractional-octave Analysis VI

- bundle_id: `labview-sound-and-vibration-toolkit-api-ref`
- source_path: `svt-fractional-octave-analysis-vi.html`
- source_url: https://docs-be.ni.com/bundle/labview-sound-and-vibration-toolkit-api-ref/raw/resource/enus/svt-fractional-octave-analysis-vi.html
- document_id: `labview-sound-and-vibration-toolkit-api-ref`

- source_description: Owning Palette: Octave Analysis VIs Requires: Sound and Vibration Toolkit Performs a fractional-octave analysis on scaled signal [EU] in accordance with the IEC 61260:2014 and ANSI S1.11-2014 standards. Wire data to the scaled signal [EU] input to determine the polymorphic instance to use or manuall

### SVT Fractional-octave Analysis VI

**Owning Palette:**
 Octave Analysis VIs

**Requires:**
 Sound and Vibration Toolkit

Performs a fractional-octave analysis on 
 **scaled signal [EU]**
 in accordance with the IEC 61260:2014 and ANSI S1.11-2014 standards. Wire data to the 
 **scaled signal [EU]**
 input to determine the polymorphic instance to use or manually select the instance.

Details

Use the pull-down menu to of this VI.

| SVT Fractional-octave Analysis (1 Ch)SVT Fractional-octave Analysis (N Ch) |
| --- |

SVT Fractional-octave Analysis (1 Ch)

[IMAGE alt='image' src='images/SVT_Fractional_Octave_Analysis_1_Ch.gif']

|  | reset filter specifies if the octave filters have to be reset. The default is FALSE. The filters are automatically reset the first time this VI is called. |
| --- | --- |
|  | restart averaging specifies if the selected averaging process has to be restarted. The default is FALSE. When you call this VI for the first time, the averaging process restarts automatically. |
|  | scaled signal [EU] specifies the scaled signal expressed in the selected engineering units. To obtain a properly scaled signal, you can use the SVL Scale Voltage to EU VI or define a virtual channel in National Instruments Measurement & Automation Explorer (MAX). t0 specifies the start time of the waveform. dt specifies the time interval in seconds between data points in the waveform. Y specifies the data values of the waveform. |
|  | t0 specifies the start time of the waveform. |
|  | dt specifies the time interval in seconds between data points in the waveform. |
|  | Y specifies the data values of the waveform. |
|  | frequency range [Hz] specifies the low and high frequencies of the 1/n octave analysis expressed in hertz. low frequency specifies the center frequency of the lowest 1/n octave band. The default is 125.00 Hz. high frequency specifies the center frequency of the highest 1/n octave band. The default is 8,000.00 Hz. |
|  | low frequency specifies the center frequency of the lowest 1/n octave band. The default is 125.00 Hz. |
|  | high frequency specifies the center frequency of the highest 1/n octave band. The default is 8,000.00 Hz. |
|  | bandwidth specifies the bandwidth of the 1/n octave filters. 01 octave11/3 octave21/6 octave31/12 octave (default) 41/24 octave |
| 0 | 1 octave |
| 1 | 1/3 octave |
| 2 | 1/6 octave |
| 3 | 1/12 octave (default) |
| 4 | 1/24 octave |
|  | dB On specifies if results are expressed in decibels. The default is TRUE. The dB reference is specified when the scaling from voltage to engineering units is applied. |
|  | error in describes error conditions that occur before this node runs. This input provides standard error in functionality. |
|  | averaging type specifies the type of averaging that is applied to the result of the 1/n octave filtering. Exponential uses specific parameters defined in exp avg settings . Equal confidence uses specific parameters defined in equal confidence level [dB] . 0Linear (default) 1Exponential2Equal confidence3Peak |
| 0 | Linear (default) |
| 1 | Exponential |
| 2 | Equal confidence |
| 3 | Peak |
|  | exp avg settings specifies how the exponential averaging is performed through both mode and time constant. mode specifies the exponential averaging mode. The Custom mode allows you to specify any time constant [ms] . 0Slow t = 1 s 1Fast (default) t = 125 ms 2Impulse t = 35 ms rising and 1.5 s falling 3Custom time constant specifies the time constant in milliseconds used for exponential averaging if mode is set to Custom. In all other cases, this parameter is ignored. The default is 125.00 ms. |
|  | mode specifies the exponential averaging mode. The Custom mode allows you to specify any time constant [ms] . 0Slow t = 1 s 1Fast (default) t = 125 ms 2Impulse t = 35 ms rising and 1.5 s falling 3Custom |
| 0 | Slow t = 1 s |
| 1 | Fast (default) t = 125 ms |
| 2 | Impulse t = 35 ms rising and 1.5 s falling |
| 3 | Custom |
|  | time constant specifies the time constant in milliseconds used for exponential averaging if mode is set to Custom. In all other cases, this parameter is ignored. The default is 125.00 ms. |
|  | equal confidence level [dB] specifies the confidence value in decibels used if averaging type is set to Equal confidence. The default is 125.00 dB. |
|  | filter settled returns TRUE when all octave filters are settled. |
|  | octave spectrum returns the octave spectrum in band power versus nominal frequencies. You can display the results in an Octave Graph or a Multiplot Octave Graph indicator. nominal frequencies [Hz] returns the nominal, or preferred, frequencies as opposed to the exact frequencies associated with each 1/ n octave band. band power [dB EU] returns the power associated with each 1/ n octave band expressed in decibels rms, if dB On is TRUE and EU^2 rms if dB On is FALSE. |
|  | nominal frequencies [Hz] returns the nominal, or preferred, frequencies as opposed to the exact frequencies associated with each 1/ n octave band. |
|  | band power [dB EU] returns the power associated with each 1/ n octave band expressed in decibels rms, if dB On is TRUE and EU^2 rms if dB On is FALSE. |
|  | unit label returns the selected engineering units. |
|  | total band power [dB EU] returns the sum of the rms power contained in all the octave bands. |
|  | octave info returns octave information generated by the octave spectrum and needed by the Octave Extended Measurements VIs and the Display VIs. Note You must wire octave info when performing extended measurements. Do not modify the octave info values. If you want to view the information, you can right-click the octave info wire and select Custom Probe»Octave Info from the shortcut menu. |
|  | error out contains error information. This output provides standard error out functionality. |

SVT Fractional-octave Analysis (N Ch)

[IMAGE alt='image' src='images/SVT_Fractional_Octave_Analysis_N_Ch.gif']

|  | reset filter specifies if the octave filters have to be reset. The default is FALSE. The filters are automatically reset the first time this VI is called. |
| --- | --- |
|  | restart averaging specifies if the selected averaging process has to be restarted. The default is FALSE. When you call this VI for the first time, the averaging process restarts automatically. |
|  | scaled signals [EU] specifies the scaled signals expressed in the selected engineering units. To obtain a properly scaled signal, you can use the SVL Scale Voltage to EU VI or define virtual channels in National Instruments Measurement & Automation Explorer (MAX). t0 specifies the start time of the waveform. dt specifies the time interval in seconds between data points in the waveform. Y specifies the data values of the waveform. |
|  | t0 specifies the start time of the waveform. |
|  | dt specifies the time interval in seconds between data points in the waveform. |
|  | Y specifies the data values of the waveform. |
|  | frequency range [Hz] specifies the low and high frequencies of the 1/n octave analysis expressed in hertz. low frequency specifies the center frequency of the lowest 1/n octave band. The default is 125.00 Hz. high frequency specifies the center frequency of the highest 1/n octave band. The default is 8,000.00 Hz. |
|  | low frequency specifies the center frequency of the lowest 1/n octave band. The default is 125.00 Hz. |
|  | high frequency specifies the center frequency of the highest 1/n octave band. The default is 8,000.00 Hz. |
|  | bandwidth specifies the bandwidth of the 1/n octave filters. 01 octave11/3 octave21/6 octave31/12 octave (default) 41/24 octave |
| 0 | 1 octave |
| 1 | 1/3 octave |
| 2 | 1/6 octave |
| 3 | 1/12 octave (default) |
| 4 | 1/24 octave |
|  | dB On specifies if results are expressed in decibels. The default is TRUE. The dB reference is specified when the scaling from voltage to engineering units is applied. |
|  | error in describes error conditions that occur before this node runs. This input provides standard error in functionality. |
|  | averaging type specifies the type of averaging that is applied to the result of the 1/n octave filtering. Exponential uses specific parameters defined in exp avg settings . Equal confidence uses specific parameters defined in equal confidence level [dB] . 0Linear (default) 1Exponential2Equal confidence3Peak |
| 0 | Linear (default) |
| 1 | Exponential |
| 2 | Equal confidence |
| 3 | Peak |
|  | exp avg settings specifies how the exponential averaging is performed through both mode and time constant. mode specifies the exponential averaging mode. The Custom mode allows you to specify any time constant [ms] . 0Slow t = 1 s 1Fast (default) t = 125 ms 2Impulse t = 35 ms rising and 1.5 s falling 3Custom time constant specifies the time constant in milliseconds used for exponential averaging if mode is set to Custom. In all other cases, this parameter is ignored. The default is 125.00 ms. |
|  | mode specifies the exponential averaging mode. The Custom mode allows you to specify any time constant [ms] . 0Slow t = 1 s 1Fast (default) t = 125 ms 2Impulse t = 35 ms rising and 1.5 s falling 3Custom |
| 0 | Slow t = 1 s |
| 1 | Fast (default) t = 125 ms |
| 2 | Impulse t = 35 ms rising and 1.5 s falling |
| 3 | Custom |
|  | time constant specifies the time constant in milliseconds used for exponential averaging if mode is set to Custom. In all other cases, this parameter is ignored. The default is 125.00 ms. |
|  | equal confidence level [dB] specifies the confidence value in decibels used if averaging type is set to Equal confidence. The default is 125.00 dB. |
|  | filter settled returns TRUE when all octave filters are settled. |
|  | octave spectra returns the octave spectra in band power versus nominal frequencies. You can display the results in an Octave Graph or a Multiplot Octave Graph indicator. nominal frequencies [Hz] returns the nominal, or preferred, frequencies as opposed to the exact frequencies associated with each 1/ n octave band. band power returns the power associated with each 1/ n octave band expressed in decibels rms. |
|  | nominal frequencies [Hz] returns the nominal, or preferred, frequencies as opposed to the exact frequencies associated with each 1/ n octave band. |
|  | band power returns the power associated with each 1/ n octave band expressed in decibels rms. |
|  | unit labels returns the selected engineering units for each channel. |
|  | total band powers [dB EU] returns the total band power for each channel. For each channel, total band power is the sum of the rms powers in all the octave bands. |
|  | octave info contains octave information needed by the Octave Extended Measurements VIs and the waterfall display for octave VIs. Note You must wire octave info when performing extended measurements. Do not modify the octave info values. If you want to view the information, you can create a custom probe. |
|  | error out contains error information. This output provides standard error out functionality. |

SVT Fractional-octave Analysis Details

The fractional-octave filters are designed to comply with IEC 61260:2014 and ANSI S1.11-2014, class 1 standards. To meet these standards, the sampling frequency must be at least three times the highest exact midband frequency for octave analysis and at least 2.5 times the highest exact midband frequency for 1/3, 1/6, 1/12 or 1/24 octave analysis.

Parent topic:

Octave Analysis VIs

## TOPIC 00148: SVT Fractional Octave Filtering VI

- bundle_id: `labview-sound-and-vibration-toolkit-api-ref`
- source_path: `svt-fractional-octave-filtering-vi.html`
