<!--NI_TOPIC bundle=labview-sound-and-vibration-toolkit-api-ref path=svt-initialize-swept-sine-task-daqmx-vi.html language=enus -->
## TOPIC 00160: SVT Initialize Swept Sine Task (DAQmx) VI

- bundle_id: `labview-sound-and-vibration-toolkit-api-ref`
- source_path: `svt-initialize-swept-sine-task-daqmx-vi.html`
- source_url: https://docs-be.ni.com/bundle/labview-sound-and-vibration-toolkit-api-ref/raw/resource/enus/svt-initialize-swept-sine-task-daqmx-vi.html
- document_id: `labview-sound-and-vibration-toolkit-api-ref`
- page_type: `leaf`
- content_type: `reference`
- source_description: Owning Palette: Stepped Swept Sine VIs Requires: Sound and Vibration Toolkit Initializes swept-sine-analysis parameters on analog input and output devices. The SVT Initialize Swept Sine Task (DAQmx) VI creates a new swept-sine task for the device based on the specified input settings. Example SVT In

### SVT Initialize Swept Sine Task (DAQmx) VI

**Owning Palette:**
 Stepped Swept Sine VIs

**Requires:**
 Sound and Vibration Toolkit

Initializes swept-sine-analysis parameters on analog input and output devices.

The SVT Initialize Swept Sine Task (DAQmx) VI creates a new swept-sine task for the device based on the specified input settings.

Example

| SVT Initialize Swept Sine (DAQmx) (1 AO, 2 AI) |
| --- |

SVT Initialize Swept Sine (DAQmx) (1 AO, 2 AI)

[IMAGE alt='image' src='images/SVT_Initialize_Swept_Sine_DAQmx_1_AO_2_AI.gif']

|  | AO source settings specifies the analog output settings associated with the generated excitation signal. excitation channel specifies the channel name of the excitation channel. max value [V] specifies the maximum value of the signal in volts. The default is 10.00 V. min value [V] specifies the minimum value of the signal in volts. The default is –10.00 V. terminal configuration specifies the input terminal configuration of the selected channel. 0 Default The default device input configuration. Refer to the device documentation for information about the default input configuration. 1 RSE The referenced single-ended (RSE) configuration measures the signal with respect to a ground directly connected to the measurement system ground. 2 Differential The differential configuration has neither of its inputs tied to a fixed reference, such as earth or building ground. 3 Pseudodifferential The pseudodifferential configuration references the positive and negative inputs for the channel to measurement system ground through different impedances. The pseudodifferential terminal configuration measures the difference between the positive and negative inputs. |
| --- | --- |
|  | excitation channel specifies the channel name of the excitation channel. |
|  | max value [V] specifies the maximum value of the signal in volts. The default is 10.00 V. |
|  | min value [V] specifies the minimum value of the signal in volts. The default is –10.00 V. |
|  | terminal configuration specifies the input terminal configuration of the selected channel. 0 Default The default device input configuration. Refer to the device documentation for information about the default input configuration. 1 RSE The referenced single-ended (RSE) configuration measures the signal with respect to a ground directly connected to the measurement system ground. 2 Differential The differential configuration has neither of its inputs tied to a fixed reference, such as earth or building ground. 3 Pseudodifferential The pseudodifferential configuration references the positive and negative inputs for the channel to measurement system ground through different impedances. The pseudodifferential terminal configuration measures the difference between the positive and negative inputs. |
| 0 | Default The default device input configuration. Refer to the device documentation for information about the default input configuration. |
| 1 | RSE The referenced single-ended (RSE) configuration measures the signal with respect to a ground directly connected to the measurement system ground. |
| 2 | Differential The differential configuration has neither of its inputs tied to a fixed reference, such as earth or building ground. |
| 3 | Pseudodifferential The pseudodifferential configuration references the positive and negative inputs for the channel to measurement system ground through different impedances. The pseudodifferential terminal configuration measures the difference between the positive and negative inputs. |
|  | AI stimulus settings specifies the analog input channel settings associated with the acquired stimulus signal. stimulus channel specifies the channel name of the analog input stimulus channel. max value [V] specifies the maximum value of the signal in volts. The default is 10.00 V. min value [V] specifies the minimum value of the signal in volts. The default is –10.00 V. terminal configuration specifies the input terminal configuration of the selected channel. 0 Default The default device terminal configuration. Refer to the device documentation for information about the default terminal configuration. 1 RSE The referenced single-ended (RSE) configuration measures the signal with respect to a ground directly connected to the measurement system ground. 2 NRSE The nonreferenced single-ended (NRSE) configuration measures the signal with respect to a single ground, but the potential may differ from the measurement system ground. 3 Differential The differential configuration has neither of its inputs tied to a fixed reference, such as earth or building ground. 4 Pseudodifferential The pseudodifferential configuration references the positive and negative inputs for the channel to measurement system ground through different impedances. The pseudodifferential terminal configuration measures the difference between the positive and negative inputs. |
|  | stimulus channel specifies the channel name of the analog input stimulus channel. |
|  | max value [V] specifies the maximum value of the signal in volts. The default is 10.00 V. |
|  | min value [V] specifies the minimum value of the signal in volts. The default is –10.00 V. |
|  | terminal configuration specifies the input terminal configuration of the selected channel. 0 Default The default device terminal configuration. Refer to the device documentation for information about the default terminal configuration. 1 RSE The referenced single-ended (RSE) configuration measures the signal with respect to a ground directly connected to the measurement system ground. 2 NRSE The nonreferenced single-ended (NRSE) configuration measures the signal with respect to a single ground, but the potential may differ from the measurement system ground. 3 Differential The differential configuration has neither of its inputs tied to a fixed reference, such as earth or building ground. 4 Pseudodifferential The pseudodifferential configuration references the positive and negative inputs for the channel to measurement system ground through different impedances. The pseudodifferential terminal configuration measures the difference between the positive and negative inputs. |
| 0 | Default The default device terminal configuration. Refer to the device documentation for information about the default terminal configuration. |
| 1 | RSE The referenced single-ended (RSE) configuration measures the signal with respect to a ground directly connected to the measurement system ground. |
| 2 | NRSE The nonreferenced single-ended (NRSE) configuration measures the signal with respect to a single ground, but the potential may differ from the measurement system ground. |
| 3 | Differential The differential configuration has neither of its inputs tied to a fixed reference, such as earth or building ground. |
| 4 | Pseudodifferential The pseudodifferential configuration references the positive and negative inputs for the channel to measurement system ground through different impedances. The pseudodifferential terminal configuration measures the difference between the positive and negative inputs. |
|  | AI response settings specifies the analog input channel settings associated with the acquired response signal. response channel specifies the channel name of the response channel. max value [V] specifies the maximum value of the signal in volts. The default is 10.00 V. min value [V] specifies the minimum value of the signal in volts. The default is –10.00 V. terminal configuration specifies the input terminal configuration of the selected channel. 0 Default The default device terminal configuration. Refer to the device documentation for information about the default terminal configuration. 1 RSE The referenced single-ended (RSE) configuration measures the signal with respect to a ground directly connected to the measurement system ground. 2 NRSE The nonreferenced single-ended (NRSE) configuration measures the signal with respect to a single ground, but the potential may differ from the measurement system ground. 3 Differential The differential configuration has neither of its inputs tied to a fixed reference, such as earth or building ground. 4 Pseudodifferential The pseudodifferential configuration references the positive and negative inputs for the channel to measurement system ground through different impedances. The pseudodifferential terminal configuration measures the difference between the positive and negative inputs. |
|  | response channel specifies the channel name of the response channel. |
|  | max value [V] specifies the maximum value of the signal in volts. The default is 10.00 V. |
|  | min value [V] specifies the minimum value of the signal in volts. The default is –10.00 V. |
|  | terminal configuration specifies the input terminal configuration of the selected channel. 0 Default The default device terminal configuration. Refer to the device documentation for information about the default terminal configuration. 1 RSE The referenced single-ended (RSE) configuration measures the signal with respect to a ground directly connected to the measurement system ground. 2 NRSE The nonreferenced single-ended (NRSE) configuration measures the signal with respect to a single ground, but the potential may differ from the measurement system ground. 3 Differential The differential configuration has neither of its inputs tied to a fixed reference, such as earth or building ground. 4 Pseudodifferential The pseudodifferential configuration references the positive and negative inputs for the channel to measurement system ground through different impedances. The pseudodifferential terminal configuration measures the difference between the positive and negative inputs. |
| 0 | Default The default device terminal configuration. Refer to the device documentation for information about the default terminal configuration. |

| 2 | NRSE The nonreferenced single-ended (NRSE) configuration measures the signal with respect to a single ground, but the potential may differ from the measurement system ground. |
| 3 | Differential The differential configuration has neither of its inputs tied to a fixed reference, such as earth or building ground. |
| 4 | Pseudodifferential The pseudodifferential configuration references the positive and negative inputs for the channel to measurement system ground through different impedances. The pseudodifferential terminal configuration measures the difference between the positive and negative inputs. |
|  | error in describes error conditions that occur before this node runs. This input provides standard error in functionality. |
|  | swept sine task out returns the swept-sine parameters. |
|  | error out contains error information. This output provides standard error out functionality. |

Example

Refer to the Frequency Response with Swept Sine (DAQmx) VI in the 
 labview\examples\Sound and Vibration\Swept Sine
 directory for an example of using the SVT Initialize Swept Sine Task (DAQmx) VI.

<!--NI_TOPIC bundle=labview-sound-and-vibration-toolkit-api-ref path=svt-intermodulation-distortion-vi.html language=enus -->
## TOPIC 00161: SVT Intermodulation Distortion VI

- bundle_id: `labview-sound-and-vibration-toolkit-api-ref`
- source_path: `svt-intermodulation-distortion-vi.html`
- source_url: https://docs-be.ni.com/bundle/labview-sound-and-vibration-toolkit-api-ref/raw/resource/enus/svt-intermodulation-distortion-vi.html
- document_id: `labview-sound-and-vibration-toolkit-api-ref`
- page_type: `leaf`
- content_type: `reference`
- source_description: Owning Palette: Multitone VIs Requires: Sound and Vibration Toolkit Computes the total intermodulation distortion and the intermodulation components of scaled signal [EU] and IMD standard . Intermodulation distortion (IMD) results when two signals combine and create undesired sidebands. The SVT Inte

### SVT Intermodulation Distortion VI

**Owning Palette:**
 Multitone VIs

**Requires:**
 Sound and Vibration Toolkit

Computes the total intermodulation distortion and the intermodulation components of 
 **scaled signal [EU]**
 and 
 **IMD standard**
 .

Intermodulation distortion (IMD) results when two signals combine and create undesired sidebands. The SVT Intermodulation Distortion VI outputs the detected tone for both high-frequency and low-frequency. The intermodulation distortion is based on a ratio of the sum of both the high-frequency and the low-frequency distortions.

Example

[IMAGE alt='image' src='images/SVT_Intermodulation_Distortion_1_Ch.gif']

|  | IMD standard specifies the standard used to generate the test signal and to measure IMD . 0 SMPTE (60 Hz, 7000 Hz, 4:1) (default) 1 DIN (250 Hz, 8000 Hz, 4:1) 2 CCIF (11 kHz, 12 kHz, 1:1) 3 CCIF (19 kHz, 20 kHz, 1:1) 4 Custom |
| --- | --- |
| 0 | SMPTE (60 Hz, 7000 Hz, 4:1) (default) |
| 1 | DIN (250 Hz, 8000 Hz, 4:1) |
| 2 | CCIF (11 kHz, 12 kHz, 1:1) |
| 3 | CCIF (19 kHz, 20 kHz, 1:1) |
| 4 | Custom |
|  | desired units specifies the engineering units of the returned measurements. IMD units specifies the units for IMD . 0 dB (default) 1 Linear ratio 2 % intermodulation component amplitude units specifies the units for amplitude in intermodulation components . 0 EU rms (default) 1 EU peak 2 EU peak-peak detected fundamental amplitude units specifies the units for the amplitude of the detected fundamental tone. 0 EU rms (default) 1 EU peak 2 EU peak-peak magnitude spectrum units specifies the units for magnitude spectrum . 0 dB (default) 1 Linear |
|  | IMD units specifies the units for IMD . 0 dB (default) 1 Linear ratio 2 % |
| 0 | dB (default) |
| 1 | Linear ratio |
| 2 | % |
|  | intermodulation component amplitude units specifies the units for amplitude in intermodulation components . 0 EU rms (default) 1 EU peak 2 EU peak-peak |
| 0 | EU rms (default) |
| 1 | EU peak |
| 2 | EU peak-peak |
|  | detected fundamental amplitude units specifies the units for the amplitude of the detected fundamental tone. 0 EU rms (default) 1 EU peak 2 EU peak-peak |
| 0 | EU rms (default) |
| 1 | EU peak |
| 2 | EU peak-peak |
|  | magnitude spectrum units specifies the units for magnitude spectrum . 0 dB (default) 1 Linear |
| 0 | dB (default) |
| 1 | Linear |
|  | scaled signal [EU] specifies the scaled signal expressed in the selected engineering units. To obtain a properly scaled signal, you can use the SVL Scale Voltage to EU VI or define a virtual channel in National Instruments Measurement & Automation Explorer (MAX). t0 specifies the start time of the waveform. dt specifies the time interval in seconds between data points in the waveform. Y specifies the data values of the waveform. |
|  | t0 specifies the start time of the waveform. |
|  | dt specifies the time interval in seconds between data points in the waveform. |
|  | Y specifies the data values of the waveform. |
|  | custom IMD signal specifies the parameters used to generate the test signal. The VI ignores this parameter unless IMD standard is set to custom. IMD type specifies the type of the generated test signal. 0 SMPTE/DIN 1 CCIF/Twin-Tone (default) low-frequency tone specifies the low-frequency tone, in hertz, used in the generated signal. The default is 14,000.00 Hz. high-frequency tone specifies the high-frequency tone, in hertz, used in the generated signal. The default is 15,000.00 Hz. amplitude ratio specifies the ratio between the amplitudes of low-frequency tone and high-frequency tone . The most commonly used ratios are 1:1 and 4:1. The default is 1.0. maximum order specifies the intermodulation components to include in the IMD measurement. The default is 5. IMD components are found at the sum and difference frequencies of the two tones, f1 and f2, used to construct the IMD test signal. The IMD frequencies are given by the following equation. IMD frequencies = m*f1 ± n*f2 The order is derived by the following equation. order = m + n |
|  | IMD type specifies the type of the generated test signal. 0 SMPTE/DIN 1 CCIF/Twin-Tone (default) |
| 0 | SMPTE/DIN |
| 1 | CCIF/Twin-Tone (default) |
|  | low-frequency tone specifies the low-frequency tone, in hertz, used in the generated signal. The default is 14,000.00 Hz. |
|  | high-frequency tone specifies the high-frequency tone, in hertz, used in the generated signal. The default is 15,000.00 Hz. |
|  | amplitude ratio specifies the ratio between the amplitudes of low-frequency tone and high-frequency tone . The most commonly used ratios are 1:1 and 4:1. The default is 1.0. |
|  | maximum order specifies the intermodulation components to include in the IMD measurement. The default is 5. IMD components are found at the sum and difference frequencies of the two tones, f1 and f2, used to construct the IMD test signal. The IMD frequencies are given by the following equation. IMD frequencies = m*f1 ± n*f2 The order is derived by the following equation. order = m + n |
|  | error in describes error conditions that occur before this node runs. This input provides standard error in functionality. |
|  | unit labels returns the unit label for each member of desired units . IMD unit label returns the unit label for IMD . intermodulation component amplitude unit label returns the unit label for amplitude in intermodulation components . detected fundamental amplitude unit label returns the unit label for the amplitude of the detected fundamental tone. magnitude spectrum unit label returns the unit label for magnitude spectrum . |
|  | IMD unit label returns the unit label for IMD . |
|  | intermodulation component amplitude unit label returns the unit label for amplitude in intermodulation components . |
|  | detected fundamental amplitude unit label returns the unit label for the amplitude of the detected fundamental tone. |
|  | magnitude spectrum unit label returns the unit label for magnitude spectrum . |
|  | magnitude spectrum returns the magnitude of the FFT spectrum. f0 returns the start frequency, in hertz, of the spectrum. df returns the frequency resolution, in hertz, of the spectrum. magnitude returns the magnitude of the FFT spectrum. |
|  | f0 returns the start frequency, in hertz, of the spectrum. |
|  | df returns the frequency resolution, in hertz, of the spectrum. |
|  | magnitude returns the magnitude of the FFT spectrum. |
|  | IMD returns the measured intermodulation distortion (IMD), which is defined as the ratio of the RMS energy of the intermodulation tones to the RMS energy of high-frequency tone . |
|  | intermodulation components returns the measured frequencies and amplitudes of the intermodulation components based on IMD standard . frequency [Hz] returns the frequency of the intermodulation component in hertz. amplitude returns the amplitude of the intermodulation component. |
|  | frequency [Hz] returns the frequency of the intermodulation component in hertz. |
|  | amplitude returns the amplitude of the intermodulation component. |
|  | spectrum info returns information about the spectrum needed by the Spectrum Extended Measurements VIs, the Display VIs, and the Tone and Distortion VIs. Note You must wire spectrum info when performing extended measurements. Do not modify the spectrum info values. If you want to view the information, you can right-click the spectrum info wire and select Custom Probe»Spectrum Info from the shortcut menu. |
|  | error out contains error information. This output provides standard error out functionality. |
|  | detected high-frequency tone returns the measured frequency and amplitude of the high-frequency tone based on IMD standard . frequency [Hz] returns the detected frequency of the tone in hertz. amplitude returns the detected amplitude of the tone. |
|  | frequency [Hz] returns the detected frequency of the tone in hertz. |
|  | amplitude returns the detected amplitude of the tone. |
|  | detected low-frequency tone returns the measured frequency and amplitude of the low-frequency tone based on IMD standard . frequency [Hz] returns the detected frequency of the tone in hertz. amplitude returns the detected amplitude of the tone. |
|  | frequency [Hz] returns the detected frequency of the tone in hertz. |
|  | amplitude returns the detected amplitude of the tone. |

Example

Refer to the One Shot IMD (DAQmx) VI in the 
 labview\examples\Sound and Vibration\Audio Measurements
 directory for an example of using the SVT Intermodulation Distortion VI.

[IMAGE alt='image' src='images/open.gif']
 Open example 
 
 [IMAGE alt='image' src='images/find.gif']

Multitone VIs

<!--NI_TOPIC bundle=labview-sound-and-vibration-toolkit-api-ref path=svt-max-frequency-within-tolerances-iec-vi.html language=enus -->
## TOPIC 00162: SVT Max Frequency Within Tolerances [IEC] VI

- bundle_id: `labview-sound-and-vibration-toolkit-api-ref`
- source_path: `svt-max-frequency-within-tolerances-iec-vi.html`
- source_url: https://docs-be.ni.com/bundle/labview-sound-and-vibration-toolkit-api-ref/raw/resource/enus/svt-max-frequency-within-tolerances-iec-vi.html
- document_id: `labview-sound-and-vibration-toolkit-api-ref`
- page_type: `leaf`
- content_type: `reference`
- source_description: Owning Palette: Weighting (Arbitrary Rate) VIs Requires: Sound and Vibration Toolkit Returns the maximum frequency within tolerances according to the ANSI S1.4-2014/IEC 61672:2013 standard and sampling frequency [Hz] , weighting , and class . sampling frequency [Hz] specifies the sampling frequency

### SVT Max Frequency Within Tolerances [IEC] VI

**Owning Palette:**
 Weighting (Arbitrary Rate) VIs

**Requires:**
 Sound and Vibration Toolkit

Returns the maximum frequency within tolerances according to the ANSI S1.4-2014/IEC 61672:2013 standard and 
 **sampling frequency [Hz]**
 , 
 **weighting**
 , and 
 **class**
 .

[IMAGE alt='image' src='images/SVT_Max_Frequency_within_Tolerances_IEC.gif']

|  | sampling frequency [Hz] specifies the sampling frequency for data acquisition in hertz. The default is 51,200.00 Hz. |
| --- | --- |
|  | weighting specifies the weighting filter of interest. Use Linear if no weighting is needed. 0 Linear (default) 1 A-weighting 2 B-weighting 3 C-weighting |
| 0 | Linear (default) |
| 1 | A-weighting |
| 2 | B-weighting |
| 3 | C-weighting |
|  | class specifies the tolerances, according to IEC Standard 61672-1, used to test the weighting filter design. 0 Class 1 (default) 1 Class 2 |
| 0 | Class 1 (default) |
| 1 | Class 2 |
|  | error in describes error conditions that occur before this node runs. This input provides standard error in functionality. |
|  | max freq within tolerances [Hz] returns the maximum frequency in hertz that is within the tolerance limits specified by type . Tolerance limits are based on the IEC 61672-2002 standard. |
|  | error out contains error information. This output provides standard error out functionality. |

Parent topic:

Weighting (Arbitrary Rate) VIs

<!--NI_TOPIC bundle=labview-sound-and-vibration-toolkit-api-ref path=svt-read-swept-frequency-response-vi.html language=enus -->
## TOPIC 00163: SVT Read Swept Frequency Response VI

- bundle_id: `labview-sound-and-vibration-toolkit-api-ref`
- source_path: `svt-read-swept-frequency-response-vi.html`
- source_url: https://docs-be.ni.com/bundle/labview-sound-and-vibration-toolkit-api-ref/raw/resource/enus/svt-read-swept-frequency-response-vi.html
- document_id: `labview-sound-and-vibration-toolkit-api-ref`
- page_type: `leaf`
- content_type: `reference`
- source_description: Owning Palette: Read Swept Sine Measurements VIs Requires: Sound and Vibration Toolkit Returns frequency-response measurements. Measurements are available as magnitude and phase and a cluster of real and imaginary parts. You can directly connect magnitude, phase, and imaginary vs real (Nyquist plot)

### SVT Read Swept Frequency Response VI

**Owning Palette:**
 Read Swept Sine Measurements VIs

**Requires:**
 Sound and Vibration Toolkit

Returns frequency-response measurements.

Measurements are available as magnitude and phase and a cluster of real and imaginary parts. You can directly connect magnitude, phase, and imaginary vs real (Nyquist plot) to an XY graph for display.

Example

[IMAGE alt='image' src='images/SVT_Read_Swept_Frequency_Response.gif']

|  | swept sine task in specifies the swept-sine parameters. Note Do not modify the swept sine task in values. |
| --- | --- |
|  | swept sine measurements specifies the swept-sine measurements to perform. RMS stimulus specifies the root mean square (RMS) of the stimulus function. Re [stimulus] specifies the real part of the stimulus function. Re [response] specifies the real part of the response function. THD specifies the measured total harmonic distortion (THD), which is defined as the ratio of the RMS energy of the harmonics to the RMS energy of the fundamental tone. scaled response magnitude specifies the extracted scaled response magnitude for analysis of the measurements. RMS response specifies the root mean square (RMS) of the response function. Im [stimulus] specifies the imaginary part of the stimulus function. Im [response] specifies the imaginary part of the response function. Harm[] specifies the measured harmonic components. |
|  | RMS stimulus specifies the root mean square (RMS) of the stimulus function. |
|  | Re [stimulus] specifies the real part of the stimulus function. |
|  | Re [response] specifies the real part of the response function. |
|  | THD specifies the measured total harmonic distortion (THD), which is defined as the ratio of the RMS energy of the harmonics to the RMS energy of the fundamental tone. |
|  | scaled response magnitude specifies the extracted scaled response magnitude for analysis of the measurements. |
|  | RMS response specifies the root mean square (RMS) of the response function. |
|  | Im [stimulus] specifies the imaginary part of the stimulus function. |
|  | Im [response] specifies the imaginary part of the response function. |
|  | Harm[] specifies the measured harmonic components. |
|  | view specifies how the different results from this VI are returned. view allows you to choose whether magnitude results are displayed in dB, phase results are unwrapped, and phase results are converted from radians to degrees. dB On specifies if results are expressed in decibels. The default is FALSE. unwrap phase specifies whether to unwrap the phase. Unwrapping eliminates discontinuities that have an absolute value greater than pi. The default is FALSE, meaning the phase is not unwrapped. When unwrap phase is TRUE, the phase is unwrapped. convert to degree specifies whether the phase results are converted from radians to degrees. The default is TRUE, which means that results are expressed in degrees. |
|  | dB On specifies if results are expressed in decibels. The default is FALSE. |
|  | unwrap phase specifies whether to unwrap the phase. Unwrapping eliminates discontinuities that have an absolute value greater than pi. The default is FALSE, meaning the phase is not unwrapped. When unwrap phase is TRUE, the phase is unwrapped. |
|  | convert to degree specifies whether the phase results are converted from radians to degrees. The default is TRUE, which means that results are expressed in degrees. |
|  | error in describes error conditions that occur before this node runs. This input provides standard error in functionality. |
|  | magnitude unit label returns the unit label for the magnitude spectrum. |
|  | phase unit label returns the unit label for the phase spectrum. |
|  | swept sine task out returns the swept-sine parameters. |
|  | dup swept sine measurements returns the configured swept-sine measurements. RMS stimulus returns the root mean square (RMS) of the stimulus function. Re [stimulus] returns the real part of the stimulus function. Re [response] returns the real part of the response function. THD returns the measured total harmonic distortion (THD), which is defined as the ratio of the RMS energy of the harmonics to the RMS energy of the fundamental tone. scaled response magnitude returns the extracted scaled response magnitude for analysis of the measurements. RMS response returns the root mean square (RMS) of the response function. Im [stimulus] returns the imaginary part of the stimulus function. Im [response] returns the imaginary part of the response function. Harm[] returns the measured harmonic components. |
|  | RMS stimulus returns the root mean square (RMS) of the stimulus function. |
|  | Re [stimulus] returns the real part of the stimulus function. |
|  | Re [response] returns the real part of the response function. |
|  | THD returns the measured total harmonic distortion (THD), which is defined as the ratio of the RMS energy of the harmonics to the RMS energy of the fundamental tone. |
|  | scaled response magnitude returns the extracted scaled response magnitude for analysis of the measurements. |
|  | RMS response returns the root mean square (RMS) of the response function. |
|  | Im [stimulus] returns the imaginary part of the stimulus function. |
|  | Im [response] returns the imaginary part of the response function. |
|  | Harm[] returns the measured harmonic components. |
|  | imaginary vs real (Nyquist plot) returns the real and imaginary parts of the frequency response function. Plotting imaginary vs real (Nyquist plot) on an XY graph results in the Nyquist plot. Re [FRF] returns the real part of the frequency response function. Im [FRF] returns the imaginary part of the frequency response function. |
|  | Re [FRF] returns the real part of the frequency response function. |
|  | Im [FRF] returns the imaginary part of the frequency response function. |
|  | error out contains error information. This output provides standard error out functionality. |
|  | phase returns the phase of the frequency response function. You can connect this output to an XY graph. frequency returns the frequency, in hertz, of the fundamental tone at each test frequency. phase returns the phase of the frequency response. |
|  | frequency returns the frequency, in hertz, of the fundamental tone at each test frequency. |
|  | phase returns the phase of the frequency response. |
|  | magnitude returns the magnitude of the frequency response function. You can connect this output to an XY graph. frequency returns the frequency, in hertz, of the fundamental tone at each test frequency. magnitude returns the magnitude of the frequency response at each test frequency. |
|  | frequency returns the frequency, in hertz, of the fundamental tone at each test frequency. |
|  | magnitude returns the magnitude of the frequency response at each test frequency. |

Example

Refer to the Frequency Response with Swept Sine (DAQmx) VI in the 
 labview\examples\Sound and Vibration\Swept Sine
 directory for an example of using the SVT Read Swept Frequency Response VI.

## TOPIC 00164: SVT Read Swept Harmonic Components VI

- bundle_id: `labview-sound-and-vibration-toolkit-api-ref`
- source_path: `svt-read-swept-harmonic-components-vi.html`

- content_type: `reference`
- source_description: Owning Palette: Read Swept Sine Measurements VIs Requires: Sound and Vibration Toolkit Returns total harmonic distortion (THD) and individual harmonics values. Use the SVT Set Swept Sine Distortion Settings VI to configure the harmonic distortion measurements returned by the SVT Read Swept Harmonic

### SVT Read Swept Harmonic Components VI

**Owning Palette:**
 Read Swept Sine Measurements VIs

**Requires:**
 Sound and Vibration Toolkit

Returns total harmonic distortion (THD) and individual harmonics values.

Use the SVT Set Swept Sine Distortion Settings VI to configure the harmonic distortion measurements returned by the SVT Read Swept Harmonic Components VI.

Example

[IMAGE alt='image' src='images/SVT_Read_Swept_Harmonic_Components.gif']

|  | desired units specifies the engineering units of the returned measurements. THD units specifies the units for THD . 0 dB (default) 1 Linear ratio 2 % harmonic component amplitude units specifies the unit for amplitude in harmonic components . 0 EU rms (default) 1 EU peak 2 EU peak-peak 3 dB ref fund fundamental amplitude units specifies the units for fundamental amplitude . 0 EU rms (default) 1 EU peak 2 EU peak-peak |
| --- | --- |
|  | THD units specifies the units for THD . 0 dB (default) 1 Linear ratio 2 % |
| 0 | dB (default) |
| 1 | Linear ratio |
| 2 | % |
|  | harmonic component amplitude units specifies the unit for amplitude in harmonic components . 0 EU rms (default) 1 EU peak 2 EU peak-peak 3 dB ref fund |
| 0 | EU rms (default) |
| 1 | EU peak |
| 2 | EU peak-peak |
| 3 | dB ref fund |
|  | fundamental amplitude units specifies the units for fundamental amplitude . 0 EU rms (default) 1 EU peak 2 EU peak-peak |
| 0 | EU rms (default) |
| 1 | EU peak |
| 2 | EU peak-peak |
|  | swept sine task in specifies the swept-sine parameters. Note Do not modify the swept sine task in values. |
|  | swept sine measurements specifies the swept-sine measurements to perform. RMS stimulus specifies the root mean square (RMS) of the stimulus function. Re [stimulus] specifies the real part of the stimulus function. Re [response] specifies the real part of the response function. THD specifies the measured total harmonic distortion (THD), which is defined as the ratio of the RMS energy of the harmonics to the RMS energy of the fundamental tone. scaled response magnitude specifies the extracted scaled response magnitude for analysis of the measurements. RMS response specifies the root mean square (RMS) of the response function. Im [stimulus] specifies the imaginary part of the stimulus function. Im [response] specifies the imaginary part of the response function. Harm[] specifies the measured harmonic components. |
|  | RMS stimulus specifies the root mean square (RMS) of the stimulus function. |
|  | Re [stimulus] specifies the real part of the stimulus function. |
|  | Re [response] specifies the real part of the response function. |
|  | THD specifies the measured total harmonic distortion (THD), which is defined as the ratio of the RMS energy of the harmonics to the RMS energy of the fundamental tone. |
|  | scaled response magnitude specifies the extracted scaled response magnitude for analysis of the measurements. |
|  | RMS response specifies the root mean square (RMS) of the response function. |
|  | Im [stimulus] specifies the imaginary part of the stimulus function. |
|  | Im [response] specifies the imaginary part of the response function. |
|  | Harm[] specifies the measured harmonic components. |
|  | error in describes error conditions that occur before this node runs. This input provides standard error in functionality. |
|  | unit labels returns the unit label for each member of desired units . THD unit label returns the unit label for THD . harmonic component amplitudes unit label returns the unit label for amplitude in harmonic components . fundamental amplitude unit label returns the unit label for fundamental amplitude . |
|  | THD unit label returns the unit label for THD . |
|  | harmonic component amplitudes unit label returns the unit label for amplitude in harmonic components . |
|  | fundamental amplitude unit label returns the unit label for fundamental amplitude . |
|  | swept sine task out returns the swept-sine parameters. |
|  | dup swept sine measurements returns the configured swept-sine measurements. RMS stimulus returns the root mean square (RMS) of the stimulus function. Re [stimulus] returns the real part of the stimulus function. Re [response] returns the real part of the response function. THD returns the measured total harmonic distortion (THD), which is defined as the ratio of the RMS energy of the harmonics to the RMS energy of the fundamental tone. scaled response magnitude returns the extracted scaled response magnitude for analysis of the measurements. RMS response returns the root mean square (RMS) of the response function. Im [stimulus] returns the imaginary part of the stimulus function. Im [response] returns the imaginary part of the response function. Harm[] returns the measured harmonic components. |
|  | RMS stimulus returns the root mean square (RMS) of the stimulus function. |
|  | Re [stimulus] returns the real part of the stimulus function. |
|  | Re [response] returns the real part of the response function. |
|  | THD returns the measured total harmonic distortion (THD), which is defined as the ratio of the RMS energy of the harmonics to the RMS energy of the fundamental tone. |
|  | scaled response magnitude returns the extracted scaled response magnitude for analysis of the measurements. |
|  | RMS response returns the root mean square (RMS) of the response function. |
|  | Im [stimulus] returns the imaginary part of the stimulus function. |
|  | Im [response] returns the imaginary part of the response function. |
|  | Harm[] returns the measured harmonic components. |
|  | THD returns the measured total harmonic distortion (THD) and frequency. frequency returns the frequency, in hertz, of the fundamental tone at each test frequency. THD returns the measured total harmonic distortion (THD), which is defined as the ratio of the RMS energy of the harmonics to the RMS energy of the fundamental tone. |
|  | frequency returns the frequency, in hertz, of the fundamental tone at each test frequency. |
|  | THD returns the measured total harmonic distortion (THD), which is defined as the ratio of the RMS energy of the harmonics to the RMS energy of the fundamental tone. |
|  | error out contains error information. This output provides standard error out functionality. |
|  | harmonic components returns the measured frequencies and amplitudes of the harmonics of the fundamental tone. harmonic [i] returns the frequency and distortion for an individual harmonic component. frequency returns the frequency, in hertz, of the harmonic component to visualize at each test frequency. distortion returns the measured amplitude of the harmonic component to visualize at each test frequency. |
|  | harmonic [i] returns the frequency and distortion for an individual harmonic component. frequency returns the frequency, in hertz, of the harmonic component to visualize at each test frequency. distortion returns the measured amplitude of the harmonic component to visualize at each test frequency. |
|  | frequency returns the frequency, in hertz, of the harmonic component to visualize at each test frequency. |
|  | distortion returns the measured amplitude of the harmonic component to visualize at each test frequency. |
|  | fundamental amplitude returns the fundamental amplitude through frequency and amplitude. frequency [Hz] returns the frequency, in hertz, of the fundamental tone at each test frequency. amplitude returns the measured amplitude of the fundamental tone at each test frequency. |
|  | frequency [Hz] returns the frequency, in hertz, of the fundamental tone at each test frequency. |
|  | amplitude returns the measured amplitude of the fundamental tone at each test frequency. |

Example swept sine task in specifies the swept-sine parameters. Do not modify the swept sine task in values. swept sine measurements specifies the swep

### SVT Read Swept RMS Levels VI

**Owning Palette:**
 Read Swept Sine Measurements VIs

**Requires:**
 Sound and Vibration Toolkit

Returns RMS levels measured on stimulus and response signals.

Example

[IMAGE alt='image' src='images/SVT_Read_Swept_RMS_Levels.gif']

|  | swept sine task in specifies the swept-sine parameters. Note Do not modify the swept sine task in values. |
| --- | --- |
|  | swept sine measurements specifies the swept-sine measurements to perform. RMS stimulus specifies the root mean square (RMS) of the stimulus function. Re [stimulus] specifies the real part of the stimulus function. Re [response] specifies the real part of the response function. THD specifies the measured total harmonic distortion (THD), which is defined as the ratio of the RMS energy of the harmonics to the RMS energy of the fundamental tone. scaled response magnitude specifies the extracted scaled response magnitude for analysis of the measurements. RMS response specifies the root mean square (RMS) of the response function. Im [stimulus] specifies the imaginary part of the stimulus function. Im [response] specifies the imaginary part of the response function. Harm[] specifies the measured harmonic components. |
|  | RMS stimulus specifies the root mean square (RMS) of the stimulus function. |
|  | Re [stimulus] specifies the real part of the stimulus function. |
|  | Re [response] specifies the real part of the response function. |
|  | THD specifies the measured total harmonic distortion (THD), which is defined as the ratio of the RMS energy of the harmonics to the RMS energy of the fundamental tone. |
|  | scaled response magnitude specifies the extracted scaled response magnitude for analysis of the measurements. |
|  | RMS response specifies the root mean square (RMS) of the response function. |
|  | Im [stimulus] specifies the imaginary part of the stimulus function. |
|  | Im [response] specifies the imaginary part of the response function. |
|  | Harm[] specifies the measured harmonic components. |
|  | dB On specifies if results are expressed in decibels. The default is TRUE. The dB reference is specified when the scaling from voltage to engineering units is applied. |
|  | error in describes error conditions that occur before this node runs. This input provides standard error in functionality. |
|  | stimulus unit label returns the unit label for RMS level (stimulus) . |
|  | response unit label returns the unit label for RMS level (response) . |
|  | swept sine task out returns the swept-sine parameters. |
|  | dup swept sine measurements returns the configured swept-sine measurements. RMS stimulus returns the root mean square (RMS) of the stimulus function. Re [stimulus] returns the real part of the stimulus function. Re [response] returns the real part of the response function. THD returns the measured total harmonic distortion (THD), which is defined as the ratio of the RMS energy of the harmonics to the RMS energy of the fundamental tone. scaled response magnitude returns the extracted scaled response magnitude for analysis of the measurements. RMS response returns the root mean square (RMS) of the response function. Im [stimulus] returns the imaginary part of the stimulus function. Im [response] returns the imaginary part of the response function. Harm[] returns the measured harmonic components. |
|  | RMS stimulus returns the root mean square (RMS) of the stimulus function. |
|  | Re [stimulus] returns the real part of the stimulus function. |
|  | Re [response] returns the real part of the response function. |
|  | THD returns the measured total harmonic distortion (THD), which is defined as the ratio of the RMS energy of the harmonics to the RMS energy of the fundamental tone. |
|  | scaled response magnitude returns the extracted scaled response magnitude for analysis of the measurements. |
|  | RMS response returns the root mean square (RMS) of the response function. |
|  | Im [stimulus] returns the imaginary part of the stimulus function. |
|  | Im [response] returns the imaginary part of the response function. |
|  | Harm[] returns the measured harmonic components. |
|  | error out contains error information. This output provides standard error out functionality. |
|  | RMS level (response) returns the root mean square (RMS) level of the response signal. The RMS level is a measure of the overall energy contained in the response signal. You can connect this output to an XY graph. frequency returns the frequency, in hertz, of the fundamental tone at each test frequency. magnitude returns the magnitude of the fundamental tone at each test frequency. |
|  | frequency returns the frequency, in hertz, of the fundamental tone at each test frequency. |

<!--NI_TOPIC bundle=labview-sound-and-vibration-toolkit-api-ref path=svt-set-swept-sine-averaging-vi.html language=enus -->
## TOPIC 00166: SVT Set Swept Sine Averaging VI

- bundle_id: `labview-sound-and-vibration-toolkit-api-ref`
- source_path: `svt-set-swept-sine-averaging-vi.html`
- source_url: https://docs-be.ni.com/bundle/labview-sound-and-vibration-toolkit-api-ref/raw/resource/enus/svt-set-swept-sine-averaging-vi.html
- document_id: `labview-sound-and-vibration-toolkit-api-ref`
- page_type: `leaf`
- content_type: `reference`
- source_description: Owning Palette: Configure Swept Sine VIs Requires: Sound and Vibration Toolkit Configures the sine-sweep settling time and integration time settings. The SVT Set Swept Sine Averaging VI allows you to customize your swept-sine application through the settings. You can reduce the default values for th

### SVT Set Swept Sine Averaging VI

**Owning Palette:**
 Configure Swept Sine VIs

**Requires:**
 Sound and Vibration Toolkit

Configures the sine-sweep settling time and integration time settings.

The SVT Set Swept Sine Averaging VI allows you to customize your swept-sine application through the settings. You can reduce the default values for the settling time and integration time to increase the speed of the swept-sine measurement. You can decrease the speed of the swept-sine measurement by raising the settings.

Example

[IMAGE alt='image' src='images/SVT_Set_Swept_Sine_Averaging.gif']

|  | swept sine task in specifies the swept-sine parameters. Note Do not modify the swept sine task in values. |
| --- | --- |
|  | settling time specifies how much time has to elapse before the measurement begins at the new test frequency. When stepping from one frequency to the next, some devices under test might require a specific amount of settling time before reaching a steady state. The actual settling time is the maximum of settle time and settle cycles . Allowing the device to reach a steady state helps ensure an accurate response measurement. settle time [s] specifies the number of seconds allowed to pass before the measurement begins. The default is 15.60 ms. settle cycles specifies the number of cycles allowed to pass before the measurement begins. The default is 5. |
|  | settle time [s] specifies the number of seconds allowed to pass before the measurement begins. The default is 15.60 ms. |
|  | settle cycles specifies the number of cycles allowed to pass before the measurement begins. The default is 5. |
|  | integration time specifies the time duration, rounded to the next whole cycle, used as the present integration measurement period. The actual integration time used is the maximum of the integration time , rounded to the next whole cycle, and the integration cycles . integration time [s] specifies the duration of the measurement in seconds. The default is 15.60 ms. integration cycles specifies the duration of the measurement in cycles. The default is 5. |
|  | integration time [s] specifies the duration of the measurement in seconds. The default is 15.60 ms. |
|  | integration cycles specifies the duration of the measurement in cycles. The default is 5. |
|  | error in describes error conditions that occur before this node runs. This input provides standard error in functionality. |
|  | swept sine task out returns the swept-sine parameters. |
|  | error out contains error information. This output provides standard error out functionality. |

Example

Refer to the Frequency Response with Swept Sine (DAQmx) VI in the 
 labview\examples\Sound and Vibration\Swept Sine
 directory for an example of using the SVT Set Swept Sine Averaging VI.

[IMAGE alt='image' src='images/open.gif']
 Open example 
 
 [IMAGE alt='image' src='images/find.gif']
 Find related examples

Parent topic:

## TOPIC 00167: SVT Set Swept Sine Block Duration VI

- bundle_id: `labview-sound-and-vibration-toolkit-api-ref`
- source_path: `svt-set-swept-sine-block-duration-vi.html`
- source_url: https://docs-be.ni.com/bundle/labview-sound-and-vibration-toolkit-api-ref/raw/resource/enus/svt-set-swept-sine-block-duration-vi.html
- document_id: `labview-sound-and-vibration-toolkit-api-ref`
- page_type: `leaf`
- content_type: `reference`
- source_description: Owning Palette: Configure Advanced Settings VIs Requires: Sound and Vibration Toolkit Configures the block duration of the swept-sine acquisition. The SVT Set Swept Sine Block Duration VI configures the buffer size of the input and output of the DSA card and controls the size of the input and output

### SVT Set Swept Sine Block Duration VI

**Owning Palette:**
 Configure Advanced Settings VIs

**Requires:**
 Sound and Vibration Toolkit

Configures the block duration of the swept-sine acquisition.

The SVT Set Swept Sine Block Duration VI configures the buffer size of the input and output of the DSA card and controls the size of the input and output buffers. The size of the buffer determines the speed of the VIs execution. A larger buffer results in a slower execution time, whereas a small buffer results in a faster execution time.

Example

[IMAGE alt='image' src='images/SVT_Set_Swept_Sine_Block_Duration.gif']

|  | swept sine task in specifies the swept-sine parameters. Note Do not modify the swept sine task in values. |
| --- | --- |
|  | block duration [ms] specifies the block duration in milliseconds. The default is 100 ms. |
|  | error in describes error conditions that occur before this node runs. This input provides standard error in functionality. |
|  | swept sine task out returns the swept-sine parameters. |
|  | error out contains error information. This output provides standard error out functionality. |

Example

Refer to the Level and FRF and Distortion with Swept Sine (DAQmx) VI in the 
 labview\examples\Sound and Vibration\Swept Sine
 directory for an example of using the SVT Set Swept Sine Block Duration VI.

[IMAGE alt='image' src='images/open.gif']
 Open example 
 
 [IMAGE alt='image' src='images/find.gif']
 Find related examples

<!--NI_TOPIC bundle=labview-sound-and-vibration-toolkit-api-ref path=svt-set-swept-sine-coupling-and-iepe-excitati.html language=enus -->
## TOPIC 00168: SVT Set Swept Sine Coupling and IEPE Excitation (DAQmx) VI

- bundle_id: `labview-sound-and-vibration-toolkit-api-ref`
- source_path: `svt-set-swept-sine-coupling-and-iepe-excitati.html`
- source_url: https://docs-be.ni.com/bundle/labview-sound-and-vibration-toolkit-api-ref/raw/resource/enus/svt-set-swept-sine-coupling-and-iepe-excitati.html
- document_id: `labview-sound-and-vibration-toolkit-api-ref`
- page_type: `leaf`
- content_type: `reference`
- source_description: Owning Palette: Configure Advanced Settings VIs Requires: Sound and Vibration Toolkit Configures coupling and integrated electronic piezoelectric (IEPE) excitation for input channels on a DSA device. swept sine task in specifies the swept-sine parameters. Do not modify the swept sine task in values.

### SVT Set Swept Sine Coupling and IEPE Excitation (DAQmx) VI

**Owning Palette:**
 Configure Advanced Settings VIs

**Requires:**
 Sound and Vibration Toolkit

Configures coupling and integrated electronic piezoelectric (IEPE) excitation for input channels on a DSA device.

[IMAGE alt='image' src='images/SVT_Set_Swept_Sine_Coupling_and_IEPE_Excitation_DAQmx.gif']

|  | swept sine task in specifies the swept-sine parameters. Note Do not modify the swept sine task in values. |
| --- | --- |
|  | DSA stimulus channel settings specifies the input coupling and integrated electronic piezoelectric (IEPE) excitation on AI stimulus channel . The settings are only applied if the measurement is performed with a DSA device. coupling specifies the coupling setting of the selected channel. The default is AC. 0 AC 1 DC 2 GND enable IEPE excitation specifies whether the onboard current excitation is enabled to power integrated electronic piezoelectric (IEPE) sensors. The default is FALSE, meaning that the onboard excitation is disabled. IEPE current [mA] specifies the current value in mA for the onboard integrated electronic piezoelectric (IEPE) current excitation. The default is 4.0 mA. |
|  | coupling specifies the coupling setting of the selected channel. The default is AC. 0 AC 1 DC 2 GND |
| 0 | AC |
| 1 | DC |
| 2 | GND |
|  | enable IEPE excitation specifies whether the onboard current excitation is enabled to power integrated electronic piezoelectric (IEPE) sensors. The default is FALSE, meaning that the onboard excitation is disabled. |
|  | IEPE current [mA] specifies the current value in mA for the onboard integrated electronic piezoelectric (IEPE) current excitation. The default is 4.0 mA. |
|  | DSA response channel settings specifies the input coupling and integrated electronic piezoelectric (IEPE) on AI response channel . These settings are only applied if the measurement is performed with a DSA device. coupling specifies the coupling setting of the selected channel. The default is AC. 0 AC 1 DC 2 GND enable IEPE excitation specifies whether the onboard current excitation is enabled to power integrated electronic piezoelectric (IEPE) sensors. The default is FALSE, meaning that the onboard excitation is disabled. IEPE current [mA] specifies the current value in mA for the onboard integrated electronic piezoelectric (IEPE) current excitation. The default is 4.0 mA. |
|  | coupling specifies the coupling setting of the selected channel. The default is AC. 0 AC 1 DC 2 GND |
| 0 | AC |
| 1 | DC |
| 2 | GND |
|  | enable IEPE excitation specifies whether the onboard current excitation is enabled to power integrated electronic piezoelectric (IEPE) sensors. The default is FALSE, meaning that the onboard excitation is disabled. |
|  | IEPE current [mA] specifies the current value in mA for the onboard integrated electronic piezoelectric (IEPE) current excitation. The default is 4.0 mA. |
|  | error in describes error conditions that occur before this node runs. This input provides standard error in functionality. |
|  | time to settle [s] specifies the amount of time to wait, in seconds, to allow the measurement hardware to settle. Specify a value for this parameter if you change input coupling or integrated electronic piezoelectric (IEPE) excitation. The default is 0.0 s. |
|  | swept sine task out returns the swept-sine parameters. |
|  | error out contains error information. This output provides standard error out functionality. |

## TOPIC 00170: SVT Set Swept Sine DC Offset VI

- bundle_id: `labview-sound-and-vibration-toolkit-api-ref`
- source_path: `svt-set-swept-sine-dc-offset-vi.html`
- source_url: https://docs-be.ni.com/bundle/labview-sound-and-vibration-toolkit-api-ref/raw/resource/enus/svt-set-swept-sine-dc-offset-vi.html
- document_id: `labview-sound-and-vibration-toolkit-api-ref`
- page_type: `leaf`
- content_type: `reference`
- source_description: Owning Palette: Configure Advanced Settings VIs Requires: Sound and Vibration Toolkit Sets the DC bias on the AO channel during the sweep. This DC bias might be necessary for a specific DUT, and the DC value does not affect the swept sine computations. swept sine task in specifies the swept-sine par

### SVT Set Swept Sine DC Offset VI

**Owning Palette:**
 Configure Advanced Settings VIs

**Requires:**
 Sound and Vibration Toolkit

Sets the DC bias on the AO channel during the sweep. This DC bias might be necessary for a specific DUT, and the DC value does not affect the swept sine computations.

[IMAGE alt='image' src='images/SVT_Set_Swept_Sine_DC_Offset.gif']

|  | swept sine task in specifies the swept-sine parameters. Note Do not modify the swept sine task in values. |
| --- | --- |
|  | DC offset [V] specifies the DC bias voltage that is added to the sinusoidal excitation for the frequency sweep. The specified offset might require a wider output range and/or wider input ranges to accommodate the biased signals. The default is 0.00 V. |
|  | error in describes error conditions that occur before this node runs. This input provides standard error in functionality. |
|  | swept sine task out returns the swept-sine parameters. |
|  | error out contains error information. This output provides standard error out functionality. |

Parent topic:

Configure Advanced Settings VIs

<!--NI_TOPIC bundle=labview-sound-and-vibration-toolkit-api-ref path=svt-set-swept-sine-distortion-settings-vi.html language=enus -->
## TOPIC 00171: SVT Set Swept Sine Distortion Settings VI

- bundle_id: `labview-sound-and-vibration-toolkit-api-ref`
- source_path: `svt-set-swept-sine-distortion-settings-vi.html`
- source_url: https://docs-be.ni.com/bundle/labview-sound-and-vibration-toolkit-api-ref/raw/resource/enus/svt-set-swept-sine-distortion-settings-vi.html
- document_id: `labview-sound-and-vibration-toolkit-api-ref`
- page_type: `leaf`
- content_type: `reference`
- source_description: Owning Palette: Configure Swept Sine VIs Requires: Sound and Vibration Toolkit Configures the maximum harmonic number and harmonic mapping array used for swept-sine harmonic measurements. The SVT Set Swept Distortion Settings VI is only required if you intend to perform harmonic analysis. Example sw

### SVT Set Swept Sine Distortion Settings VI

**Owning Palette:**
 Configure Swept Sine VIs

**Requires:**
 Sound and Vibration Toolkit

Configures the maximum harmonic number and harmonic mapping array used for swept-sine harmonic measurements.

The SVT Set Swept Distortion Settings VI is only required if you intend to perform harmonic analysis.

Example

[IMAGE alt='image' src='images/SVT_Set_Swept_Sine_Distortion_Settings.gif']

|  | swept sine task in specifies the swept-sine parameters. Note Do not modify the swept sine task in values. |
| --- | --- |
|  | maximum harmonic specifies the highest harmonic number for swept-sine total harmonic distortion (THD) computations. For example, if maximum harmonic is 3, harmonics 2 and 3 are the only harmonics used to compute THD. The default is 5. |
|  | harmonics to visualize specifies the individual harmonic components to return. The valid range for each element in harmonics to visualize is between 2 and maximum harmonic . The default is 0. |
|  | error in describes error conditions that occur before this node runs. This input provides standard error in functionality. |
|  | swept sine task out returns the swept-sine parameters. |
|  | error out contains error information. This output provides standard error out functionality. |

Example

Refer to the Level and FRF and Distortion with Swept Sine (DAQmx) VI in the 
 labview\examples\Sound and Vibration\Swept Sine
 directory for an example of using the SVT Set Swept Sine Distortion Settings VI.

[IMAGE alt='image' src='images/open.gif']
 Open example 
 
 [IMAGE alt='image' src='images/find.gif']
 Find related examples

Configure Swept Sine VIs

## TOPIC 00172: SVT Set Swept Sine EU VI

- bundle_id: `labview-sound-and-vibration-toolkit-api-ref`
- source_path: `svt-set-swept-sine-eu-vi.html`
- source_url: https://docs-be.ni.com/bundle/labview-sound-and-vibration-toolkit-api-ref/raw/resource/enus/svt-set-swept-sine-eu-vi.html
- document_id: `labview-sound-and-vibration-toolkit-api-ref`
- page_type: `leaf`
- content_type: `reference`
- source_description: Owning Palette: Configure Swept Sine VIs Requires: Sound and Vibration Toolkit Configures how stimulus and response signals are scaled to engineering units (EU). Example swept sine task in specifies the swept-sine parameters. Do not modify the swept sine task in values. stimulus channel info specifi

### SVT Set Swept Sine EU VI

**Owning Palette:**
 Configure Swept Sine VIs

**Requires:**
 Sound and Vibration Toolkit

Configures how stimulus and response signals are scaled to engineering units (EU).

Example

[IMAGE alt='image' src='images/SVT_Set_Swept_Sine_EU.gif']

|  | swept sine task in specifies the swept-sine parameters. Note Do not modify the swept sine task in values. |
| --- | --- |
|  | stimulus channel info specifies information about the stimulus channel. This information defines the output specifications for this VI. sensor sensitivity [mV/EU] specifies the sensitivity of the sensor in mV/EU. The default is 1000.00. dB reference [EU] specifies the reference value to use when you compute the results in decibels and when you scale a signal to engineering units. dB reference [EU] uses the unit that you specify in engineering units . The default is 1. When engineering units is V, use a dB reference [EU] of 1. When engineering units is m, use a dB reference [EU] of 0.7746. When engineering units is Pa, use a dB reference [EU] of 20E–6. weighting filter specifies if any weighting filter has been applied to the signal. This control does not apply any weighting to the signal. weighting filter must always return linear for calibration measurements. The default is linear . Use weighting filter only if the acquired signal has been weighted by an external device before it is acquired by a DAQ device. If a weighting filter was applied to the signal before reaching the DAQ device, using weighting filter prevents subsequent VIs from applying any additional weighting to the weighted signal. engineering units specifies the engineering units. The value is V. If you are using a microphone, select Pa. If you are using an accelerometer, select g, m/s^2, or in/s^2. If the units you need are not listed, select custom and enter the unit to use in custom label . custom label specifies the string used for units when engineering units is set to custom. If engineering units is not set to custom, this parameter is ignored. The default is EU. pregain [dB] specifies the pregain applied by an external amplifier expressed in decibels. A value of 0 dB specifies no pregain. The default is 0.00 dB. Use pregain [dB] only if an external device amplifies the signal before a DAQ device acquires the signal. If you apply any gain with the DAQ device itself, the driver software takes this into account and you do not need to enter the information. |
|  | sensor sensitivity [mV/EU] specifies the sensitivity of the sensor in mV/EU. The default is 1000.00. |
|  | dB reference [EU] specifies the reference value to use when you compute the results in decibels and when you scale a signal to engineering units. dB reference [EU] uses the unit that you specify in engineering units . The default is 1. When engineering units is V, use a dB reference [EU] of 1. When engineering units is m, use a dB reference [EU] of 0.7746. When engineering units is Pa, use a dB reference [EU] of 20E–6. |
|  | weighting filter specifies if any weighting filter has been applied to the signal. This control does not apply any weighting to the signal. weighting filter must always return linear for calibration measurements. The default is linear . Use weighting filter only if the acquired signal has been weighted by an external device before it is acquired by a DAQ device. If a weighting filter was applied to the signal before reaching the DAQ device, using weighting filter prevents subsequent VIs from applying any additional weighting to the weighted signal. |
|  | engineering units specifies the engineering units. The value is V. If you are using a microphone, select Pa. If you are using an accelerometer, select g, m/s^2, or in/s^2. If the units you need are not listed, select custom and enter the unit to use in custom label . |
|  | custom label specifies the string used for units when engineering units is set to custom. If engineering units is not set to custom, this parameter is ignored. The default is EU. |
|  | pregain [dB] specifies the pregain applied by an external amplifier expressed in decibels. A value of 0 dB specifies no pregain. The default is 0.00 dB. Use pregain [dB] only if an external device amplifies the signal before a DAQ device acquires the signal. If you apply any gain with the DAQ device itself, the driver software takes this into account and you do not need to enter the information. |
|  | response channel info specifies information about the response channel. This information defines the output specifications for this VI. sensor sensitivity [mV/EU] specifies the sensitivity of the sensor in mV/EU. The default is 1000.00. dB reference [EU] specifies the reference value to use when you compute the results in decibels and when you scale a signal to engineering units. dB reference [EU] uses the unit that you specify in engineering units . The default is 1. When engineering units is V, use a dB reference [EU] of 1. When engineering units is m, use a dB reference [EU] of 0.7746. When engineering units is Pa, use a dB reference [EU] of 20E–6. weighting filter specifies if any weighting filter has been applied to the signal. This control does not apply any weighting to the signal. weighting filter must always return linear for calibration measurements. The default is linear . Use weighting filter only if the acquired signal has been weighted by an external device before it is acquired by a DAQ device. If a weighting filter was applied to the signal before reaching the DAQ device, using weighting filter prevents subsequent VIs from applying any additional weighting to the weighted signal. engineering units specifies the engineering units. The value is V. If you are using a microphone, select Pa. If you are using an accelerometer, select g, m/s^2, or in/s^2. If the units you need are not listed, select custom and enter the unit to use in custom label . custom label specifies the string used for units when engineering units is set to custom. If engineering units is not set to custom, this parameter is ignored. The default is EU. pregain [dB] specifies the pregain applied by an external amplifier expressed in decibels. A value of 0 dB specifies no pregain. The default is 0.00 dB. Use pregain [dB] only if an external device amplifies the signal before a DAQ device acquires the signal. If you apply any gain with the DAQ device itself, the driver software takes this into account and you do not need to enter the information. |
|  | sensor sensitivity [mV/EU] specifies the sensitivity of the sensor in mV/EU. The default is 1000.00. |
|  | dB reference [EU] specifies the reference value to use when you compute the results in decibels and when you scale a signal to engineering units. dB reference [EU] uses the unit that you specify in engineering units . The default is 1. When engineering units is V, use a dB reference [EU] of 1. When engineering units is m, use a dB reference [EU] of 0.7746. When engineering units is Pa, use a dB reference [EU] of 20E–6. |
|  | weighting filter specifies if any weighting filter has been applied to the signal. This control does not apply any weighting to the signal. weighting filter must always return linear for calibration measurements. The default is linear . Use weighting filter only if the acquired signal has been weighted by an external device before it is acquired by a DAQ device. If a weighting filter was applied to the signal before reaching the DAQ device, using weighting filter prevents subsequent VIs from applying any additional weighting to the weighted signal. |
|  | engineering units specifies the engineering units. The value is V. If you are using a microphone, select Pa. If you are using an accelerometer, select g, m/s^2, or in/s^2. If the units you need are not listed, select custom and enter the unit to use in custom label . |
|  | custom label specifies the string used for units when engineering units is set to custom. If engineering units is not set to custom, this parameter is ignored. The default is EU. |
|  | pregain [dB] specifies the pregain applied by an external amplifier expressed in decibels. A value of 0 dB specifies no pregain. The default is 0.00 dB. Use pregain [dB] only if an external device amplifies the signal before a DAQ device acquires the signal. If you apply any gain with the DAQ device itself, the driver software takes this into account and you do not need to enter the information. |
|  | error in describes error conditions that occur before this node runs. This input provides standard error in functionality. |
|  | swept sine task out returns the swept-sine parameters. |
|  | error out contains error information. This output provides standard error out functionality. |

Example

Refer to the Level and FRF and Distortion with Swept Sine (DAQmx) VI in the 
 labview\examples\Sound and Vibration\Swept Sine
 directory for an example of using the SVT Set Swept Sine EU VI.

<!--NI_TOPIC bundle=labview-sound-and-vibration-toolkit-api-ref path=svt-swept-sine-vi-tree-daqmx-vi.html language=enus -->
## TOPIC 00173: SVT Swept Sine VI Tree (DAQmx) VI

- bundle_id: `labview-sound-and-vibration-toolkit-api-ref`
- source_path: `svt-swept-sine-vi-tree-daqmx-vi.html`
- source_url: https://docs-be.ni.com/bundle/labview-sound-and-vibration-toolkit-api-ref/raw/resource/enus/svt-swept-sine-vi-tree-daqmx-vi.html
- document_id: `labview-sound-and-vibration-toolkit-api-ref`
- page_type: `leaf`
- content_type: `reference`
- source_description: Owning Palette: Stepped Swept Sine VIs Requires: Sound and Vibration Toolkit Shows all the user-level VIs on the Swept Sine VIs palette in a functional format. Performing swept-sine measurements requires all the VIs in the first column of this palette. Other subpalettes offer additional functionalit

### SVT Swept Sine VI Tree (DAQmx) VI

**Owning Palette:**
 Stepped Swept Sine VIs

**Requires:**
 Sound and Vibration Toolkit

Shows all the user-level VIs on the Swept Sine VIs palette in a functional format. Performing swept-sine measurements requires all the VIs in the first column of this palette. Other subpalettes offer additional functionality. Swept-sine measurements developed with the NI Sound and Vibration Toolkit or the Sound and Vibration Measurement Suite follow the data-flow format depicted on the block diagram of this tree VI.

Details

[IMAGE alt='image' src='images/SVT_Swept_Sine_VI_Tree_DAQmx.gif']

SVT Swept Sine VI Tree (DAQmx) Details

The SVT Initialize Swept Sine (DAQmx) VI sets the hardware device settings.

The Configure Swept Sine VIs configure the required measurement parameters.

The SVT Set Swept Sine Distortion Settings VI is required only if you intend to perform harmonic analysis.

The Configure Advanced Settings VIs (optional) configure specific measurement and data acquisition parameters.

The Get Swept Sine Configuration VIs (optional) get specific measurement and data acquisition parameters.

The SVT Start Swept Sine (DAQmx) VI performs the hardware configuration and starts the acquisition.

The SVT Swept Sine Engine (DAQmx) VI controls the generation of the test signal on the output channel (AO) and the acquisition and processing of the data on the stimulus and response channels (AI).

The Read Swept Sine Measurements VIs extract the desired measurements from the low-level measurements computed by the swept-sine engine.

The SVT Close Swept Sine (DAQmx) VI stops the generation and acquisition and clears the tasks associated with output and input.

Parent topic:

Stepped Swept Sine VIs

<!--NI_TOPIC bundle=labview-sound-and-vibration-toolkit-api-ref path=svt-telecommunications-weighting-filter-fixed.html language=enus -->
## TOPIC 00174: SVT Telecommunications Weighting Filter (Fixed Rates) VI

- bundle_id: `labview-sound-and-vibration-toolkit-api-ref`
- source_path: `svt-telecommunications-weighting-filter-fixed.html`
- source_url: https://docs-be.ni.com/bundle/labview-sound-and-vibration-toolkit-api-ref/raw/resource/enus/svt-telecommunications-weighting-filter-fixed.html
- document_id: `labview-sound-and-vibration-toolkit-api-ref`
- page_type: `leaf`
- content_type: `reference`
- source_description: Owning Palette: Weighting and Filtering VIs Requires: Sound and Vibration Toolkit Applies the selected telecommunications weighting filter to a single waveform or multiple waveforms. This VI complies with ITU-T O.41 and Bell System Technical Reference 41009 standards. Each instance of this VI mainta

### SVT Telecommunications Weighting Filter (Fixed Rates) VI

**Owning Palette:**
 Weighting and Filtering VIs

**Requires:**
 Sound and Vibration Toolkit

Applies the selected telecommunications weighting filter to a single waveform or multiple waveforms. This VI complies with ITU-T O.41 and Bell System Technical Reference 41009 standards. Each instance of this VI maintains its own state; use the N Waveforms instance of this VI to process an array of channels. This VI operates on time-continuous data; it resets on a discontinuity. Wire data to the
 **scaled signal [EU]**
 input to determine the polymorphic instance to use or manually select the instance.

Example

| SVT Telecommunications Weighting Filter (Fixed Rates) (1 Waveform)SVT Telecommunications Weighting Filter (Fixed Rates) (N Waveforms) |
| --- |

SVT Telecommunications Weighting Filter (Fixed Rates) (1 Waveform)

[IMAGE alt='image' src='images/SVT_Telecommunications_Weighting_Filter_Fixed_Rates_1_Ch.gif']

|  | scaled signal [EU] specifies the scaled signal expressed in the selected engineering units. To obtain a properly scaled signal, you can use the SVL Scale Voltage to EU VI or define a virtual channel in National Instruments Measurement & Automation Explorer (MAX). t0 specifies the start time of the waveform. dt specifies the time interval in seconds between data points in the waveform. Y specifies the data values of the waveform. |
| --- | --- |
|  | t0 specifies the start time of the waveform. |
|  | dt specifies the time interval in seconds between data points in the waveform. |
|  | Y specifies the data values of the waveform. |
|  | weighting specifies the type of weighting to apply to the spectrum. The weighting is applied to the spectrum in the frequency domain. Use Linear if no weighting is needed. 0 Linear (default) 6 CCITT 7 C-message |
| 0 | Linear (default) |
| 6 | CCITT |
| 7 | C-message |
|  | error in describes error conditions that occur before this node runs. This input provides standard error in functionality. |
|  | reset filter specifies whether this VI resets the weighting filter. The default is FALSE. The first time you call this VI, the filter restarts automatically. |
|  | weighted signal [EU] returns the weighted signal based on the signal and weighting input specifications. t0 returns the start time of the waveform. dt returns the time interval, in seconds, between data points in the waveform. Y returns the data values of the waveform. |
|  | t0 returns the start time of the waveform. |
|  | dt returns the time interval, in seconds, between data points in the waveform. |
|  | Y returns the data values of the waveform. |
|  | error out contains error information. This output provides standard error out functionality. |

SVT Telecommunications Weighting Filter (Fixed Rates) (N Waveforms)

[IMAGE alt='image' src='images/SVT_Telecommunications_Weighting_Filter_Fixed_Rates_N_Ch.gif']

|  | scaled signals [EU] specifies the scaled signals expressed in the selected engineering units. To obtain a properly scaled signal, you can use the SVL Scale Voltage to EU VI or define virtual channels in National Instruments Measurement & Automation Explorer (MAX). t0 specifies the start time of the waveform. dt specifies the time interval in seconds between data points in the waveform. Y specifies the data values of the waveform. |
| --- | --- |
|  | t0 specifies the start time of the waveform. |
|  | dt specifies the time interval in seconds between data points in the waveform. |
|  | Y specifies the data values of the waveform. |
|  | weighting specifies the type of weighting to apply to the spectrum. The weighting is applied to the spectrum in the frequency domain. Use Linear if no weighting is needed. 0 Linear (default) 6 CCITT 7 C-message |
| 0 | Linear (default) |
| 6 | CCITT |
| 7 | C-message |
|  | error in describes error conditions that occur before this node runs. This input provides standard error in functionality. |
|  | reset filter specifies whether this VI resets the weighting filter. The default is FALSE. The first time you call this VI, the filter restarts automatically. |
|  | weighted signals [EU] returns the weighted signals based on the signal and weighting input specifications. t0 returns the start time of the waveform. dt returns the time interval, in seconds, between data points in the waveform. Y returns the data values of the waveform. |
|  | t0 returns the start time of the waveform. |
|  | dt returns the time interval, in seconds, between data points in the waveform. |
|  | Y returns the data values of the waveform. |
|  | error out contains error information. This output provides standard error out functionality. |

Example

Refer to the Weighting Filters (Simulated) VI in the 
 labview\examples\Sound and Vibration\Weighting Filters
 directory for an example of using the SVT Telecommunications Weighting Filter (Fixed Rates) VI.
