## TOPIC 00193: SVT UFF58 Write Record 6 VI

- bundle_id: `labview-sound-and-vibration-toolkit-api-ref`
- source_path: `svt-uff58-write-record-6-vi.html`
- source_url: https://docs-be.ni.com/bundle/labview-sound-and-vibration-toolkit-api-ref/raw/resource/enus/svt-uff58-write-record-6-vi.html
- document_id: `labview-sound-and-vibration-toolkit-api-ref`
- page_type: `leaf`
- content_type: `reference`
- source_description: Owning Palette: UFF58 Advanced VIs Requires: Sound and Vibration Toolkit Writes the values of the fields in record 6 of the UFF58 (universal file format) file. Record 6 contains the degree of freedom identification. Details function refnum specifies the reference to a function in the UFF58 file. DOF

### SVT UFF58 Write Record 6 VI

**Owning Palette:**
 UFF58 Advanced VIs

**Requires:**
 Sound and Vibration Toolkit

Writes the values of the fields in record 6 of the UFF58 (universal file format) file. Record 6 contains the degree of freedom identification.

Details

[IMAGE alt='image' src='images/SVT_UFF58_Write_Record_6.gif']

|  | function refnum specifies the reference to a function in the UFF58 file. |
| --- | --- |
|  | DOF identification contains information about the degree of freedom. function type specifies the function type. 0 general or unknown (default) 1 time response 2 auto spectrum 3 cross spectrum 4 frequency response function 5 transmissibility 6 coherence 7 auto correlation 8 cross correlation 9 power spectral density (PSD) 10 energy spectral density (ESD) 11 probability density function 12 spectrum 13 cumulative frequency distribution 14 peaks valley 15 stress/cycles 16 strain/cycles 17 orbit 18 mode indicator function 19 force pattern 20 partial power 21 partial Coherence 22 eigenvalue 23 eigenvector 24 shock response spectrum 25 finite impulse response (FIR) filter 26 multiple coherence 27 order function function ID number specifies the function identification number. version number specifies the version number or sequence number. load case ID number specifies the load case identification number. A value of 0 indicates single point excitation. response entity name specifies the response entity name. Enter NONE if it this input is unused. response node specifies the response node. response direction specifies the direction of the associated response. The numeric value associated with the selected enumeration is written to record 6, field 7. -1 -X Translation -2 -Y Translation -3 -Z Translation -4 -X Rotation -5 -Y Rotation -6 -Z Rotation 0 Scalar (default) 1 +X Translation 2 +Y Translation 3 +Z Translation 4 +X Rotation 5 +Y Rotation 6 +Z Rotation reference entity name specifies the reference entity name. Enter NONE if this input is unused. reference node specifies the reference node. reference direction specifies the reference direction. -1 -X Translation -2 -Y Translation -3 -Z Translation -4 -X Rotation -5 -Y Rotation -6 -Z Rotation 0 Scalar (default) 1 +X Translation 2 +Y Translation 3 +Z Translation 4 +X Rotation 5 +Y Rotation 6 +Z Rotation |
|  | function type specifies the function type. 0 general or unknown (default) 1 time response 2 auto spectrum 3 cross spectrum 4 frequency response function 5 transmissibility 6 coherence 7 auto correlation 8 cross correlation 9 power spectral density (PSD) 10 energy spectral density (ESD) 11 probability density function 12 spectrum 13 cumulative frequency distribution 14 peaks valley 15 stress/cycles 16 strain/cycles 17 orbit 18 mode indicator function 19 force pattern 20 partial power 21 partial Coherence 22 eigenvalue 23 eigenvector 24 shock response spectrum 25 finite impulse response (FIR) filter 26 multiple coherence 27 order function |
| 0 | general or unknown (default) |
| 1 | time response |
| 2 | auto spectrum |
| 3 | cross spectrum |
| 4 | frequency response function |
| 5 | transmissibility |
| 6 | coherence |
| 7 | auto correlation |
| 8 | cross correlation |
| 9 | power spectral density (PSD) |
| 10 | energy spectral density (ESD) |
| 11 | probability density function |
| 12 | spectrum |
| 13 | cumulative frequency distribution |
| 14 | peaks valley |
| 15 | stress/cycles |
| 16 | strain/cycles |
| 17 | orbit |
| 18 | mode indicator function |
| 19 | force pattern |
| 20 | partial power |
| 21 | partial Coherence |
| 22 | eigenvalue |
| 23 | eigenvector |
| 24 | shock response spectrum |
| 25 | finite impulse response (FIR) filter |
| 26 | multiple coherence |
| 27 | order function |
|  | function ID number specifies the function identification number. |
|  | version number specifies the version number or sequence number. |
|  | load case ID number specifies the load case identification number. A value of 0 indicates single point excitation. |
|  | response entity name specifies the response entity name. Enter NONE if it this input is unused. |
|  | response node specifies the response node. |
|  | response direction specifies the direction of the associated response. The numeric value associated with the selected enumeration is written to record 6, field 7. -1 -X Translation -2 -Y Translation -3 -Z Translation -4 -X Rotation -5 -Y Rotation -6 -Z Rotation 0 Scalar (default) 1 +X Translation 2 +Y Translation 3 +Z Translation 4 +X Rotation 5 +Y Rotation 6 +Z Rotation |
| -1 | -X Translation |
| -2 | -Y Translation |
| -3 | -Z Translation |
| -4 | -X Rotation |
| -5 | -Y Rotation |
| -6 | -Z Rotation |
| 0 | Scalar (default) |
| 1 | +X Translation |
| 2 | +Y Translation |
| 3 | +Z Translation |
| 4 | +X Rotation |
| 5 | +Y Rotation |
| 6 | +Z Rotation |
|  | reference entity name specifies the reference entity name. Enter NONE if this input is unused. |
|  | reference node specifies the reference node. |
|  | reference direction specifies the reference direction. -1 -X Translation -2 -Y Translation -3 -Z Translation -4 -X Rotation -5 -Y Rotation -6 -Z Rotation 0 Scalar (default) 1 +X Translation 2 +Y Translation 3 +Z Translation 4 +X Rotation 5 +Y Rotation 6 +Z Rotation |
| -1 | -X Translation |
| -2 | -Y Translation |
| -3 | -Z Translation |
| -4 | -X Rotation |
| -5 | -Y Rotation |
| -6 | -Z Rotation |
| 0 | Scalar (default) |
| 1 | +X Translation |
| 2 | +Y Translation |
| 3 | +Z Translation |
| 4 | +X Rotation |
| 5 | +Y Rotation |
| 6 | +Z Rotation |
|  | error in describes error conditions that occur before this node runs. This input provides standard error in functionality. |
|  | dup function refnum returns the same value as the function refnum input. You can wire this output to another VI. You can wire this output to the file refnum input of another UFF58b or UFF58 Advanced VI. |
|  | error out contains error information. This output provides standard error out functionality. |

Enter NONE if this parameter is not used. axis units label specifies the axis units label. Enter NONE if this parameter is not used. |

| 3 | strain |
| 4 | temperature |
| 5 | heat flux |
| 6 | displacement |
| 7 | reaction force |

| 11 | pressure |

| 14 | frequency |

| 18 | sound intensity |

|  | error in describes error conditions that occur before this node runs. This input provides standard error in functionality. |

- bundle_id: `labview-sound-and-vibration-toolkit-api-ref`

- source_description: Owning Palette: UFF58 Advanced VIs Requires: Sound and Vibration Toolkit Writes the values of the fields in record 9 of the UFF58 (universal file format) file. Record 9 contains the ordinate or ordinate numerator data characteristics. Use this VI with the SVT UFF58 Write Record 10 VI if you need to

### SVT UFF58 Write Record 9 VI

**Owning Palette:**
 UFF58 Advanced VIs

**Requires:**
 Sound and Vibration Toolkit

Writes the values of the fields in record 9 of the UFF58 (universal file format) file. Record 9 contains the ordinate or ordinate numerator data characteristics. Use this VI with the SVT UFF58 Write Record 10 VI if you need to write ordinate numerator and ordinate denominator data characteristics.

Details

[IMAGE alt='image' src='images/SVT_UFF58_Write_Record_9.gif']

|  | function refnum specifies the reference to a function in the UFF58 file. |
| --- | --- |
|  | ordinate data characteristics sets the ordinate or ordinate numerator data characteristics in record 9 of the UFF58 file. specific data type specifies the data type information. 0 unknown (default) 1 general 2 stress 3 strain 4 temperature 5 heat flux 6 displacement 7 reaction force 8 velocity 9 acceleration 10 excitation force 11 pressure 12 mass 13 time 14 frequency 15 rpm 16 order 17 sound pressure 18 sound intensity 19 sound power length units exponent specifies the length units exponent. force units exponent specifies the force units exponent. temperature units exponent specifies the temperature units exponent. axis label specifies the axis label. Enter NONE if this parameter is not used. axis units label specifies the axis units label. Enter NONE if this parameter is not used. |
|  | specific data type specifies the data type information. 0 unknown (default) 1 general 2 stress 3 strain 4 temperature 5 heat flux 6 displacement 7 reaction force 8 velocity 9 acceleration 10 excitation force 11 pressure 12 mass 13 time 14 frequency 15 rpm 16 order 17 sound pressure 18 sound intensity 19 sound power |
| 0 | unknown (default) |
| 1 | general |
| 2 | stress |
| 3 | strain |
| 4 | temperature |
| 5 | heat flux |
| 6 | displacement |
| 7 | reaction force |
| 8 | velocity |
| 9 | acceleration |
| 10 | excitation force |
| 11 | pressure |
| 12 | mass |
| 13 | time |
| 14 | frequency |
| 15 | rpm |
| 16 | order |
| 17 | sound pressure |
| 18 | sound intensity |
| 19 | sound power |
|  | length units exponent specifies the length units exponent. |
|  | force units exponent specifies the force units exponent. |
|  | temperature units exponent specifies the temperature units exponent. |
|  | axis label specifies the axis label. Enter NONE if this parameter is not used. |
|  | axis units label specifies the axis units label. Enter NONE if this parameter is not used. |
|  | error in describes error conditions that occur before this node runs. This input provides standard error in functionality. |
|  | dup function refnum returns the same value as the function refnum input. You can wire this output to another VI. You can wire this output to the file refnum input of another UFF58b or UFF58 Advanced VI. |
|  | error out contains error information. This output provides standard error out functionality. |

## TOPIC 00196: SVT Weighting Filter VI

- bundle_id: `labview-sound-and-vibration-toolkit-api-ref`
- source_path: `svt-weighting-filter-vi.html`
- source_url: https://docs-be.ni.com/bundle/labview-sound-and-vibration-toolkit-api-ref/raw/resource/enus/svt-weighting-filter-vi.html
- document_id: `labview-sound-and-vibration-toolkit-api-ref`
- page_type: `leaf`
- content_type: `reference`
- source_description: Owning Palette: Weighting (Arbitrary Rate) VIs Requires: Sound and Vibration Toolkit Applies the selected weighting to scaled signal [EU] . This VI complies with ANSI S1.4-1983, ANSI S1.42-2001, IEC 61672-1:2002, and JIS C 1509-1:2005 standards. Use Each instance of this VI maintains its own state;

### SVT Weighting Filter VI

**Owning Palette:**
 Weighting (Arbitrary Rate) VIs

**Requires:**
 Sound and Vibration Toolkit

Applies the selected weighting to 
 **scaled signal [EU]**
 . This VI complies with ANSI S1.4-1983, ANSI S1.42-2001, IEC 61672-1:2002, and JIS C 1509-1:2005 standards. Use Each instance of this VI maintains its own state; use the N Channel instance of this VI to process an array of channels. This VI operates on time-continuous data; it resets on a discontinuity. Wire data to the 
 **scaled signal [EU]**
 input to determine the polymorphic instance to use or manually select the instance.

Example

| SVT Weighting Filter (1 Ch)SVT Weighting Filter (N Ch) |
| --- |

SVT Weighting Filter (1 Ch)

[IMAGE alt='image' src='images/SVT_Weighting_Filter_1_Ch.gif']

|  | scaled signal [EU] specifies the scaled signal expressed in the selected engineering units. To obtain a properly scaled signal, you can use the SVL Scale Voltage to EU VI or define a virtual channel in National Instruments Measurement & Automation Explorer (MAX). t0 specifies the start time of the waveform. dt specifies the time interval in seconds between data points in the waveform. Y specifies the data values of the waveform. |
| --- | --- |
|  | t0 specifies the start time of the waveform. |
|  | dt specifies the time interval in seconds between data points in the waveform. |
|  | Y specifies the data values of the waveform. |
|  | weighting specifies the weighting to apply to the signal. The weighting is applied in the time domain. Use Linear if no weighting is needed. 0 Linear (default) 1 A-weighting 2 B-weighting 3 C-weighting |
| 0 | Linear (default) |
| 1 | A-weighting |
| 2 | B-weighting |
| 3 | C-weighting |
|  | error in describes error conditions that occur before this node runs. This input provides standard error in functionality. |
|  | reset filter specifies whether this VI resets the weighting filter. The default is FALSE. The first time you call this VI, the filter restarts automatically. |
|  | weighted signal [EU] returns the weighted signal based on the signal and weighting input specifications. t0 returns the start time of the waveform. dt returns the time interval, in seconds, between data points in the waveform. Y returns the data values of the waveform. |
|  | t0 returns the start time of the waveform. |
|  | dt returns the time interval, in seconds, between data points in the waveform. |
|  | Y returns the data values of the waveform. |
|  | error out contains error information. This output provides standard error out functionality. |

SVT Weighting Filter (N Ch)

[IMAGE alt='image' src='images/SVT_Weighting_Filter_N_Ch.gif']

|  | scaled signals [EU] specifies the scaled signals expressed in the selected engineering units. To obtain a properly scaled signal, you can use the SVL Scale Voltage to EU VI or define virtual channels in National Instruments Measurement & Automation Explorer (MAX). t0 specifies the start time of the waveform. dt specifies the time interval in seconds between data points in the waveform. Y specifies the data values of the waveform. |
| --- | --- |
|  | t0 specifies the start time of the waveform. |
|  | dt specifies the time interval in seconds between data points in the waveform. |
|  | Y specifies the data values of the waveform. |
|  | weighting specifies the weighting to apply to the signal. The weighting is applied in the time domain. Use Linear if no weighting is needed. 0 Linear (default) 1 A-weighting 2 B-weighting 3 C-weighting |
| 0 | Linear (default) |
| 1 | A-weighting |
| 2 | B-weighting |
| 3 | C-weighting |
|  | error in describes error conditions that occur before this node runs. This input provides standard error in functionality. |
|  | reset filter specifies whether this VI resets the weighting filter. The default is FALSE. The first time you call this VI, the filter restarts automatically. |
|  | weighted signals [EU] returns the weighted signals based on the signal and weighting input specifications. t0 returns the start time of the waveform. dt returns the time interval, in seconds, between data points in the waveform. Y returns the data values of the waveform. |
|  | t0 returns the start time of the waveform. |
|  | dt returns the time interval, in seconds, between data points in the waveform. |
|  | Y returns the data values of the waveform. |
|  | error out contains error information. This output provides standard error out functionality. |

Example

Refer to the Sound Level Meter (DAQmx) VI in the 
 labview\examples\Sound and Vibration\Sound Level Measurements
 directory for an example of using the SVT Weighting Filter VI.

[IMAGE alt='image' src='images/open.gif']
 Open example 
 
 [IMAGE alt='image' src='images/find.gif']
 Find related examples

Parent topic:

Weighting (Arbitrary Rate) VIs

<!--NI_TOPIC bundle=labview-sound-and-vibration-toolkit-api-ref path=svx-buffer-orbit-timebase-plot-vi.html language=enus -->
## TOPIC 00198: SVX Buffer Orbit Timebase Plot VI

- bundle_id: `labview-sound-and-vibration-toolkit-api-ref`
- source_path: `svx-buffer-orbit-timebase-plot-vi.html`
- source_url: https://docs-be.ni.com/bundle/labview-sound-and-vibration-toolkit-api-ref/raw/resource/enus/svx-buffer-orbit-timebase-plot-vi.html
- document_id: `labview-sound-and-vibration-toolkit-api-ref`
- page_type: `leaf`
- content_type: `reference`
- source_description: Owning Palette: Machine Vibration VIs Requires: Sound and Vibration Toolkit Buffers orbit or timebase plot data and returns the buffered data. This VI requires continuous input data. The buffered data might have gaps between or overlap with previous buffers. Do not use the buffered data for limit te

### SVX Buffer Orbit Timebase Plot VI

**Owning Palette:**
 Machine Vibration VIs

**Requires:**
 Sound and Vibration Toolkit

Buffers orbit or timebase plot data and returns the buffered data. This VI requires continuous input data. The buffered data might have gaps between or overlap with previous buffers. Do not use the buffered data for limit testing or data logging.

[IMAGE alt='image' src='images/SVX_Buffer_Orbit_Timebase_Plot.gif']

|  | reset? specifies the initialization of the internal states. The first time this VI runs, or when reset? is TRUE, the internal states initialize to zero. If reset? is FALSE, this VI sets the internal states to the final states from the previous call to the VI. The default is FALSE. |
| --- | --- |
|  | orbit/timebase plot in specifies the buffered orbit or timebase data. This data type is identical to orbit plot and timebase plot. orbit/timebase plot attribute includes direction information. The number of revolutions contained in orbit/timebase plot determines the order resolution in the output full spectrum . probe x specifies the amplitude of probe X. probe y specifies the amplitude of probe Y. reference specifies the time reference of the probe X and probe Y. plot type specifies the type of the input orbit or timebase plot. The default is filtered plot. 0 Filtered Plot (default) 1 Unfiltered Plot attribute specifies additional information about the plot. LabVIEW uses this information to display the data properly and to perform extended measurements. |
|  | probe x specifies the amplitude of probe X. |
|  | probe y specifies the amplitude of probe Y. |
|  | reference specifies the time reference of the probe X and probe Y. |
|  | plot type specifies the type of the input orbit or timebase plot. The default is filtered plot. 0 Filtered Plot (default) 1 Unfiltered Plot |
| 0 | Filtered Plot (default) |
| 1 | Unfiltered Plot |
|  | attribute specifies additional information about the plot. LabVIEW uses this information to display the data properly and to perform extended measurements. |
|  | buffer size (rev) specifies the maximum number of revolutions to buffer for display. The default is 1 rev. |
|  | error in describes error conditions that occur before this node runs. This input provides standard error in functionality. |
|  | orbit/timebase plot out returns the buffered orbit plot or timebase plot. probe x returns the buffered amplitude of probe X. probe y returns the buffered amplitude of probe Y. reference returns the buffered time reference of the probe X and probe Y. plot type returns the type of the buffered orbit or timebase plot. The default is filtered plot. 0 Filtered Plot (default) 1 Unfiltered Plot attribute returns additional information about the buffered plot. LabVIEW uses this information to display the data properly and to perform extended measurements. |
|  | probe x returns the buffered amplitude of probe X. |
|  | probe y returns the buffered amplitude of probe Y. |
|  | reference returns the buffered time reference of the probe X and probe Y. |
|  | plot type returns the type of the buffered orbit or timebase plot. The default is filtered plot. 0 Filtered Plot (default) 1 Unfiltered Plot |
| 0 | Filtered Plot (default) |
| 1 | Unfiltered Plot |
|  | attribute returns additional information about the buffered plot. LabVIEW uses this information to display the data properly and to perform extended measurements. |
|  | error out contains error information. This output provides standard error out functionality. |

Parent topic:

Machine Vibration VIs

<!--NI_TOPIC bundle=labview-sound-and-vibration-toolkit-api-ref path=svx-buffer-polar-plot-vi.html language=enus -->
## TOPIC 00199: SVX Buffer Polar Plot VI

- bundle_id: `labview-sound-and-vibration-toolkit-api-ref`
- source_path: `svx-buffer-polar-plot-vi.html`
- source_url: https://docs-be.ni.com/bundle/labview-sound-and-vibration-toolkit-api-ref/raw/resource/enus/svx-buffer-polar-plot-vi.html
- document_id: `labview-sound-and-vibration-toolkit-api-ref`
- page_type: `leaf`
- content_type: `reference`
- source_description: Owning Palette: Machine Vibration VIs Requires: Sound and Vibration Toolkit Buffers polar plot data according to different buffer modes. This VI requires continuous input data. The buffered data might have gaps between or overlap with previous buffers. Do not use the buffered data for limit testing

### SVX Buffer Polar Plot VI

**Owning Palette:**
 Machine Vibration VIs

**Requires:**
 Sound and Vibration Toolkit

Buffers polar plot data according to different buffer modes. This VI requires continuous input data. The buffered data might have gaps between or overlap with previous buffers. Do not use the buffered data for limit testing or data logging.

[IMAGE alt='image' src='images/SVX_Buffer_Polar_Plot.gif']

|  | reset? specifies the initialization of the internal states. The first time this VI runs, or when reset? is TRUE, the internal states initialize to zero. If reset? is FALSE, this VI sets the internal states to the final states from the previous call to the VI. The default is FALSE. |
| --- | --- |
|  | buffer mode specifies the mode to buffer the input polar plot data. 0 Infinite length —Specifies to buffer the input polar plot data from the first block to the current block. 1 Last block data (default)—Specifies to buffer the input polar plot data for the current block. 2 Custom —Specifies to buffer the input polar plot data according to the value you specify in buffer size . |
| 0 | Infinite length —Specifies to buffer the input polar plot data from the first block to the current block. |
| 1 | Last block data (default)—Specifies to buffer the input polar plot data for the current block. |
| 2 | Custom —Specifies to buffer the input polar plot data according to the value you specify in buffer size . |
|  | polar plot data in specifies the input polar plot data values. magnitude specifies the magnitude value of the input polar plot data. phase specifies the phase value of the input polar plot data. time/rev/RPM specifies the time, revolution, or speed at which you measured the magnitude and phase . attribute specifies additional information about the input polar plot data. LabVIEW uses this information to display the data properly and to perform extended measurements. |
|  | magnitude specifies the magnitude value of the input polar plot data. |
|  | phase specifies the phase value of the input polar plot data. |
|  | time/rev/RPM specifies the time, revolution, or speed at which you measured the magnitude and phase . |
|  | attribute specifies additional information about the input polar plot data. LabVIEW uses this information to display the data properly and to perform extended measurements. |
|  | buffer size specifies the maximum number of points to buffer for display. Use buffer size when you set buffer mode to Custom . |
|  | error in describes error conditions that occur before this node runs. This input provides standard error in functionality. |
|  | polar plot data out returns the buffered polar plot data values. magnitude returns the magnitude value of the buffered polar plot data. phase returns the phase value of the buffered polar plot data. time/rev/RPM returns the time, revolution, or speed at which this VI returns the buffered magnitude and phase . attribute returns additional information about the buffered polar plot data. LabVIEW uses this information to display the data properly and to perform extended measurements. |
|  | magnitude returns the magnitude value of the buffered polar plot data. |
|  | phase returns the phase value of the buffered polar plot data. |
|  | time/rev/RPM returns the time, revolution, or speed at which this VI returns the buffered magnitude and phase . |
|  | attribute returns additional information about the buffered polar plot data. LabVIEW uses this information to display the data properly and to perform extended measurements. |
|  | error out contains error information. This output provides standard error out functionality. |

Parent topic:

Machine Vibration VIs

## TOPIC 00200: SVX Buffer Shaft Centerline Plot VI

- bundle_id: `labview-sound-and-vibration-toolkit-api-ref`
- source_path: `svx-buffer-shaft-centerline-plot-vi.html`
- source_url: https://docs-be.ni.com/bundle/labview-sound-and-vibration-toolkit-api-ref/raw/resource/enus/svx-buffer-shaft-centerline-plot-vi.html
- document_id: `labview-sound-and-vibration-toolkit-api-ref`
- page_type: `leaf`
- content_type: `reference`
- source_description: Owning Palette: Machine Vibration VIs Requires: Sound and Vibration Toolkit Buffers shaft centerline plot data according to different buffer modes. This VI requires continuous input data. The buffered data might have gaps between or overlap with previous buffers. Do not use the buffered data for lim

### SVX Buffer Shaft Centerline Plot VI

**Owning Palette:**
 Machine Vibration VIs

**Requires:**
 Sound and Vibration Toolkit

Buffers shaft centerline plot data according to different buffer modes. This VI requires continuous input data. The buffered data might have gaps between or overlap with previous buffers. Do not use the buffered data for limit testing or data logging.

[IMAGE alt='image' src='images/SVX_Buffer_Shaft_Centerline_Plot.gif']

|  | reset? specifies the initialization of the internal states. The first time this VI runs, or when reset? is TRUE, the internal states initialize to zero. If reset? is FALSE, this VI sets the internal states to the final states from the previous call to the VI. The default is FALSE. |
| --- | --- |
|  | buffer mode specifies the mode to buffer the input shaft centerline plot data. 0 Infinite length —Specifies to buffer the input shaft centerline plot signals from the first block to the current block. 1 Last block data (default)—Specifies to buffer the input shaft centerline plot signals for the current block. 2 Custom —Specifies to buffer the input shaft centerline plot signals according to the value you specify in buffer size (samples) . |
| 0 | Infinite length —Specifies to buffer the input shaft centerline plot signals from the first block to the current block. |
| 1 | Last block data (default)—Specifies to buffer the input shaft centerline plot signals for the current block. |
| 2 | Custom —Specifies to buffer the input shaft centerline plot signals according to the value you specify in buffer size (samples) . |
|  | shaft centerline in specifies the input shaft centerline plot data. attribute specifies additional information about the input shaft centerline plot data. LabVIEW uses this information to display the data properly and to perform extended measurements. probe x specifies the DC gap value of the probe X. probe y specifies the DC gap value of the probe Y. reference specifies the DC gap reference of the input shaft centerline plot data. |
|  | attribute specifies additional information about the input shaft centerline plot data. LabVIEW uses this information to display the data properly and to perform extended measurements. |
|  | probe x specifies the DC gap value of the probe X. |
|  | probe y specifies the DC gap value of the probe Y. |
|  | reference specifies the DC gap reference of the input shaft centerline plot data. |
|  | buffer size (samples) specifies the maximum number of samples to buffer for display. Use buffer size (samples) when you set buffer mode to Custom . |
|  | error in describes error conditions that occur before this node runs. This input provides standard error in functionality. |
|  | shaft centerline out returns the buffered shaft centerline plot data. attribute returns additional information about the buffered shaft centerline plot data. LabVIEW uses this information to display the data properly and to perform extended measurements. probe x returns the buffered DC gap value of the probe X. probe y returns the buffered DC gap value of the probe Y. reference returns the DC gap reference of the buffered shaft centerline plot data. |
|  | attribute returns additional information about the buffered shaft centerline plot data. LabVIEW uses this information to display the data properly and to perform extended measurements. |
|  | probe x returns the buffered DC gap value of the probe X. |
|  | probe y returns the buffered DC gap value of the probe Y. |
|  | reference returns the DC gap reference of the buffered shaft centerline plot data. |
|  | error out contains error information. This output provides standard error out functionality. |

## TOPIC 00201: SVX Buffer Waterfall Graph VI

- bundle_id: `labview-sound-and-vibration-toolkit-api-ref`
- source_path: `svx-buffer-waterfall-graph-vi.html`
- source_url: https://docs-be.ni.com/bundle/labview-sound-and-vibration-toolkit-api-ref/raw/resource/enus/svx-buffer-waterfall-graph-vi.html
- document_id: `labview-sound-and-vibration-toolkit-api-ref`
- page_type: `leaf`
- content_type: `reference`
- source_description: Owning Palette: Transient Spectra VIs Requires: Sound and Vibration Toolkit Buffers signals and returns the buffered signals up to a maximum count. If you use this VI with the Waterfall Graph, the display mimics the behavior of a chart. Wire data to the waterfall graph data input to determine the po

### SVX Buffer Waterfall Graph VI

**Owning Palette:**
 Transient Spectra VIs

**Requires:**
 Sound and Vibration Toolkit

Buffers signals and returns the buffered signals up to a maximum count. If you use this VI with the Waterfall Graph, the display mimics the behavior of a chart. Wire data to the 
 **waterfall graph data**
 input to determine the polymorphic instance to use or manually select the instance.

| SVX Buffer Waterfall Graph (Waterfall Data)SVX Buffer Waterfall Graph (N Traces)SVX Buffer Waterfall Graph (1 Trace) |
| --- |

SVX Buffer Waterfall Graph (Waterfall Data)

[IMAGE alt='image' src='images/SVX_Buffer_Waterfall_Graph_Waterfall_Data.gif']

|  | reset on change to dx specifies whether the buffering VI checks for consistency in dx between the input data and the buffered data. If reset on change to dx (F) is TRUE and the dx of the input data is not equal to the dx of the buffered data, the buffer resets. The default is FALSE. |
| --- | --- |
|  | reset? clears the buffer. The default is FALSE. |
|  | waterfall graph data specifies an array of 2D waveform signals in three dimensions. z0 specifies the initial value of the first plot on the z-axis. dz specifies the interval between plots on the z-axis. plots specifies the data on the waterfall graph. x0 specifies the initial value on the x-axis. dx specifies the interval between points on the waterfall graph. Y specifies the values of the points on the waterfall graph. |
|  | z0 specifies the initial value of the first plot on the z-axis. |
|  | dz specifies the interval between plots on the z-axis. |
|  | plots specifies the data on the waterfall graph. x0 specifies the initial value on the x-axis. dx specifies the interval between points on the waterfall graph. Y specifies the values of the points on the waterfall graph. |
|  | x0 specifies the initial value on the x-axis. |
|  | dx specifies the interval between points on the waterfall graph. |
|  | Y specifies the values of the points on the waterfall graph. |
|  | error in describes error conditions that occur before this node runs. This input provides standard error in functionality. |
|  | number of spectra specifies the maximum number of spectra to buffer for display. number of spectra is similar to the chart history of a chart. The default is 40 spectra. |
|  | waterfall graph data out returns an array of 2D waveform signals in three dimensions. You can display this information with the Waterfall Graph. z0 returns the initial value of the first plot on the z-axis. dz returns the interval between plots on the z-axis. plots returns the data on the waterfall graph. x0 returns the initial value on the x-axis. dx returns the interval between points on the waterfall graph. Y returns the values of the points on the waterfall graph. |
|  | z0 returns the initial value of the first plot on the z-axis. |
|  | dz returns the interval between plots on the z-axis. |
|  | plots returns the data on the waterfall graph. x0 returns the initial value on the x-axis. dx returns the interval between points on the waterfall graph. Y returns the values of the points on the waterfall graph. |
|  | x0 returns the initial value on the x-axis. |
|  | dx returns the interval between points on the waterfall graph. |
|  | Y returns the values of the points on the waterfall graph. |
|  | error out contains error information. This output provides standard error out functionality. |

SVX Buffer Waterfall Graph (N Traces)

[IMAGE alt='image' src='images/SVX_Buffer_Waterfall_Graph_Plot_Array.gif']

|  | reset on change to dx specifies whether the buffering VI checks for consistency in dx between the input data and the buffered data. If reset on change to dx (F) is TRUE and the dx of the input data is not equal to the dx of the buffered data, the buffer resets. The default is FALSE. |
| --- | --- |
|  | reset? clears the buffer. The default is FALSE. |
|  | plots specifies the data on the waterfall graph. x0 specifies the initial value on the x-axis. dx specifies the interval between points on the waterfall graph. Y specifies the values of the points on the waterfall graph. |
|  | x0 specifies the initial value on the x-axis. |
|  | dx specifies the interval between points on the waterfall graph. |
|  | Y specifies the values of the points on the waterfall graph. |
|  | initial z0 specifies the initial value of the first plot on the z-axis. initial z0 is ignored after the first plot unless reset? (F) is TRUE. |
|  | dz specifies the interval between plots on the z-axis. |
|  | error in describes error conditions that occur before this node runs. This input provides standard error in functionality. |
|  | number of spectra specifies the maximum number of spectra to buffer for display. number of spectra is similar to the chart history of a chart. The default is 40 spectra. |
|  | waterfall graph data out returns an array of 2D waveform signals in three dimensions. You can display this information with the Waterfall Graph. z0 returns the initial value of the first plot on the z-axis. dz returns the interval between plots on the z-axis. plots returns the data on the waterfall graph. x0 returns the initial value on the x-axis. dx returns the interval between points on the waterfall graph. Y returns the values of the points on the waterfall graph. |
|  | z0 returns the initial value of the first plot on the z-axis. |
|  | dz returns the interval between plots on the z-axis. |
|  | plots returns the data on the waterfall graph. x0 returns the initial value on the x-axis. dx returns the interval between points on the waterfall graph. Y returns the values of the points on the waterfall graph. |
|  | x0 returns the initial value on the x-axis. |
|  | dx returns the interval between points on the waterfall graph. |
|  | Y returns the values of the points on the waterfall graph. |
|  | error out contains error information. This output provides standard error out functionality. |

SVX Buffer Waterfall Graph (1 Trace)

[IMAGE alt='image' src='images/SVX_Buffer_Waterfall_Graph_Single_Plot.gif']

|  | reset on change to dx specifies whether the buffering VI checks for consistency in dx between the input data and the buffered data. If reset on change to dx (F) is TRUE and the dx of the input data is not equal to the dx of the buffered data, the buffer resets. The default is FALSE. |
| --- | --- |
|  | reset? clears the buffer. The default is FALSE. |
|  | plot specifies the data of a single plot on the waterfall graph. x0 specifies the initial value on the x-axis. dx specifies the interval between points on the waterfall graph. Y specifies the values of the points on the waterfall graph. |

|  | dz specifies the interval between plots on the z-axis. |
|  | error in describes error conditions that occur before this node runs. This input provides standard error in functionality. |
|  | number of spectra specifies the maximum number of spectra to buffer for display. number of spectra is similar to the chart history of a chart. The default is 40 spectra. |
|  | waterfall graph data out returns an array of 2D waveform signals in three dimensions. You can display this information with the Waterfall Graph. z0 returns the initial value of the first plot on the z-axis. dz returns the interval between plots on the z-axis. plots returns the data on the waterfall graph. x0 returns the initial value on the x-axis. dx returns the interval between points on the waterfall graph. Y returns the values of the points on the waterfall graph. |
|  | z0 returns the initial value of the first plot on the z-axis. |
|  | dz returns the interval between plots on the z-axis. |
|  | plots returns the data on the waterfall graph. x0 returns the initial value on the x-axis. dx returns the interval between points on the waterfall graph. Y returns the values of the points on the waterfall graph. |

|  | dx returns the interval between points on the waterfall graph. |

## TOPIC 00202: SVX Combine Filtered and Unfiltered Plots VI

- bundle_id: `labview-sound-and-vibration-toolkit-api-ref`
- source_path: `svx-combine-filtered-and-unfiltered-plots-vi.html`
- source_url: https://docs-be.ni.com/bundle/labview-sound-and-vibration-toolkit-api-ref/raw/resource/enus/svx-combine-filtered-and-unfiltered-plots-vi.html
- document_id: `labview-sound-and-vibration-toolkit-api-ref`
- page_type: `leaf`
- content_type: `reference`
- source_description: Owning Palette: Machine Vibration VIs Requires: Sound and Vibration Toolkit Combines filtered and unfiltered orbit plots or timebase plots into a single plot. Use this VI to see the relationship between the unfiltered signal and the signal filtered by significant orders. filtered plot specifies the

### SVX Combine Filtered and Unfiltered Plots VI

**Owning Palette:**
 Machine Vibration VIs

**Requires:**
 Sound and Vibration Toolkit

Combines filtered and unfiltered orbit plots or timebase plots into a single plot. Use this VI to see the relationship between the unfiltered signal and the signal filtered by significant orders.

[IMAGE alt='image' src='images/SVX_Combine_Filtered_and_Unfiltered_Plots.gif']

|  | filtered plot specifies the filtered orbit plots or timebase plots through set specifications. probe x specifies the amplitude of probe X. probe y specifies the amplitude of probe Y. reference specifies the time reference of the probe X and probe Y. plot type specifies the type of the orbit or timebase plot. 0 Filtered Plot (default) 1 Unfiltered Plot attribute specifies additional information about the plot. LabVIEW uses this information to display the data properly and to perform extended measurements. |
| --- | --- |
|  | probe x specifies the amplitude of probe X. |
|  | probe y specifies the amplitude of probe Y. |
|  | reference specifies the time reference of the probe X and probe Y. |
|  | plot type specifies the type of the orbit or timebase plot. 0 Filtered Plot (default) 1 Unfiltered Plot |
| 0 | Filtered Plot (default) |
| 1 | Unfiltered Plot |
|  | attribute specifies additional information about the plot. LabVIEW uses this information to display the data properly and to perform extended measurements. |
|  | unfiltered plot specifies the unfiltered orbit plots or timebase plots. probe x specifies the amplitude of probe X. probe y specifies the amplitude of probe Y. reference specifies the time reference of the probe X and probe Y. plot type specifies the type of the orbit or timebase plot. 0 Filtered Plot (default) 1 Unfiltered Plot attribute specifies additional information about the plot. LabVIEW uses this information to display the data properly and to perform extended measurements. |
|  | probe x specifies the amplitude of probe X. |
|  | probe y specifies the amplitude of probe Y. |
|  | reference specifies the time reference of the probe X and probe Y. |
|  | plot type specifies the type of the orbit or timebase plot. 0 Filtered Plot (default) 1 Unfiltered Plot |
| 0 | Filtered Plot (default) |
| 1 | Unfiltered Plot |
|  | attribute specifies additional information about the plot. LabVIEW uses this information to display the data properly and to perform extended measurements. |
|  | error in describes error conditions that occur before this node runs. This input provides standard error in functionality. |
|  | combined plot returns the combined orbit plots or timebase plots for comparison. probe x returns the amplitude of probe X of each orbit plot or timebase plot. probe y returns the amplitude of probe Y of each orbit plot or timebase plot. reference returns the time reference of the probe X and probe Y of each timebase plot. plot type returns the plot type for each orbit or timebase plot. attribute returns additional information about each orbit plot or timebase plot. LabVIEW uses this information to display the data properly and to perform extended measurements. |
|  | probe x returns the amplitude of probe X of each orbit plot or timebase plot. |
|  | probe y returns the amplitude of probe Y of each orbit plot or timebase plot. |
|  | reference returns the time reference of the probe X and probe Y of each timebase plot. |
|  | plot type returns the plot type for each orbit or timebase plot. |
|  | attribute returns additional information about each orbit plot or timebase plot. LabVIEW uses this information to display the data properly and to perform extended measurements. |
|  | error out contains error information. This output provides standard error out functionality. |

Machine Vibration VIs

<!--NI_TOPIC bundle=labview-sound-and-vibration-toolkit-api-ref path=svx-convert-colormap-to-waterfall-vi.html language=enus -->
## TOPIC 00203: SVX Convert Colormap to Waterfall VI

- bundle_id: `labview-sound-and-vibration-toolkit-api-ref`
- source_path: `svx-convert-colormap-to-waterfall-vi.html`
- source_url: https://docs-be.ni.com/bundle/labview-sound-and-vibration-toolkit-api-ref/raw/resource/enus/svx-convert-colormap-to-waterfall-vi.html
- document_id: `labview-sound-and-vibration-toolkit-api-ref`
- page_type: `leaf`
- content_type: `reference`
- source_description: Owning Palette: Transient Spectra VIs Requires: Sound and Vibration Toolkit Converts Colormap data to Waterfall Graph data. The SVX Convert Colormap to Waterfall VI will convert the specified spectral results and return the information plotted on a Waterfall Graph in an array of 2D waveform signals.

### SVX Convert Colormap to Waterfall VI

**Owning Palette:**
 Transient Spectra VIs

**Requires:**
 Sound and Vibration Toolkit

Converts Colormap data to Waterfall Graph data.

The SVX Convert Colormap to Waterfall VI will convert the specified spectral results and return the information plotted on a Waterfall Graph in an array of 2D waveform signals.

[IMAGE alt='image' src='images/SVX_Convert_Colormap_to_Waterfall.gif']

|  | colormap specifies the spectral results of a transient analysis on an intensity graph with a rainbow color scale. spectral map specifies the 2D array of magnitude data for the spectra. X scale specifies the scaling information associated with the first index of the 2D spectral map. offset and multiplier specify scaling values such that the final value equals (deltaX)*X + Xo. offset specifies the offset, or initial value, for scaling data using (deltaX)*X + Xo. If you change the offset, the scale no longer uses 0 as the origin of the plot. multiplier specifies the multiplier, or interval, for scaling data using (deltaX)*X + Xo. Y scale specifies the scaling information associated with the first index of the 2D spectral map. offset and multiplier specify scaling values such that the final value equals (deltaY)*Y + Yo. offset specifies the offset, or initial value, for scaling data using (deltaY)*Y + Yo. If you change the offset, the scale no longer uses 0 as the origin of the plot. multiplier specifies the multiplier, or interval, for scaling data using (deltaY)*Y + Yo. attribute specifies additional information about the spectra. LabVIEW uses this information to display the data properly and to perform extended measurements. |
| --- | --- |
|  | spectral map specifies the 2D array of magnitude data for the spectra. |
|  | X scale specifies the scaling information associated with the first index of the 2D spectral map. offset and multiplier specify scaling values such that the final value equals (deltaX)*X + Xo. offset specifies the offset, or initial value, for scaling data using (deltaX)*X + Xo. If you change the offset, the scale no longer uses 0 as the origin of the plot. multiplier specifies the multiplier, or interval, for scaling data using (deltaX)*X + Xo. |
|  | offset specifies the offset, or initial value, for scaling data using (deltaX)*X + Xo. If you change the offset, the scale no longer uses 0 as the origin of the plot. |
|  | multiplier specifies the multiplier, or interval, for scaling data using (deltaX)*X + Xo. |
|  | Y scale specifies the scaling information associated with the first index of the 2D spectral map. offset and multiplier specify scaling values such that the final value equals (deltaY)*Y + Yo. offset specifies the offset, or initial value, for scaling data using (deltaY)*Y + Yo. If you change the offset, the scale no longer uses 0 as the origin of the plot. multiplier specifies the multiplier, or interval, for scaling data using (deltaY)*Y + Yo. |
|  | offset specifies the offset, or initial value, for scaling data using (deltaY)*Y + Yo. If you change the offset, the scale no longer uses 0 as the origin of the plot. |
|  | multiplier specifies the multiplier, or interval, for scaling data using (deltaY)*Y + Yo. |
|  | attribute specifies additional information about the spectra. LabVIEW uses this information to display the data properly and to perform extended measurements. |
|  | error in describes error conditions that occur before this node runs. This input provides standard error in functionality. |
|  | waterfall graph data out returns an array of 2D waveform signals in three dimensions. You can display this information with the Waterfall Graph. z0 returns the initial value of the first plot on the z-axis. dz returns the interval between plots on the z-axis. plots returns the data on the waterfall graph. x0 returns the initial value on the x-axis. dx returns the interval between points on the waterfall graph. Y returns the values of the points on the waterfall graph. |
|  | z0 returns the initial value of the first plot on the z-axis. |
|  | dz returns the interval between plots on the z-axis. |
|  | plots returns the data on the waterfall graph. x0 returns the initial value on the x-axis. dx returns the interval between points on the waterfall graph. Y returns the values of the points on the waterfall graph. |
|  | x0 returns the initial value on the x-axis. |
|  | dx returns the interval between points on the waterfall graph. |
|  | Y returns the values of the points on the waterfall graph. |
|  | X axis unit label returns the unit label of the x-axis. |
|  | Y axis unit label returns the unit label of the y-axis. |
|  | error out contains error information. This output provides standard error out functionality. |
|  | Z axis unit label returns the unit label of the z-axis. |

<!--NI_TOPIC bundle=labview-sound-and-vibration-toolkit-api-ref path=svx-convert-waterfall-to-colormap-vi.html language=enus -->
## TOPIC 00204: SVX Convert Waterfall to Colormap VI

- bundle_id: `labview-sound-and-vibration-toolkit-api-ref`
- source_path: `svx-convert-waterfall-to-colormap-vi.html`
- source_url: https://docs-be.ni.com/bundle/labview-sound-and-vibration-toolkit-api-ref/raw/resource/enus/svx-convert-waterfall-to-colormap-vi.html
- document_id: `labview-sound-and-vibration-toolkit-api-ref`
- page_type: `leaf`
- content_type: `reference`
- source_description: Owning Palette: Transient Spectra VIs Requires: Sound and Vibration Toolkit Converts Waterfall Graph data to Colormap data. The SVX Convert Waterfall to Colormap VI will convert the specified array of 2D waveform signals and return the information plotted in a Colormap of spectral results as an inte

### SVX Convert Waterfall to Colormap VI

**Owning Palette:**
 Transient Spectra VIs

**Requires:**
 Sound and Vibration Toolkit

Converts Waterfall Graph data to Colormap data.

The SVX Convert Waterfall to Colormap VI will convert the specified array of 2D waveform signals and return the information plotted in a Colormap of spectral results as an intensity graph.

[IMAGE alt='image' src='images/SVX_Convert_Waterfall_to_Colormap.gif']

|  | plot type specifies the type of colormap to display. The default is Frequency-Time. 0 Frequency-Time (default)—Displays the time on the x-axis and frequency on the y-axis. 1 Time-Frequency —Displays the frequency on the x-axis and the time on the y-axis. 2 Order-Time —Displays the time on the x-axis and the order on the y-axis. 3 Time-Order —Displays the order on the x-axis and the time on the y-axis. 4 Frequency-RPM —Displays the RPM on the x-axis and the frequency on the y-axis. 5 RPM-Frequency —Displays the frequency on the x-axis and the frequency on the y-axis. 6 Order-RPM —Displays the RPM on the x-axis and the order on the y-axis. 7 RPM-Order —Displays the order on the x-axis and the RPM on the y-axis. |
| --- | --- |
| 0 | Frequency-Time (default)—Displays the time on the x-axis and frequency on the y-axis. |
| 1 | Time-Frequency —Displays the frequency on the x-axis and the time on the y-axis. |
| 2 | Order-Time —Displays the time on the x-axis and the order on the y-axis. |
| 3 | Time-Order —Displays the order on the x-axis and the time on the y-axis. |
| 4 | Frequency-RPM —Displays the RPM on the x-axis and the frequency on the y-axis. |
| 5 | RPM-Frequency —Displays the frequency on the x-axis and the frequency on the y-axis. |
| 6 | Order-RPM —Displays the RPM on the x-axis and the order on the y-axis. |
| 7 | RPM-Order —Displays the order on the x-axis and the RPM on the y-axis. |
|  | waterfall graph data specifies an array of 2D waveform signals in three dimensions. z0 specifies the initial value of the first plot on the z-axis. dz specifies the interval between plots on the z-axis. plots specifies the data on the waterfall graph. x0 specifies the initial value on the x-axis. dx specifies the interval between points on the waterfall graph. Y specifies the values of the points on the waterfall graph. |
|  | z0 specifies the initial value of the first plot on the z-axis. |
|  | dz specifies the interval between plots on the z-axis. |
|  | plots specifies the data on the waterfall graph. x0 specifies the initial value on the x-axis. dx specifies the interval between points on the waterfall graph. Y specifies the values of the points on the waterfall graph. |
|  | x0 specifies the initial value on the x-axis. |
|  | dx specifies the interval between points on the waterfall graph. |
|  | Y specifies the values of the points on the waterfall graph. |
|  | error in describes error conditions that occur before this node runs. This input provides standard error in functionality. |
|  | colormap returns the spectral results converted from the waterfall data. You can display this cluster as a Colormap to show the transient results on an intensity graph with a rainbow color scale. spectral map returns the 2D array of magnitude data converted according to the scaling parameters. X scale returns the scaling information associated with the first index of the 2D spectral map. offset returns the x value associated with elements (0, n) of spectral map . multiplier returns the x-axis spacing between consecutive elements in a column. Y scale returns the scaling information associated with the second index of the 2D spectral map. offset returns the y value associated with elements (n, 0) of spectral map . multiplier returns the y-axis spacing between consecutive elements in a row. attribute returns additional information about the spectra. LabVIEW uses this information to display the data properly and to perform extended measurements. |
|  | spectral map returns the 2D array of magnitude data converted according to the scaling parameters. |
|  | X scale returns the scaling information associated with the first index of the 2D spectral map. offset returns the x value associated with elements (0, n) of spectral map . multiplier returns the x-axis spacing between consecutive elements in a column. |
|  | offset returns the x value associated with elements (0, n) of spectral map . |
|  | multiplier returns the x-axis spacing between consecutive elements in a column. |
|  | Y scale returns the scaling information associated with the second index of the 2D spectral map. offset returns the y value associated with elements (n, 0) of spectral map . multiplier returns the y-axis spacing between consecutive elements in a row. |
|  | offset returns the y value associated with elements (n, 0) of spectral map . |
|  | multiplier returns the y-axis spacing between consecutive elements in a row. |
|  | attribute returns additional information about the spectra. LabVIEW uses this information to display the data properly and to perform extended measurements. |
|  | X axis unit label returns the unit label of the x-axis. |
|  | Y axis unit label returns the unit label of the y-axis. |
|  | error out contains error information. This output provides standard error out functionality. |
|  | Z axis unit label returns the unit label of the z-axis. |

- bundle_id: `labview-sound-and-vibration-toolkit-api-ref`

- source_description: Owning Palette: Transient Spectra VIs Requires: Sound and Vibration Toolkit This VI creates a line that you can plot on a waterfall graph. Use the waterfall graph property node lines to draw the line on the graph. SVX Waterfall Frequency CursorSVX Waterfall Add CursorSVX Waterfall Order CursorSVX Da

### SVX Create Waterfall Cursor VI

**Owning Palette:**
 Transient Spectra VIs

**Requires:**
 Sound and Vibration Toolkit

This VI creates a line that you can plot on a waterfall graph. Use the waterfall graph property node 
 **lines**
 to draw the line on the graph.

| SVX Waterfall Frequency CursorSVX Waterfall Add CursorSVX Waterfall Order CursorSVX Data Cursor at Constant Z |
| --- |

SVX Waterfall Frequency Cursor

[IMAGE alt='image' src='images/svx_Waterfall_Frequency_Cursor.gif']

|  | coercion (nearest) specifies whether the specified cursor position will be coerced to the closest, next higher, or next lower spectrum value; or whether the spectrum will be interpolated to satisfy the specified cursor position. 0 coerce to nearest 1 coerce to next highest 2 coerce to next lowest 3 interpolate |
| --- | --- |
| 0 | coerce to nearest |
| 1 | coerce to next highest |
| 2 | coerce to next lowest |
| 3 | interpolate |
|  | cursors specifies the list of cursors to append the generated cursor to. |
|  | waterfall data specifies the waterfall data to plot the cursor on. z0 specifies the initial value of the first plot on the z-axis. dz specifies the interval between plots on the z-axis. plots specifies the data on the waterfall graph. x0 specifies the initial value on the x-axis. dx specifies the interval between points on the waterfall graph. Y specifies the values of the points on the waterfall graph. |
|  | z0 specifies the initial value of the first plot on the z-axis. |
|  | dz specifies the interval between plots on the z-axis. |
|  | plots specifies the data on the waterfall graph. x0 specifies the initial value on the x-axis. dx specifies the interval between points on the waterfall graph. Y specifies the values of the points on the waterfall graph. |
|  | x0 specifies the initial value on the x-axis. |
|  | dx specifies the interval between points on the waterfall graph. |
|  | Y specifies the values of the points on the waterfall graph. |
|  | frequency specifies the constant frequency value to plot across all spectra of the waterfall graph data . |
|  | speeds specifies the RPM or frequency of each spectra of the waterfall graph data . You must set spectrum speeds for plotting a frequency cursor on an order spectrum. spectrum speeds and waterfall graph data must have the same number of elements. |
|  | spectrum info specifies the properties of the computed spectrum. Note You must wire spectrum info when performing extended measurements. Do not modify the spectrum info values. If you want to view the information, you can right-click the spectrum info wire and select Custom Probe»Spectrum Info from the shortcut menu. |
|  | error in describes error conditions that occur before this node runs. This input provides standard error in functionality. |
|  | appended cursors returns the list of cursors including the newly generated cursor. |
|  | duplicate waterfall data indicates the waterfall data the cursor is plotted on. Z indicates the initial value of the first plot on the z-axis. Y indicates the interval between plots on the z-axis. plots indicates the data on the waterfall graph. x0 specifies the initial value on the x-axis. dx specifies the interval between points on the waterfall graph. Y specifies the values of the points on the waterfall graph. |
|  | Z indicates the initial value of the first plot on the z-axis. |
|  | Y indicates the interval between plots on the z-axis. |
|  | plots indicates the data on the waterfall graph. x0 specifies the initial value on the x-axis. dx specifies the interval between points on the waterfall graph. Y specifies the values of the points on the waterfall graph. |
|  | x0 specifies the initial value on the x-axis. |
|  | dx specifies the interval between points on the waterfall graph. |
|  | Y specifies the values of the points on the waterfall graph. |
|  | cursor data returns the data for the selected cursor. |
|  | cursor index returns the index of the created cursor in the cursors out array. |
|  | duplicate spectrum info indicates the properties of the computed spectrum. Note You must wire spectrum info when performing extended measurements. Do not modify the spectrum info values. If you want to view the information, you can right-click the spectrum info wire and select Custom Probe»Spectrum Info from the shortcut menu. |
|  | error out contains error information. This output provides standard error out functionality. |
