<!--NI_TOPIC bundle=labview-sound-and-vibration-toolkit-api-ref path=svx-ni-daqmx-ai-configuration-xcontrol.html language=enus -->
## TOPIC 00206: SVX NI-DAQmx AI Configuration XControl

- bundle_id: `labview-sound-and-vibration-toolkit-api-ref`
- source_path: `svx-ni-daqmx-ai-configuration-xcontrol.html`
- source_url: https://docs-be.ni.com/bundle/labview-sound-and-vibration-toolkit-api-ref/raw/resource/enus/svx-ni-daqmx-ai-configuration-xcontrol.html
- document_id: `labview-sound-and-vibration-toolkit-api-ref`
- page_type: `leaf`
- content_type: `reference`
- source_description: Owning Palette: Configuration Controls Requires: Sound and Vibration Measurement Suite, NI-DAQmx Provides easy front panel configuration of typical sound and vibration applications using NI-DAQmx analog input hardware. You also can use this XControl to set DAQmx properties and attributes, such as IE

### SVX NI-DAQmx AI Configuration XControl

**Owning Palette:**
 Configuration Controls

 **Requires:**
 Sound and Vibration Measurement Suite, NI-DAQmx

Provides easy front panel configuration of typical sound and vibration applications using NI-DAQmx analog input hardware. You also can use this XControl to set DAQmx properties and attributes, such as IEPE and coupling.

[IMAGE alt='image' src='images/svx_ni-daqmx_ai_configuration_xcontrol.gif']

The XControl creates an NI-DAQmx analog input task, based on the values you select in the XControl. The XControl configures the created NI-DAQmx task with channel attributes, such as channel type, coupling, and excitation, and timing attributes, such as sample rate and samples per channel. This XControl always sets the sample mode to acquire finite samples. You can use the NI-DAQmx VIs to operate on the DAQmx task output of the XControl. For example, you can wire the XControl directly to the DAQmx Read VI, as shown below.

[IMAGE alt='image' src='images/daqmx_configuration_read.gif']

You also can use the NI-DAQmx VIs to get, set, and reconfigure properties of the task or synchronize the analog input task with other DAQmx tasks. For example, use the DAQmx Timing Property Node to change the sample mode to 
 **Continuous Samples**
 , as shown below.

[IMAGE alt='image' src='images/daqmx_configuration_reconfigure.gif']
 
SVX NI-DAQmx AI Configuration XControl Options

| Option | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Channel Table | Specifies the channels and channel configuration. NI-DAQmx Channel —Specifies the NI-DAQmx channel to configure. Channel Type —Specifies the type of the channel. The selectable types are: Voltage (V) —Voltage or Tachometer SPL (Pa) —Sound Pressure Level Force (lbf) Force (N) Acceleration (g) Velocity (in/s) Velocity (mm/s) Prox Probe (mil) —Proximity Probe Prox Probe (mm) —Proximity Probe Range —Specifies the range of the selected channel. mV/EU —Specifies the sensitivity of the selected channel. IEPE —Enables the integrated electronic piezoelectric (IEPE) excitation. The default IEPE excitation level is 2 mA. The IEPE coerces if the device does not support the default IEPE excitation level. Coupling —Specifies the coupling setting of the channel. AC DC Location —Specifies the measurement location as a string. When Channel Type is Prox Probe and you enter an integer, the output is converted to an integer pair in the waveform attributes. Orientation —Specifies the direction of the measurement as a string. When Channel Type is Prox Probe and you enter an integer, the output is converted to an angle (in units of degrees) in the waveform attributes. |
| Timing Settings | Specifies the settings for each timing mode. Timing Mode —Specifies the timing mode: Time, Frequency, or Order. Time Min Sample Rate (Hz) —Specifies the minimum sample rate in Hz. The default is 25600. Block Size —Specifies the block size in samples. The default is 2560. Block Duration (s) —Indicates the block duration in seconds. The default is 0.1. Frequency Max Frequency (Hz) —Specifies the maximum frequency in Hz. The default is 2560. Sample rate is set to 10 x Max Frequency . Resolution (Hz) —Specifies the frequency resolution in Hz. The default is 10. Block Duration (s) —Indicates the block duration in seconds. The default is 0.1. Order Max Speed (RPM) —Specifies the maximum running speed in RPM. The default is 9600. Block Size —Specifies the block size in samples. The default is 2560. Max Order —Specifies the maximum order to analyze. The default is 16. Block Duration (s) —Indicates the block duration in seconds. The default is 0.1. |
| Reference Settings | Specifies the settings for reference sensors. Reference Type —Specifies the type of reference sensor to configure. Analog Tachometer —Applies tachometer settings to a selected channel. Tachometer Channel —Specifies the channel that contains the tachometer signal. Pulses/Rev —Specifies the number of pulses the tachometer generates during each revolution. Slope —Specifies the type of edge that indicates the arrival of a tachometer pulse. Rising —Specifies that a rising edge indicates the arrival of a tachometer pulse. Falling —Specifies that a falling edge indicates the arrival of a tachometer pulse. Threshold —Specifies the trigger level that a tachometer pulse must cross to be detected by the tachometer. When Threshold is Inf , this XControl sets the threshold level automatically. Direction —Specifies the direction in which the shaft rotates. CCW —Specifies that the shaft rotates in a counterclockwise direction. CW —Specifies that the shaft rotates in a clockwise direction. Analog SW Trigger —Applies software trigger settings to the selected channel. Trigger Channel —Specifies the channel that contains the software trigger. Level —Specifies the threshold value (in EU) that the signal must cross before a trigger is detected. Slope —Specifies the type of edge that indicates the arrival of a software trigger. Rising —Specifies that a rising edge indicates the arrival of a software trigger. Falling —Specifies that a falling edge indicates the arrival of a software trigger. Hysteresis —Specifies the amount (in EU) above and below Level through which the signal must pass before a trigger level crossing is detected. Pretrigger —Specifies the percentage of the block duration to reserve for samples before the trigger condition is detected. The valid range is 0 to 99 percent. |
| Logging Settings | Specifies the settings for TDMS logging. Enable TDMS Logging —Enables DAQmx logging of the waveform data. TDMS File Path —Specifies the absolute path to the TDMS file. |
| Status and File Operations | —Indicates if there is an error with the entered settings. Press to display the error message in a pop-up window. This button only appears if there is an error. —Loads the configuration from a previously saved XML file. You also can right-click the XControl and select Load configuration from file to load a previously saved configuration. —Saves the current configuration to an XML file. You also can right-click the XControl and select Save configuration to file to save the current configuration. |

Parent topic:

Configuration Controls

<!--NI_TOPIC bundle=labview-sound-and-vibration-toolkit-api-ref path=svx-orbit-and-timebase-plot-vi.html language=enus -->
## TOPIC 00207: SVX Orbit and Timebase Plot VI

- bundle_id: `labview-sound-and-vibration-toolkit-api-ref`
- source_path: `svx-orbit-and-timebase-plot-vi.html`
- source_url: https://docs-be.ni.com/bundle/labview-sound-and-vibration-toolkit-api-ref/raw/resource/enus/svx-orbit-and-timebase-plot-vi.html
- document_id: `labview-sound-and-vibration-toolkit-api-ref`
- page_type: `leaf`
- content_type: `reference`
- source_description: Owning Palette: Machine Vibration VIs Requires: Sound and Vibration Toolkit Prepares unfiltered or filtered orbit plot data or timebase plot data for displaying on an Orbit Plot or Timebase Plot . Use this VI to prepare magnitude and phase data for entry into an orbit or timebase plot. Wire data to

### SVX Orbit and Timebase Plot VI

**Owning Palette:**
 Machine Vibration VIs

**Requires:**
 Sound and Vibration Toolkit

Prepares unfiltered or filtered orbit plot data or timebase plot data for displaying on an 
 [Orbit Plot](/csh?context=https://www.ni.com/docs/en-us/bundle/labview-sound-and-vibration-toolkit/page/sndvibtk/oat_obt_indt.html)
 or 
 [Timebase Plot](/csh?context=https://www.ni.com/docs/en-us/bundle/labview-sound-and-vibration-toolkit/page/sndvibtk/oat_timbs_indt.html)
 . Use this VI to prepare magnitude and phase data for entry into an orbit or timebase plot. Wire data to the 
 **even-angle signals**
 input to determine the polymorphic instance to use or manually select the instance.

| SVX Orbit and Timebase Plot (Unfiltered)SVX Orbit and Timebase Plot (Filtered) (N Ch, 1 Order)SVX Orbit and Timebase Plot (Filtered) (N Ch, M Orders) |
| --- |

SVX Orbit and Timebase Plot (Unfiltered)

[IMAGE alt='image' src='images/SVX_Order_and_Timebase_Plot_Unfiltered.gif']

|  | reset? specifies the initialization of the internal states. The first time this VI runs, or when reset? is TRUE, the internal states initialize to zero. If reset? is FALSE, this VI sets the internal states to the final states from the previous call to the VI. The default is FALSE. |
| --- | --- |
|  | even-angle signals specifies the even-angle-spaced samples from paired channels. The samples from the paired channels are synchronous to the rotational speed. dr specifies the revolution interval between two sequential samples. r0 specifies the starting index value for counting revolutions. Y specifies the value of the even-angle-spaced sample in a scaled engineering unit. |
|  | dr specifies the revolution interval between two sequential samples. |
|  | r0 specifies the starting index value for counting revolutions. |
|  | Y specifies the value of the even-angle-spaced sample in a scaled engineering unit. |
|  | even-angle channel info specifies the information about the measurement configuration that this VI uses to acquire the resampled data. Note You must wire even-angle channel info when performing extended measurements. Do not modify the even-angle channel info values. If you want to view the information, you can create a custom probe. |
|  | even-angle position info specifies the position information of the even-angle signals . positions [s] specifies the time, in seconds, at which to measure the magnitude and phase . samples/rev specifies the number of samples in one revolution. |
|  | positions [s] specifies the time, in seconds, at which to measure the magnitude and phase . |
|  | samples/rev specifies the number of samples in one revolution. |
|  | speed profile specifies the arrival time and the rotational speed of each tachometer pulse. time [s] specifies the arrival time, in seconds, of each tachometer pulse. speed [RPM] specifies the rotational speed, in revolutions per minute, of each tachometer pulse. |
|  | time [s] specifies the arrival time, in seconds, of each tachometer pulse. |
|  | speed [RPM] specifies the rotational speed, in revolutions per minute, of each tachometer pulse. |
|  | error in describes error conditions that occur before this node runs. This input provides standard error in functionality. |
|  | channel properties specifies the shaft rotation direction, angle value of the proximity probe, and pair information of each channel. direction specifies the direction in which the shaft rotates. 0CCW (default)Counterclockwise 1CW Clockwise probe angle [deg] specifies the angle value of the proximity probe. The default is 0 deg. pair specifies the pair number of each channel. You must specify the same pair number for a pair of proximity probes. |
|  | direction specifies the direction in which the shaft rotates. 0CCW (default)Counterclockwise 1CW Clockwise |
| 0 | CCW (default)Counterclockwise |
| 1 | CW Clockwise |
|  | probe angle [deg] specifies the angle value of the proximity probe. The default is 0 deg. |
|  | pair specifies the pair number of each channel. You must specify the same pair number for a pair of proximity probes. |
|  | segment settings specifies a start and end value for the revolution value at which to compute the timebase plot and orbit plot. start [rev] specifies the start revolution value at which to compute the timebase plot and orbit plot. The default is 0 rev. The value of start [rev] must be less than the value of end [rev] . end specifies the end value. The default is 0.00. If you set end to 0.00, this VI uses positive infinity as the end value for time or speed in run-up test or uses 0.00 as the end value for speed in run-down test. |
|  | start [rev] specifies the start revolution value at which to compute the timebase plot and orbit plot. The default is 0 rev. The value of start [rev] must be less than the value of end [rev] . |
|  | end specifies the end value. The default is 0.00. If you set end to 0.00, this VI uses positive infinity as the end value for time or speed in run-up test or uses 0.00 as the end value for speed in run-down test. |
|  | orbit/timebase plot returns the orbit or timebase data for displaying on an orbit plot or timebase plot. probe x returns the amplitude of probe X. probe y returns the amplitude of probe Y. reference returns the time reference of the probe X and probe Y for the timebase plot. plot type returns the type of the orbit plot or timebase plot. attribute returns additional information about the plots. LabVIEW uses this information to display the data properly and to perform extended measurements. |
|  | probe x returns the amplitude of probe X. |
|  | probe y returns the amplitude of probe Y. |
|  | reference returns the time reference of the probe X and probe Y for the timebase plot. |
|  | plot type returns the type of the orbit plot or timebase plot. |
|  | attribute returns additional information about the plots. LabVIEW uses this information to display the data properly and to perform extended measurements. |
|  | end reached? indicates whether the input signal reaches the end revolution value you specified in segment settings . When end reached? is TRUE, this VI does not return new orbit plots or timebase plots. |
|  | error out contains error information. This output provides standard error out functionality. |
|  | actual segment settings returns the segment settings that this VI used to compute the timebase plot and orbit plot. start [rev] returns the start revolution value that this VI used to compute the timebase plot and orbit plot. The default is 0 rev. end [rev] returns the end revolution value that this VI used to compute the timebase plot and orbit plot. The default is Inf. |
|  | start [rev] returns the start revolution value that this VI used to compute the timebase plot and orbit plot. The default is 0 rev. |
|  | end [rev] returns the end revolution value that this VI used to compute the timebase plot and orbit plot. The default is Inf. |

SVX Orbit and Timebase Plot (Filtered) (N Ch, 1 Order)

[IMAGE alt='image' src='images/SVX_Order_and_Timebase_Plot_Filtered_N_Ch_to_1_Order.gif']

|  | reset? specifies the initialization of the internal states. The first time this VI runs, or when reset? is TRUE, the internal states initialize to zero. If reset? is FALSE, this VI sets the internal states to the final states from the previous call to the VI. The default is FALSE. |
| --- | --- |
|  | samples/rev wanted specifies the number of samples to display in one revolution. The default is 64. |
|  | magnitudes [RMS] specifies the order magnitude of signals from paired channels. time/rev/RPM specifies the time, revolution, or speed at which you measured the magnitude . magnitude specifies the magnitude of one specific order at the time/rev/RPM . |
|  | time/rev/RPM specifies the time, revolution, or speed at which you measured the magnitude . |
|  | magnitude specifies the magnitude of one specific order at the time/rev/RPM . |
|  | phases [phase lag] specifies the order phase of signals from paired channels. time/rev/RPM specifies the time, revolution, or speed at which you measured the phase . phase specifies the phase of one specific order at the time/rev/RPM . |
|  | time/rev/RPM specifies the time, revolution, or speed at which you measured the phase . |
|  | phase specifies the phase of one specific order at the time/rev/RPM . |
|  | magnitude/phase position info specifies the position information of the magnitudes and phases [phase lag] . positions [s] specifies the time, in seconds, at which to measure the magnitude and phase . samples/rev specifies the number of samples in one revolution. |
|  | positions [s] specifies the time, in seconds, at which to measure the magnitude and phase . |
|  | samples/rev specifies the number of samples in one revolution. |
|  | magnitudes info specifies information about the magnitudes [RMS] . Note You must wire magnitudes info when performing extended measurements. Do not modify the magnitudes info values. If you want to view the information, you can create a custom probe. |
|  | error in describes error conditions that occur before this node runs. This input provides standard error in functionality. |
|  | channel properties specifies the shaft rotation direction, angle value of the proximity probe, and pair information of each channel. direction specifies the direction in which the shaft rotates. 0CCW (default)Counterclockwise 1CW Clockwise probe angle [deg] specifies the angle value of the proximity probe. The default is 0 deg. pair specifies the pair number of each channel. You must specify the same pair number for a pair of proximity probes. |
|  | direction specifies the direction in which the shaft rotates. 0CCW (default)Counterclockwise 1CW Clockwise |
| 0 | CCW (default)Counterclockwise |
| 1 | CW Clockwise |
|  | probe angle [deg] specifies the angle value of the proximity probe. The default is 0 deg. |
|  | pair specifies the pair number of each channel. You must specify the same pair number for a pair of proximity probes. |
|  | segment settings specifies a start and end value for the revolution value at which to compute the timebase plot and orbit plot. start [rev] specifies the start revolution value at which to compute the timebase plot and orbit plot. The default is 0 rev. The value of start [rev] must be less than the value of end [rev] . end specifies the end value. The default is 0.00. If you set end to 0.00, this VI uses positive infinity as the end value for time or speed in run-up test or uses 0.00 as the end value for speed in run-down test. |
|  | start [rev] specifies the start revolution value at which to compute the timebase plot and orbit plot. The default is 0 rev. The value of start [rev] must be less than the value of end [rev] . |
|  | end specifies the end value. The default is 0.00. If you set end to 0.00, this VI uses positive infinity as the end value for time or speed in run-up test or uses 0.00 as the end value for speed in run-down test. |
|  | orbit/timebase plot returns the orbit or timebase data for displaying on an orbit plot or timebase plot. probe x returns the amplitude of probe X. probe y returns the amplitude of probe Y. reference returns the time reference of the probe X and probe Y for the timebase plot. plot type returns the type of the orbit plot or timebase plot. attribute returns additional information about the plots. LabVIEW uses this information to display the data properly and to perform extended measurements. |
|  | probe x returns the amplitude of probe X. |
|  | probe y returns the amplitude of probe Y. |
|  | reference returns the time reference of the probe X and probe Y for the timebase plot. |
|  | plot type returns the type of the orbit plot or timebase plot. |
|  | attribute returns additional information about the plots. LabVIEW uses this information to display the data properly and to perform extended measurements. |
|  | end reached? indicates whether the input signal reaches the end revolution value you specified in segment settings . When end reached? is TRUE, this VI does not return new orbit plots or timebase plots. |
|  | error out contains error information. This output provides standard error out functionality. |
|  | actual segment settings returns the segment settings that this VI used to compute the timebase plot and orbit plot. start [rev] returns the start revolution value that this VI used to compute the timebase plot and orbit plot. The default is 0 rev. end [rev] returns the end revolution value that this VI used to compute the timebase plot and orbit plot. The default is Inf. |

## TOPIC 00208: SVX Polar Plot VI

- bundle_id: `labview-sound-and-vibration-toolkit-api-ref`
- source_path: `svx-polar-plot-vi.html`
- source_url: https://docs-be.ni.com/bundle/labview-sound-and-vibration-toolkit-api-ref/raw/resource/enus/svx-polar-plot-vi.html
- document_id: `labview-sound-and-vibration-toolkit-api-ref`
- page_type: `leaf`
- content_type: `reference`
- source_description: Owning Palette: Machine Vibration VIs Requires: Sound and Vibration Toolkit Prepares polar plot data for displaying on a Polar Plot . Use this VI to show magnitude versus phase for a specific order. Wire data to the magnitudes [RMS] and phases [phase lag] inputs to determine the polymorphic instance

### SVX Polar Plot VI

**Owning Palette:**
 Machine Vibration VIs

**Requires:**
 Sound and Vibration Toolkit

Prepares polar plot data for displaying on a 
 [Polar Plot](/csh?context=https://www.ni.com/docs/en-us/bundle/labview-sound-and-vibration-toolkit/page/sndvibtk/oat_polar_indt.html)
 . Use this VI to show magnitude versus phase for a specific order. Wire data to the 
 **magnitudes [RMS]**
 and 
 **phases [phase lag]**
 inputs to determine the polymorphic instance to use or manually select the instance.

| SVX Polar Plot (1 Ch)SVX Polar Plot (N Ch, 1 Order)SVX Polar Plot (N Ch, M Orders) |
| --- |

SVX Polar Plot (1 Ch)

[IMAGE alt='image' src='images/SVX_Polar_Plot_1_Ch.gif']

|  | magnitudes [RMS] specifies the order magnitude of signals from one channel. time/rev/RPM specifies the time, revolution, or speed at which you measured the magnitude . magnitude specifies the magnitude of one specific order at the time/rev/RPM . |
| --- | --- |
|  | time/rev/RPM specifies the time, revolution, or speed at which you measured the magnitude . |
|  | magnitude specifies the magnitude of one specific order at the time/rev/RPM . |
|  | phases [phase lag] specifies the order phase of signals from one channel. time/rev/RPM specifies the time, revolution, or speed at which you measured the phase . phase specifies the phase of one specific order at the time/rev/RPM . |
|  | time/rev/RPM specifies the time, revolution, or speed at which you measured the phase . |
|  | phase specifies the phase of one specific order at the time/rev/RPM . |
|  | magnitude info specifies information about the magnitudes [RMS] . Note You must wire magnitude info when performing extended measurements. Do not modify the magnitude info values. If you want to view the information, you can create a custom probe. |
|  | error in describes error conditions that occur before this node runs. This input provides standard error in functionality. |
|  | channel properties specifies the properties of the signal channel. direction specifies the direction in which the shaft rotates. 0CCW (default)Counterclockwise 1CW Clockwise probe angle [deg] specifies the angle value of the proximity probe. The default is 0 deg. pair specifies the pair number of the signal channel. The default is 0. |
|  | direction specifies the direction in which the shaft rotates. 0CCW (default)Counterclockwise 1CW Clockwise |
| 0 | CCW (default)Counterclockwise |
| 1 | CW Clockwise |
|  | probe angle [deg] specifies the angle value of the proximity probe. The default is 0 deg. |
|  | pair specifies the pair number of the signal channel. The default is 0. |
|  | unit label returns the unit label of the polar plot. |
|  | polar plot returns the values and information for the polar plot. magnitude returns the magnitude value of the polar plot. phase returns the phase value of the polar plot. time/rev/RPM returns the time, revolution, or speed at which this VI computes the magnitude and phase . attribute returns additional information about the polar plot. LabVIEW uses this information to display the data properly and to perform extended measurements. |
|  | magnitude returns the magnitude value of the polar plot. |
|  | phase returns the phase value of the polar plot. |
|  | time/rev/RPM returns the time, revolution, or speed at which this VI computes the magnitude and phase . |
|  | attribute returns additional information about the polar plot. LabVIEW uses this information to display the data properly and to perform extended measurements. |
|  | error out contains error information. This output provides standard error out functionality. |

## TOPIC 00209: SVX Shaft Centerline Plot VI

- bundle_id: `labview-sound-and-vibration-toolkit-api-ref`
- source_path: `svx-shaft-centerline-plot-vi.html`
- source_url: https://docs-be.ni.com/bundle/labview-sound-and-vibration-toolkit-api-ref/raw/resource/enus/svx-shaft-centerline-plot-vi.html

- page_type: `leaf`
- content_type: `reference`
- source_description: Owning Palette: Machine Vibration VIs Requires: Sound and Vibration Toolkit Prepares shaft centerline plot data for displaying on a Shaft Centerline Plot . A shaft centerline plot displays the averaged positions of the shaft center. Use this VI to measure the movement of the shaft center over time.

### SVX Shaft Centerline Plot VI

**Owning Palette:**
 Machine Vibration VIs

**Requires:**
 Sound and Vibration Toolkit

Prepares shaft centerline plot data for displaying on a 
 [Shaft Centerline Plot](/csh?context=https://www.ni.com/docs/en-us/bundle/labview-sound-and-vibration-toolkit/page/sndvibtk/oat_shft_ctln.html)
 . A shaft centerline plot displays the averaged positions of the shaft center. Use this VI to measure the movement of the shaft center over time.

[IMAGE alt='image' src='images/SVX_Shaft_Centerline_Plot.gif']

|  | reset? specifies the initialization of the internal states. The first time this VI runs, or when reset? is TRUE, the internal states initialize to zero. If reset? is FALSE, this VI sets the internal states to the final states from the previous call to the VI. The default is FALSE. |
| --- | --- |
|  | DC gaps specifies the DC gap of the proximity probes. time/rev/RPM specifies the time, revolution, or speed at which you measured the DC gap value . DC gap value specifies the DC gap value at the time/rev/RPM . |
|  | time/rev/RPM specifies the time, revolution, or speed at which you measured the DC gap value . |
|  | DC gap value specifies the DC gap value at the time/rev/RPM . |
| unit labels specifies the unit labels of the DC gaps . x unit specifies the engineering unit for the time/rev/RPM . DC gap unit out specifies the engineering unit for the DC gap. |  |

|  | DC gap unit out specifies the engineering unit for the DC gap. |
|  | gap position info specifies the position information of DC gaps . positions [s] specifies the time, in seconds, at which you measured the DC gap value . samples/rev specifies the number of samples per revolution. |
|  | positions [s] specifies the time, in seconds, at which you measured the DC gap value . |
|  | samples/rev specifies the number of samples per revolution. |
|  | error in describes error conditions that occur before this node runs. This input provides standard error in functionality. |

The default is 0 deg. pair specifies the pair number of each channel. You must specify the same pair number for a pair of proximity probes. |

| 0 | CCW (default)—Counterclockwise |
| 1 | CW —Clockwise |
|  | probe angle [deg] specifies the angle value of the proximity probe. The default is 0 deg. |

LabVIEW uses this information to display the data properly and to perform extended measurements. probe x returns the DC gap value of the probe X. probe y returns the DC gap value of the probe Y. reference returns the DC gap reference. |
